reviews.comment: He is a tough grader, you must submit assignments on time and attend class. If you don't put the work in, you won't pass. However, he gives all the notes in handouts, and he tells you exactly what will be on the exams. He has a great personality and is super fun, but is strict for your own benefit so that you legitimately learn the material.

He's wonderful, his classes are hard but he has heart and he cares. Honestly, probably one of the easier classes to pass if you have him, he'll really put the work in to make sure you at the very least pass. Make sure you go to the office hours though

The way assignments are due at the end and immediately at the beginning of the following week. Needs to be more considerate of students taking multiple courses. Has homework projects and exam due on the same week almost every other week. He needs to get evaluated by other professors in the department don't think he is following school guidelines.

Took this class online and it wasn't difficult at all. He posts videos for you to follow along with for every topic and they're very clear and helpful. The quizzes and tests aren't hard at all. I probably emailed him asking for help or something 5 times and he responded within an hour each time with detailed explanations and help. (Summer 2025).
His jokes be hittin

I really like him personally and he's very funny. Unfortunately his exams are weighted so heavily and they're really difficult. You'll frequently get questions on the exam that were not covered a lot.  Sometimes looking at the exams and quizzes feels like looking at new material. You also get points taken off very frequently.

discrete math changed my brain chemistry. I wrote a review after exam 2, I just took exam 3 and I took what I said back, don't take discrete math if you wanna leave as the same person. not too much on adriano he tries his best, but the questions on the exam were on sections of the chapter or slideshow that we sped through in class.

He is a funny and compassionate guy. However, his Discrete class is one of the most challenging courses you will ever take. You will like him as a person but his communication skills and social awareness are very poor for a professor.  AVOID AT ALL COSTS...except you can't because the alternative is Dana Fine. Don't believe me? Go see for yourself.

Discrete math changed my life and you start to adjust to his accent after like 2 weeks. He gets a bad rep for being strict but after your first exam you realize that everything he drills into your head like studying in groups and not using your intuition is right and he was right all along, he says the harsh truth but really cares about his childre

I am taking his 181 discrete math class right now. He does not let you drift by through class, forces you to engage and pay attention which is good. Funny and cares about if you understand and are doing well. People who don't like him are probably just lazy. If you want to prepare for his class review algebra.
he is only so tough with grading and lectures because he wants people to learn not just pass
Should have looked at the reviews before taking the class. Barly any practice problems given. just tells you how you do it for one problem and says that's it... lecture is done.
Accent combine with quick speech makes a horrible combo

Goes off-topic every class, never prepares enough for exams, and the review exams are often different from the actual exams. I only took him because he was the only professor who would fit into my schedule and I would not recommend him. He's an energetic guy but a horrible professor. Would suggest taking over the summer instead of having him.
He's a great professor I wish I had him for more of my classes
he sucks

Professor Marzullo is hilarious in lectures and knows how to grab your attention but be very aware if you're going to take his class you need to be ready to work. You will not do well if you do not put in the work. He gives small extra credit for exams if you do the review but that's all the leniency he gives. He holds his students accountable.

Funny professor that tries to keep all of us alive in a 2 hour math class. Gives extra points on exams if you do the review and drops lowest hw/quiz grades at the end of the semester. Not too hard, just do the homework and review problems and you'll be good.

He teaches a difficult class, but he tries his hardest to make it as easy as possible. The class was very test heavy but they were all scaled, and he dropped a few homeworks and quizzes. He is very passionate and caring.

His lectures can be pretty confusing, containing few examples and basically just reciting the slides.

Does not belong teaching this material. Hard to communicate with, whenever you have questions or need help, he tells you to just "pay attention more" or look at homework, hardly helps you out. If you dig yourself a hole in this class, good luck getting out, because this class gets worse and worse each unit, extremely hard.

love this man. he tries getting along with the class and cracks jokes a lot. violently italian. not a bad thing but he can be eccentric. sings randomly in class. he genuinely cares about wanting his students to pass and wants to be approachable. you definitely need to attend class to pass. would definitely take him again.

Ill give it to you straight this guy is teaches very difficult subjects and his tests are difficult.  Never once has he lied about the tests, quizzes, homework, etc.  He is always there for you!  He will always push his students to work their absolute hardest.  Definitely the toughest A+ Ive ever earned, but working for it felt like an honor!!

I had Professor Marzullo for an online math course and I think the way he had it set up was great. Even though it was an online course, he had slides available for each lesson and he gave practice assignments to complete (not for credit) for all quizzes and exams. He also responds to emails in a timely manner. Recommended if you have math trouble.

Adriano loves math a bunch, and if you show him you like math too he cares about you and wants to see you succeed in not only class, but life in general. His tests can be a big step though, you might see some stuff on there that really makes you think so make sure to study hard. A hard teacher to crack an "A" with (mine was a 92.6 that rounded up)
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Hilarious

reviews.comment: Adriano has no problem failing out his students, going to office hours dont help he just directs you to the homework. He is very confusing and doesnt make sense most of the time. You will have to learn everything on your own he teaches difficult subjects that other professors skip. Do not take this class.

Prof. Marzullo is the best mathematics professor I have ever had. You won't find a professor who cares more about or is more fascinated by the field of mathematics than Adriano. He cares immensely for his profession and is by far the most passionate professor I've ever had. He cares about his students, IF THEY ARE WILLING TO PUT IN THE WORK. 11/10.

Adriano is a bit hard to understand due to his accent for some people. Sometimes he would go off topic in order to inspire us. Also, he expects you to know the basics of Calculus 1, 2, and 3 for this class. He is tough at understanding at the beginning but it gets easier at the end. His exams are to check if you've mastered the topics.

Having taken plenty of math classes for my major and being an engineering where that's our thing, math. I've never struggled so hard in a class where I understood the material. (That I taught myself.) Grading unclear and he gets mad if you question it. The worst.

Lots of homework and individual studying to understand the concepts. His accent makes him a little difficult to understand but overall was considerate to the class's struggles. You have to work for your grade but I very much respect him as a math professor.

This teacher was difficult to understand. I had him for Calc 2. I advise that right from the start go to his office hour because he has high expectations. Also dont assume you have the content in class understood its obviously 100x harder in the test. I ended up barely passing but that's because of lots of tutoring and practice.

His accent is tricky, but you get used to it. Tests and quizzes are very important, and even sometimes if you feel prepared it is easy to fail them. He will occasionally give take home quizzes or exams and encourages you to work with others. I really respected him as a teacher although I did have a calc background that helped.

Adriano's teaching style is not for everyone. As long as you pay attention in class and ask questions, you should be fine. Don't expect to be hand fed. He is merely an instructor and can only provide you with information unless you visit him in his office. There is a lot of work, but he does it to prepare you for the next semester.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--Inspirational

reviews.comment: At start, his language barrier was insane. The TA didn't even know what he was saying half the time. As the semester progressed, understanding what he was saying became easier but found that I was already in such a deep ditch, I had to withdraw. If you can balance understanding what he's saying with learning how to do calculus then this is your man
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Super duper unpredictable, emotional teacher meaning if hes in a bad mood that week expect a lot of work. Sometimes it seems like he cares a lot but then when you go for help he could care less. LOTS of quizzes and tests, changes style all the time. Teacher CALC via LECTURE with minimal examples.
Meh. A walking contradiction. Lacks originality or depth.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: She's truly a wonderful person. If you attend her class and complete the assignments, passing is quite easy. She encourages us to work independently, which is great, and often has us watch films, making it a very positive experience.

I had Prof Arford for CJS 190 (wasn't an option to pick this), and the class was extremely easy. If you need an easy A, take this class. Other than that, nothing stuck out about her. She is monotone and opinionated.

Be Careful with her class because she's not what everybody is saying about her, she explains stuff but has no clue of what she's talking about, she does let you retake the test "but what in the world are you learning"... I don't even think she should be teaching, teaching the material to you is like she's reading it of something. Avoid her at max..
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: monotone voice, she makes me want to fall asleep in CJS 361.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Arford has been BY FAR my favorite professor to take for any CJ courses at UMD. She doesn't mess around, and she WILL fail you if you don't do the work, but she makes it easy when you can see how passionate she is about what she does. Her passion will rub off on you and because of that makes you want to succeed in her course.

Professor Arford is by far one of the most passionate and respected teachers I've had in college. If you're committed to the class and keep up with assignments, class will be very enjoyable. She will open your eyes to the truth and injustices associated with our everyday lives, as well as ways to go about fixing them.
Professor Arford is a great professor with a lot of passion. I learned a lot in her class, and the work load was very reasonable. Keep up with the readings and show up to class and you will get an A.

Arford was amazing. She is very kind and will open your eyes to injustices. not a crim justice major, but very much enjoyed this class. There is a lot of work, plus a group project at the end, but it was very enjoyable. Discussions are hard because no one wants to participate. If you do, you'll get a lot out of it!

Tammi is one of the best professors in the department. She is down to earth, laid back, respectable, and hilarious. She will open your eyes to injustices that surround you and fuel your motivation to fix them. Her passion is contagious. I highly recommend taking one of her classes. You won't regret it.

Prof. Arford opens your eyes to the many injustices of the world. Her textbook choices are very interesting reads and emotional reads as well. She plays songs and poetry in class and has deep intellectual discussions with her students. This isn't a job for her, it's her passion and you can feel it.

She is an amazing professor and is passionate about this course. Homework is weekly blogs & 3 reading questions. 3-4 page paper at the end. Attendance isnt mandatory, but show your face. I wish I had taken this class more seriously, or put a little bit more effort. Skipped a few weeks of homework, and turned the final paper a little late. Got a C+
Prof. Arford is amazing!!! One of the best professors at UMD! Highly recommend her, you won't regret it! Cares for her students and you can tell she enjoys teaching. She is just awesome.
Excellent professor! Really cares about her students, if you show effort and ask for help and feedback, she is there for you. Highly recommended professor.

Prof. Arford is amazing! The material is complex and dry, but somehow she makes it fun. You have to work for your grade, she won't cut you any brakes if you're lazy and don't do the readings. She is always there to help you when you ask for it though. You can tell she cares for Her students. Mouth of sailor, but very kind and understanding.
Attendance mandatory, class is interesting, very helpful, easy writing assignments, always willing to offer extra help Really Recommend

I graduated a few years ago, If you're actually interested in finance, try to talk with him, tons of wisdom, and he has jokes if you pay attention. There's also a student managed fund that will give you a bunch of experience managing money, best college experience I got. Ask him about that if you want to get ahead of everyone else.

avoid this dude . all bro talks about is " jumbos " and " bonds" . He acts like were masters students when we are FU**Ing undergraduate students . Scales all of his exams because everyone fails them and the median is always under a C - . Should be a wake up call ... anderson . Umass .. fire this man at once .
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: how this professor is still in the faculty is beyond me. Quite literally teaches nothing but at least he's energetic.
Avoid this dude like the plague. Boring lectures and the exams have nothing to do with what he rambles on about during the class. He scales the tests because everyone fails them.
Holy hard. His practice test review is JUST scribbles of problems that you can barley read. His teaching style just feels off.

Professor Michael Anderson is definitely passionate about finance but this class is so hard. He seems like he doesn't care about if you understand the material or not. What you learn in class, homework and study guide won't be the same on the exam. His exams are so hard it make you feel if finance its really for you, just be ready to suffer

He scales every exam but I don't think he understands the concept of teaching very well. He teaches like he's talking to his friends with masters in finance and they're talking about random topics. it's very frustrating.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Prof. Anderson is extremely intelligent and passionate about finance and his job. He was mostly anti-tech until Covid-19 forced his classes to move online, and he did a great job. Exams questions can be esoteric. Material itself is advanced, so bring a notebook and study the documents he sends each week.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: No Mycourses, professor is corny, means well but can't teach at all. assumes you know too much. All lectures are based off his trash handwriting in class. His handwriting is atrocious seriously, it is illegible and makes learning the dense material even harder. He goes off on off topic tangents alot too. This school has terrible selection of staff
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Professor doesn't believe in MyCourses. Course is unorganized. Everybody in this class would have failed if he didn't scale our exams, which he luckily does. Exams are really tough. Attendance at every class. Lectures are long. You need to really like finance and focus to do well in this class. Online homework is tricky. Final paper due.

Michael Anderson is a tough professor. I would never take a class with him again. He just assumes you understand the material. He does not have an online page with notes and only hand-writes notes on the projector. His handwriting is very hard to read and his exams are extremely hard. He curves almost everything but even so, his exams are hard.

Anderson is a genius and an amazing professor. He wants you to succeed and is fair, helpful, and passionate about the subject. You must attend class, put in effort, and take good notes. Totally worth it. Appreciated both courses I took with him.

In order to pass this class you need to be passionate about Finance. This class is lecture heavy and he does not directly teach from the textbook. He teaches you conceptual theory in class. On the exam he may ask problems that require reverse engineering, which should be no problem if you understand the concept. Huge curves on tests. You must Study
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: He is literally one of the best professors at Umass. If you want to learn finance take him. He is passionate, extremely knowledgable , and fair. As long as you go to class, take good notes, and study a decent amount for exams you will be fine. You get your moneys worth by taking one of his classes.

I chose professor Anderson because he was the only professor to speak English. This class was so tough and in the end I had to stop by his office for extra help. The study guides were extremely helpful but the exams were still tough. Lectures were long and he doesn't use PowerPoint so you have to try to read his terrible handwriting. Smart guy

I have him now for Financial Institutions.  He was the only professor teaching the course at a time I could make. Pros:This guy is really smart, loves finance, and is the only professor that I've had so far that is American. That's it really. Cons:Long lectures, no power point slides to reference, lecture doesn't match text book, tests super hard,

Horrible Professor. Exam material does not match class lectures and homework assignments. Suggested Problems in the book are nothing like the questions he asks on the exam. He told us to buy the Study Guide because he said it was useful, but it really isn't and the explanations are not helpful at all. Waste of 50 dollars.

Not a good professor, what he talks about in class has next to no relevance to the material on the tests and homework. He just goes on and on, about nothing of substance. If he taught the class, and not relied on the textbook to the do the teaching, he would be an ok professor, but he doesn't that is why he is so bad.
Anderson is the man, plain and simple. He makes boring finance topics interesting with his teaching methods. Holds class long sometimes but he always keeps you on your toes. Very smart guy.
They man tries to be funny but it falls very flat.  Nothing that he talks about in class is what is on the exams. If it weren't for the fact that he scales, everyone would fail this class.

Funny and nice guy, but his class is very difficult. 3 exams, but he scales a little bit. No attendance, but be sure to come to class and write everything down b/c it's different than the text. Exams half problems and half MC and T/F. Very hard tests so study hard! Helpful for questions, and gives you a study guide. If u take him, be ready to do wo
This guy will gladly help you on anything you ask, never seems out to get the student.
Had him for two classes, always ready to help.  SHOW UP and PAY ATTENTION and you will have no worries.  But then again, the same is true for most courses out there.
Very good teacher. Good job. Thank you

I've had Anderson for 3 courses, I can respect how he genuinely has a liking for finance, along with the fact that he comes up with the material as he goes, and does not read off of slides... His exams are tough but w/ good preparation ur fine,  it is all graded relative, so beat ur classmates
Cool Guy

Really good guy - funny, fair and I think one of the best teachers I've had in my 5 years - and there are not many in this category. Excellent textbook choice. If you read the textbook before the class - you're absolutely safe. And if you have questions he can help you. Good job!!!!!!!!!!
ROCK ON
No Comments
He's the man
She was very kind but strict on attendance and took off points on grade if over 5 absences.

I genuinely wish she could teach every single one of my classes. Her class is strictly lecture, but she finds a way to make the content actually interesting and gets the class to care about what they're learning. Class is so easy to get an A in as long as you keep up with due dates

There is a LOT of information for this class and it was a hard class but I wouldn't take it with any other professor. Dr Alves makes it a lot easier and if you have questions she WILL answer them. Just stay focused. The tests are a bit challenging but she explains things ver well and she will help you if you ask her for it. TAKE HER!!!!

The content in this class wasn't my favorite but Dr Alves is such a great professor. She honestly just wants everyone to succeed and pay attention. She's tough but honest and it helped me stay on task and pay attention. Just do your work and pay attention and you'll pass. I would take her every semester if I could.

Her lectures are great, especially if you read the chapter ahead of time, because she will explain anything the book didn't do a great job on. She is unbelievably knowledgeable, so ask questions. She is highly energetic and has a strong personality. Pay attention, and don't use your phone. It's rude anyway. The book is also good, try to read it.

take her, always drop the lowest grades for each thing, like for exams it best 2 out of 3, her class is good but make sure that you are paying attention. her exams are 12 questions plus 4 short answers, she doesn't take attendance every day it's random, and sometimes when we have assignments in class she has discussion boards, RW, She is good

She is an easy professor to take, but her teaching methods are not the best. That does not matter all that much because her class was very easy. Many people like her personality and I would recommend taking her class.

quizzes are after every chapter it's in the book and online. Her exams are online, but there are only 4 she drops one of them and replaces the grade with your lowest exam grade. TAKE HERRRRR. promise you pick her and you will pass. The only thing is she takes attendance randomly, and she has some Fridays where you have to be there to do the work

Surprisingly one of my toughest courses even though it was supposed to be an introduction class. She grades very harshly. Attendance is mandatory and she is always upset. She grades unfairly and changes the grading criteria during classes. Before submitting anything ask her in class if you did it right. Do all of the work.

This class is an easy A as long as you show up. Homework that you can keep completing until you reach a desired grade, and straightforward tests. This is a true 101 class, in my opinion. However, you have to show up! She allows 3 absences then deducts 10 points off of your final grade for each.

Alves is a chill professor. Very easy going, organized and her lectures can be a little long, but for the most part they are interesting. Sometimes she records attendance so be careful. Overall, super easy class and only consists of hw's and exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Test heavy--Graded by few things--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Lectures were all info-dumps, made it hard to understand topics while taking notes. Professor flies through lectures just to get through them. Exams and HWs are all online, which is helpful, but still really challenging topics. If you take this class, I recommend studying a lot and asking questions during class. Also take notes on slides

5 tests that are 50% of your grade. there's itnquizitives to do every week and a half. There is extra credit at the end of the semester which is easy. You have to show up because she does attendance randomly. Overall should be an easy A if you just show up to class.

Tests were all online and for the most part took information straight from her lectures. The homework was from the book and was time consuming but easy overall. She did take attendance and would take 11% off your grade for missing more than 3 classes so you have to show up but overall she is fun to have an its an easy class
Overall your grade comes from application friday's, online quizzes, and online exams. Shes fun and funny but her lectures are rather long and all the info is the in the book

She can get off topic but in a good way. All her lecture definitely do influence your grade based off of your notes. It is a very fast paced course but it can definitely be interesting and easy as long as you stay on top of your stuff. Quizzes are easy and honestly so are the exams. Plus she offers extra credit!

This was the professor's first time teaching this class, and she went a little overboard on the homework because of this it feels like. She is very nice and can be funny, but also has very unclear grading/formatting criteria. This isn't that much of a problem though because she is a very easy grader.

Scary at first, but you can tell shes truly passionate about her job. She really tries to make difficult info easy to understand and she doesnt include some really hard and unnecessary topics on quizzes and tests to make things easier. Absolutely loved her and lectures were really helpful.
Very funny and personable professor. She is very direct about whats on the exams. Drops your lowest scores. Easy class if you pay attention

Dr Alves will most likely scare the cr*p out of you when you first meet her, i'm not going to lie.. But she is actually amazing, and if I could take more classes with her I would! She cares about what she teaches so of course she'll be hard on you. Just show her you're trying! She does give pop quizzes but trust me, you'll figure out her algorithm.

Dr Alves gives great lectures by not only supplying you with the slides, but adds to them when she feels Pearson hasnt done you justice. Will bring a brain at least once. Cancels class every time she gives an exam (4 per semester). Attendance isnt necessary but you learn more from how she lectures.

I took Dr. Alves for research methods Fall '18 & loved her! This was her first year teaching and rocked it. The class itself is a lot of work as all research methods are. She goes above and beyond to make sure your group is collecting data and edits sections of the final paper. There will be 2 short essays, 1 test & a semester group project/paper.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Dr. Alves brought a lot of energy to the class and was able to engage our Cognitive psych class well. Tough considering much of the class is about reading research and pop-quizes about metathinking. Participation is graded.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Eh. Doesn't take attendance and though she's well educated and kind- you probably learned more in high school Psych or AP Psych. The class is dumb easy and I would take it again in a heartbeat- she just isn't entertaining. If you're looking for an easy 101 course, look no further.

Professor Alves is very knowledgeable about what she teaches. Her lectures are amazing, she knows what areas students need to focus on to be successful. Although this class was an easy A, there were a lot of things you can learn from her class. She's very easy-going and funny

Dr. Alves wants her students to not only succeed but understand what they are learning. She came in halfway through the semester after our other teacher was fired, told my group our design was worthless and helped us design our experiment. Her enthusiasm for the subject is apparent and I am taking her again for another class.

Dr. Alves is an absolute gem. She really cares about her students. MAKE THE EFFORT TO GET TO KNOW HER. She tells a lot of funny stories which makes class really entertaining. She wants to see you be successful and pass her class. She is very accessible outside of class and responds to email promptly. She is the absolute best!!

Dr. Alves is the best professor by far in the psychology department. All of the quizzes and exams are online. There are two writing assignments throughout the semester and they are easy. She tells you what she is looking for and is easy to access outside of class. She really cares about her students. Definitely take the chance to get to know her!

Professor Alves is really nice and funny. Her class is easy, good lectures (all tests and quizzes are online). She does give 3 or 4 written minor assignments which she brags about never giving anyone a perfect score (you will most likely still do fine). You can tell she really loves her job and cares for her students. Attendance is not mandatory.

Dr. Alves loves her job. She likes to get to know her students (if you sit in the front row) and gives you many chances to get your work done. She is very lenient and shouldn't be taken advantage of. The course isnt hard, but if you're in psych, you know that reading is important and being able to know the material gone over in class.

Took her at RIC last year but couldn't find an option to grade her through them. One of those classes you just dread showing up to. She'd have us sit in a circle and listen to her delusional yap sessions. Anti-carceral feminism? Not a fun class. Extremely emotional prof and the end of year assignment relied on group work which is always a gamble.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: weekly readings and videos, 3 quizzes, 10 discussion boards, final project, and final paper. Easy class if you can keep up. Readings for MW, Videos to watch on Friday, and discussion boards on fridays, when theres no DB theres a quiz. Quizzes cover are very specific to info in videos or reading, not based on overall concepts of videos /readings
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: The freshmen dragging Dr. Ruderman-Looff CLEARLY don't get how CJS works. She's just like other CJS profs. Expect a lot of reading. There are a lot of group assignments, but she gives you time in class to work. Honestly I had the best group experience in this class because everyone showed up and did their part. Group evals were a plus.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Tough grader

reviews.comment: In Prof. Looff's own words, "I'm not interested in criminology. I'm interested in social justice and how race plays into the CJS." Don't expect to learn anything about criminal justice. All her assignments are political. If you are Republican or just not far left, keep quiet or you will be silenced. The political posters behind her desk say it all.

Professor Ruderman-Looff is the most unprofessional and downright awful Prof. I've had. She is one sided and teaches her personal beliefs which are not always factual. She gives "deliberately vague" instructions and refuses to answer questions, give study guides, or any formal instruction. She taught me if I can deal with her, I can do anything.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: Upon request she allowed me to take one of her quizzes 2 days late as I had 2 finals that same day. She also allowed constant make ups for DBs. All you have to do (like with most profs) is be honest with her and ask. However! if you do not test well without a study guide, take class with caution. That is my only warning! Overall very kind!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Test heavy

reviews.comment: I am not sorry, but I would never take one of her classes again she says one thing but means another. Then after a project is due tells you it's wrong and makes you redo it at the last minute. She is very one-sided. She pushes a lot of her views into an on the class. She doesn't help you when you ask questions. She is horrible and doesn't help.
There are no words. She's the worst Professor I've ever had.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: She explains the material in a way that's easy to understand. Also gives lots of extra credit. No midterms or finals, just exams that make up the majority of the grade. Definitely have to study for this class but she makes it easy to pass.

I had professor Arkerson for development psych and just passed her psy 250 class. Her classes are mostly lecture with some "pop" in-class activities. Technically there isnt attendence taken, but you are graded through these random class activities that happen a few times in the semester. I had no problem on her exams just by studying lecture slides

The app that is used for homework sucks if you get it wrong the score goes down and take a couple questions for the score to go up. Before each exam I study for 2 hours the day before. there's no midterm or final.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Arkerson was great for psych101. There were four tests and you only needed to take the final if you missed an in-class test. If you go to class and stay on top of your homework, you should get an amazing grade.

Super easy class, theres five exams and she drops the lowest grade plus some pretty easy homework for each module. The material isnt difficult, so just listen in lectures and take notes but direct energy to your more difficult classes this one is cozy.

If you take this class, show up and pay attention. Complete the assignments, they are paced well and not too much at once. Make sure you actually study for the tests a day or two before bc the content is hard to remember. She does a great job explaining tho! She does a Jeopardy review that earns you points on the exam, she drops lowest exam.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: I loved her class ! She is soft spoken and sweet . She will hold you accountable. I know this class material is very hard but if you focus on the book more youll be fine .
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: This class was a challenge, but I liked Professor Arkerson a lot. She understood that the material was hard and offered lots of extra credit. She's very quiet, and her voice is soft so it's easy to get distracted in class. I would recommend taking notes on your own before class so you can focus on what she says. Make sure you study for the exams!!

Had her for 250 and Physio Methods. Good Professor for Methods Course. Class has reasonable expectations, and she's always available for extra help. She'll let you hand things in a few days late (but don't make it a habit) and there is a lot of extra credit opportunities so make sure you take advantage of it.

Very kind woman, makes the class comfortable. Take it with a friend so it will be easier. Frequent quizzes online, make sure to get 100 on all, it will help your grade later. Several tests, not too hard but make sure to study. Final is NOT cumulative!  Always do extra credit, its easy and a LOT of points. Sort of hard work, but lots of fun.

For a tough topic, Dr. Arkerson does a great job of making the material understandable. She doesn't have the most extroverted personality but it's easy to look past that. She scales when necessary (but usually only a few points) and offers extra credit for going to review days.

It was a pretty boring class and pointless because she went over powerpoints that were on mycourses for us to see on our own. As long as you bring something with you to do during class it won't be bad. It's an easy class. No homework. Iclicker is needed. If you took Psychology in highschool you'll pass.

Although I am a Psychology Major, bio is not one of my interests. However, Professor Arkerson made this class interesting. She tries to make the material interesting. She does not take attendance and gives a lot of extra credit. Every exam is scaled and she drops your lowest test grade. She posts all material online. I would recommend her.

PSY 101 was really boring. I love psychology but every week being a vague, shallow covering of a different topic was pretty lame. Arkerson's slight drone and unengaging textbook recitation made my favorite subject one of my least favorite classes this semester. Easy enough though, I'm looking forward to better classes later on.

Really easy teacher, she isn't very lively in class but she does not take attendance, u can get a good grade by just studying notes, in response to the guy below yes she throws a lot of info at you but you can get a C or D on the test and she will scale it to an A.. extremely friendly and warm wonderful teacher u just have to study
Very imformative, but too much info for an intro, less is more! Multiple strategies for teaching which is a plus compared to most teachers there!

She is very good at explaining the material. Even though she doesn't take attendance, in order to do well, you must show up. She can be a bit monotone and dull at time but is overall a very good teacher that stimulated interest in the topic. Very interesting course.

Arguably one of the worst profs. to have for pysch. She taught 101 like it was a 200-level class & taught 250 like it was a 300-level class. Throws tons of info @ you as if youve already mastered it. Explanations arent clear. Shes very dry & boring. its hard to pay attention or learn anything. Unless you love bio, you wont do very well in her class

Deep down, shes very passionate about her work. She's caring and seems to have a heart. However, I would not recommend her as a professor. She isn't open to ideas that are not strictly her own. Nothing you could do, contribute, or come up with will be good enough. She grades according to this impossible standard she created. Avoid this teacher :/

Incredibly rude and doesn't care to help students. I was hospitalized for 2 weeks in the start of the semester. Instead of trying to help me catch up, she told me to drop the class multiple times. She told me that my "lack of attendance" would really affect my grade (v illegal btw). I had to report to the dean about her.  Passed just to spite her.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--So many papers

reviews.comment: If you're not eng major, or don't like group work, avoid this class. The content is interesting enough, but if you have a heavy courseload this class will make it worse. 2-3 hour movie + class discussion + HW + ridiculous group assignments. Group assignments are zoom recordings, she grades harsh. Doesn't respond to emails.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--So many papers

reviews.comment: She gives lots of work with little time to get it done, you read one book after another..after another..after another!! Her grading is unrealistic and you have to answer everything perfectly to get full credit.  I didnt even learn anything in this class.. it was pretty much a waste of time and money

Fairly nice and interesting. Grades hard on Discussions. Expects WAY too much, and that's coming from someone who had a 4.0 before transferring to this school. Beware of group projects-if the class didn't read/participate in discussion, you're blamed. I wouldn't go out of my way to take her again, but I wouldn't avoid her either.

The combination of the demanding work load as well as well as textbook usage, this is not a professor I would recommend to anyone. Her attitude is poor and she seems to only care about writing a grade on your paper instead of actually helping you learn the material. Not one assignment had me interested in the course content. Avoid if possible.
Demanding work load. Students pass back graded papers - inappropriate! BORING!
English litrerary studies. Not enjoyable.
Extremely high expectational level for a 200 level class. No personality. Not flexibale.

A genius, she let's us all learn within our personal way of relating to the world.  The course, though, is a left-brained trip that no one has even gone into before.  May the 'force be with you' in other words.  Taking her courses again, is absolutely what I would consider.
A brain exercising course, with a professor who knows as much as the author of our text, with a lot more social-humanitarism so we, as students, can find our way around in the world of critiquing.

Professor Arora is a great teacher. Although her class is difficult, if you do the work and participate in class here and there, you will do fine. It was a lot of work, but I did all the assignments, went to class, and paid attention and I got a B. She is one of the rare teachers that actually wants her students to succeed. Definitely take her.

A tough class, but Professor Arora's ENL 258 will prepare you for any other literature class you might have to take at the University. The reading and workload is heavy. She gives several pop-quizzes throughout the semester, so attendance is mandatory. Overall, she is a very kind and helpful teacher.

Professor Arora is new to UMass which may explain her lack of direction and clear instruction.  The work seemed rushed and she expected too much from students without providing proper instruction.  Maybe she'll figure it out soon, but until then, students beware! She demands a lot of last minute difficult assignments.

I had him as my TA, and I felt so lucky after hearing about some of the other TAs. He really knows what he's talking about, gives you a good amount of useful information before labs, walks around and actively helps everybody, and is very welcoming to office hours to ask for feedback on quizzes, write-ups, and lab material.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Dr. V was an okay professor. I respected him. I loved the extra credit opportunities he made available throughout most of the semester! However, I hope he gets better at asking questions and desiring answers from everyone in the class, not just a select few. Also, BEWARE: a single chapter of his slides is nothing less than 80 slides to study.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Respected

reviews.comment: Dr V is awesome, super nice, and cares about what he is teaching. He loves helping students and answering questions. Loved his medmicro lab, I was never bored and always had a good time.

Alan ran my lab for Anatomy I. He was hilarious, caring, and very informative. He made class fun, and helped students as much as he could. If I can take his class for a future course, I will be doing it in a heartbeat. Very well spoken, makes sure everyone knows what they are doing, and is very knowledgeable on what he teaches.
He was pretty cool and the majority of the syllabus consisted of 50% tests and quizzes and 50% final paper.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Dr. V is great! His exams are directly from his lectures and he always highlights the content hell test on. There are no surprises. He offers a TON of extra credit. The homework is not hard but very time consuming. This is a class that will require a bit of studying but is very very doable.

Dr. Ventetuolo is fantastic. I have nothing negative to say about him at all. He is a fair, but realistic grader. He takes the time to meet with you outside of class if you request it. Loves to answer questions, even if they were not directly related to the material. He has gone above and beyond to encourage me and be supportive.

Honestly, I have never been so aggravated by a professor in my life. He constantly looks at me while my hand is raised and makes a point to not answer my questions. Its not just me, its the whole class though. Honestly I should have dropped this course. He seemed interesting in the beginning but he just doesn't give anyone a minute to ask questions
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Hard exams many people withdrew. Nursing majors: TAKE THIS OVER SUMMER
Test are pretty hard so make sure you participate, come to class and ask questions. Amazing class and teacher.

Very nice and easy going professor. Exams were semi hard. This class isnt supposed to be for nursing majors but feels like you have to be a nursing major to understand. He takes his time after and during class to make sure you understand. But its boring because he just reads from powerpoint. YOU NEED THE BOOK

seems like a decent guy, but just a terrible teacher. You're essentially there to listen to him read a powerpoint to you, no actual teaching what so ever. unless you already love science I highly recommend never taking a class of his. It's disappointing that this type of teaching is even allowed at a University.

I found his class hard!  His 103 was anatomy.  His lectures are very boring because he speaks pretty monotone.  It's hard to study for tests because the Powerpoint slides are over 100 and there's no study guide at all so it's kind of impossible to know what's going to be on the exams.  I ended up withdrawing.  Also gotta pay $130 for textbook code

Ventetuolo is such a kind man who loves teaching  microbiology. He knows a lot about micro and his labs are fun. Apparently there is less chapters covered so lectures are long, slow, and I sometimes fall asleep. Not many exams but they are not to hard & no final. I manly used the Power point to study but the book helps sets it in your mind.

His classes are easy because it is online tests. Make sure to have some knowledge of the info though because the tests can be difficult if you haven't looked over anything. He's also a really nice guy but lectures make me want to fall asleep. I at least once a week and so did pretty much the rest of the class

Dr. V was a very knowledgable professor but his lectures were long and he could ramble on sometimes. I believe his tests varied from easy to difficult but over the class test averages on most exams were around 78. Reading is helpful but not mandatory. PRINT OUT THE NOTES!

Very interesting class on the cell biology of the human body that I would recommend for non bio majors. Lectures are for the most part clear and helpful. Tests (4) are pretty much your whole grade and can be challenging, but with moderate preparation you should be fine. Dr. V is very helpful if you approach him and the class is an easy B.

Prof. Ventetuolo is a wonderful teacher.  He responds almost immediately to your questions.  He made the topic very interesting.  I highly recommend him, and would definitely take another class with him in the future.

One of the best professors I've ever had. Clear lecture, straight forward test. Cares about the material and the student. VERY knowledgeable. Highly recommend this professor! If you can take him, DO! He's very helpful and tries to make even dull things interesting, usually succeeds. Lots of practical applications . Most helpful man ever. LOVE HIM!
Easy, all test online. He's old, I guess some ppl find that hot but I was disappointed and you guys lied to me. Attendance not mandatory
Great professor. My favorite class and professor so far.I learned so much in his class, book about AIDS is great to read. Very interesting class!

Great professor! Took med micro over the summer. He was extremely helpful and interesting and definitely knows his stuff. The tests can be difficult definitely study and you'll be fine! For sure the best professor I've had yet!
AIDS Class. Very nice guy who makes the material understandable. Attendance was optional, so I didn't really go much, but I still did very well on the exams and paper. Easy, interesting class!

Took his Aids class and appreciated that he understood the people in the class aren't there because they are science majors. He made the material very understandable. Answers to the tests are all in the chapter guides.There are also a number of questions based off of the videos shown in class.You must watch those and notes are a good idea.great guy

I attended his seminar on AIDS. Attendance is not mandatory. Grades were based off three tests and one paper. The tests are online and open book. The questions came directly from the chapter review and the answers are in the back of the book. Easy class

Professor V is the man! For med micro he gave online quizzes, a few tests online and half the final online! He will change test dates if enough people want him to and he is very helpful in class as well as in lab. He also drops your lowest test grade! He makes the class interesting so I didn't mind going, but attendance is not required!

Difficult to understand properly & used some confusing math at first, however later on once student's started to treat him as a human & got over his accent & stopped trying to undermine him, he became an amazing professor who consistently offered to go over homework every class, gave extra credit for each test, & open notes. overall great Teacher.

His lectures are very confusing and hard to follow. I had to learn entirely from youtube videos and just skipped the lecture by the end because I was learning nothing being there and could take the attendance grade hit. He also gets very angry at the class often yelling and insulting students for making mistakes and asking questions.

I did not understand his lectures he explained things poorly. When people did not get it he would get very frustrated at himself and the class. This made him very unapproachable for questions as he would get annoyed. He then would ask why no one asked questions. He sometimes would also be demeaning and somewhat insult students for not understanding

Really Diff Eq. not Calc 3. He was hard to understand his accent is thick and he talks quiet. His exam grading was really bad if you mess up a singular part in the beginning that question is most likely 0-2 points out of 20. He allows cengage make up assignments to bring your exam grades up which is good but overall very difficult to learn from.

I could understand his accent but most of my classmates complained about it. He explained concepts very poorly, screwing over my future for calc. He is really funny, once making a joke about "not leaving tears on the exams so he could still read and grade it". It's up to you to teach yourself. Id drop to algebra just for a different professor.
Nice instructor, many opportunity to improve grade!

I had a hard time in the beginning because he has a hard time explaining thoroughly since he is crazy smart. You catch onto his methods but you need to go to class or you'll be SO lost. Gives sm extra points. He seems scary and abrupt at times but is the kindest person which wants his students to do good If you REALLY show you try you can't fail.

I had to take him for my MTH 146 class and I wish I didn't. He gave Homeworks and Quizzes every other day no matter what, even over thanksgiving break. He's hard to understand because of his accent and I had to teach myself everything outside of class and get tutoring a lot. Only reason I got an A was because of multiple quiz, hw, and exam atempts

If you are in this class don't be scared by the reviews.Attendance is mandatory but you can join zoom you do not need to show up to his class. All assignments are done online on the e-textbook and makeup attempts are allowed on all homework and quizzes. If you are a good student and stay on top of your work this class shouldn't be too hard.
Avoid Him.

By the time you get into the last few units of this class he will start solving the problems using excel and online calculators, he will not explain what he is doing in excel or how to do it, you may not use either on exams. He teaches by answering the homework questions on the board, won't explain anything, and he frequently gets answers wrong.

Was not great at explaining things and had an accent. Now I have no problem with accents but if your accent is not native, people will get confused sometimes, so you shouldn't get upset if you have to repeat yourself. He would get mad if you asked him to repeat himself. Tests were very hard as well. You are basically fending for yourself.

I am a freshman and it has not even been two weeks in the class and i know it's gone be hell make sure you don't attend this class. You understand every other word he says, and he don't teach for the class he teaches for himself.

Dr. Artamoshin is an outstanding Professor. He will go through any problem a student is struggling with step by step until the student understands the information. Accessible through email and office hours (zoom or in person) and will do more practice problems if the student is having trouble. Allows make up exams. Highly recommend this Professor.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Avoid at all costs!!!! This professor is unfit to teacher others. Started with class of 30 dropped to 5 but only 2 of us passed. You will be talked down to and belittled for 50 minutes, repeatedly told the material is easy and a child could do it. Homework averages about 88-120 questions a chapter, for about 25 chapters. Extremely heavy on homework
Just avoid at all costs, for your own good.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Be prepare to teach yourself the whole time. He has a very thick accent, and it is very difficult to understand what he is saying.
Don't take this class.

Very tough to understand and his teaching style is... questionable, and he will constantly ridicule you for not understanding a problem. All teaching is done by going over homework. Be prepared to teach yourself the material, as his lectures are basically impossible to understand. What helps his case is the test makeups and the extra credit pts.

Do not take any class with him. HEAVY accent, stands at the front of the class and does problems basically to himself, is rude when someone asks a question. never answers emails. Only good thing is we have taken most of our tests outside of the classroom. Class is basically study hall twice a week because he doesnt care what you're doing.

GOING TO TEACH YOURSELF THE MATERIAL! Extremely thick accent and be prepared to have him make you feel dumb if you don't understand something. Zoom classes and test if sick. Lectures is going over hw the whole time. Doesn't care if you're paying attention. Accessible through email. Recommend getting friends in this class if you want to pass.

If you want to get the best out of your tuition do not take this class you will end up teaching yourself while the professor is hypocritical calling you he student useless and stupid when in fact he is the useless professor. If you are afraid of asking questions in class in general do not take this class because you will likely be criticized.
Terrible Professor. Most, if not all, teaching is done by going over the homework. Hard to understand with thick accent. Can be rude and condescending sometimes.

This man gives me anxiety, only take if you already know it or are fully capable of teaching yourself the entire course material with no guidance besides chapter names. Be prepared for verbal abuse.
Notable quotes:
"At least there are some smart students."
"Why are you struggling? Its simple!"
"No, I already showed you how to do that problem"

Relatively easy grader, but it wasn't worth the poor instruction. His lecture format of working on problems from homework sets means you'll have to go further on your own if you want true understanding of this course. He gives a ton of extra credit for participation. Prepare to bomb tests if you slack on the homework.

If you're not someone who is willing to do self study and actually put in the time to teach yourself, YOU WILL NOT PASS. He gives lots of extra credit but sometimes you won't get any because you don't know what's going on.

Probably the worst teacher/professor I have ever had. Impossible to understand through his thick accent and mumbling to himself. Has horrible lectures... talks about a topic for 15 minutes at end of class and then assigns it for (graded) homework he expects to be done by the next class.

Worst professor I have ever had, I would rather teach myself/ learn on YouTube than be taught by him. I got a tutor and learned more from the tutor in 2 hours than half a semester with professor Sergei. Gives you lots of extra credit and isn't necessarily hard to pass, but you will not learn the curriculum well if you only listen to him.

Sergei is disrespectful (regularly tells students who do not grasp a concept "This is elementary school math. You are a disgrace to your country." in a calculus course...), and spends lectures mumbling at the front of the class in a heavy Russian accent. The only benefit to taking him is he doesn't care if you pay attention to him.

If you don't take this class, make sure you don't take this guy. I took this class for my first semester of freshmen year as I thought it was required. This guy has a super thick Russian accent is hard to understand. His lectures are confusing and expects you to know it right away. I had to drop out since I knew I was going to fail. STAY AWAY!

He's a really good person, but his accent is so thick that it can be hard to understand what he says. If you have friends who can help you through the class, then he is an easy A, but if you don't you will struggle a lot.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Sergei has a hard to understand accent which makes his class so difficult. If you miss one class you can miss how to do something extremely simple but he will begin to explain it the next class. The man is a genius but he doesn't belong in the first calc class that students take. He gives a lot of extra credit which makes up for bad test grades
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Nice professor, gives many extra points to improve grade, strongly recommend.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: I found him to be a tough grader on tests. Didn't learn much in class expect to teach yourself outside of class. Often says " this is elementary school math" when you cant solve a problem. Takes final as your grade if it is higher then you average. Has no patience can be irrational. Failed all 3 tests still got an A

There is a very bad language barrier between him and his students as he is very hard to understand and he barely understands what you are saying to him. He gives extra credit, a lot of credit for homework, and his tests are honestly quite easy. I had to teach myself most of the course, however, and if you don't put in effort, you struggle a lot.

A fairly tough professor, but he's the best Calculus professor at Umass as of 2018. He can be difficult to understand at times and is a bit sassy with students, but even if you do awful in the class, if you do well on the final he'll set your final grade to that and vise versa. Gives PLENTY of extra credit that count as actual test points.

You can go to every class and you won't know what's happening in class. If you ask a question be prepared to get yelled at and don't even try to make a mistake because he will go off on a tangent that you should have already learned it. That it's easy and a child could do it. He'll also spend half the class fixing his own examples on the board.

Heavy accent that dibilitates your ability to understand certain concepts that he "attempts" to teach his class. Gives a ton of homework, also on weekends. Does not teach very well as he explains next to nothing but expects you to understand everything. Often he asks you how to do something, say you're wrong, then explain it exactly how you did.

I dont like math, but I need to take calc 1&2&3. I have 90% attendance, HW and 3 Tests. I was very worried about my score. My final exam is not heavy to me. Dont worry tests and final is simple. Effective teaching style in the classroom. Professor gives formula sheet. Clear grading criteria. I very surprised but get A-.
Professor gives students a lot extra point at the class work, in order to improve your scores on final exam. Take his class.

Absolutely awful, the worst college professor Ive had. Most of the time you were better off skipping class and just teaching yourself everything. If you ask him a question hell insult you, and ask how you got this far. Very hard to understand his accent, but it doesnt matter anyway since nothing useful ever came out of his mouth.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: This prof is my least favorite from my college experience. The class was unnecessarily hard because he didn't present any information like it was new and just overall was very hostile to students. Had to teach myself everything. He randomly made an policy near the end that if you didn't have 100% attendance you fail the class. Avoid at all costs.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Overall, he is a really nice guy. If you do not seek help from outside sources, you will not pass. His accent is not inhibiting, however, he does not go over practice problem. He expects chapters to be read prior to class and will not go over material that is already in the book. He scales grades, which really is the reason a lot of students passed

This guy is the stereotypical bad professor. Tough accent, very rude if you have trouble understanding concepts, and is unclear about his expectations. Says he will help answer questions but he becomes furious when you actually ask for help. He gives lots of opportunities for extra credit. Easy to pass, but hard to learn anything from him.

His lessons are hard to follow and usually left me more confused than before. I taught myself pretty much everything before each test, but his tests were easy if you used the study guide and he gave a lot of extra credit.
reviews.rating_tags: TEST HEAVY--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Overall his accent does make him somewhat difficult to understand, and the class doesn't have a proper syllabus, the one that our class used was dated for 2015 for a class that met at a different time as well. However he is very knowledgeable of the subject he teaches, however he doesn't really know how to teach that well. Also the guy is ripped.
reviews.rating_tags: GRADED BY FEW THINGS--EXTRA CREDIT--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Beware of two things: His incredibly strong accent and his monotone voice. He believes in extra credit and gives you plenty of chances. Nice guy, but also very manipulative since you can pressure him to use cheat sheets on an exam. Brand new at the university though.
reviews.rating_tags: GRADED BY FEW THINGS--EXTRA CREDIT--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: This professor gave the worst class I have ever taken in my entire school career. He is an absolutely awful teacher and makes many mistakes in his work when he solves problems. More specifically, he draws out his work and overcomplicates things which confuses students even more. I am good at math but he makes me doubt all of my life decisions.

He didn't follow the syllabus in class so you were never prepared for class Also what he went over in class never had anything to do with the homework he assigned. In class he went over things in the most complicated ways and never explained how he got from one place to another. Learning new topics was nearly impossible without help from a tutor.

While he seems to know what he's talking about, this Professor doesn't know how to teach. The only people who understand the material he's trying to teach are those who already know what's going on. He tries to teach things that aren't on the syllabus, and will insult students when they ask questions. Won't explain if people are confused.

Compared to highly recommending you take him, I will be recommending you DO NOT TAKE HIM. Treats everyone poorly unless you are actually doing well in the course. All he does is read off the slides, and the slides already have barely any information. Called me out during the final for using a calculator when a guy next to me was using his PHONE.

Worst teacher ever. He treats his students as if they're dumb and brags that not many people pass his courses. He expects us all to fail, and teaches as such. His lectures are poorly organized and don't actually teach you anything. His handwriting is illegible. Grading is unfair and tests are weighted way too heavily to do well in this course.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Amazing teacher!

Your tests including the final make up 85% of your grade so don't bomb them. If the whole class does bad he likes to give a make-up test to raise your grade a little bit. Class isn't too difficult but try to pay attention and you will be fine. Lots of Cengage homework but he assigns it all at the beginning of the semester so you can do it whenever.

The professor is very caring, if you have any concerns, talk to him, he will understand. If you do his HWs and understand them, you will get good grades on the exam. He doesn't trick you. (His exam is more basic, and Dr. Fine's exam is more challenging.)

He loves his long and boring lectures. Doesnt really care for student input most of the time. If youre taking his class, be ready to sit through 50 minutes of him just talking and making jokes that are mediocre. I only passed this class with online help and my peers.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Boring lowkey
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: He is very caring, and will always help when you are struggling with the material. If you are willing to take time to read the book outside class, then you have a good chance of passing. The only issue was completing the tests within the 50 minutes of class. Worksheets are given as well as many extra credit opportunities to raise your grade.

Very passionate mathematician and teacher. His hand-writing is atrocious but his explanations are brilliant. I used the textbook on my own and really had to think for some of the homework. His review sheet for tests and for the final is very vague and the questions on his tests really test your understanding of concepts.
He writes on the whiteboard for 50 minutes and barely pauses for breath the whole time. Take notes. Goes off on interesting tangents sometimes. Lots of opportunities for extra credit.

Professor Hausknecht brings a lot of professional experience to the table as a very talented and inspiring mathematician. He truly has a passion for the subject he teaches and he is dedicated to his students and genuinely concerned about how they do in his class.

I couldn't read what he wrote on the board or feedback on anything graded and not many students were able to finish exams within the 50 min period especially in MTH 182 and wouldn't scale them. He does offer extra credit, but they are just programming assignments and really hard questions on exams. Avoid him and Prof Fine for both discrete classes.
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Terrible lectures. Seriously is out of touch
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: I have not gone a single class without erasing my notes because of how many mistakes he makes while teaching. He makes mistakes creating exams, he even made mistakes writing emails to the whole class and sent two revisions once. Professors should not be making this many mistakes so frequently.

He has less than great handwriting, and he sometimes mumbles while giving a lecture, but is overall a brilliant mathematician, and it's clear he knows what he's talking about. He gives plenty of opportunities to do extra credit, and is frequently available for extra help, and will explain the process in-depth. Ask questions and you'll pass easily.

I love professor Hausknecht. I have taken two classes with him and am looking forward to another next semester. He is a very intelligent and passionate man but to get the most out of his classes and lectures requires student work also.  He really cares about his students and will do everything he can to help them especially if you are kind to him.

As others have mentioned he makes mistakes quite often when writing examples in class and his handwriting is hard to understand at first. His teaching is alright when teaching to the entire class but he's excellent if you approach him with questions after class or during office hours. He'll make sure that you fully understand the material.

Most difficult class I've ever taken when it comes to trying to understand the lessons. He's so hard to understand, scribbles on the board and makes a lot of mistakes during lectures, and the tests are really difficult if you aren't able to teach yourself.

He will not give you a make-up for a quiz even if you're in the hospital. He doesn't care. He gets rude when you call him out on it. Oh and he puts the wrong material on the board when he lectures. Be prepared to learn from the book only. Worst professor I've ever had.
I never understood anything he was saying. He was never clear and he would just ramble on about whatever. I failed his class.
He is a great instructor.
I believe Professor Hausknecht is the best math instructor that Umassd has. He cares about the students, if they care about themselves. If you need help, he is there for you.

He makes mistakes in class and doesn't notice. His hand writing is horrendous, makes it hard to take notes. It is difficult to understand him and he losses his train of thought often. Testquiz are reasonable if you know the material. He is a very nice guy though and know his stuff, but a terrible teacher. Expect to learn it all by yourself.

Do NOT take his class. Constantly makes mistakes. Multiple times taught a full lesson then at the end told the us it was wrong. Other times he won't admit he is wrong and blames students. Doesn't explain how to do problems either. Hard to understand. Expects everyone to be as smart as him. Save yourself the trouble and avoid him at all costs.
This is the worst professor in the history of time. Seriously they need to fire this guy now. What a joke

Really difficult to follow his train of though: starts thoughts over several times. Seems really intelligent though. As far as the book: you won't need it. Honestly, the material's simple enough that if you show up for the quizzes, test reviews, and exams, you'll probably do fine. P.S. it's easier to pay attention if you only pay half attention.

Speach problem is not too bad most of the time, sometimes it is unbearable and I have walked out of class. He tends to restart sentences 5-6 times before finishing them. Theres no doubt he is extremely smart but he teaches like he is in a rush and it can be tough to keep up.
Awesome teacher. Really helpful when you go to see him. Wants to help you and make sure you understand the material. Homework easy. Test more difficult, but not too bad if you look over notes.
He is really good. I learnt a lot in his class. He stresses on the concepts a lot, and that is a good thing. He will help if you approach him. Attendance not mandatory.

Kinda sounds like Tommy Chong and tends to repeat concepts. Math oriented people should have no problem. Very willing to help you if you don't understand a concept and if you write down everything on the board you will have the  formulas you need for the tests. Textbook isn't necessary for calc as the homework is online. Brilliant but awkward.

Had him for Calc 3 and Modern Algebra. The only reason I did well in calc was because it was my second time taking the class. Class for modern algebra is boring, he doesn't create a lesson plan, and can't complete a thought. He is very helpful when you see him outside of class, but again, can't put a sentence together.
Worst professor I've ever had. Incomprehensible. I am stupider now that I have had to sit through his class.

I had him for scientific programming.  I didnt really learn anything, he doesnt teach the material.  He just gives you assignments that you type up, so when you have to do your own programs you are lost.  But he is fully willing to help you out and practically do the assignments for you.  Despite the class he is a very nice man. Awk around girls.

This guy is prolly one of the very few math teachers that has helped my understand what was going on. Most professers just give you the lecture and let you figure it out. The reason I got an A is because he helped me come to practical terms with the curriculum. I recommend this guy to alot of people. Hell appear boring if you dont like math alot.

He is a very nice man and teacher, which are usually hard to find at UMD.He cares alot about his students, so go see him as much as possible if you are confused.Well, in class is a different story...it is very boring and sometimes he is hard to follow.But don't give up, stay on track and Go SEE him!His tests are relatively similar to inclass/hw!

This was the most boring class I have ever taken.  Imagine Magoo teaching a college class.  His assignments are easy and his tests are hard...but he lets you make a cheat sheet for the test.  A good class for an elective, but you will never go to class because it is so painfully boring.
Best dude, ever. He likes beer, I like beer. My housemate is staring at me.
Hausknecht: Master of Boredom

This man is clearly brilliant, but he is not a good teacher. He cares for his students, but he doesnt display it in his classes. It is recommened you get to know him personally if you really need help, because in class help is non existent.

Wow, all I can say is he's a smart guy, he just does a horrible job teaching it to us. He plays around on his laptop all class long and the class corrects him all the time. couldn't finish a sentence if his life depended on it. Smart guy... BAD TEACHER!

easy course, which was the saving grace for me. And, English is his native language, which was a plus. He was not very convincing in explaining some of the less-intuitive concepts however. Breezed through the easy and hard parts at the same pace.
The start of this course wasn't too bad but got really dry at the end. Labs and projects are pretty easy.
In the class right now... Please kill Me

He's a brilliant man but can't teach his way out of a wet paper bag.He stutters and completes thoughts in his head before he's said it and simply moves on to the next concept.You'll pass his class but will get slammed in the next one.
Very smart man but has toruble comunicating. Think about Milton from office space teaching computer graphics. Very vague about what is expected from projects and when they are due.
switched majors because of him
Very intelligent individual.  Has a passion for Graphics.
No Comments
No Comments

He is by far one of the worst professors i have ever taken.  I would never take another class,  put a yohoo on his head and it will fully be  shaken up by the end of the class.  He is definetly sexxxxy
He is very smart, but that is a bad thing for people who need the steps explained cause it is not clear.  He also cannot anunciate very well, or write very clearly.
No Comments
2 hour classes boring, but he knows his stuff, odd
He knows his stuff, but his lectures are boring.
Weird professor but not a bad one
Weird guy...           weird.
lots of discussion boards.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Sooooo many group assignments. I think the only assignment I did individually was my final paper, which doesn't seem bad until no one else in your group does anything all semester. Also don't fall for the "free absences" she gives out, she will take away points for being absent no matter what. Class is easy enough to get through though.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: ENL 307 was terribly boring. I don't think reading like 6 or so entire plays within an entire semester and writing about them is fun nor worth thousands of dollars whatsoever.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: julie is a good porfessor but passive agressive class participation matters and u need to go to every class there's a lot of group work but she is kind and will give u decent grades if u attend and ask questions.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Bowman is very dedicated to her students' success and is always open to student feedback in how to improve their experience in her courses. I would definitely recommend taking a class with her, as someone who generally doesn't enjoy English courses, she was able to make the experience 10 times more enjoyable.

Straight up she is passive aggressive but I can tell she somewhat cares about what she does. Group work. She can grade late so don't be afraid to stand up for yourself and get your work graded because she can be so forgetful. Attendance is mandatory. Not the best but not the worst.

Treats a 100 level class like its Honors Writing. Unclear grading criteria and does not seem to care or want to work with students to help them succeed. Don't take this professor unless you are looking to lower your GPA in a class that should be doing the opposite.

She's a very outgoing and kind person, but just not a great professor in my experience. Her grading takes ages and she isn't very clear in what she wants us to do in some rubrics. She also is very 50/50 on whether you'll get a quick or even helpful response. She does seem like she's trying her best but doesn't know quite how to teach effectively.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Her instructions were extremely unclear at times, and overall her class was basically just a book club, where we would discuss in-class readings for its entirety.
