She is, however, extremely nice and caring, and gives plenty of extra credit and is lenient with grades. She does take FOREVER to grade assignments though, so be patient.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Prof. Bowman is super nice and caring and we had some fun discussions in class. She listens to the students feedback and even went so far to talk to the other Honors teachers to see if we could do a slideshow instead of a paper (and the papers are pretty easy). Overall, Prof. Bowman is a great professor and I would definitely take again.
Grading took ages. Rubrics were messy. Time lines were also all over the place for finals. Would not take course if you want to hear direct feedback instantly.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: This class had sooo much homework, readings, and papers, but it was not Professor Bowman's fault because it was an honors course. She is so kind and caring, and does everything she can to make sure her students are successful. Would highly recommend taking one of her classes.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Caring

reviews.comment: I really like Prof Bowman. She takes feedback from students on the kinds of assignments we do and actually picks pretty good books to read. There isn't a lot of work besides reading and lectures can be boring since we just talk about the book over and over.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: For ENL200H, she mentioned that she went through 12 different books in the semester. But she decided on 3/4 only for this semester. Honestly, her grading criteria is not clear and she take forever to grade. She decided to teach the course "intuitively" and said that we are basically a "book club" that read through texts. Very nice, but all over.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She was nice
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: She is literally the best professor I have had in a long time. She is absolutely wonderful and caring, that the coursework does not even matter. LOVE HER!!!

It's been a brutal semester, and I fell way behind in all my classes. I thought I was going to fail this class, but Dr. Bowman was super sympathetic and gave me a chance to catch up. And when I was able to be there, I was able to get most of the concepts pretty easily.

Best English Professor ever!! I took her for Honors ENL101 and it was the first English class I ever enjoyed! Clearly states what needs to be done to do well. Gives lots of time to complete assignments and even allows you to revise and resubmit work sometimes! Class discussions are super interesting and helpful. Will do well if you complete work!
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: She's a hard professor, but really makes the work that you do actually good.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: I never thought I would pass an English class, but she was supportive and the class was much much better than I expected.

Professor Bowman is super sweet and shes always available if you have questions or concerns. Her class is pretty straight forward, if you listen and pay attention you'll get an A. Absolutely love her!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring

reviews.comment: Graduating from the Connect Program, I had Professor Bowman in Spring of '18. At first she can be rocky, but she is quirky and shy. She was my most flexible professor this Spring. The theme was Utopia for the course. Sometimes rather dull, she was able to liven it with her enthusiasm. Book is reference rarely but PDF is online! I loved her!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: As said before, she is all over the place. The final is absolutely awful (for ENL 200) , so long and unnecessary. She's nice, but would not recommend taking her.
Has the students teach most of the class on group projects. Unclear directions on assignments.
doesnt have much structure to her classes and her lectures seem to go on forever about nothing important. Gave us an essay and then an essay final 2 days later
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: She is all over the place, incredibly scatterbrained & it reflects in your grade. Changes what she wants you to do about 30 times a class & is awful at communicating, plus she refuses to use my courses she uses a running google doc list, its bad. Her being unclear & failing to answer emails leads to poor grades bc no one knows what she wants
made the class uncomfortable by.. exposing himself.

Really cool guy! Always makes sure everyone understands concepts before moving on, and seems to care a lot about how we do. I appreciate how clear the grading criteria is, and he always reminds us about homework and quizzes.

Prof. Kokluce is a wonderful person and a great insructor. Besides the slides he always uses his own examples. Solves many questions on the board until he is sure that everyone understand the topic. Responds to e-mails very quickly. Overall great professor. He taught Algebra class.

Great professor who actually cares about his students! He was always available during office hours or via email. His accent can be hard to understand sometimes, but he is always willing to repeat what he is teaching or rephrase it in a way that is easier to understand.

Prof. Kokluce is very difficult to understand as he has an extremely thick Eastern European accent. He often does not know what he is talking about, and he will lecture for 20ish minutes, and then tell us to forget everything because it was wrong. He seems like a nice man, but I would not recommend this class.
He's literally so kind, and he helps a lot with so many topics of statistics. He has an accent, but still teaches good, overall so nice
She is so good at making me understand math xD

She shouldn't be rated this low, she teaches in an very easy to understand manner. Her tests and quizzes are very easy to complete so long as you study for them. If you familiarize yourself with the subjects there won't be a single hard question on the test. The web assigns are also way harder than the questions on the test.
Why is she rated so low? She is a good teacher and a nice person. She is very good at making her students understand the topics.

Taking this class helped me a lot with learning material that I knew absolutely nothing about before taking this class. She never hesitated to answer questions and consistently showed all steps to problems on the board. Attend all lectures and complete all of the homework assigned and you should be good.
Such a great Prof. has helped me understand many concepts I hadn't grasped during high school calc. Highly recommend

She is not good at teaching she just straight glosses over important parts of calculus which are included on the final by the way. She uses a syllabus that seems like it is meant for a high level class only allowing three absences.

Pacing is way too quick. About 20 mins to learn each new material. Is poor at explaining, often glossing over complex details while going in depth about basic stuff. Doesn't seem too planned either; gives no thought to how anyone will note her lecture material. Only reason I'm caught up is because I'm teaching MYSELF the course material with Google

She did out problems on the board and actually tried to teach the content. She actually helped me understand things and she was very reasonable; her test questions were actually based on what we learned and not like next level crap that we wouldn't know. 10/10, would beg her to let me take her again.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Literally what is up guys she's the best one.

This professor does not explain course material well. Notes on the board do not match what she is saying out loud, even if you take notes you're still going to be confused. The only thing that kept me afloat was the TA and youtube. Homework does not match what goes on in class at all. Easy topics in class but hard topics for homework.
She's a good Professor who doesn't ridicule silly questions when asked.
Extra credit given to those who attended extra help. Very lecture heavy but she taught the material well. If you took calc in HS this class will be a breeze
Homework completely different then in lecture material
Really great professor! Her lectures are easy to understand and she answers all questions well. Tests are only hard if you don't study. I would recommend
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: She used to change topics everyday so it was hard for me to pick up

Sara Bildik is a really good professor. She is willing to go over things even if they seem like stupid questions and she explains where she gets every answer if you ask for clarification. The tests are hard but the quizzes and homework will boost your grade as long as you do well on them. Go to the STEM center if you need extra help.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Nice professor from one view. Was in the process of bringing my grade up until the pandemic hit and things went online. The moment that happened I fell to a point of tough recovery that was unsuccessful. Homework assignments online, Tests required "lockdown browser" and timed. Notes not posted on webassign, and were hard to find. had to drop.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Her lectures were straight forward and she really tried to help you understand the material. Compared to the other Calc 1 professors, she is by far the best. She gave about 3 homework assignments a week and 4/5 quizzes. She does drop the lowest quiz grade too which was nice.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: I took her last year for calc 1 and it was not a good experience. I basically got to teach myself the entire class because instead of teaching us, she spoke extremely quietly as she sped through different things. I did not miss a single class and took a bunch of notes but still had to teach myself everything. I thought myself to a B (85%).
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Sara is a nice knowledgeable person. However, she is slow to respond on emails and often skips over concepts and does examples without the information behind them. Often the class examples do NOT match the homework. Hw is done online and no calculators allowed. Best advice is get weekly tutoring appointments to increase your understanding.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: I took AP calculus and high school and I qualified to skip Calc I in college but I decided to take it again since I wasn't confident in my foundation. Professor Bildik has solidified by knowledge of the basics through her outstanding teaching methods. She is amazing at explaining things to students and is one of the best educators I have ever had.

Took her for Calc 1, excellent teacher, really good at explaining material. I had literally no math background going into this class and she made it easy for me. Put in the work, study and you'll do just fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: truly one of the best professors ive ever had. two open notes exams, but honestly as long as you show up to class you won't even need your notes. his lectures are extremely interesting and his anecdotes about his work/patients are very inspiring! very awesome class I feel like a learned so much
Extremely Easy class, just need to show up, everything is open note
All you have to do is show up to class and take notes. There are two exams, midterm and final, and both are open note. He assigns a book but, he doesn't use it. 10/10 wish he could teach everything

Dr. B. is the BEST psych professor at UMD. There was only a midterm, final and an extra credit paper. Although attendance is not mandatory, you should still go, you WON'T pass if you don't! The only reason I received an A was because I attended class. Take a class with Dr. B., he is kind, funny,VERY knowledgeable and an amazing professor!

Great professor, only grades you on a midterm and final based off of lecture material. Both are very easy even if you missed class. Attendance is optional, allows an extra credit paper to add 10 points to your midterm if you do bad. Has really interesting lectures so it is an enjoyable class and he always ends early.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: One of the best Psychology professors in the department. All of his classes are super easy and super interesting. Attendance was not mandatory for this class but I recommend going every day! Lectures are really engaging, and he loves discussing topics with the class. Only a midterm and final, both open note. Highly recommend taking this class

Dr. Barrett is an all around great professor. There's only a mid-term and final exam (OPEN NOTES). There is also an optional extra credit paper to increase your midterm grade -- this class is a great "dipping of the toes" for the counseling world and I would recommend him for this course to try. I would also recommend him for ANY other course :)

He was honestly the best professor I ever had. He made me want to pursue a career in this field and he was so inspiring and kind. Genuinely an awesome human being and is one of the few at Umass Dartmouth that actually cares about their students. He left teaching people at medical school for a paycut just to inspire the younger kids. Best guy ever!

not too bad of a professor; attendance isn't mandatory but I would recommend going because some stuff from the mid term and final exam (which are your only grades) came from class discussion and aren't in the notes on myCourses; no need to really participate since it is such a large class (100+); would def take again!

Super easy psychology lecture. Attendance isn't mandatory but he is so interesting to listen to I wouldn't want to miss class anyway. Only need the book if you plan on skipping class and need to catch up. He is full of interesting stories which he uses as examples in his lectures. Multiple choice mid-term and final plus an extra credit paper.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He's lecture heavy so you must show up to class but he makes it enjoyable. Yes there's very few grades (2) but he makes it based off of you. Meaning its based on the midterm & final which are both open notes - having said that, you can do an extra credit that is worth 10% of your midterm. Get 88 on mid the XC is 8.8pts its very easy if you show up.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Barrett is by far the best professor I've had! Lecture heavy which I loved, but he is extremely intelligent and interesting to listen to. He tells great stories that help explain the material. As long as you take good notes you will do fine on the exams. No homework or required attendance but I always looked forward to going to his classes!
The best professor you will ever take. The sweetest, funniest man. He is so brilliant and it was a pleasure to be able to learn from him.

Dr. Barrett was amazing. He only grades based off of your midterm and final, and allows you to submit an extra credit paper if you want a higher grade. The class was always interesting, and he included a lot of great scenarios that he experienced to help with the understanding of the topics he was teaching. Attendance wasnt necessary but it helped.
Great class to attend with no pressure and open note exams. He was super funny and I enjoyed my time in lecture

One of the best professors I've had here thus far. Only 2 grades in the class which are the midterm and final, but both are open note but even without are super easy. I opted out of using the template he provides for notes and took my own, and still passed with an A+. Love this guy to pieces; very fun to talk to outside of class!!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Dr. Barrett is an amazing professor! Midterm and Final both open notes and an extra credit paper that will boost your midterm grade if needed. He tells a lot of stories with his lectures which makes it easy to understand the topic. Would definitely take him again!

He's a great person and great professor. Attendance isn't taken and I only used the textbook once so it is a waste of money. Make sure to take notes though because you only get two grades, which is your midterm and final.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Great and very knowledgeable professor, easy notes, slide shows, doesn't take attendance. Learned a lot, would take again
Awesome professor just be sure to take notes because the mid term and final are open notes.

He is honestly the best professor I have had here. Not only is he enjoyable to take, his class is super easy. He lets you print out the slides and use them and the notes you take on your midterm/final. That being said, those are literally the only two grades. Perhaps that is a tentative word of caution to those that struggle on test day.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Amazing lectures--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Only had him for CAS then transferred but he was one of the only professors at UMD who seemed to actually care about his students and want to help them. Definitely would take him if you can- great guy!

Barrett is very cool! Grading is based only on your midterm and your final, which are both open notes! You should definitely show up to class because notes are not posted on mycourses (besides the powerpoint template.) He's a great guy and very open to answering questions.

great professor! great grading and testing. uses his own work experience to bring course material and real-life scenarios together.- in his 101 level, there were only 2 exams.. 1 midterm and 1 final.. both open notes. recommend going to class in order to do well

Dr. Barrett was hilarious and would tell stories that linked to the subject at hand. He also had us analyze case examples using different methods. I would take him again. His grade is based on two exams which are very easy if you pay attention to class and read the powerpoints.

I loved having him, one of my favorite professors I've ever had. The only two grades you get for this class are the midterm and the final BUT they're open note! So if you show up to class, take good notes, it's an easy class.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: This teacher was awesome. His lectures were fun and interesting. Only had 2 exams (midterm and final) and they were open book. Also, book is not needed as long as you go to every lecture class and take notes on what he says.
reviews.rating_tags: GRADED BY FEW THINGS--Amazing lectures--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: He is the best possible professor to have in college. You have one midterm and one final and they're both open notes. Attendance is not mandatory but there are some questions he asks on the exam that are not obviously stated in the slides he hands out but his lectures are interesting. If you want an easy A and actually learn something take him!

He's one of my favorite professors in UMD. Dr. Barret is very knowledge, he cares not only about what he's talking about but also his students. His lectures are very interesting & informative, you will actually get something out of it. Since he's easygoing it's easy to skip his classes but honestly you won't learn much by doing that. Show up!

By far your best option for PSY101. Only gives midterm and final, w an optional pass/fail, extra credit paper that will add 10 pts to your midterm. Attendance is optional, however I highly suggest going because the answers on both exams come directly from his lectures and powerpoints. Besides, lectures are always interesting w real life examples.

Take a class with this man! You won't regret it. You'll actually learn something without the stress of constantly preparing for tests and homeworks. Only graded things are mid-term and final exam - both are open notes. Which follow the Power Point presentation in the exact order. Really nice guy, no tricks or stress involved.

Fantastic professor. No homework, no classwork. His lectures are interesting, just pay attention and maybe some notes and you'll pass the Midterm and final no problem. He'll really make you interested in the subject too. If you can take him, do it.

Definitely take him he is the easiest professor I ever had! He is a sweet old man. Found lectures interesting. Only 2 exams that's are open notes and most of the answers are on the power point slides he gives you. You can do 5 page extra credit paper that is pass or fail for 10 points on your midterm. No text book. Easy A and best elective ever.

He's a great teacher. He really knows what he's talking about and wants you to understand as well. Plenty of stories that are related to the teachings and keep your interest during class. In my class, only a mid-term and final made up your grade. Both tests are open note and go in exact order of the slides he gives you.

Amazing man.  Funny, intriguing, and experiences.  Easiest class I have taken.  2 Exams, both open notes.  No home work ever.  Note packets given to you (extra notes are optional but you don't need most of them). Extra credit assignment given (optional) and attendance (optional - but get the notes if you miss too many classes).  Good material too!

Awesome teacher, hes really nice and pretty funny. First test is open book, really easy(take good notes). Second and last test is not cumulative, but not open notes. Extra credit. Ask questions and he will clarify. If you like the subject you will really enjoy this class. Doesnt take attendance, but its important. If you skip, get good notes.

One midterm, one final (both open notes and multiple choice), and a five page extra credit paper on anything you want that has to do with psychology. Very easy class, just go to class and take good notes.
He made this part of the counseling sequence easy and I learned alot! One midterm and one final but they are open book. No textbook is required. Would definitely take him again!

Such an amazing teacher! He uses personal stories and examples to make the material understandable and interesting. He does not assign homework, but going to class is important to get the notes. The two tests given are open notes and multiple choice. This class really got me interested in the subject. Also offers an extra credit paper.

Brian William's storytelling ability allows him to bring students to the front lines of history. His 1st hand experience and continual world travel to the most dangerous and complex areas of the world combined with his relationships allow Brian to give students a nuanced perspective. UMD basically has the modern-day Indiana Jones on their faculty!

There is no better narrator of world history who can consistently weave the past with the present.  His boots on the ground personal experience and commitment to telling the truth of the last 25+ years of the modern Middle East woven with history of the past 600+ years has been invaluable to unlocking my understanding of the world around me.

I was burned out by my engineering classes and happened to take a mandatory history course by this professor. I had a great time with it and had lots of fun. It really helped me get my spirits back up.

he's extremely condensanding with the way he speaks about certain topics, goes on extreme rambles for 30-45 minutes out of the entire classtime, no hw besides exams and doesn't show what your grade is in real time. he's extemely passionate though, and wants you to pass. i wouldn't take his clasd again due to how strict he can be, but he's not bad

Williams is the GOAT. Makes class very fun, engaging, and worth going to every lecture. He makes it extremely clear what he wants from you as a student and the grading and assignments are very fair. Also a great guy who will work with you if you have other things going on.

Professor Williams feels like a modern-day Indiana Jones; he will challenge you, fascinate you and respect all the efforts that you make to excel in your class. Also, he will not be afraid to admit the truth and secrets of rather gruesome material. He will treat you more like a friend than a student. A shining star overall. ,

Professor Williams is passionate about history and makes the class engaging, even for non-majors. Attend lectures, take notes, and read the textbook - it's essential to do well. Unstuck ai is a great study tool that complements the class well.

I don't know how he doesn't have a perfect rating. He has the easiest class and if you pay attention you will end his class with an A+. He is extremely funny and has a wonderful personality. I got to meet with him outside of class to get a missing assignment and he shows how much he cares about his job. If you need a HST class take Prof Williams.

Draws a LOT on the board and pretty much only lectures, sometimes using slides He is VERY passionate and an amazing and super cool person. You'll think you are unprepared for an exam, but end up getting an A+ anyways Very fair professor that gives captivating lectures even for non-majors. Mandatory attendance but he doesnt always take it

Good professor, really knows what he is talking about, can be condescending, says stuff like "you need to know this to have an intelligent conversation," then mentions a random battle from 500 BC. Tests are essay heavy and you must describe things in detail, there are long papers, novels, and a Netflix series you need to keep up with to pass class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Professor Williams is such an amazing professor! He doesn't give you boring dates to memorize, instead, he teaches you the fun parts of history like the battles and leaders, and is very passionate about the subject. He's had real experiences in some of the countries and taught me the importance of maps. If you love history, take his classes!

Its like having Indiana Jones as a professor, but low-key cooler. Really knowledgeable, with real experience, and so excited to teach you about it. Loves to talk and answer questions and his classes are fascinating. Lots of reading and writing, but def worth it and he works with you. Take his class!!

Probably my favorite professor at Umass! One of the most inspirational people ever. He taught me so much in just a few months. Lots of reading but his books are good. He's also very understanding, and has a ton of experience in the countries he teaches about. I'm a Psych major and want to minor in History now bc of him. Great guy.

Dr. Williams is an excellent and rare professor. He is one of the few instructors who will teach modern history and really shows how it impacts the world. He has real experience on what he lectures and writes about in his books. He has worked with various agencies/local groups in Middle Eastern Countries in the effort to prevent and study terrorism

Dr. Williams is a top-quality professor! He is hilarious, extremely knowledgable (with real-life experience, and interesting. He does not sugar coat the truth and makes you see topics from multiple angles. I would certainly recommend taking his courses but you must read to pass, space out your reading so you don't cram it all in last minute.

Dr. Williams is a very passionate and knowledgeable professor. I had some issues related to COVID and he let me take the mid term several weeks late. I was nervous, honestly, and he gave me time to rack my brain. Incredibly patient and compassionate. The main thing here is - there is a LOT of reading, and your grade depends on if he knows you read
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I can't recommend his classes enough especially for non-majors. The content is great for anyone with an interest in the Middle-East and Central Asia, PSC majors especially. If you have an interest in journalism, these courses are great for exposure to current events. The reading can be a lot but I found it all to be enjoyable. Good discussion too.

He is nice and Cares for students and will help you if you're willing to put effort BUT if you do not like history or a non-history major can be boring tends to ramble He does not upload slides you have to be present to take notes and his notes can be all over the place He does offer extra credit Will do good if you pay attention and Read
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: I am a Psychology major and I took Dr. Williams class as an elective. He is as passionate of a professor as you will find. Every place he teaches you about he has been too and experienced first hand. Attendance is not mandatory but class notes are crucial especially if your going to slack on the readings. Very knowledgeable and awesome teacher.

I promise you, you won't regret this course. Williams was the BEST professor I have had at UMass Dartmouth. Being a CJS major, I was skeptical to take a History Elective but it absolutely was worth it. Read the books, they're actually very entertaining and interesting. If you don't, you'll struggle. If you don't believe my words, check his rating.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Hilarious

reviews.comment: William's is THE professor to take a class with at UMD. Honestly the best classes I've taken at this school. He keeps class interesting and takes you on a journey as he's teaching. Made me passionate about a subject I had little interest in. Great teacher. Don't hesitate, his classes fill up quick!

Professor Williams is an incredibly passionate & caring man. I had to miss a week of classes & he was incredibly understanding/accommodating. Even if you arent a history buff youll get into what he teaches because his excitement is infectious. He knows his stuff - hes lived it & studied it for decades. I highly recommend taking one of his classes!

I am not a history buff at all, but this was such an eye opening class. A lot of work for an elective, but if you go to class and do the readings then you'll be fine. It was nice to finally have a professor that doesn't just read off of powerpoints or practically have you teach yourself. He makes you want to go to class, because its so interesting.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Awesome professor. Extremely knowledgeable and cares about what he teaches. There are times you'll leave class amazed at some of the content he teaches. Semester consists of easy map quiz, quick one-on-one meeting in his office for mid term, four page paper final and 10 page paper due at end of semester which is easy if you have been reading.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Get ready to read--Tests? Not many

reviews.comment: Awesome guy with incredible stories. Come to class, pay attention and ask a few questions. He loves participation and makes absolutely sure everyone is doing their readings. The books are essential to passing the class, as his exams draw directly from them.
This guy is amazing, he is the best prof. at UMass Dartmouth even if you aren't a history major take as many classes as you possible can with him, you'll learn more than you could ever imagine

This was honestly my favorite class at the University, The lectures were very informative and eye opening. Attendance is mandatory to understand material, overall a fantastic lecturer, Will be in your top five favorite professors you've had.

He is hands down the best professor I have ever taken and is the best class as well.  I had him for the Terrorism in the Middle East class.  I think everyone should take him as a professor and this class as well.  You will pass as long as you read, he hates students that do not read and will call you out on your test if you don't. amazing class

Professor Williams' classes offer great depth and insight into the history of the Middle East and the extremist groups which plague the area. I have taken both Empires in Central Asia and History of Terror in the Middle East and they were easily two of my favorite classes I ever took at Umass Dartmouth.

A legend among men. He has been to some of the most dangerous places in the middle east and lived to tell the tale. He will make you forget about people like the Kardashian's and help you focus on real life.  Take his class. DO IT.  For Real. Enroll RN.

I took professor WIlliams Eastern Empires course and it was perhaps the best class I have taken in all my years at UMD. The material I learned is unforgettable. Take it yourself to find out more. Williams is a kind, hilarious, and unforgettable educator that goes, literally, over mountains to bring his students the most in depth and enjoyable class
Fun professor, knows how to make a class be interested in what he's teaching. Cool guy. Serious when needed but very helpful, will be there for you

Professor William's only tough policy is on his attendance.  If you miss three classes you fail.  He holds two a week on the course I took, but if you show up it's flawless.  Extra Credit Galore for doing the reading, he wants you to show off!  The class I took was HST Major specific.  It was all about Barbarians, and very inspiring.
Best professor at UMD! Don't miss taking him!!!!!

Hands down the best professor at UMD. His classes on Central Asian History and Terrorism in the Mid. East were fascinating. I looked forward to each and every class and was very disappointed when he cancelled(which was often). He is extremely energetic and helpful. He feeds off positive energy form his students. Also extremely intelligent. Take him

I'd heard great things about Williams before taking the class, and he definitely lived up to expectations. He has so much real-world experience to compliment his lectures, and the class is always interesting. I gained so much perspective on the wars in the Middle East.

Dr. Williams is most definitely my favorite professor at this school. He is absolutely brilliant and knows everything about the subject he teaches. Reading is definitely necessary to success. The world needs more professors and people like him. He made me passionate about the subject he teaches. I looked forward to going to every single class.

This class was very enjoyable, the professor teaches with a lot of enthusiasm and energy. You'll leave class the first day thinking he talks wayyyyyy too fast but you get used to it. The tests are pretty easy and you get to really show how much you know. TAKE IT!!
such a smart knowledgeable teacher always ready to help. Interesting class. quizzes are easy he gives many ways to show you know the information. BEST CLASS I HAVE TAKEN

Dr. Williams is a very nice and knowledgeable professor. He talks fast, and it can be difficult to understand what he says at times. Attendance is mandatory but not hard because his class is very interesting. Definitely recommend taking a class with him. Got a B and read a total of maybe 10 hours all semester.

Have taken a few classes with Professor Williams and I can honestly say they were the best classes I've taken at the University and I'm not even a History major! An absolute asset to the UMass Dartmouth community. Every student should take a class with him! Truly brings everything to life.

He is a really good professor, and i had an A+ all up until the last weeks. We had an 8 page paper and he said he give feedback if we turned it in ahead, but i never got that feedback. So he's not great with feedback but still a good prof who LOVES his job. Recommend!!

One of the few classes at Umass D where I actually looked forward to going.  Professor Williams is very engaging and makes class fun.  Like others have said, if you want to do well you have to go to class and do the readings.
The most knowledgeable and interesting professor I've had at UMD. Take his class. It will change your life.

Brian is a cool interesting guy. he takes history and brings it to life! The class was very interesting and i enjoyed it alot. his tests are difficult. there are 4 books and you need them all. you will fail without the books you need to quote parts of the readings on exams. 2 map quizes were easy. nice guy and not a bad class.  6-7 page paper too.
Best teacher Umass Dartmouth has

Dr. Williams is a great professor and will keep you interested in the class. I am currently taking him for Al Qaeda and Modern Terrorism and this guy really knows his stuff! Very passionate and explains everything so students should never feel lost. Attendance is mandatory and his exams are blue book and easy so long as you pay attention in class.

Absolute best professor I have ever had.  This man lives what he teaches and actively loves educating students on current affairs and the world.  I've taken three classes with him, he grades fairly, and bases all of his tests on the class readings and lecture notes.  If you go to Umass take one of his classes, he is an absolute asset to the school.

Taking a class with Professor Williams is like being taught by Indiana Jones. It's definitely worth taking, I learned more in his class than in any other I've taken at UMASS. You have to do the reading, but the books are really good. And if you miss one class, you will miss a lot. But most people don't want to because class is so much fun.

best professor you will ever have. williams goes to the countries he teaches about over summer so he knows an incredible amount about his subject. makes his classes really interesting, youll actually want to go. you need to read the books. hes sexy too.
He is an amazing teacher he made me want to become a history major he is wicked sexy too

Best teacher I have ever had. He is absolutely amazing. No matter what the subject is, he will get you enthralled into it where you will preach to people about. Definitely made me want to be a history major. You have do to the reading, and come to class. But trust me, you will want too. Super hot too and dresses fresh to death ;)

A mind-blowing class, and an incredible professor. You'll never look at the world the same way after taking a Williams class. You'll come out with an unquenchable thirst for knowledge and understanding of other nations. He truly knows his stuff re: the Middle East/Central Asia. You MUST do the reading to pass, but the books are good.
I love mr williams. he's a great professor, easy to understand and fun to listen to. he really brings history to life.

Absolutely amazing!! He's a great professor, take him if you have the chance!  Lectures can be a little jumpy and he missed class several times but class sessions were always fascinating, the time passed so quickly!  Make sure you do the reading, he likes you to prove you've read on the tests.  Come to class, do the readings and you'll be fine!

He is a great teacher. first of all, buy the books, you'll need them. He likes to put questions on the test about things that you should have read, including the online readings. He loves to show movies, which is cool because we got to watch current movies like 300.

Took history of the Ottoman Empire with Prof Willaims- prior to the class i didnt even know WHAT the Ottoman Empire was. He's AMAZING. He knows SOO much about history and other cultures, and knows it from THEIR point of view, rather than an outsider looking in. Especially with the current events in the Middle East, his class was invaluable- I learn
Professor Williams is awesome.  His class on Afghanistan and Taliban was great.  He loves essay exams, must do readings!!  Great teacher.... very hot...

I want to be Professor Williams. Being in his class is the next best thing to being Indiana Jones. He's SO energetic. He cancelled class early to go advise the government on Iraq, he's interviewed Taliban members, he can talk forever on Chechen genocide.  His classes are info-packed and you have to do the reading, but his style is laid back.
Very Enthusiastic.

I loved Williams so much, I took another class with him.  He doesn't take attendance, but it's worth going to class anyway.  He knows what he's talking about and almost made me want to switch majors.  Williams is fantastic!

Really interesting and informed. Really knows how to back up his statements with facts. Zero bias exept against uninformed rhetoric. Will definatly take him next semester. Tests are not bad as long as you read. Cool lectures and movies.
No Comments

Dr. Williams made me want to be a history teacher. It's hard to have passion about something when every teacher is so boring. He's excited about the stuff he teaches, funny as hell, and a brilliant guy. Just do me a favor and stop taking his classes! They fill up in about a day, so pick em early. A++. Best teacher I've ever had.

Awesome class, and really good professor. Makes class really interesting and has been to most of the countries he talked about. This was my favorite class at umd so far. One 10 page paper, midterm and final. attendence is not taking. A lot of reading. I am taking him again!

Blah, forgot the course number. Anyway, VERY nice guy. IS VERY passionate, and every other sentence is a joke (about the topic). Even people who hate history love his classes. All we had were two EASY map tests, a midterm, and a final. Knows EVERYTHING about the Middle East, Central Asia, Russia, the Balkans, and Eastern Europe. Young and energetic

Im not a history major, but i took two classes with Brian and i loved them.  He totally knows what hes talking about and loves the subject.  He makes you want to learn more about the things hes passionate about.  Two thumbs up for him

Awsome teacher! I am a history hater but he really got me into it. He gives two map quizes, a midterm and a final. SO EASY! He is a good looking guy too and wears really nice clothes. Makes history interesting by telling it like a story teller. Has visuals and first hand experiences. He has been everywhere it seems and loves to tell you about it.

If there is a small country in Eastern Europe/Siberia that no 1 can pronounce, he has been there. The workload is very reasonable & has more to do with in-class lectures then it does the readings. He brings in a lot of his experiences from where he lived & has plenty of good jokes to go along with it.
Makes you earn your grade but makes the subject real interesting.You have to read and study but his class is great. Will definatly take again.
I love this professor.  Hes really a great teacher, and ohmygodwhatahotty!  Sometimes he talks to fast but I really like the class.

One of the best professors at UMD. He really loves his job and makes what he's teaching interesting, even if it's the most boring subject. I learned a ton in his class and as long as you actually go to class, you'll pass.
Best prof. I have had. Knows exactly what he is talking about and tells you exactly what you need to know. I'd take him for any class.
No Comments
Have had two classes w/ prof. Williams and have learned so much more than i have in any other history class to date

He is definately a man full of knowlege. Looks and brains do go hand in hand for this professor. I took a couple classes with him and his teaching/grading/testing methods are predictable.  Just take good notes and write whatever he says down and memorize it for the final and midterm and you'll end up with the class with an A!!

Possesses great first hand knowledge of Central Asia, politics, characters, and history of the region. Lively lectures with use of multi-media presentations. Fair grader, but slow in returning papers and tests.

Well, I thought his lectures were educational until I was looking at the Quran and found that everything he said contradicts actual Islam; I think he's biased.  I wouldn't suggest he teach if he doesn't teach the truth.  He degrades the religion and ignores important points while emphasizing points that have little material. Oh yeah so disorganized

unfair grader!!! that's all i have to say...doesn't give any credit to people who actually show up everyday and trust me it's not too many. has alot of passion in his teaching and is energetic and oh yeah, not too bad on the eyes either
HOT. So interesting. Makes his classes fun and entertaining.
Brian Williams is my hero, if you are not retarded you will like his classes and do good, he needs to lay off the turtlenecks though
hottttt and sooo goood!
this man is awesome.
Very good teacher.  Talked a little bit fast, but his lectures were always interesting.  Hes wicked hot!!

Def. a looker and his intelligence makes him that much hotter.  Enjoyed his class BUT his exams are a little difficult and he gives no study guide or anything to help and the books for the course are interesting but most of it assigned in the last few weeks of school. Def. would take again though.
Gorgous! and so interesting! Knows so much and he has been everywhere. Enjoyed the class a lot and did well for my grades :) I Love HiM!

Very interesting lectures, makes learning fun, makes jokes! Nice enough guy, but somewhat disorganized...took him 3 weeks to return a short map quiz! Despite that, his classes are always packed...that should tell you something! Not bad to look at either;)
this is easily my favotite teacher.
Good professor.  Very passionate about history.  His lectures are awesome, his class is never boring.  If you put effort into his class you should get an A.  A little disorganized at times.

I had him for World Civ I, and Realm of Islam. He teaches in a way you remember, and his goal is for you to learn, not to fail you. As long as you have half a brain you should walk out of his class with an A.
Not the most organized class I ever took, but interesting, easy, and Williams is a very enthusiastic educator.  I would recommend taking this class if you need to take a history.
Brilliant guy and a pretty easy class, its pretty clear what he wants.  He is, though, so out of touch when it comes to students.  If you need help he tries to shrugg you off pretty quickly.
hot teacher, great guy, interesting class...take him if you can
ALL I CAN SAY IS HOTT!!!

What is he doing at UMass? Easily the best teacher I have come across.  Makes even people who despise history love it and learn a lot. I dare the biggest history-haters to take his course and not like it.

Soooooooooo hot. On top of that, he is passionate and interesting about the subject he teaches.  The class requires reading, papers, and some maps quizes, and tests.  It is not an easy grade, but it's not too difficlut either. Oh, and that smile...
This guy is great. Take him for any class you can.
easy class, but he doesn't know a good paer when he sees one.
very passionate about history. kinda rubs off on you.
No Comments
He is the hottest teacher I have ever seen :)
No Comments
No Comments
He was very helpful with any small assignments. The only thing is to show up to class in order to stay ahead!

He's hilarious and one of the most helpful English teachers I've had in a while. The assignments are very self-explanatory and explained by him. The three major assignments are three writing assignments that are made very simple by him and graded very kindly.
Botvin is a wonderful professor, very chill guy. Keep in mind that attendance is vital in the second half of the semester, as there is a group project.
I would 10/10 take this class again. He is very understanding if you put in the work. If you show up to class and do the work assigned, you will get an A.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Literally one of the most understanding and nicest professors I have ever had. He is so relatable and friendly that it makes the class 10x better. The class is easy since he explains things clearly, but if you are confused he is ready to help you and not judge you for the confusion. 10/10 and would take him again

I took ENL102 online. Josh does everything he can to make the class reasonable in terms of workload and low stress. He's a really sweet professor and clearly cares about his students. 10/10 would take again. Lectures were typically no more than 30 minutes which is great for an online class.

Josh is literally the best professor I have ever had. There are 0 complaints I can give about him. He literally let us do chemistry work in class if we had fallen behind, and we would get the work done on time anyways. He is hilarious and truly cares about his students, and will guide them through their first semester beyond just teaching English.

prof Botvin has been one of my favorite teachers/professors ever. He was always so lenient on grading and would allow so many extensions if needed. As long as you put in some type of work you were all set for a grade. His class was so light-hearted and genuinely so easy to follow along with. Wish I could take him again 5/5!!!
best professor i've ever had
reviews.rating_tags: Hilarious--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: When I say Prof Botvin is the best, HE IS THE BEST. He is such a funny, caring, and respectful man. Also, his teaching style is very effective and engaging. Of all the UMD staff I went to him for help with other classes, selecting a minor, and much more. Take his 102 Marvel class it is so fun.

Josh is a professor for the students. I should've left a review sooner, but he sets you up for success in class! All the work done throughout the semester sets you up for the final. He's great with getting student input and adjusting based on what we think.

Best professor I've had so far!! Such a great person overall and is very understanding. He is really kind and genuinely cares about his students. His lectures are very easy going and as long as you just do the work (which he generously will extend for you, if you ask) you'll get an easy A. I would for sure take another one of his classes again!!
great professor he slays a lot
Professor Botvin is the GOAT
Literally the best prof I've ever had. Work is very doable as long as you don't procrastinate. Joshua is hilarious and very caring and understanding.
So nice and very lenient, understands you can't get everything in on time and accepts late work to a very reasonable extent.
Professor Botvin is a very articulate professor, he makes Technical Communications an interesting topic and really cares about his students and wants to see them succeed.
best english class I've taken

Botvin is definitely one of my favorite teachers. His class is easy because he explains clearly and offers a lot of support if you ask for it. As long as you be straightforward with him and email if you are behind or have to miss class he's totally understanding and will make sure you can catch up. Take his classes for an easy A :)

Outstanding professor!!! 10/10, I cant praise this professor enough. They are very caring and understanding. Easy, fun, simple classes, projects, homework and essays. Material covered is very clear and perfectly understood. They are a great example of what a teacher should be. Highly encourage everyone to take for English. Looking forward to 102!!!

He's really understanding. He gets how hard being a student is and is very flexible without being a pushover. Prof. Botvin makes the class engaging and interesting. You'll get a really good understanding of the material if you pay attention a good 75% of the time. By far one of the best teachers I've ever had.
Awesome guy, very funny, very easy to get along with, wants you to do your best, and feels like he is a part of the class discussion rather than a lecture.

Just an amazing teacher. Really cares about students passing. Easiest class but you still learn. As long as you put some effort into your assignments, it will be a breeze. Takes attendance and gives you most of time to finish your assignments in class so he can help you.

Probably my favorite teacher coming into my freshman year at Umass Dartmouth, a super energetic person, loves his students, a great speaker who just loves what he does, He is very lenient with work just shoot him an email and just keep him in the loop. Apply for his class!! you won't regret it.
Hes very lenient and gives chances to fix mistakes !

Professor Botvin was absolutely amazing for ENL 101 and wish he was teaching ENL 102. He takes his time to put thoughtful comments on your work and if you are feeling stressed send him a message and he will work it out with you. He is super chill super kind and the best English teacher/professor I have ever had. You won't regret taking him.

For Fall 2020, he chose to be fully remote without meeting on zoom. Although we don't meet, he took time to reach out to every student which was awesome! He doesn't take points off for assignments that are late, as long as they're due before the end of the semester. Not a lot of homework, and he's extremely helpful on major assignments!
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Loved this class and professor! Workload was light and wasn't extremely complicated. He was willing to help you, especially during class. His criteria and coursework is clear and knows how to manage time wisely. Would definitely take his class again.
Easily one of the better professors I have taken at UMD. Really easy on grading and is not too strict on everything being on time. Very understandable professor. Take his class!

He is a great Professor. Makes class interesting. A fairly easy grader although it was an 8am I truly enjoyed waking up to attend. The projects and homework assignments are easy. If you leave your phone on front desk while in class he will give you extra credit at the end of the semester. All in all great professor and great class!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: He's a good teacher but gives a lot of papers back to back. If you don't keep up you will fall behind. Attendance is key. If you done go he WILL fail you.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--SO MANY PAPERS

reviews.comment: Professor Botvin is awesome. He makes class fun but also makes sure you get the work done. He grades easily and does not expect anyone's work to be perfect. There are minor assignments and about 4 major assignments (you have plenty of time to complete everything). He's such a cool guy and the whole class loved him, I would recommend him!
Such a great professor! He was very helpful and he also made the classes fun while still being serious. I definietly would recommend him!

Awesome professor! Funny while serious and realizes some things he has to teach are boring. He's casual and laid back and has a very shared vision of class. Projects are not difficult and neither are homework assignments. I learned a lot and would recommend!
Very intimidating, students rarely participate because they are scared to be wrong and get judged by her

This class was unbelievably boring and did not hold my attention throughout the entire time. I bs'd 90% of the class which majorly brought down the difficulty and time, but even still... it was still annoying, which is more of the hard part. She did not know how to use canvas, and is stuck like 30 years in the past, with the boring lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She is actually the worst professor I've ever had. She is so disorganized and rude. She changes the directions of the assignments she give a lot making it unclear what you are supposed to do.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I advise you not to take this class. This professor is all over the place. She'll give you a syllabus to abide by, but not follow it herself. Dates of when things are due are not straightforward and all over the place. She is passive aggressive and will blame the student for mistakes she makes herself. My advice, try your best and pray
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Basically only has the syllabus online, all work has to be submitted with paper, and then doesn't submit grades online. Gave us a group project, didn't tell us the groups until the week before, and when we did have the groups, she only said the names, so we didn't know who in the class it actually is. Accent so heavy you can understand maybe half.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: teacher has the largest victim mentality on the planet. Is convinced that she is largely disadvantaged in every shape and form, does not realize that affirmative action helped her get placed into college

if you would like to learn nothing then this is the class for you. if you want a lazy teacher that never emails back and assigns the most vague prompts, than this is the class for you. I hope she's not being paid for her work, can't imagine her being a good fit for even a community college
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: This professor is a joke, Attendance is mandatory, she grades very little and when she does grade your work, she will not give you good feedback, so you don't know how to correct your future assignments. She is also horrible with replying to emails, so prepare to be ignored if you do try emailing her. I wouldn't recommend this class to anyone!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: A little late to class almost every day, but no big deal I guess. However, what I disliked about her is how passive aggressive she is with her students... I was scared to ask questions because every time anyone did, her response would ALWAYS be rude, passive aggressive, etc. She seemed to always feel the need to talk down to students.

BEWARE OF THS PROFESSOR. She is EXTREMELY disorganized, she forgets she did not hand back completed exams/assignments but claims she did. Will make students feel small for asking questions; super condescending. Goes back and forth on assignment due dates. You can get 95's on every assignment and an 80 on ONE and your final grade will be a B.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: When I say don't take this professor, I wholeheartedly mean just don't do it. Don't think I'm just one case or a bad student cause I am in the honors college and have a 3.88 GPA and have never gotten below an A- in college. When I say she has 0 idea what she's doing I mean 000. She's unorganized and stubborn. Don't waste your money or time.
Dr. Teboh is an excellent professor. She cares deeply about the subjects she teaches. Show effort and you will do well.

Shows up ten minutes late to each class and sometimes not at all with no email. She will argue with you if you try to get help, and tell you that you did not show up to class, even though you were speaking to her the same day. She treats a 104 elective class as if its level 400.

i don't leave reviews often, but felt this one warranted one. the class technically isn't hard until it's hard. showing up is easy, the things she goes over is easy, the online quizzes are actually pretty hard and there are tons a week, and then the midterm was on nothing we learned at all. she is incredibly rude to her students and talks down.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: DO NOT. I mean DO NOT ever take a class with this small minded lady. She lacks teaching skills, never replies to emails and is never accessible outside of class. Whenever she messes up, she doesnt admit to being wrong, instead shell blame it on you. She is terrible and shouldnt even be teaching at this school if were being honest

Teboh is a passionate teacher, and is very knowledgeable about the subject she teaches. Despite this, she has her faults. She'll tell you to look the syllabus for answers but she would forget when things are due in class. If you have questions, be prepared to be talked at like a child. She comes across as very disrespectful to her students.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Easy A, would come to class late but none the less use the time to get yourself some breakfast or work on another assignment. some students had a hard time and blamed her alot for being unorganized, keep in mind shes teaching 3 other classes. very straightforward with her assignments. assigns books but u can get away without cracking one open.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Teboh is a kind-hearted woman w/ a passion for topics she's studied. My attendance was certainly not perfect, though I still achieved a B+ for the course. Instructions sometimes unclear, she speaks 7 languages & teaches multiple courses; it's hard for her to stay organized. But if you show up, pay attention, put an oz of effort in - good 2 go
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: I never write reviews but this professor is awful. Teboh has no idea whats going on. She gives you information that is both irrelevant as well as incorrect. Do not take this class. She doesn't know how to teach and will make you feel like an idiot if you question her. She frequently disrespects her students and is very rigid with her teaching style
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Teboh's class was what some people would call a joke. 1 map test (10%?), a take home midterm (20%), one paper (20%), and a take home final (20%). I don't think I read one paragraph from the textbook. She will not use the syllabus. She will show 10 minutes late to class EVERYDAY. Couldn't tell you one thing learned. Take her if you want an easy A.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Tests? Not many--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Worst professor I've ever had. I had to withdraw from the course because she expects you to teach yourself, and when she teaches (if she even does) she talks about irrelevant things that don't matter. Groups present every other class and shes always late. She is extremely rude and will not help you.

Worst professor. Shows up late to class doesn't actually teach anything and shows movies all the time. Expects you to know information all on your own and classes are rarely related to anything significant. Had to do group projects that one group would present a day in class and that would be the whole class. So many wasted days. Don't take her.

At the end of the course I may say "That wasn't bad." But it's a love-hate relationship. She isn't clear with her criteria, the syllabus will be changed, and if you don't read, you won't pass. Map tests are easy, and the research paper is a doozey (8-10 pages!). Not unbearable, but hard to deal with 75% of the time. She can seem mean sometimes.

easily the worst professor I have ever had. From day one she will tell you that you a "making dumb excuses" if you simply ask her to clarify what she means by something... very rude attitude. and always grades stuff wrong so keep an eye out for that if ur in her class. extremely easy to get a good grade though, if you can stand her presence.

Professor Teboh's Modern Africa HST 290 class was one of the easiest history classes I took at UMD.  There were a couple of easy map tests, one research paper, a presentation, and a take home final.  She usually came to class about 10 minutes late and often let us leave 15 minutes early (Tues, Thurs class).

Be advised, she will always be 10 mins late to class. But just wait it out. This was such an easy course! I found it to be an interesting class, and as long as you show up and participate you will do fine.

She was the worst professor I have ever had. She was always 10+ minutes late to class. She did not cover any meaningful material and expected you to know material she never dicussed in class for your oral presentation, term paper, midterm and final. For the map quiz you have to memorize all of the countries in Africa & selling counts. Run!

all she does is scribble notes on the board and yell at you if you don't seem interested. tests are too hard considering she doesn't teach you anything. she's always late! she made us write a 10pg paper on topics we knew nothing of and write 5pgs on a movie that was irrelevant! AVOID AVOID AVOID!

Worst professor I've had. Lectures were not clear at all and there was no organization to their order. Honestly, I didn't learn a thing because you barely had to know details. The projects/exams are way too complex for an "elective" class. Stay away from Teboh, I wish I heeded the warnings. The textbook's wording is laughable.

Probably the easiest class i ever took.  First day she didn't show up.  Mid term was take home and the final was to make a crossword puzzle in class.  She is really nice if you answer her questions and do your readings.  I did no readings and still got a B

class was very interesting, pretty easy A, attendance is not mandatory although she does take attendance-it doesn't matter if you miss a few classes but don't skip all the time. No final bc she had enough grades, the midterm was a take home, we did write a lot of papers but some you could write with a partner, so overall a pretty easy class

Not a good teacher at all. Scribbles notes on the board and expects you to teach yourself. Always at least 10 minutes late and lets you out of class at least 10 minutes early (the only good thing). No need to buy both books they have the same information in them. Easy A if you do work, but she gave an easy final.

Relax with the negativity people. Don't let first impressions fool you. She is a good teacher, that may tend to repeat herself but it's for your benefit. She may come across as hard but as the semester continues she shows a great sense of humor. And she will help you if you need it. No probs with Prof. B. She's good.
Easy class. Has nothing to do with history. Professor Teboh is intelligent and entertaining. Easy A.

She yelled at me the second day of class for no reason.  She is rude to students sometimes.  She has changed apparently from last semester, and is very difficult, I don't know why she got easy ratings, perhaps because this is upper level course.  She is not late, and has not missed any classes yet.  She is smart, but a little to sassy.  She assigns

Worst prof i have ever had.  Easy A, but if you want to learn, AVOID AT ALL COSTS.  She canceled half of our assignments and 1/5 of our classes.  I know a lot about this subject (Africa) and trust me, she is talking out of her ass.  she teaches her opinions as if they are fact.

Worst prof i have ever had.  If you want an east A, TAKE HER.  If you want to learn, AVOID AT ALL COSTS.  she canceled 1/5 of the classes.  it was a three hour class, she came late and let us out an 1/1.5 hours early.   I know a lot about this subject (Africa) and trust me, she is talking out of her ass.  she teaches her opinions as if they are fac
Hold on, this is to edit the previous comment. She NEVER showed up on our midterm! How awful!

Awful professor! Was late to our FIRST class!Mostly always 10-15 mins late! Was also late the day of our Midterm! Yes, she gives a midterm. VERY confusing. When she gives notes, she scribbles all over the board, and makes no sense. When lecturing, goes over the words already written on the board with her marker! Avoid!

wicked boring class awfullll, only buy the course book! don't buy any of the others she wants you to get b/c she doesn't follow the syllabus whatsoever so you won't even end up needing them, gives a couple short quizzes if she doesn't think the class is reading, sends attendance sheet around, shes almost always 5-10 mins late

I don't know what everyone is so bothered about.  She's fine.  She does tend to repeat herself and sometimes she's a bit late, but not always.  I had her for Modern Africa, she knows her stuff and is very nice.  Quizzes and papers are very easy.  If you want an easy "A", take this course.

She is always late to classes and sometimes she doesn't even show up! Aleast she always has her outfits together...lol anyways a waste of money if you ask me AVOID at all cost shes irresposible and blames her tardiness on others.
She is always late to classes and sometimes she doesn't even show up! Aleast she always has her outfits together...lol anyways a wastye of money if you ask me AVOID at all cost
Always shows up 15-25 minutes late.

She is really nice and the class is super easy but its true the woman is never on time. She also can be kind of confusing about her assignments and whats expected of you. She is also a little disorganized but if you can tolerate her flaws you'll get an easy A.
Has absolutely NO idea what she is talking about..."Antarctica is not a country"

She was always 5-10 minutes late from class, and one day when she was 15+ min late and we all left, she had the audacity to tell us that next time she was that late WE should come see if she's there. She "lost track of the time". She's pretty boring and repetative in lecture, the books weren't nesecary for in-class and her attitude bugged me.

Late for Class.  Unorangized.  Repeated herself during several times during a lecture.  Did not go into details in lectures, but expected details which the text did no provide for exams and essays.  Did not give you presentation topic until a few days before due.  Believes "Consistancy [amongst class sections] is relative."  Advoid.

This class was horrible! She repeated herself every class and expected you to be prepared for group projects, tests and have whole books read in about 2-4 days. Didn't explain things well and was always at least 10 min late. Many people complained, but she was generous and gave me a B after she gave me unfair bad grades on everything else! Avoid!!!

Very nice lady, very informative, very late. She DID get a little upset with the class since nobody was bringing their books to class, studying, or taking the class seriously about halfway through. Anyway, you need to learn all of Africa for a map test, have an easy video report, and a final. Aside from the map test, easiest class I  had in college

This class was the easiest A I've received in college. She's always 20 mins late to class, especially towards the end of the semester and she's really laid back. The class gets boring sometimes but she can be pretty funny too.
Late for class everyday but class was real easy. I learned more from the readings then the actual class but it was very relaxed. I would take her again if I need easy grade
THIS PROFESSOR NEVER SHOWS UP TO CLASS.   She is really nice and really easy but she is never in class EVER. Easy A.

This class quite literarily had be in tears 3 times a week, she knows her information do not  get me wrong but when it comes to teaching forget it, it consists of take notes, study for hours a day, and pray you pass the exam. No assignments just exams and quizzes.  unfortunately this class lead me to changing my major. its doable but at what cost.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Very difficult exams and does not give clear information on what to study before exams. With my experience I would study the wrong material or if I studied the correct material, barely any of what I knew was on the exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Barrett is so passionate about A&P and really wants her students to understand. Her class is entirely lecture based, and it's sometimes hard to read her handwriting. The exams are multiple choice, but you definitely want to attend class and study well for them. She drops the lowest quiz grade, and gave minimal pop quizzes for attendance.

She is a great prof and she knows when her students are struggling, gives chances to bring grades up. You have to study A LOT to pass this class at least 12hours a week.  Review all the chapters/figures before each exams. Active recall & Feyman technique will be your best friend. Attendance is not mandatory you better be there if you want to pass!
Barret will NOT do you wrong, show up and take meticulous notes and study and you will be chilling, if you put in your work she will get you through it trust, very funny and caring, loves questions!
DON'T TAKE THIS CLASS GO TO ANOTHER SCHOOL.

she is a good teacher. She gives pop quizzes for extra credit on exams and offers 3 questions for extra credit on exams. she is always willing to help you out but exams are really hard and she lectures very quickly.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Barret is a professor that cares, but also teaches a weed-out class. She is very clear with her notes and what the exams will be on, all being multiple choice with a min per question, but the course itself is EXTREMELY challenging for some. just over 1000 flashcards had to be made to cover everything in my study group. Funny, enjoyable, but hard.

Dr Barrett is an amazing professor and is extremely personable and funny. She is a great lecturer and is always more than happy to go over things 1 on 1. She gives lots of extra credit on exams and gives you good examples of how to apply the anatomy material learned in class in real life. Dr Barrett is amazing :)

Professor Barrett is a great professor and she provides a lot of extra credit on exams! She's always available to answer questions and she breaks down the content for you. Although, if you want to be successful you need to put in a lot of work into studying for this class for sure, especially the lab portion. Also, try to not skip lectures.

I've had Dr. Barrett for A&P I and II. She is a great professor. You can tell she is passionate about the subject and is very knowledgeable. She is very approachable and is always willing to answer questions. She explains everything very well in her lectures. She is kind and wants the best for her students. I would recommend her any day.

Yes, her class is hard, but put effort in the beginning and you will do fine slacking off in the end. She gives so much extra credit its almost impossible to fail. DO NOT get behind. Study in small increments because you cannot wait until the night before. Dont bother reading the textbook, just copy her lecture slides TRUST ME. Exams are very clear

Dr. Barrett is a great professor who wants to see her students succeed in the classroom. The class is based off of 4 exams and 6 quizzes, so be prepared to spend a lot of time studying the material. Do not make the mistake I did of bombing the first exam and have to play catch-up the entire semester. She gives lots of extra credit on the exams.

Honestly, all you need to do to pass this class is PAY ATTENTION!! The content is tough to learn if you've never taken anatomy and physiology before, but just make sure that you take notes on EVERYTHING she says and STUDY! The lectures aren't recorded, so please make sure to attend. If you every have questions she'll for sure help you.

Dr. Barrett is an amazing teacher, she starts the year off by giving you so many ways to succeed in her class. She goes in-depth in her lectures and my advice would be to take what she tells you, study it, then challenge yourself to further your understanding of the lecture material. As long as you do that, you should be able to pass her class.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Barrett is an amazing professor! As long as you study the lecture slides, and take notes during lectures, you'll do great. You're only graded on 4 or 5 exams and online quizzes, so make sure you know your stuff. She also has a lot of extra credit opportunities. Not having homework was a relief as well. She's very caring towards students.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Barrett is an amazing individual, but she tends to drag out her lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The material of the class itself is difficult to understand. As long as you pay attention she does a good job going through the material and is really helpful when you reach out and ask questions or seek guidance. She is very encouraging and will help you if you are struggling.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Barrett made my freshman-year nursing experience great. This class is extremely difficult but in order for her to help you you NEED to reach out and put the effort in.  She is willing to help in any way possible if she knows you are trying. Exams may be tough but she usually will point out what will be on them. She deserves a lot more credit
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Was not a easy class professor dint help much
She was sick for the entire semester and did not explain any of the work. GOOD LUCK.

Very nice and will meet with you outside of class. I failed by 1 point (76) in nursing program. Other reviews say it was easy, not keeping in mind students who probably have never previously taken a course like this or just struggle more than others.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I would never take a class with this professor again if I was given the option to. I have never felt so depressed by a class, no matter how hard I studied and used different tactics, she makes it also or impossible to pass. The pro is that she gives a lot of bonuses. I would not recommend, this class almost ruined me.

Dr. Barrett is very passionate and knowledgeable of anatomy and physiology. If you take the time to show up to class, take good notes, read the textbook and put in time to study you can excel in this class. The exams consist of everything on the chapter(s) slideshows. Take the time to ask questions if you do not understand.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This class is an easy A, all you have to do is make sure you put in the effort! People claim she is hard but the  they feel that way b/c they aren't spending much time studying as they should be. This is the easiest nursing class so if this class is troubling for you then I'd rethink nursing b/c classes like patho/medsurg are 10x harder than this.
reviews.rating_tags: Test heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class
