reviews.comment: By far one of the hardest courses I have ever taken but not because of the material. Barrett makes it next to impossible to pass the tests and does a poor job lecturing. Will also cancel class whenever she feels like it. Also beware of the high reviews, as it seems she writes them herself. You will have to teach yourselves; good luck!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: doesnt give anything to study for exams. Tells you the chapters and thats about it.

I am a junior and I see a lot of bad reviews for this class and this class is required for nursing majors. If you expect to look up her test and quizzes online then this class is not for you and neither is nursing. This class is one of the easiest nursing classes. If you cannot put the work in you should choose another major.

Professor Barrett is not a bad professor. As long as you get your work done, rewatch her lecture notes and go to her office hours you will be fine. People want the easy way out of her class and it doesn't work that way. You have to put effort into your work for the class.

If you have an actual question, ask her and she will help you. Do not ask her basic questions that she explains during lectures or questions that are clearly stated in the textbooks. This is a difficult course by nature; there is a lot of content to learn. Expect to study outside of class regardless of which professor teaches the course.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Literally , if you study for her exams and quizzes , you will still fail. Class averages are awful for class of 2021.. if the class average is low .. clearly the teacher is doing something wrong.
Worst teacher ever always cancel class she dont care if you pass or not not willing to help at all
awful. doesnt care if you pass she just wants to look good and make money. her exams are insanely hard unless you spend all day every day studying. shes unrealistic, dry, and rude.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: dry, personalityless, rude, and does not care if we pass or not
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This was one of the hardest classes I have ever taken. She gives quizzes that are almost impossible to finish on time same with exams. I struggled extremely hard in this class but worked 12 hours a week OUTSIDE of class time and also took this class in high school so I had background knowledge and still did terribly.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: shes way too difficult for online classes. cut us some slack
flies through lectures and excepts us to know ALL material
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: avoid if possible. she doesnt care about her students she just lectures and thats it, doesnt care if we pass or fail. way too hard for a freshman class

She has no personality and goes way too fast during lecture. She also gave a student a 0 on an project because they used a photo from online and didnt cite the source. All you do is study. prepare to teach yourself, her lectures are hard to follow. She never responds to anybodys emails either which is pathetic because we are paying her for help.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: She's an amazing professor. Be ready to take notes. she allows you to record her lectures in class, it's nice to listen to them after class. Her test are not hard if you study. what she teaches is on the test. so make sure you understand the material.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: HORRIBLE PROFESSOR!!! DOWN RIGHT DOES NOT GIVE A CARE ABOUT HER STUDENTS. IS ONLY A PROFESSOR FOR THE MONEY.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: This is hands down the worst professor I have ever had. She is not caring and would rather see you fail than succeed. She is not approachable , nor is she understanding. She is an unfair grader. She has no personality and does not enjoy her job.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I really liked her. Very straight forward but you have to study and take the class seriously. There were really not that many curve balls. She is very kind and has a good personality and is very clear when she explains things. She really knows her stuff but also seems very humble. Lecture pace is somewhat fast but again not difficult, just a lot.

The class is hard but its doable i feel like as long as you show her that you actually care about her class and your grade she is always willing to help, she gives the same amount of effort as you give. I suggest come to class on time and sit as close to the front as possible and i would recommend printing out your power points it helps a lot.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Don't believe more than half of the positive reviews. If you do not need to take this class don't take it. I needed it for a pre-req for the accelerated nursing program, I always felt discouraged even though I am a hard worker and would always study. Unfortunately she is the only professor in UMD who teaches Anatomy both semesters.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: This class is very challenging. Barrett lacks explaining and answering students questions well. Her lecture notes do not have all the added info you need to know. Go to class and make sure you voice record lectures. Also, make friends so you can help each other on tasks (quizzes). Its possible to earn a decent grade but you have to STUDY WEEKLY!!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Dr. Barrett is the absolute sweetest! Definitely my favorite lecturer for freshmen year! Although her class if hard, if you put in the study time, you'll do awesome! Be sure to pay attention! Overall great professor, wish I could have her for every class.
Make sure to start studying from the first day. The tests are worth so much that if you slip up on any of them, it is hard to bring your grade up.

incoming freshman should be ready to work if taking they are taking this class for nursing. Barrett is an awesome person and is more than willing to help outside of class, (also really good for lab) beware that lectures are dense and long, exams are not fun but they are doable if you study hard.
Her lectures are very boring and she speaks really quickly and does not explain things well at all, she adds lots of details she says you will need to know but in the end aren't on the exams.

MY ABSOLUTE FAVORITE PROFESSOR OF ALL TIME. Her lectures are so straight forward as long as you pay attention you can't get lost. Her tests are never a surprise; multiple choice and never puts anything on it that she does not talk about in lecture. Would I recommend? DEFINITELY. Nursing students, this class is an easy A.

I ABSOLUTELY ADORE BARRETT <3 She is very smart, a good lecturer, and a wonderful professor. The class can be challenging at times, but Barrett is always available for office hours and will go over stuff with you. She is also very down to earth and easy to talk to. Literally would take this class 100x again because of Barrett!
I love Diana! Such a sweet lady who makes A+P so much fun! She is wicked accessible and super great to talk to. 10/10 would recommend taking her class!
Honestly one of the sweetest professors. Although the tests/quizzes are pretty hard she is always ready to help you when  you ask!! Make sure you study!

One of my favorite professors here, such a nice person and is incredibly laid back and easy going. 3 online quizzes. She will go above and beyond to help students and will explain concepts in detail. an easy A and a great professor!
Such a nice lady if your nice to her. Always wants to help you!

Professor McCliment is amazing and literally impossible to dislike. She makes the classes fun and not overbearing because the class is ONLY TAKEN AS AN ELECTIVE. With that being said, if you somehow dislike her, you're the definition of a contrarian who needs to smile for once.

Course is a great GPA booster - everyone gets an A if you submit anything. She knows her subject well but she's not interested in giving feedback or answering questions. 5 items were graded in one go, so don't expect her to read anything. It was a transfer course for me and I seriously didn't expect to be viewed as a waste of time by my prof
The easiest teacher in my opinion all the tests were online and attendance isn't mandatory

She was literally the best. Her lectures were very easy to understand, and all testing was open note. She knows that most people taking her class aren't bio majors, so she gives only the necessary information for the exams and does an exam review the class before. The only other assignments are a few discussion boards. Definitely recommend.

Great professor! Had her for Bio 101 and she is great at explaining concepts and making lectures interesting. Not many homework assignments (3 or 4 discussion boards throughout the semester) and 4 tests (including final). She is super understanding and kind and you can tel how passionate she is about her job. Definitely take her!!!

Very clear and very engaging lectures. There was little homework and when there was it was fairly easy. Attendance was occasionally taken with a one question quiz that she gives u the answer to. Very interesting topics. She is so passionate about biology. highly recommend, would take again, one of the easiest classes ive taken.
super nice and easy class (bio102) attendance isn't required only complaint is she never answers her email but other than that she's awesome

BIO 101 covers credit that anyone needs, and I HIGHLY recommend that you take her. She is so easy and nice, and tests are easy and online. Although attendance is not required, show up to class you get extra credit and the lectures are nice. This is an easy GPA boost, just take her classes.
I had this professor for BIO 101 and 102. She is amazing take her!!!!

Super nice and cares a lot about science. Made Biology actually interesting, and when I was sick it is super easy to make up whatever you missed online! Not a lot of homework and easy tests!!!! Recommend her definitely!
Class was super easy, show up take notes, have an online open notes test and your chilling. She also does a review guide in class before every test.
cares about her students more than grades will help you with tests work notes records lectures and makes class so fun and a good enviorment to be in

I had McCliment for molecular bio (BIO 427) and she was a great professor who cared more about understanding than she did about grades. We had two in class tests which you could rewrite to earn points back and a take home final and one major writing assignment that she had us start early on. She did disappear during finals week but that was it.
This professor is god-tier. If you have her try your best to keep her. The coursework is easy and you actually learn unlike many professors here.
Great professor very enthusiastic, attendance optional, very kind and makes the lectures very intriguing.
This was one of the easiest science classes I've taken. She makes lectures interesting and makes sure to accomodate to students needs. As long as you pay attention and take notes its an easy A.
Prof. McCiment was so good to us during my online spring semester 2021. I always loved Bio but she made it more interesting. Highly recommend!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: He's a good professor with pretty engaging lectures, and gives study guides (definitely use them!!!) plus extra credit for exams if the class average is low. Prepare yourself for biochemistry and micro-bio. Just put a lot of work into this class, especially if you're not a Biology major.

Okay this was last year for me but I thought I'd give my input. He is a very tough professor, definitely try not to take him if you can help it. Most of the class when I was in there would fail tests and I'm pretty sure the average for one test was a 33 at some point. For the final, the only reason I passed was because he raised the grade by 10pts.

Although I did relatively well in his class, I wouldn't recommend his course if you are not good at bio already. He has very strict course expectations because it'll only get harder in the future. Way too many exams in one semester for his class. If you cant pass his class, you will not be able to succeed in bio.
Worst class ever dont take him

You're only graded by quizes and tests. This is not an intro level course. The exams have questions that he has never taught you. He even says on the first day of class, and in the syllabus, that he gives questions he will never teach you. You will only do good if you know a lot of bio, he tests you on alot  of things he never teaches.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He gives so much information every class and doesnt fully explain it even tho its beginner level. Had to teach myself for hours after every class. Ur grade is mainly on exams. The class fails every exam but he doesnt do anything abt it. Teaching style isnt good unless u know bio. if u care abt ur gpa dont take it unless u wanna breakdown alot.

I cannot make this up when I tell you every one was failing the class. He gave no homework and only exams everyone would bomb the exam and he would only give us 1 point for extra credit. There was little effort done to help us bring our grades up and all he would do is just read off of the slides. I would rate this class a 0/10. AVOID AT ALL COSTS!

Dr. Winslow is a welcoming and nice guy, and a great welcoming professor for new incoming students. He can be a bit lecture heavy, but the overall workload is not too bad, and you will do well on exams if you study the content. Had a good experience with him for my freshman year.
he knows what he is talking about, but the course is rigorous and extremely difficult. expect several mandatory breakdowns.
Terrible class

First thing he said first lecture is most people drop the class. This was true. He is a very tough grader and does not give feedback. Every lecture is his talking the entire time reading off the slides. Very boring. He is not easy to reach by email he often times just never responds. I would not take this class unless you are good at bio already.

I took his 121 BIO course as a Computer Science student and can honestly say that he is the worst professor that I have ever had at this school. Exam 1 class average was 54, Exam 2 was practically the same. Despite this he takes 0 accountability for his terrible and non engaging teaching style and makes backhanded comments. AVOID AT ALL COSTS...
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Lecture heavy. Review material outside class and make sure you understand it before the next lecture. Go to PAL sessions if you need help, def cleared up some difficult concepts. Do the work and you'll easily pass
Boring, didn't actually teach a lot, but i passed so clearly the content he showed was good. Met one of my best friends in this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: He's a great guy. Not the best professor. For his exams, the class average has consistently been in the mid 50s. He provides little extra credit for you to attempt to boost that score. Weekly textbook assignments are multiple choice and pretty easy. Lectures have so much information it's hard to keep up. He kinda rambles too much in class as well

Despite it being probably my least favorite class, he is one of the best professors i have. super friendly, respects everyone. his lectures are very long and so packed with information that it can be hard to keep up with. average exam grade is failing. i like him but im not taking biology again, because i was put in biochem instead of normal chem.
Not very approachable, lectures dont make much sense, would not recommend and will not be taking again.

I really enjoyed his lectures. The tests are really hard but once you take the first one you know how he tests and you can prepare better. I recommend getting help through PAL, the tutoring center, or even just youtube to study. Also, he offers easy weekly assignments that cushion your grade.

If you are a Computer Science major I would recommend just taking a Physics class as your lab science. This is not a beginner-level class and you are expected to know a lot when you start. Tests were very difficult and he was a pretty bad teacher.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Very easy class other then tests
This class was hard I won't lie, but if you start studying early you should be okay. Lectures can be a little boring but there is not a lot of work outside of class at all.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Winslow is very inviting and does a great job introducing biology to first years. His class can be difficult at times but he gives students plenty of tools and opportunities to do well. If you're willing to put in the effort and time you can do very well in his classes.

Winslow is an amazing freshman professor. If you keep up in class (lectures or textbook) tests and homework are a breeze. He's also a great lab instructor and will answer any questions you have. Very fun and good to talk to for bio.

he just was terrible, he could not explain things clearly where the average person could understand. His office hours were a waste of my time, he does not care about my success at all, not changing the format of tests when kids are consistently getting 40s and lower, his way of teaching putting a ppt on the screen and you just take notes,
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Exams are worth 70% of your overall grade so if you fail even one exam, you are screwed. He just lectures the whole class so if youre not an auditory learner, youre also screwed. Nothing in the class made sense and no extra credit opportunities were given. He also expects you to study 6-12 hours a week which is unrealistic with your other classes
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Friendly guy, covered the material well and prepared you for future bio courses. If you take his lab be ready to write full reports.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Hilarious--So many papers

reviews.comment: Pretty cool guy. Friendly, accomadating etc. His class was super test heavy, but if you studied you did great. Important to not just memorize the material but think of what the content from the lectures means in a real world context. I'd definitly be his student again (this was during COVID so I was online, so didn't get to meet him but want 2)
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I've taken the prerequisite bio 121 class the previous semester, and it was no where near as difficult as this semester. The tests are very content heavy, which means you really need to understand the concept even if it is not specifically mentioned in the book or lectures. And for that reason, this class is difficult. A 100 is near impossible.

Test's are a large part of this class so if you're not a strong test taker like myself then this class will prove to be difficult. Studying was very hard because he didn't clarify specifics so just try your best to write down anything new to you.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Not the best of Professors. Pretends to care, but a facade only lasts so long. Is not flexible in the slightest to meet a student's unique needs. Why is he so hard in an introductory course? To make the nursing program more competitive?
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I was a 4.0 student before I took his class, that went downhill fast. His lectures are disorganized and the science he teaches is slightly bias and it shows. His lecturing style is really hard to learn from and his exams don't mirror what he stresses in class. As a bio major I dreaded this class which has never been the case for me.

He's a nice guy and he clearly cares about his students. I actually learned a lot and I put effort into his class because his exams r hard. But as long as you study (a lot), you'll do fine. To study, just spend like 5 days listening to the recorded lectures and do flashcards. I highly recommend that you take his class seriously or you will fail.

This Professor is not willing to work with you to help you do well. His exams are difficult, and they are 70% of the course. Does not allow any extra credit (for bogus reasons) and does not accept any excuses in missed assignments/quizzes (barring medical). In short, he is a professor who has not yet learned to see his students as people.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: A majority of your grade is based on doing the readings and getting a timed quizzed on the reading. Make sure you pay attention while you read the chapter and understand it because quiz questions can be very specific. Exams are concept based, try to make up your own questions when studying. Takes attendance every class.

One of my favorite professors. He is super nice and knowledgeable about the topic. I would highly recommend this class for any incoming freshman. If you have a question he is always available to answer any question. All PowerPoints are online which makes studying easy.

Don't take the classes if you need a Science elective as the exams are really difficult if you don't know college-level biology well. Really caring and passionate about the subject and willing to scale exams when needed though. Also takes attendance through Socrative so get ready to take good notes and study hard to even think about a good grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Caring

reviews.comment: Really a wonderful man. He is a very caring, understanding, and funny guy. Going to miss not having him for biology next semester. Usually scales tests depending on class average, and has two online quizzes a week, one about what you were confused on and one using the textbook on the next chapter.

Winslow is a really easy-going professor and the lectures are great. There is not a lot of homework at all, and what there is it is easy. The lectures are posted online, but he does ask questions in class that get graded for participation. Overall a good class that does require some studying, but otherwise is not hard or time-consuming.

Winslow is an awesome professor who really cares about teaching and his students. Material is pretty straightforward but he explains it in a fun and clear way so you won't get bored. You need the textbook for reading quizzes. Tests cover what you learn in class and in the textbook so as long as you stay on top of the material, you can't do poorly.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Amazing lectures--Would take again

reviews.comment: He is a funny guy and good at biology, of course. His tests are mainly multiple choice. usually two quizzes a week but the kicker is that they are online and only worth .25 points each or something like that. Has a study guide for students to use before tests. Is willing to scale tests when applicable. He is approachable and he really does care.

So I took AP biology in high school and the book we use in 121 is literally the same book. This class is SO easy if you have taken AP bio already. I passed the test It just didn't count for me. Lectures are straightforward and easy to follow. Always lets you ask questions and is overall a nice guy.

Professor Winslow was a great professor for not just BIO 121, but BIO122 and BIO132. He really knows the material and loves the subject. He makes the lectures interesting and grades fairly. If you show up to class, you'll pass. I would definitely take him a third semester!

He is one of the best professors I have ever had. He is inviting and cares about the well-being of his students. Though his tests are hard, he prepares his students for them very well, as he creates study guides and holds an in class review before each test. He is a quality person.
Classes are basically PowerPoint lectures. Quizzes are easy. Study a lot from the lecture notes for the exams. Exams are manly open response with some multiple choice and drawing diagrams.
It was overall an interesting class. He gives lectures through powerpoints. Weekly quizzes are easy. Only a few tests were given all semester. Test questions are based off of lectures.
He's a good teacher and advisor. Make sure you dedicate a lot of time to studying so you actually know what your talking about.
Lectures are posted online do if you miss class you can catch up. If you do TOPHAT go to class

If you put in effort to understand the material, you will not be disappointed by this man. There are no tricks or any bull that would jeopardize your grade other than yourself. A VERY refreshing class in that respect. Work for an A and that's what you'll get.
He's a good guy
He is a GREAT guy! If you need help go to his office hours and he will be there. He answers questions and really want the students to learn the material. If you don't study, you won't do well though.

I received an A+ in difficult AP Biology course, I had to take this class anyway, his tests are almost all short answer to questions that are very insignificant to the subject material. Not much homework if any, you don't really need the book but may be a good investment. classes very boring.

He's a good professor . Lectures could be more intresting. This is my second time taking class with him the 122 is kind of the same set up as the 101 as far as reading the slides and not needing the book. Winslow explains thing in way so that you can understand. The only difference in taken the 122 is the test are short answer not too many m.c.

Dr Winslow is an amazing professor. Definitely one of the best out of the bio department. He cares very  much about his students and their quality of education. He will put the time and effor into making sure his students understand material. He is also very funny and kind. You do have to work for your grade, but he is there to help you.

Winslow is a really easy professor. His tests come straight from his powerpoint, the textbook is rarely used. His lectures can get boring because of his voice, but still go because he does use his iClicker questions as attendance. Overall easy class and good professor.

Dr. Winslow is a very good professor. He explains the material thoroughly. If you show up to class & pay attention, you will pass. Tests can be challenging, just study the power points from lecture. Participation is important, he has at least 1 iClicker question per lecture. He's always in his office so if you're struggling, go see him for help!!!!

This class is really straight forward and as long as you read the textbook you won't fall behind. iClickers are a must and they are used to determine whether you're in class or not (attendance). Seeing how I'm a student that tried to learn from the power points alone, this class was very challenging to me. Studying is definitely essential!

Don't get me wrong, he was very nice BUT class was boring. I fell asleep almost everytime and I sat in the front. His study guides weren't helpful. He even told us not to use the study guide as a guide for the tests. The tests were hard if you didn't understand the material. I don't recommend this class unless you already understand BIO.
The easiest class i have ever had.  He is a good teacher and I learned a lot but the workload is low.  If he teaches a class for bio majors, I'm sure it is much harder.

He seems like a really nice approachable guy. His lectures are a bit on the boring side, though. He tries to spice them up, but for the most part his voice kind of puts you to sleep. Tests are a huge portion of your grade, but are reasonably graded and easy to study for. An easy assignment once a week and then a clicker question to take attendance

Pretty boring class. Never used the textbook. Exams are easy if you look over the slide a few times. I have never payed attention in class and have gotten 100's on the tests. I clicker is worth 5% for attendance and a weekly discussion question comment is worth 15%. Very doable and easy take it for a science elective.

If your not a Bio major you will be lost after the first exam. Exams are 75% of your grade and he doesn't explain very well, at all. His slides don't help much either. Class is boring as hell. I honestly did not like this dude at all, not cause he wasn't nice, he's just not a good teacher for a 101 class.

I found this class to be a really great gen-ed for non-bio majors! I agree that his lectures are sometimes boring but he is passionate about the subject. If you go to class and read over notes and the text, you can do really well in this class. I'm taking his Bio 102 class next semester too!

Definitely a biology nerd but doesn't really show it unless you ask him direct questions. His lectures are pretty dry and he drones a lot, I honestly only remember things he taught that I already knew for the most part. Tests were pretty easy and straightforward and he's a cool guy. Taking him again next semester.

Great professor! Tests aren't bad at all, he prefers you to know the concept more than the tiny details, no homework just discussion group, he always posts the notes online as well. He's very helpful and tries to make biology very understandable. Sit in the front row and you will do great!
He was super nice but his class is really boring. Reads right off slides. Exams are hard but not bad if you study. Passible class just not much fun.

He is a remarkably intelligent, understanding and sweet professor. Classes aren't mandatory; the homework level is not demanding and his tests are easy if you review his powerpoints beforehand. For homework you have to answer a question on a discussion board for 15% of your grade.It's easy. Going to class only helps because it's 10% of your grade.
There's no need to buy the book! He's very helpful but you need to really study so you won't fail the tests. Go to his office hours if you need help. Overall good professor

Prof. Winslow is a really great guy and I learned a lot in his class. Going to class isn't mandatory but it really helps to understand the material. He teaches mainly from powerpoints, but he has a good sense of humor and tries to make things interesting.
Clicker used, easy if you take notes.

This class was really easy. Attending class will only help your grade. If you understand what's happening on the Power Point slides, you'll do fine in his class. He has office hours and will respond to your emails. He will help you if you ask.

Professor Winslow is a very nice guy. Though his lectures may seem long or boring they are backed pull of information that he puts on his tests. Though coming to class isn't mandatory it can only improve your grade because you get points for using your clicker. His tests aren't too hard if you study. Make sure you look over your class notes and ppt

Prof Winslow is a nice guy but his teaching style is somewhat lacking. Def read the book because his explanations in class are all over the place. As long as you study extra hard in his class you'll pass. READ THE TEXTBOOK and look at his powerpoints.

Professor Winslow was an alright professor. There's nothing real special about him and his slides are not that clear. He also allowed students to pass in weekly assignments the following week, which for someone who does there's on time, got me pretty upset. His wording on the exams are confusing and he's too into the material and goes off track.

The material had potential, but the course was difficult to learn from. Expectations and grading standards often felt unclear, and feedback was not very helpful for improvement. Students who prefer highly independent learning may do better, but it was not an effective fit for me.

I took AED 616 graduate program. She's extremely knowledgeable but sadly it becomes irrelevant when students don't feel motivated. She wants you to succeed, but it comes with some condescending approaches. Also, don't miss class!

I think she is a good person who cares and wants her students to do well. That being said, I think she sometimes forgets that we have lives outside of her class.  She will find something wrong with whatever assignments you submit, no matter how many hours you put into it. She is very unclear and the homework load is ridiculous.

I've had Cathy alot- the thing about her is that she is super smart & wants to see everyone succeed but you have to show that you want to to her. She wants to save the world through art ed which makes her very intimidating & makes you want to impress her, which seems hard from her demeanor sometimes but she understands the stress and wants to help

Professor Miraglia is probably one of the best professors/teachers I've ever had. She is so intelligent and explains every topic super well. She truly wants the students who actually try to do well. The course content can be very tough but I'm convinced having her as a prof is the reason I did so well and actually retained the information.

Professor Miraglia is such a kind caring person and truly wants all her students to pass. The material can become overwhelming being a 4 credit class but if you show up you'll pass! She will always try her best to reword things in a way to make it easier to understand! 100% would take her again
horrible horrible horrible

Don't get me wrong Miraglia was a very nice person but professor wise she's horrible. She claims that she doesn't believe in study guides meanwhile expects us to know what's on the exam. Mad us believe that failing our exam was our fault even though we had to guide telling us what we needed to study. Nobody studied the right things!!!
LOVE LOVE LOVE MIRAGLIA. She is absolutely hilarious, and knows the topics like the back of her hand, and will help you whenever you need it!
I had for 4 semesters in a row and she is simply amazing. She cares for her students and talk to us a lot in lab/class. While lab may be challenging, she is very helpful and is a great resource.

She is extremely knowledgeable in her field, relates information to topics students can understand, and has a great sense of humor which certainly makes her lectures worthwhile. Attendance is not mandatory but showing up certainly is worthwhile. This was a really fun class and I recommend taking this course with her.

Prof Miraglia is great but I have to say shes a better lecturer than lab professor. Shes more tough in lab and expects you to know some stuff. She does help you though if you are stuck and is very helpful on some days.

This is one of the best teachers. She cares for her students and their grades. She wants you to pass and while tests can be difficult if you attend class and listen you will be fine. Also will give out extra credit at the end of the semester to help your grade. She is definitely the professor you want to take for both MLS 221 and 211.

This professor genuinely cares about her students and puts time and effort into each of her lectures. She also has a great sense of humor and while the course content is confusing, it's understandable if you pay attention and take notes. She will do her best to make sure every student passes but you still need to put in the work.

Prof. Miraglia is easily one of the best professors at UMD. I've never met a professor before who genuinely wants you to pass so bad, like she genuinely cares. She does her best to explain difficult concepts and she actually lectures. Study and you should be fine to pass the class. She's easy on the eyes and smart as hell. Take her!!
She really cares about her students succession and is very helpful. Hands down best professor I have ever had. A lot to learn, but she is great!

Professor Miraglia is really nice and has a passion for teaching. She tries her best to explain the material to you but honestly its just a tough class with a lot of materials. She gives extra credit on her exams and open note quizzes with unlimited time. Just try your best and you will pass. Miraglia does her best to help everyone.

Professor Miraglia is a breath of fresh air from other Med Lab professors. She's laid back and is really understanding. She knows what she's talking about and expects her students to as well. She will take her the time to better explain if there's time in lecture. Attendance not mandatory, but GO TO CLASS! You will fall behind if you don't keep up.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Professor Miraglia is great! The material isnt too difficult, but there's a ton of it. That being said, Prof Miraglia does her best to explain concepts thoroughly. She is especially helpful during office hours
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: Wonderful professor with a positive attitude. She's extremely helpful and approachable. There are a lot of notes, but they are comprehensive and really prepare you for the tests. Class will be easy for you if you stay on top of your game.

Great Professor! She is very helpful, and very knowledgeable in what she teaches. Shows a great interest in the subject. Cares about her students passing. Resting b face is on 99.9% of the time! but don't let that intimidate you shes very approachable. Office hours are flexible! Happy and willing to answer questions!

Love Miraglia! She is an awesome teacher, very knowledgable in the subject matter. Very accommodating and easy to talk to. She wants her students to succeed and does a very good job at helping them do so.
Professor Miraglia is  very helpful and nice. Her great attitude makes you wants to do well in her courses.

Great professor, really willing to help you out. Her tests are a b***h though, if you study your ass off consistenly you should do well. Don't show up? Ur getting an F. Textbook is only for the hw she gives, otherwise its useless. She FLIES through her lectures, so get a recorder, bring your laptop, 9 pgs of notes/hr kinda deal come prepared.
Awesome teacher! Not only is she hot, but she is very fair and approachable.
She goes through the slides really fast during lecture, but they are posted on canvas.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: She is easily one of the best professors I've had. While she is a tough grader, the workload isn't excessive and her expectations are always clear. She pushes you because she wants you to actually learn the material, not just pass. If you're willing to put in the effort, you'll realize her toughness is just her way of pushing you to succeed.

I felt belittled and not respected in this class. The professor's tone was often dismissive and the environment felt discouraging. I also witnessed her tell a student they likely wouldn't succeed in a program because English isn't their first language, which was very inappropriate. I wouldn't recommend this course.

Can't lie this was one of my first college courses that I did horrible in. I definitely was the class outkast I was often called on when no one knew the answer, I hated this class omg it's usually mandatory so just sending you your warnings.
Great Teacher many people make her seem so bad, her class is hard if u don't put the work into it but otherwise she's amazing and her class is interesting.
Professor Pierce is not that bad. It's kind of easy to talk to her. She is a tough grader and has very strict expectations.

Ms.Pierce made me feel as if I was an Outkast there was times where she made me feel as if I wasn't as smart as everyone else in my class. The very first day I stepped into that MLS class Ms.Pierce did not make me feel welcomed, each time I tired to reach out for help she would never get back to me. Do not take this class.
Professor Pierce was mean at times, didn't really help when I'd email her. Overall if I could go back I would not take her please run and save yourself.

One time where we were doing the graphs in class and my data didn't make sense but all my numbers were correct. She did it herself and got the same data I did. She constantly was saying that she couldn't figure out why it wasn't adding up but said she was going take major points off even though she couldn't figure it out herself. DO NOT TAKE HER!

Professor Pierce has a really low rating on here and I just don't think thats accurate. She is genuinely so kind and really wants all of her students to succeed, and she is really easy to talk to. The class isn't easy and you will have to put in the work, but it's worth it. Would definitely recommend!

Reluctant to actually teach. A professor who does not want to teach, just grade and judge students. Expects students to be experts on a course they are only just now taking. Ask questions and she will make you feel as if you are wasting her life's energy source. Seems as though she does not like her job or the departments structure. Not a good fit.

Great person, not so great professor. Hard to acess outside of class, slow at grading papers, feedback is not given in a timely manner. She is knowelgable in her field but that doesnt help when she is not accessabile outisde of class. Unfourtunatley it is difficult to address conflict resolution with this professor as well.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Great person, not so great professor. Hard to acess outside of class & feedback is not given in a timely manner. Time conscientious of her time but not students. Students had 2 Labs done in the same amount of time as one lab would be due and she spent most of the class going over math probelms, the majority of students expressed they understood.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Perez-Cano is an outstanding professor. She is extremely caring and genuinely invested in her students' success, both in learning a foreign language and at UMassD in general. It is clear through her lectures and the way she spends time with students in class that she wants everyone to do well. I would absolutely recommend her to anyone. 10/10.
So so good!
Definitely take professor cano, she's amazing
I would recommend taking her if you can. Whether it's for a language requirement or an elective requirement, take her.
just so sweet and so understanding and she clearly wants all her students to do well
la mejor profesora (she taught me that)
favorite professor!

super understanding, knows what she's teaching, is smart, and makes getting an A achievable
i recommend talking professor cano
she is not just a professor, but a TEACHER! lover her!
best 101 professor out there
funny professor who is super sweet and understanding
Fantastic! Best professor

shes actually pretty rude and very inappropriate as a professor. she mentioned to the whole class what i had emailed her PRIVATELY and yet still disregarded what i sent her. and dont bother having to go pee, she'll roll her eyes and shit talk you behind your back in spanish

Professor Tania is amazing! She actually is the sweetest professor I have ever had. I greatly recommend anyone to take her classes. She truly cares for all students, and is great as ensuring everyone is able to succeed in the class. She is the best at UMassD.

Profesora Perez-Cano is hilarious, relatable, and understanding. Graded assignments are laid out in advance and aren't too demanding in terms of content. She doesn't have a late policy, so turning in assignments on time is a priority. Her class was one of my favorites this semester, and she's the best professor I've had in the Spanish department.
My favorite language class I have taken!
This class made me actually enjoy Spanish and I wish I took her for all of my language requirements. Highly recommend taking her!
Tania was a fun professor to have. She is also very passionate about what she teaches. She also cares about her students and wants them to succeed. She was super flexible during corona as well.

I personally liked having her as my Spanish professor. She made me feel comfortable when learning the language and made it interesting. She also cares for her students and wants the best for them. Take her if you can.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Of all the spanish profs Ive had at the university shes been the most helpful by far. Ive had trouble speaking and using spanish, so her classes really improved my speech and gave me much needed practice. Her grading criteria is very clear, and she always makes time outside of class. Great caring prof and Im thankful to have been in her class

I really love prof Tania. She is understanding and such a great person to be taking Spanish with. She always supports us, even when our Spanish is a little off. If you are uncomfortable with Spanish in any way, I would highly recommend her. She won't make you feel bad about it. She is very clear on what she expects and helps you get there.

The class was straightforward and one of the easiest courses I took that semester. She's very sweet and if you need any help, she's always open to help. Her assignments were simple and helped when trying to understand the content and engage in it. Make sure you participate in class and speak in Spanish, of course.

Professor Perez Cano was just an okay teacher. I feel like I didn't really learn much from her class, but that may have been because it was online this semester. The coursework isn't bad, but she would assign things that weren't in the syllabus and not tell us, she would just expect us to know. She was also a little rude at times.

Textbook and attendance are required. He does tend to make ALOT of jokes. Assignments aren't too bad. Group assignments are pretty common and are many parts. The class duration is long aswell so prepare. He does give feedback sometimes it helps sometimes it can get a little confusing.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Dr. Esposito is an amazing professor. The ONLY reason you wouldn't succeed in his courses would be if you don't show up to class or pay attention. He doesn't ask for much from his students; he gives out study sheets for some courses, annotated notes, and will answer your question if it wasn't already explained. Definitely recommend as a professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
I am so glad I took this class! He made the class so enjoyable and made a lot of funny jokes throughout the class. 
His exams weren't too bad; he gave us study guides and practice problems that were basically the exams. 
I am taking another class with him, and I enjoy it.

He overdoes the jokes instead of applying the effort to his teaching, not the worst professor, but he isn't the most helpful. Once, he told me he couldn't help me with content that he himself was teaching, basically left me to fend for myself, no answers to the questions I had about the assignment he does not teach well; you have to teach yourself.

Have to buy two expensive books just to learn basic french for 102. stay away please for your sanity especially if you work or have many responsibilities. This class will take up so much of your time- mf too much

for a french102 course way too many assignments. More than my 300-400 level courses. Nearly everything graded is treated as an exam, so you have to do well on everything. Didn't even go over the syllabus the first day (which, btw is written ENTIRELY in french)- and expects you to be fluent. lets collectively stay away and hope she gets fired.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: rude and just talks and talks
never take.

Very strict and tough class, she was sometimes flat out rude to other students in the class and very disrespectful. This class was harder than my 500-600 level grad classes and the structuring was horrible. A quiz nearly every class and she would sometimes teach things right before the quiz because she forgot. One on one she was kind, but stay away
Too much work, doesn't care about students, more work than my 300 level English classes

the work in this class is very tedious HOWEVER she is a kind women and as long as you participant in group discussions and turn in the majority of the work you will pass. she tends to be disorganized. DONT take this class if you aren't willing to participant and aren't a big reader. this class is all about analyzing novels.
don't take this class unless you want to read a lot and do busy work
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: I'm an honors student and this class it has been near insanity with the amount of work given. Those who say just do the work and you'll be fine are very much so not considering that many people struggle with learning languages. She gives little guidance and when the whole class is struggling fails to self reflect that maybe she needs to slow down.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Professor Cheyne is an awesome professor who actually cares about her students. Her work mainly consists of showing up to class.There are weekly online quizzes but super easy Her assignments aren't too much but if you come to class, you will be able to get work done. If you have truble, just set meetings with her. She's a great professor

This lady is nice but she is a horrible professor. I took this thinking it would be an entry level French class but she taught it as if we were all fluent. The textbook with all of the homeworks is entirely in French also. I had to withdraw after she assigned a 200 word paper completely in French and said she would fail us if we used a translator

Dr. Cheyne gets more bad reviews than she deserves. She may be occasionally disorganized but gives students grace when she messes up (like falling behind). Don't complain about having do to work, if you do the work, you'll do well, but it's not easy. Very particular grading. With so many assignments, you can afford to slip up on a few and do well.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Class was manageable until the corona pandemic. The fact that the workload was enormous wasn't really a problem until we were all at home. She was really unsympathetic toward any of us and did nothing to help cut us some slack even in these extreme circumstances. Ruined French for me. Don't take this class unless you love French or are masochistic.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--So many papers

reviews.comment: She gives so much work its almost impossible to not go crazy. On top of that she does multiple quizzes and exams. Wouldnt recommend her at all.

She gives an absurd amount of homework. It gets to the point where you're just doing the homework to get it done and you're not actually learning anything. She also has little mercy. If you miss a quiz you cannot make it up. Her personality is kind but she can be passive aggressive. THE HOMEWORK IS CRAZY THOUGH.
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--SO MANY PAPERS

reviews.comment: Single worst class I have ever taken. Never gives feedback, grading is always extremely late. Only given two exams and a final. Was never on track with syllabus at all. Could never contact her, she had a problem with her emails and she KNEW but did nothing to work around it. Less of a lecture and more random info and unimportant talking.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Gives a lot of homework ,  you'll want to drop the first week of classes and also the final was 11 pages long !

I took this class online. I wish I didn't. I spent more time on this class than any class I've ever taken. The assignments / tests / quizzes are vague and usually jammed packed with spelling mistakes / unclear instructions. It feels like there is a lot of unnecessary busy work.

A required class for the honors program, yet it felt thrown together and confusing. Vague instructions were frustrating and there was a lot of reading that wasn't even discussed in class. Nice lady but boring/confusing class.

This class is necessary for honors students even though it was not very helpful. The class itself is very boring. She is a fair grader, but a bad lecturer. Se gives a lot of essays, reading assignments and projects.

Professor Cheyne is so nice and passionate about what she teaches. Expect to do a lot of writing, whether it be low stakes (1-2 pages) or high stakes (8+ pages). Expect a decent amount of reading. Class participation is really important!! Class was sometimes confusing. Grading criteria isn't all that clear & assignments aren't always given back.

Coming from FRN 102 to FRN 201, this class is impossible. She gives about 45 activities due at the end of every other week, the exams are mostly open response questions all in french. You have to teach yourself the lessons from the book and she gets angry when the entire class doesnt know a concept.

This professor expects a lot of you more than what you know. if you don't understand something or didn't learn something "you should have, moving on" is here response. extremely unhelpful terrible grader. she needs to rethink who she is teaching. finally her favorite phrase it seems is "for all you French majors out there". really what Frnch majors

Took this class for an online semester never taken French before. I expected it to be simple, learning the alphabet and introducing the language since it is an "Elementary" level course. Entire syllabus is in french as well as the assignments. Expects 39 assignments due weekly written in french. Work load is literally insane. Don't Take This Class!

Professor Cheyne is AWESOME! She packs in a fair amount of work, but the class is totally manageable if you do the assignments. Her class is what a college class should be like: demanding and rewarding.   All the poor reviews on here are because of a lack of work. It's not high-school anymore folks; college is supposed to be challenging!

Took this class online over the 5 week summer session & it was horrible. I've taken french before, the material itself wasn't hard but her expectations for this class were very unrealistic for FRN 101. She didn't start grading the assignments until the last week of class and didn't finish it all before the end of class. The work load is insane!

Although she assigns a lot of work and is strict, you'll learn a lot from her by the end of the semester. As long as you try...hard, pay attention, do all the assignments, ask her questions when necessary and do the extra credits, you'll do well. Study hard also, you'll need it.

She is alittle rude at times, the work load is kind of heavy due to the fact that you have to do chapters in the book on your own...the exams are long for the short 50 minutes...overall she is hard, choose her at your own cost...

She is hard and the work load is just ridiculous. She talks to you in straight french like you are in an advanced level. She doesn't teach she just talks to you and then throw the homework at you with no notes whatsoever. Never taking her again
prof cheyne may seem strict but shes a nice lady to, she loves french and i learned alot. gave help outside class.

LOVE HER!!!!She's a very good teacher, she loves french and she cares about her students..if you don't come to class she doesn't really mind...goes over hw sometimes...TAKE HER IF YOU HAVE THE CHANCE. She's AWESOME
Cares deeply about her subject and her students. Fully amends to help her students grasp everything. If you want to learn, take her!
Awesome teacher! Kind, cares about students, tries hard to keep students interested and to participate. She absolutely loves French, and is always enthusiastic.

Great class, great teacher.  Very nice and has a good sense of humor.  You can expect to work in her class and she loves it when you speak up, don't be afraid!  Tests are a little intimidating but if you really study the grammar, you'll be fine.

Not the type of class that you're gonna get a easy grade. You really need to work on it. She does not allow any use of tech during class. You cannot retake a test, she takes attended serious, the book is expensive.

Deb will not sugarcoat anything. She told my class full of nursing majors if we can't understand what she's teaching we will never be nurses and to switch our major. After the first exam she lets you use a cheat sheet for the rest of them so write everythinggggg on there. if you pay attention and study you'll be fine.
She such a sweetheart. last three exams and final you can use cheat sheet. Very helpful!

Deb is by far my favorite professor I have had this semester. She made me feel like I was able to do anything. I had to retake math 145 because my last professor was down right awful and I was failing. I have been getting all A's now with Deb. She makes learning fun and easy. I now enjoy going to math.
Do your online work and you'll be fine. Tests every unit, but they're easy, and if you do well at the start, you don't have to attend some classes
As long as you try and pay attention you will do fine in this class. Debbie is great! She does tutoring too, Defiantly seek out help with her.
As long as you pay attention in class you will pass. She tutors outside of class for people who need help and is a really good professor.

Professor Correa is a very good teacher. MTH146 requires a lot of hard work and dedication. I recommend tutoring with Professor Correia if you are struggling. She is very patient and will move mountains for you. 10/10 would recommend. HARD WORK WORKS.

She was okay as a teacher but had anger problems and would make scenes in front of the whole class. Most students failed a quiz and she was extremely angry and defensive next class. Also would take attendance randomly throughout zoom and if you didn't answer her you would be removed from class. She did care about her students and would help.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Great professor, awesome to have and easy to pass if you show up to class and do the work. Would recommend.
so easy

Very fair professor, will help you if you reach out. I learned new and relevant material and enjoyed class at times. Math does not come to me naturally, and I struggled at times just because I didn't study enough. Study, do all of your assignments, ask for help if you need it, and you will be fine!

This professor was so sweet, caring, adorable, and helps you to the best of her ability. My grade is low because I have a very hard time with math, and did not seek tutoring like I should have. Please buy the book you will need it!  If you have a hard time with math, please seek tutoring with her or someone else! Good freshman class.

She is a great Professor. Helps her students anytime. Take her class, she is amazing.  Funny she also recommend you for tutoring. Easy to reach out meaning she emails back quickly. No need of the book. Homework's are not that hard if you take notes in the class. You have to go to class everyday because the notes are important for online quizzes.
Great teacher, tutor, and awesome woman ! Highly recommend!
great teacher, wow

Very nice woman, but he teaching skills are not the best. If you are not good a math I wouldn't recommend her, but if you are, she is ok. She offers tutoring and you should go. Beware of tests! if you do bad in one, your grade will be affected badly!
She's a very nice woman who will help you where ever you need it. Her tests are pretty easy and she doesn't assign a lot of homework. Make sure you participate.
She is the best
Shes Awesome, she break it down for you and helps you with anything you have trouble with. she just Great!!!

I love this woman. She is by far the best professor I've had. The beginning of the semester was tough because I hadn't taken algebra since high school. With some hard work and tutoring with her after class, it paid off. She's a great professor and helps you any way she can as long as you show up to class and do the work. You must take her class.
Love this woman! I'm terrible at math and I got a B+ in her class. Very sweet woman, will help anyone, makes the material somewhat easy and class is laid back!
She means well and you'll do OK if you do the work, but dear God can she lose control of a class fast, your success really depends on who is in your class and if they cause trouble.
Very helpful and supportive.  Will help you if you help yourself.  If you've been out of school for a while and need and are worried, don't be, she'll "school" you up.

Very sweet, explains things well but can get confusing when teaching in front of a big class. I highly recommend meeting with her one on one. Homework load isn't bad at all and she's always there to help. I'm bad at Math and got an A-. Definitely take her!

LOVED LOVED LOVED tis teacher she was super helpful!! met with me for tutoring like 3 times a week she is very nice and wants to see her students pass if u go to her she will help you! one of my best professor of all time.

I only took this class to fulfill my math req. If you did well on the placement test but don't want to take a hard math class, take this one! If you got to trig or precalc in high school the material here will be EASY! It was basically a review. Correia is really nice and easy to listen to. Great professor!
GREAT CLASS. IF YOUR MATH SKILLS ARE NOT VERY STRONG..THIS IS THE CLASS 4 U.  YOU WILL LOVE IT.. JUST DO YOUR HOMEWORK AND STUDY..YOU'LL BE FINE

A great teacher that is laid back and is always willing to help. Has a really slight accent that a lot of people think is nice. Friendly, and kind hearted towards her students. She even learns the most of the students names.

She was just awful. If you're not good at math, you could possibly be worse after this class. It was hard to understand her accent sometimes and she would personally pick on myself and my friend if we didn't do something right. She would try and make us feel stupid about it and rub it in our faces. She was awful. End of story.

Awesome prof! Such an easy class, really wants you to do well. HW assigned, but not mandatory. Attendance is taken, but not mandatory, but you should go to understand. Very clear, I always understand the material. Quizzes and tests are easy as long as you understand the material in class. SHES SO NICE! I'd take her again

Love, love, love her! She is so cute and funny. I've always been bad at math and I took her course- she explains everything throughly and really cares about helping students. I've got A's and A+'s on all her tests and quizzes. Highly recommend taking her class!

Great Professor! She really cares for her students. She is always there if you need help, although her work may come easy to some, she not stupid she can tell if your trying in her class or not. She is very fair. One of my best professors I learned a lot from her. Her classes fill up quick!
shes awsome

She's very cheerful and enthusiastic but is a hoorible teacher if you're actually trying to learn. She constantly gets off topic and has no control over the class. She uses complex ways of explaining easy concepts and confuses half of the class. Class is boring. She assigns homework but you don't really have to do it. Only uses the book in class.
She is so cute and funny. She makes everyone laugh and if we don't get it she will resay everything she just said. Very easy. Love her! Take her definately!
I love her. to death.
She is really easy and very nice. If you need help she is willing, and very friendly.

I love professor Correia, she is so sweet! Very helpful about everything. Assigns homework nightly, but does not grade it. Gives examples in class. The hardest part of the course comes at the end, but just do your homework and study moderately and you will do fine!
great class great teacher hot too
Very nice, tries to be as helpful as possible and listens to her students, makes all attempts she can to compromise with students, knew all students' names, class was fun
She is very nice and sweet. Class is soo easy at first and then gets a little harder. It is sometimes rushed but she is really helpful. Never checks for collects homework.
so easy, no work whatsoever, pretty much gave grades

Dr. Piva is truly a great professor! I struggled a ton at the beginning of the semester because i was just looking up answers for all of the graded assignments and homework. DO NOT DO THAT!!!! Take notes on assignments and teach yourself/go see her until you understand. This course is definitely not easy but she makes it manageable!!
An actual angel of a human being and really wants you to succeed. Take the time to ask questions and fully understand concepts and you can pass this class.

Go to office hours or tutor, don't struggle on your own. The materials are not that hard once you understand. The stuff on Aleks is what is on the exam, better to start strong, it will help you in the long run. The only prof who teaches nursing chem. Do not let other's opinion of her cloud your judgment. Does give unnecessary information.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Dr. Piva is a great professor. The labs were super easy on ALEKS, and all the material for exams was taken directly from ALEKS as well. There were A LOT of vocab assignments, and a homework and graded assignment every week on ALEKS, but it helped greatly with understanding the material. She doesn't take attendance, but it helps greatly to go.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: The labs are very easy, all the homework and quiz questions are on the exams. As long as you understand the material on Aleks, everything is so easy. She's so nice and actually wants you to succeed.

Dr. Piva is a great professor. Her lectures are well organize, and I really appreciated how she color coded her notes during class. This really helped when the material got harder cause it was easy to follow and study the notes after. She is very caring and wants her students to succeed, and she's willing to help when something isn't clicking.
Hard but she does a good job making sure we understand.

Although she color codes in front of you what she says does not make sense. The book we use to write our notes in is packed with unnecessary information that we do not use making it more confusing than it needs to be. My class had 36 people fail an exam and instead of reflecting on her own teaching she blamed it on the students. I don't recommend.

Amazing professor, who truly cares for her students success. Color codes her notes in front of you, even as someone who barely passed high school chemistry, this class was one of my favorites to go to and was easy to follow. She guides you through your first semester outside of just chemistry, even giving us ways to calm down before every exam.

I was sooo scared for my first chemistry class but Piva was amazingg and you wont have any issues with this class as long as you pay attention, study, and do the weekly hw assignments. Shes pretty strict about no phones and lectures are very structured and thorough.

Dr.Piva is by far one of the best professors I've had and she is an amazing teacher. Her lectures are very clear and she takes the time to make sure her students understand the content she is teaching. There are weekly homework and vocab assignments that correspond to the week's learning. She is amazing!!

I am not a student at umass, but I needed an online chem class for my major at my university so I took gen chem 1 and 2 with her and it was the best thing ive ever done. Piva saved my program because I did amazing in her class. there is a ton of homework and exams but the website she used for both was very easy and very helpful. TAKE PIVA!!!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: She's literally so sweet and you can tell she wants everybody to succeed. Gives clear and helpful answers to any questions and makes complex topics very easy to learn. As long as you engage and do your work you will have no issues in this class.
LOVE HER!!! Lectures are engaging and easy to follow. She breaks things down into simpiler concepts. She's nice as a person and fun to talk to outside of the classroom setting
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Professor Piva was an amazing professor throughout the semester. She always clarified questions that I had about the course material, and she is easily accessible outside of the classroom. I would definitely take a class with her again.

Dr.Piva is a great chemistry professor and really cares about her students. All you really need to do is pay attention during her lectures (which are super organized and engaging) and you'll be fine. The exams are literally the same questions from the homework and quizzes she assigns. If you ever need any help she is very kind and will gladly help.

Dr. Piva cared about her students whenever I wanted to have a 1on1 with her to get more help she always made time. She is very clear in her lectures and does take attendance. She goes through her notes with you and breaks each section down and incorporates her students in the conversation to make sure we understand.

If your nursing major taking  this class just know to be prepared cause  it a lot especially if you dont play on paying attention in the class. Dr. Piva is  nice you gotta pay attention and def your mastering

Piva is a great professor! This course comes with lots of work per week but that it's only there to help you on exams/quizzes. If you ever have trouble with a topic she will have no problem going 1 on 1 with you & that really helps! I got an A+ but I had to work for it, just make sure you are doing your work and understanding it & u will do great!

Dr Piva is a very intelligent and caring professor. She really knows what she is talking about. Chemistry is a difficult class but if you do your homework, ask questions, and email her, you will pass. I hate chemistry but Dr. Piva helped me get through it. Just do your work
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Caring

reviews.comment: she goes pretty fast through lectures when we get behind but overall she really cares about her students and makes chem a lot less confusing

Hands down one of the BEST professors. Organic Chem can be tough, but she makes it so understandable. She is very clear with her notes as well as what she is asking. She is ALWAYS there for her students and is willing to go the extra mile for them. She provides plenty of opportunities for extra credit to boost your grade! LOVED this class and her!!

Dr Piva Is the most caring professor ive met so far and her ultimate goal is to make sure that each and everyone of her students comes out with flying colors. She gives you each and every way she can to better your grade. I would take her again no questions asked.
Best professor I have ever had! She truly cares about her students, and is willing to go to great lengths to help her students succeed. I would take her again in a heartbeat!

Although there isn't a tangible textbook, there is an access code that you will need to buy for homework and quizzes. Overall, Piva is really personable and gets to know her students. She does use a microphone that at first can be hard to understand, but you get used to it.

Dr. Piva is THE most caring professor I've ever had. She deserves all the credit. Organic chem is always tough but if you ask/reach out she WILL guide you. She takes time out of her schedule to go over exams or answer questions. She simplifies topics as best she can so students can understand. 10/10

She is always there to help if any questions arise. Recitation helps also to get more familiar with Dr. Piva. The homework and quizzes help a lot towards your grade. She is so sweet and really cares about you and what you need to do well.
I've never understood or enjoyed chemistry until I took her class. Most amazing chemistry professor and her notes are presented in a way that is easy to understand.

She is AMAZING!!! Hard class. need to be on top of your game but she does everything she can to help you! She is truly one of those professors that wants to see you succeed. She puts her students first always. Work hard, be respectful, go to class. I love her!
amazing professor, do your work and online homework and you will pass.
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Shes wonderful, just read your notes, use your book for examples for tests, and youll do fine
Very caring, best college professor I have had, cares lots about her students and wants everyone to succeed. Very easy to talk to.
good

Her explanations are clear to understand and if it's not, she will explain a different way and draw visuals to help you understand. She's very kind and her grading is very fair. The only bad thing I can say is she crams a lot of information in before exams when she is behind schedule

She was very nice and cared about her students. The only thing was that her Chem class was very rushed and she shoved a ton a material down your throat right up until the exam. She had lot's of opportunities for studying, which was good. Overall, she was very nice, just rushed the course a little.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: She is great! She answers questions whenever you need them. She is great at explaining. You definitely have to study.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: The book isn't necessary, but the code for Mastering Pearson's is. She is one of the nicest professors. She is very good at explaining what she is teaching and if you have questions, ask, she's always more than happy to answer them. Quizzes and homework are a weekly occurrence. Packing up early pop quiz. You ABSOLUTELY have to attend lecture.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: She is a knowledgeable professor, just doesn't do a great job teaching. I ended up teaching myself all  the concepts. Her lectures are dry and hard to follow at times.

Great Prof to learn from in CCB. A lot of lecture but does her best to make sure it isn't boring. Very fair grading. Had a lot of issues with my textbook from the third party but she did her best to accommodate too. I think she has a lot of experience in the industry too which is important because not every prof can say they have their CPA

Professor Claudia Torres did a great job in her first semester teaching. Weekly McGrawHill homework, quizzes, and SmartBook assignments keep you on track, with SmartBook offering extra credit. Tax can be confusing, but she explains everything clearly and in depth and is always available to talk outside of class.

Prof Tores wants students to succeed. Very lecture heavy and lot of info to remember but I expect that. Extra Credit opportunities too which is nice. Its nice to learn from someone who worked in industry already.
Tim was a great teacher, he made class very easy, along with his tests. He does assign a decent amount of homework but its not too bad.

You can tell that Professor Diamantoni really wants you to succeed. All homework assignments on WebAssign are due on the last day of the semester, and he gives you multiple opportunities to boost your grade. You have to go to 3 tutoring sessions to get full marks in that category, but you have excused absences and he drops the lowest exam grade.

Very nice, but i didnt appreciate that he required us to do tutoring sessions that if you didnt go to 5 by the end of the sem, you would have a 0% for that category, each session bumps it up by like 15% I think?? This was last spring for me so i dont remember much
he doesn't allow calculators most of the time
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Very easy to pass all homework due on last day and you get like 7 absences and he drops lowest test grade and the online text book is very helpful cause he does ramble a little bit but very nice and easy class
Great guy, however, not a good professor. His way of teaching is different, in a weird way. He talks a lot and sometimes lack teaching because of it. I don't recommend taking him.

He is a great teacher and has a passion about the information he is teaching. His attendance policy is amazing and gives you up to 7 absences. He allows you to do homework assignments on your own time. There are 4 exams but one gets dropped, meaning if you do good on the first 3 you dont even need to take the last one. genuinely a good guy

one of the better professors i've had, super kind and caring and will always look out for the students. tests are super easy and he drops the lowest grade so the 4th one is usually optional. great guy

Personally I'm not good at math but Prof Diamantoni made me love it! His lectures are scattered but he presents the info that he expects you to know for the exams. If he sees you put in the work he'll meet you half way. Would definitely take another class with him and recommend him to anyone!

Hands down best math Prof. I had in a while. Be ready to learn he is great at explaining and is very understanding if you don't get the topic. He is willing to help you outside the class especially if you are struggling a lot. His grading criteria is fair and pretty straightforward.  Put in the work and you will do fine.

If you pay attention in class, math will become one of the easiest subjects in your life. Homework is reasonable and not too long. He is always available for tutoring and he will help with questions you have. His attendance and grading policy is super lenient. He's also just a fun guy, he's traveled a lot and good to chat with!

Prof. Diamantioni is a very engaging and caring professor. You can really tell he cares about his students. Sometimes classes focus too much on one problem or go overtime, and graded attendance (while being only worth 10%) can be bothersome. There are also 2 tutoring sessions for 6% of your grade. I enjoyed this class and would recommend!
