I'm a transfer student and this is my first semester at UMD. I like this professor a lot. Very friendly and helpful. He's understanding if you're a student that struggles with your work. He does a good job explaining the course material the first time. I'd definitely recommend this professor to others.

Professor Diamond was a real gem this semester. We've utilized WebAssign, and I took advantage of the Ask Your Teacher button for every homework assignment; I always got an answer! It really helps to ask questions if you don't understand something in his class. Otherwise, you'll be like me and spend 5 hours on homework every night. Thank you, T.D.!
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: One of the few professors who I believe really puts the work in. Very energetic in class even under the constant stress a professor faces. His entire day is dedicated to the success of his students. Very underappreciated. Would 100% take him again.
hes a really nice guy but seems to be struggling with the online classes from coronavirus. he doesnt teach everything thats on the homework on zoom lectures and i find myself teaching myself a lot

Professor Diamantoni is an extremely energetic and kind professor, and he is passionate about teaching. He cares about his students and how they learn. He is a very talkative, and we often wouldn't start learning material until ten minutes after class started, and we didn't cover all the material that we were supposed to.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Caring

reviews.comment: One of my favorite professors! Very understanding and approachable. If multiple exams fall on same day or week he will often move his to reduce stress for students. Exams, homework, daily warmups, and attendance are all weighted almost equally so grade not so heavily affected if lacking in one area. Use warm up, notes, and hw problems for exams.

he is a caring professor but his grading is kind of strange. he grades each question individually from A-F which increases your chances of getting a better grade but it is a little confusing. he drops the lowest test grade and tries to explain things in different ways for multiple people to understand but oftentimes just confuses students more
Awesome one of the best math professor, highly recommends taking his class.

Timothy is the most caring Math teacher I have ever had! Really wants you to succeed and makes sure you understand the material. He is not a fan of Webassign but is required to assign it. Show up to class, do the homework and he will like you. Such a sweet man.

He's great at teaching math but his exams are the complete opposite of what he teaches. Maybe he used to be a great professor, but he seems to have gotten more difficult as the years go by. He's all over the place with his teaching, never shows up on time, and basically sets up his classes for failure. Will never take a class with him ever again.

Mr. Diamantoni makes his class simple; if you show up and do the homework, you'll do well. He's very nice and knows how to explain math concepts to people who are not the greatest at the subject. Definitely take him if you can!
He is the worst teacher i have had for math. His tests are so stupid he grades each question using letters, and has his own way of doing things and expects you to understand it. DO NOT TAKE HIM.

He has gotten so much harder over the past few years and his level of professionalism is not very high. Often complains about the math department in class and I may or may not have witnessed him giving his cell number to another female in our class. Overall I wouldnt take him again. He has gone from being a good professor to a not so good one.
This professor is a really great lecturer, he knows his math and he high encourages his students to work on their algebra skills.  His exams are a bit tuff if you don't study.

I had him freshman year, he was a great professor however, he has really change the past year due to being burned out from the students. He grades harsher, his tests are a lot more difficult, and study like crazy for his exams. If you can't answer the question immediately good luck on exams because he gives a lot of problems.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring

reviews.comment: This was my first Calc class. I took pre-calc in high school. He tells a lot of stories, but is a good teacher and is willing to tutor you if you ask.

I loved having him this past semester and i really wish he was teaching my next class too. He really takes the time to make sure every student understands the material. He doesn't force to you pay attention or pass in your work but he grades you based on effort. The only way to fail his class is to not show up.

Diamantoni was amazing. He is crystal clear with his explanations and is always willing to help. He is funny, sincere and wants each student to do well. He doesn't yell at you if you don't pay attention in class but don't expect a good grade either. As long as you do the homework and pay attention, his classes are super easy.

Diamantoni is amazing. I had him for MTH 148 & I just had to take him again for MTH 149. I wish I could take him for every other math course I need. He is straight-forward, homework is optional, and is extremely helpful. Typically there was a basic quiz every week and 4 exams (including the final). If you have the chance, take his class! EASY A!!!!

Not many tests, frequent pop quizzes but super easy, I thought it was an extremely easy class but if you don't like math that may change. If you need a math credit, take this class with him and if you pay attention you will get an A. Great guy, and very cultured.

Diamantoni is a great Math professor. He is truly passionate about math and it shows. Awesome at explaining topics if you do not understand them. Is always available for tutoring as well. Pop quizzes are usual but if you do the homework he assigns they are no big deal. Definitely recommend.

Honestly the best professor I have had here at UMD. He actually cares about his students and wants to see them excel. He's really good at explaining and is always there when you need help. I highly recommend taking him if you can because he truly is a great teacher. I suck at math but he made it so much easier for me to understand.

He is the single best professor I've had. He really cares about teaching and gets really excited about it. Imagine Bill Nye teaching calculus. He even looks like him, sorta. Take his class if you can! If you're unlucky like me, great professors like this are few and far between! I'm always on a lookout during registration time for his name.
This man knows his math. He does everything he can to keep you interested and get you a good grade. Take him!!
great guy and great teacher. take his class if you have a choice
This man loves math.  He does everything he can to keep you interested and get you a good grade.  Take him!!
HE IS THE MAN !!!  Take him if u can

his class is was easy and hard sometimes because I'm good at math i knew somethings which made it easy but for new things he goes rather fast and quick so you really have to attend class. but he's a really nice teacher.

Prof. Timothy is my favorite professor in UMD! he is the most genius math professor I have ever met. I RECOMMEND EVERYONE TO TAKE HIM! he is more than perfect and he we are still friends until now. What else can I say other than he is Smart, Easy grader, Knows what he is doing, and again, GENIUS. shoutout to you professor tim -m

Diamantoni was one of the best math teachers I've ever had. He's very thorough and clear with his teachings and doesn't expect everyone to know the same amount. Tests are graded very fairly and no question is worth more than the other. Essentially, showing that you understand the concept is good enough for him.  I highly recommend this professor.
awesome
I would recommend him a thousand times..Take him..hes super easy and awesome. Mth 103 was such an easy class!! AGAIN I SAY, TAKE HIM
He is really helpfull if you need it. The class is really easy if you go to class and pay attention.
Great teacher. I would recommend him to any freshman. his class is easy and he thoroughly explains everything so that you can get it. he is also funny.

Really nice guy. Can be funny too. He gives you the homework well in advance so you can get a head start, then goes over it in class. PAINFULLY thorough when it comes to explaining, but comes in handy if you don't understand something. He can also help you outside of class if you ever need it.
Best teacher! If you go to class, you'll do fine. If you need help all you have to do is ask and he'll give you all the time you need until you understand the material.
I recommend people espeically freshmen to take this class, very easy, the math is easy to understand, study the material and you'll get a A..very good professor
Awesome teacher, very helpful and makes math interesting! Strongly recommend.

Such a good teacher! loved him, he dosn't make the material hard to learn at all and is very approachable if you do not understand somthing. If you go to class and take good notes the class is a breeze
Best professor ive ever had. Treats students as equals. More fair than any professor.

He's an awesome guy. And so down to earth about everything. He doesn't teach things in a complex abstract way. Before I took this class, I had no interest and was nervous about Calc, but he made it so clear and easy. One of the best teachers I've had.

Great Teacher. Attendence isnt mandatory but you wont to attend. His classes are really fun and he knows exactly what he's talking about and can explain it very well. Would deffenitly take his class again. Higly recomend.

He's an easy grader and loves to help his students out in any way he can. He's very nice and one of the best Math teacher's I've ever had. Even if your not in his class he tutors at the Math and Business Center so I would recommend him not only as a teacher but as a tutor as well.

great teacher, had him for calc 2. i went into it thinking how hard people say calc 2 is and i got an A in the class. he explains everything to you like your a baby. its really easy to pass his class. hes great and helpfull. he is a little slow though, he should have covered more material than he did.

Easiest math you will ever take. Attendance isn't mandatory but if you don't go to class you won't do well on the tests which is your entire grade. He basically lays out everything you need for the semester and on the test. If you don't do well in this class you're just lazy.
Top notch, great professor, Umass D should hire more like this. Get rid of the deadwood...

Not much to say about the man except that I really enjoyed his classes (had him for 101 and 102).  He is extremely fair and gives students every opportunity to do well.  He is very straight-forward and all the quizzes and exams come directly from the homework.

Best Math teacher i ever had. He really wants his students to pass his class.Very helpful.One test every month in one semster=4test for da semster.Don't really need to show up to class. Just pass his test and your good. Easy A.

I have never enjoyed a math class before this one.. and I especially never expected to get an A in one!  He is an amazing professor; he really cares about his students grasp on the material and never tries to trick anyone. Plus, he is just a really cool, approachable, and laid back guy. If you go to class and pay attention you will do fine.

This professor is a great guy. he makes it easy and is willing to help with anything as long as you participate and show effort you should be fine. hes the best math teacher i have ever had. he keeps it interesting & his test are simply the homework problems! Everyone should take him for math

Is a lecture class.  Easy A, but he is annoying.  He talks about kangaroo jerky more than math.  Homework is optional, but he just gives answers in class.  He never returns anything graded, his spelling is that of a kindergatener.  He makes everything harder than it really is, and appears to have no idea how to do math. Complains about his job.
Fun teacher, and keeps to what is covered in class. Very descriptive and clear. homework is up to you but if you dont do it, youll probably fail.
He's aggravating at times. You need to know a little of the material, or he could end up confusing you. Easy A
No Comments

VERY frustrating for Finite Math. he's never taught it before so us students were constantly correcting him. other than that really easy. the tests are the homework, just make sure u check the book and dont rely all on him.
Tim is a classy guy.  He explains the material thoroughly and his exams are based on class discussions.

Great guy, easy teacher.  Explains things very clear, and if you pay attention the class is a breeze.  His quizes are no more than 3 questions each which are exactly like the ones he does in class.  I would definalty recommend him for math 107.
Excellent at what he does. Keeps class somewhat interesting with his antics. As long as you pay attention in class you will do well. I would absolutely recomend him.

Dimantoni class is all about skill building.  You build your skills and he looks like an umpa lumpa only taller and better at math and doesnt eat as much candy.  Pay attention youll do grimey.  Shave your elbows before each tuesday class and everything will work out.

This guy looks like the dad from Bobby's World.  Haha, but he's so easy and straightfoward.  His whole class was based on the calculator, so just make sure you have one.  The key is that he loves to talk, so if you bs w/ him after class he'll be as helpful as possible.
He is a great teacher. he is funny and explains everything every well in the class. the homework before the test is usually the test. One of my favorite professors.
this class is all about skill buidin.  If you do ur skill buidin ur skills will build emmensely. Dimantoni loves skill buidin and you will get it every nite.  Skill build!!!
Great guy.  You dont have to do the homework.  He goes over it the next day so take notes on that.  The homework before the quiz is the quiz.

Simply amazing teacher. Quite possibly my favorite teachers of all time.  He's quirky, which kept my attention through out the whole class. He is very precise in his teaching. He explains everything to a tee which is a wonderful. I learned a lot with him and I highly recommend his class.
easy if you go to class, very nice guy!i would take him again.
Easy class nice guy! Take it!!!
he's a good teacher
very godo teacher..breaks down each question..answers students questions..makes class fun..i do not mind going to math class this sememester and i ususally do not liek math on bit

The rugged Mullet-man! He's a decent grader. He makes things harder than they really are, and he's really picky about the homework!  He needs to learn how to spell better. He says that if you bunji jump in New Zealand naked it's free.
Doesn't take attendance. Tests are only a few questions, but long and hard. Decent guy with sense of humor, but a hard grader. His explainations when learning new material aren't always clear.
Best math teacher I have ever had.  Hes really easy to understand and willing to go the extra mile to help his students.
No Comments
Very helpful teacher, will help you after class or whenever he can, has a great sense of humor, i suggest taking him
What is with the MULLET?  A pretty good teach but very tough grader.  I believe he just makes up the grades because he doesn't show you anything you did wrong....
No Comments
No Comments

I had her fall 2024. she's a GREAT professor when you pay attention to what she is teaching .. art history. we are getting art degrees people! you should know the history of art?! I love that shes tech free because I genuinely feel I learned so much in that class from writing down and paying attention. technology is making us go backwards mentally.

Rarely gives A's, expects students to have a high level reading comprehension in order to prove you did the reading for every class. Also assigns a lot of overlapping projects and homework and is very slow to reply to emails. For the last 2 art history classes I have at this school, I'm going to do everything in my power not to get her again

Assigns several massive assignments at the same time, will mark you as absent if you show up even a minute late for class, acts like getting accommodations and diagnosis are quick and easy, and gives several super long and difficult readings to do a week. Very condescending and inconsiderate. Trying to catch up in this class put me behind in others
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: This was the 6 p.m. Wednesday night class. The only requirement was to show up, but still got mad when we were using phones. My first semester here  was the same for her and you could tell. Had her second semester for ARH102 as well and she never got better.

She didn't teach the class a lot and when she wasn't she would reach over students to tell them to turn their phone off becoming more of a distraction. She made jokes that weren't funny. When she was teaching she would roam the side stairs calling on random people. Most people do not like her and I agree. AVOID CLASS AT ALL COSTS.

Expects everyone to read full chapters of the readings before class every time. She barely gives extra information on the slides and expects all notes to be made from the readings you did yourself. She also has a card-pulling method to call on each person during every class. If you don't give a good enough answer, she will mark down participation.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Terrible Professor Very strict with no exceptions for almost anything, specifically attendance even with strenuous circumstances Very confrontational and disrespectful to students who pay to take her class.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: First of all, good luck getting a hold of her outside of class. She doesn't treat anyone with respect, and any suggestion you give is immediately shut down in the most passive aggressive way possible. Also, get ready for public humiliation! Not only do you get called on in class, it's completely random and done with flash cards like kindergartners!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: All she'd do every class is quiz everyone in class to see if everyone did the lengthy reading for homework as she did a lecture going over everything that was in the reading. In class participation is 30% of your grade and it's entirely dependant on how well you understood the reading. I found this style of teaching to be counterproductive.

She makes assignments unnecessarily difficult to complete/find and doesn't allow technology whatsoever in class. All people who have a hard time focusing without tech and hand writing notes beware of this professor. If I could remake my schedule, I'd rather take an 8 AM than her class because of how awful her teaching style is.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: people who dont like her are only mad because she is tech free. she is one of the only professors that actually cares about teaching and engages her students and making sure that we are understanding and LEARNING in a CLASSROOM. people are mad because they cant get away with playing video games or watching youtube in an art history class with her.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: I had high hopes coming into this class thinking that Jamie was just getting used to being at UMass. I was incredibly wrong. She treats people with such a lack of kindness its astonishing. No excused absences, even with doctors' notes. Will fail if you have 6 absences even if you have a medical excuse. Get ready for a whole lot of reading.

Attendanceisrequired.Youget3absencesusethemwisely.
Eachextraonedropsyourgrade5points.6absences=automaticF.Noabsencesareexcused. 
Miss3+inarow?ContactStudentAffairs.Athletessubmitformsondayone.Trackyourownattendance.
Iflate,checkyou'remarkedpresent.Getnotesfromclassmates.
3+latearrivalsorearlyexitsover5minutes=1absence.
Longbreaksorsleeping=absence.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment:
- Hard Grader
- Tech Free Classroom
- Participation matters and is graded too.
The assignment directions make no sense at all. She makes you present and then gives you a bad grade after having all the criteria listed in your presentation. 
- HW is mainly reading, or as she calls it a "CLOSE READING"

This was for ARH 200

OMG. I thought it was chill elective. BIGGEST MISTAKE OF MY LIFE. Instead: a memorization marathon of obscure dates, artists, styles terms. Every lecture is a verbal avalanche. The exams? Absolute hell. Artist, title, date, medium, context, and comparisons—for everything. I'd rather take FOUR 8 A.M.s than one more of her class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I had originally debated on switching out but decided to just tuff it out. BIGGEST MISTAKE OF MY LIFE I would give my left arm to never take this class to begin with.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: She acts as if this is your major. Be prepared to spend hours studying and reading

Absolutely confrontational and will call out specific students. treats students like children. "Tech free classroom"; all notes must be taken on paper. Extremely lecture heavy. Feel free to take her class if you want to challenge yourself, but I wouldn't recommend it to anybody.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: she's Very confrontational and likes to call specific students out , requires paper notes for every lecture, and treats students like high schoolers, she has a horrible attitude. she called me out and asked me to leave for having my phone in my lap not even using it during a lecture...Do not take this class with her if you can avoid it.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: She's very qualified to teach, but she gives very annoying and very time-consuming reading homework, especially for a class that's an elective. She doesn't let you use any devices in the classroom, and she'll call you out if she catches you using them. I really regret not swapping this class. If you have the option to not take her, DO NOT TAKE HER.

This professor is not understanding whatsoever, demands for all the notes to be taken on paper despite the fact that she uses technology in the classroom but doesn't allow her students to under any circumstances. She is confrontational and doesn't treat her students as respected college students; instead, she treats them as if they are children.

Have not even finished this course and I can safely say this professor deserves a 1 star rating. Very confrontational, requires paper notes for every lecture, and treats students like high schoolers. Please do not take this class with her if you can avoid it.
Extremely rude and unnecessary. It takes a miracle for her to respond to her emails.

the professor provides lots of useful information about the subject but also assigns lots of work, especially if the class is not for your major. Attendance is mandatory and participation is required for credits.

She's very smart, and is 100% qualified, HOWEVER there was so much homework that I literally couldn't do anything else without being stressed out of my mind. The readings are almost always over 15-20 pages due for every class, and you have to participate to get any credit for it. Never again. The only good part about this class, was no textbook.

Pretty cool guy! The learning curve for R was pretty rough but otherwise the class wasn't too hard. Super forgiving in terms of grades/assignments. I will say he kinda put all the work at the end of the semester which was a pain.
His test is similar to the practice exam. Go to his office for the practice exam, that's how I got an A from his class.
3 Test, No homework, and can not understand him. The material is hard too, he is great in office hours but in class is like watching pain dry. Probability
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Sure, his accent may be on the heavy side at times, but like most people, I enjoyed his lectures.

Took this course as required by major. Lectures were fairly clear. Went to office hours a few times, and prof was very helpful. Glad I was able to understand the materials by the end of the semester, though struggling a bit in the beginning. Strongly recommend to take him.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Took an intro level course to data science and he flys through the slides and expects students to learn a new programming language(R) in about two weeks. I have taken other languages and R is definitely the most confusing. Goes too fast during lectures and can be very difficult to understand at times. Would not recommend.

Smart professor in his field, but should not be teaching. He likes to go off on random problems jumping from problem to problem so its hard to follow along and make sense of everything. The language or accent barrier can get very hard to understand at times, making it even harder to understand.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: A professor who cares about students. A difficult course, but lectures were fairly clear. The last part of the course requires calculus, and time spent on calculus was worthwhile.

His accent is very distinguished. He leaves little room for question and often times silences the class. Every lecture he runs though a complete example without much explanation. Its very annoying during the lecture he has a habit of saying "this is very simple see", and while that may true from his perspective, from a student's perspective its not
reviews.rating_tags: BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: He makes math great again. Extremely helpful, goes the extra mile to clarify any questions you have. His slides are thorough, but really boring as a result.
I have to say, Professor DeLuca was a great professor. I had a great experience and I am excited to have her again!

Good professor, attendance is mandatory. Can be challenging but she is good at giving feedback and very nice. A good amount of reading for homework but all interesting things so not too bad. Again, very nice and an interesting class, just a good amount of work but pretty easy overall.

Mandatory attendance and lots of readings to do almost every night so keep in mind if youre not into it. Most of the grades were a few papers which are graded easily and are pretty quick to do. Easy class if you go and pay attention, she wants to help you get a good grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Professor Deluca is a really great and caring professor. I always felt comfortable talking to her and her class was fairly laid back. She is very clear on instructions and when things are due which I really appreciate. Her class was never boring to me and usually felt like an open conversation.
love her. she is literally the best professor on this campus. such a passionate driven woman and she is so understanding. i will take as many classes as physically possible with her
Excellent professor, clear expectations, one heavily graded group/partner project.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Group projects--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Ms. DeLuca is great! I took intro to rhetoric with her and it was very informative. She truly cares about her students and wants them to do well. She makes an effort to get to know everyone and makes class fun. If you have the opportunity to take a class with her I seriously recommend it!

I really enjoyed Dr. Desroches' class. I was EXTREMELY misled by some of the reviews on this platform regarding her attitude as a professor. 

She was VERY accessible outside class, she laughed at my jokes (which was so nice), she's not at all a strict grader! All that's needed to excel in her class is participation, reading, and a drive to excel!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Take her if you like high school teachers. Was kind of petty.

I took her in person for NUR 370 and she is so sweet & cares so much about student success. You need to make sure you are paying attention to lectures & not slacking off & with that the test will be easy for you. The grade in this class is what you put into it. As long as you try and put effort in, you will pass. Would definitely take her again!
Shes a hard grader, doesnt care about you if you are passing or not she seems nice but  shes really not.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Easily the nicest and best professor I have ever had. 10/10 would take her again if I could.

I am not going to say his work is the easiest, but Duong is a great Professor. You can tell he is knowledgeable in finance through his lectures. He does his best to teach the hard content, no matter who teaches it, at a level most comprehensible. He is responsive if you try to email him and accessible in his office hours

Horrible . Avoid if you can . He makes you do this " stock simulation project in real market hours " . as a full time worker as well who works over nights I cant even do this . If he sees this and I hope he does . Screw you . Making us. spend uneccesary money . Connect access is already expensive enough . Now 40$ for a acct im not going to use ?
all on pearson, not too difficult

He's an absolute genius; he does have an accent but its very understandable and its easy to follow in the class once you get used to it. Homework is very simple and he gives good reviews for the exams. If you put in the necessary effort it's a very good class.

The content is incredibly difficult to learn in an online setting and the professor does not convey all the material he puts on the test. I am a career 3.7 GPA UMD student and this will be the first class I get a C in. He babbles and doesn't explain his tests (which are incredibly difficult)well at all. DO NOT TAKE HIM IF YOU CAN AVOID IT!!!

All of the assignments were on MCH Connect. Aside from the course material being difficult it isn't too bad of a class. I definitely have struggled throughout the semester with this course but feel if I had taken an in-person class with this professor I would have understood the material better and had an easier time completing assignments.

This is a Master's level course in Investments taught as if it is a PhD level math course. I get that Investments are all about the math; but there is little preparation for the ACTUAL problems given on tests. It does not help that in his video lectures and problem sets, the professor makes multiple math errors himself creating further confusion.

I took this prof for a 5-week intensive course over the summer. FIN383 is a very tough course within the major, as its a prerequisite for other major courses. Prof. Nguyen doesn't make the material any tougher than it needs to be. However, you really need to be on top of it. Tests are online. Read, take good notes and make sure you ask questions.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Very OK. Three tests which are very hard but he adds points to everyone's grade. Problems done in class and in review are nothing like on the test. Have to do a stock market game and report after. Online homework which is also difficult. However, he says as long as you attend and try you will not get anything lower than a C- in his class.

Prof Kagan is a good professor. Our grade is 70% quizzes, which we are allowed to use our notes on and retake. The MyOpenMath HW system can be annoying, but atleast it is free and helpful to study off of.

Dr. Kagan is one of the best professors I've ever had. If you can take him for any physics course, do it. His lectures are engaging, and he keeps everything light-hearted and fun. There are unlimited retakes on all quizzes/tests, and you can redo homework until the end of the semester. His homework website is a little janky, but it works.

Good professor talks a lot during his lectures but will write out step by step problems that's will be similar to the quizzes he gives. You also don't have any exams only 5 quizzes and you can retake the quizzes as many times as possible. Lectures are all recorded and posted online so you don't have to attend every class.

He's a very kind and understanding guy. If you need help or have a question, you can email him or go to his office hours. He's very approachable. He has infinite retakes on quizzes, and he scales the last two quizzes to be worth less if they would drag down your grade. He gives out a lot of homework.

Pretty good Professor. Super nice and cares about physics a lot. Extremely accessible outside of class. Post lectures online if you cannot make it in. No test but quizzes are worth 70% of the grade. Retakes on hw and quizzes as much as you want. Final was just 2 quizzes and 4 optional retakes. Recommend!

Super passionate that you either love it or hate it. Really focuses on the conceptual side of physics. 70% of your grade was 2 question tests (that you could retake up until the end of the semester). Would recommend.

Great Teacher, recommended him to all my friends who also had to take physics 111 and i'm taking his class for physics 112. A little more work than a HS physics class so if you did good there you'll do great here

Professor Kagan is the reason I switched my major into physics, simple as that. He offers an in depth well thought out explanation for all the physics concepts he introduces and I've definitely spent many hours in his office discussing topics ranging from advanced physics to karaoke! Couldn't recommend him enough, he works very hard and it shows.

Professor Kagan is the type of Professor you could talk to for hours about anything. He is incredibly knowledgeable, funny, and quirky. He has rad lectures but covers only concepts and doesn't give you good quantitative problems in lecture. The lack of quantitative material will make homework and tests difficult but not impossible.

Homeworks are literallty the worst, overly complicated but you can find everything through chegg which means that you can study the solutions from chegg and try to figure out how to find the answers. If you can do this you can do pretty well on the tests. If you don't mind actually working kagan is good. Probably my favorite professor.

Had him last year for classical mechanics and this year for electricity and magnetism, he changed the way he has test, but they still suck. He gives you practice problems for the conceptual and quantitative part of the test, usually one of the questions are on the test. Labs are awful none of the equipment works and they grade harder than PHY 111

The lectures were all on concepts, but we were only tested on applied physics (as it was the class). The exams were so hard, he didnt teach any of the material. I had 7 books (from friends who had taken phy) to prep for exams & I couldn't find examples that would look like exams. Dont worry about ur grade though, I learned nothing but ended w/ A-.

If you want to love physics take this class ..  don't have assumptions about him because of what people here say. I had assumptions in the beginning and i was doing bad. Then I start to engoy the class and love physics and started to do good. My physics background is zero. But i learning a lot and love his class so much. He give the knowledge

Currently in his Classical Mechanics class, and it's a mess. Vague explanations, shoddy online HW system, assignments outpace lectures, wonky labs, and I swear he's making stuff up (his pet concept "Source Energy" is not a real thing). Gives you lots of do-overs, so you'll definitely pass, but you won't feel like you learned anything.

I would not take again if given the option. Unfortunately there isn't an option so you have to take two semesters of Kagan. Lectures are slow, boring, and theoretical for an applied physics course. Tests and homework are way beyond what he teached in lecture. Very funky grading system. Lab was poorly equipped and grading was unclear.

Horrible professor, probably the worst professor I have ever had. His grading is very difficult to understand and makes the homework and mini tests exceptionally hard. He is not helpful in class or outside of class. He is too smart for his own good.

the homework questions arent very well defined.  If your not good at math or dont have physics experience beware. I had limited Calc 1 experience but am very good at math and I have very limit Physics experience and I struggled but I got a B+.  If youve taken and understand Calc 1 and you have alot of Physics experience its an easy A

Kagan sucks, simple as that. Unfortunately, he is the only one who teaches Physics I and II. I somehow finished with a B- and I have no idea how. If Umass Dartmouth has another professor teaching Physics I andor II    I would highly suggest you take them.

If you need to take Physics and for some reason it is not essential to your major, take it with Kagan. He is not a good "teacher" and his homework is impossible. However, on his test he gives you multiple tries throughout the semester to boost your grade on different sections. I finished with a B- and I realistically did not learn anything.

If you're expecting to actually learn anything, don't. The homework is insanely hard, harder than the minitests he assigns. Due to this, you don't learn a whole lot. You're given multiple retakes of each minitest throughout the course, so a good grade is guaranteed.

Professor Kagan is awesome. He really knows what he's talking about and absolutely loves physics. He'll also spice his lectures up with i-clicker questions and nods to sci-fi and pop culture (get ready for learning force). His tests let you redo any question he's given, just make sure to manage your time on them. Overall, an excellent professor.

Great professor! Extremely helpful and he makes his class interesting, even if he does have a dorky sense of humor. He does a great job of explaining concepts using illustrations and graphs, which is great if you're a visual learner. Highly recommended.

Kagan is an absolute brilliant man. Though he is helpful and nice, the questions he expects us to know how to answer are difficult, and his explanations in general are a bit too complex. At times, I feel he forgets we are not on the same level as him. Overall, good and intelligent professor, but can be very difficult.

Really Nice guy, works hard at his job. He does an excellent job of explaining concepts and making sure that his explanations and slides on his powerpoints are clear airtight. Always answers emails promptly. His exams can be very challenging, but he scales them a lot, so don't be surprised to feel you completely bombed the exams but still passed.

A good professor, and an alright class. Teaches super simple stuff in class, the homework system gives much tougher problems, and his tests are even harder than the homework. He is happy to help students understand tests/homeworks to better prepare themselves next time, and he scales the grades. I averaged a 65 on the exams and still got an A.

Professor Kagan is a great teacher of a tough subject. Rarely uses numbers and does all calculations simply with variables which makes it much easier to explain. Really nice guy, always more than happy to help and has a great sense of humor! The homework system used (CAPA) isn't by his choice and can be hard to get used to but he is accommodating.

Very good professor, in my opinion. You either love him or you hate him, mainly because he rarely uses numbers in class. Knows exactly what he's talking about, and is good at explaining it in detail. Goes into a little too much detail sometimes and the class can drag, but I appreciated tat detail during harder sections. If you can, take him.

He's very smart but he teaches all concepts. As a hands-on learner it's hard for me to keep up with that when you're suddenly assigned problems and you have no idea how to approach them. He's a great teacher though, don't be afraid to ask questions, and he'll do his best to help you with anything he can. Lenient grader.
Classes are not mandatory, but they should be. She is an amazing professor who keeps things very entertaining. Very easy information and topics.

She is absolutely by far my favorite professor. All she wants is her students to learn and succeed. There's group projects but they are easy and weekly homework but it's interesting and fun. No final exam or midterm.

Class is not too difficult and Prof. Dekkers is amazing. Graded by an assignment and discussion each week, a project at the end and 2 career assignments, no exams. Super kind and wants students to succeed. Highly recommend

Professor Larson is an amazing professor. I've taken him twice now and would 100% take him again. He cares so much about his students and is very passionate about his material. He's a bit difficult to contact outside of classes, but if you look beyond that he's amazing. He loves to joke around and make everyone feel welcome and included.
Honestly the best professor I have had here at UMD so far. Definitely take a class with him if you want to have a professor that genuinely cares about the students that he teaches.

Larson is a pretty awkward guy but he means well, he is super easy going. If you do not understand something he is willing to work with you. One group project super easy, 2 papers, Friday assignments are online and no class on Fridays. Easy going, easy class, I recommend.
Take Cjs 257 with him! You will learn a lot and it's a course that you can put on your resume. I plan on taking cjs 350 with him next semester :)

Dr. Larson is the one of the best professors in the CJS department! He cares about his students and what they are learning about, but he also cares about them outside of the classroom too. This class was Beyond Borders and we covered a lot about migration, and what's going on in the news with immigration and the borders. The workload wasn't too bad

CJS 257 - Inside/Out Program. The class itself was interesting. Because it was the Inside/Out program we only met once a week. One paper every week that had to be about three pages. Final paper was seven pages. Readings every week. Lots of class discussions and groups.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--SO MANY PAPERS--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: he's a good one. Take him
Great professor, work is not hard. Just so every work giving and you will be ok.

Dr. Larson is a brilliant professor whom you can learn so much from. Keep an open mind.  He honesty is one of the best professors at UMD. He assigns a lot of reading but he has good taste, you'll enjoy every packet.

Really boring professor. His class always dragged on. The classwork wasn't that hard but it's just a tough class to get through. You don't really have to read as long as you skim over it and find one word that describes it because he goes around the room and asks for one.

Professor Larson is a little socially awkward but it is clear he really cares about his students and wants us to succeed. The assignments for this class were all simple (4 one page papers, a five page final paper, discussion posts and simple group projects) The material covered in the 205 class is very dry but Eric did his best. Easy A.
Seems like a nice guy, but class was not interesting at all. Very boring assignment and class discussions. Really dreaded going to this class, didn't really learn anything.

A lot of online assignments and readings. 5 online postings due every week. Online discussion, research questions, blogs, and wikis. Long and complicated readings. Takes awhile to grade your papers, unorganized, felt more like a middle school class to be honest.

He is kind of awkward, but he is a nice guy. Overall the class is pretty easy. It is based upon class participation, in class debate, at least 2 papers and quizzes. You can hardly show up and still do fine in the class, just participate.  It's a interesting class though... at times. Would take him again = easy A

This class was EXTREMELY boring. Do not take it with him unless it's absolutely necessary. He is a very nice guy and always available to help with any issues, but is VERY unclear and confusing on assignments. No text books, but you'll spend $40-50 in printing money. Assigns WAY too much reading which is extremely dry and hard to understand.

There was no text book however, most students ended up spending $40 in printing. You have 2 binders of readings throughout the semester.Prof gives an overload of reading, & his points aren't clear. However,he is easy to talk to and get in touch with.Offers extra credit to boost grade on exams. There was only a midterm and a final,lots of papers
no book!  great teacher. If you do all assignments you will pass. PARTICIPATE and he will love you. ALWAYS available for help after hours and tons of extra credit oppurtunity. Would take again!
the absolute worst i dont know why he isnt fired yet.
I want this man to be fired for all the wasted time he took from me. You will learn nothing in this class.

Professor Milham often goes on tangents and stops class when it comes to technology use, he would tell students to leave his lecture. He also argues with students and believes he's right. He also reads some of the online students responses and goes on rants about it. 

However the work is very easy and unlocked until the end of the semester,.

Dont take him.
Unless you want a Professor who thinks hes always right. I mean read through all these reviews on him.
Taking him online at the moment, WISH I dropped him. Not sure why Umass still has him here...
This professor has a condition called narcissism where he feels that hes always right, just legit witnessed him picking an argument with a student like act your age, you're not tough
didnt get anything out of his class went heavy on rants was a quite rude professor was not worth the stress do not take if u need a 3b course
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Please do not take this class if you don't need to, there are so many better 3B courses to choose from. I learned nothing from this course, it was easy but I was miserable all semester. It was difficult to determine what was relevant in the course, as he would go on tangents every class, and go over class time.

The class itself was pretty easy. I went to every lecture. There is four units to the class but we only got to the 2 unit. His lectures are the main focus of all assignments but he's a pretty slow lecturer and tends to go off on unrelated tangents - many of which regarded cheating to the same few people who would always be at class.

the class is easy but he's kind of rude and seems to care more about forcing the information down students' throats than their well being. attendance isn't required and you can pass if you don't go but i didn't learn much.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Super easy class but not worth your time... a class that you will learn absolutely nothing in. Hates any usage of cell phones/talking and is very picky when it comes to how assignments are turned in. Attendance is not mandatory. Overall, just a super rude professor that could've made this experience a lot better than it was. Would not recommend it

THE. WORST. even tho the class is SO easy, i rated it difficult because he will go OUT OF HIS WAY to accuse u of cheating and trick u. he will track ur IP address and find u. he is INSANE. PLEASE SAVE YOURSELF AND DO NOT TAKE HIM. I would 1000% rather than a harder class than take him again. his class is also SO PAINFUL to be in. invest in netflix

Participation matters to Professor Milham. You definitely will need to participate in discussions, this is a large portion of your grade. Professor Milham is very passionate about what he is teaching and wants to see his students do well. You will learn a great deal about racism and the inequalities that have occurred within the black community.

For the Class: Show up, take half a page of notes, use quizlet for chapter question sets, review notes and quizlet for tests, and you pass A+. If you struggle in this class, you shouldn't be in college. For the Professor: Standard 'I'm a computer science/engineering/physicist expert' with a music major. He owns his classroom, so don't be an idiot.

Everyone on here gave this man a bad rating and I cant figure out why. I was tentative taking his class because of the awful ratings and I have not had a bad experience yet. His assignments are all very straight forward and easy and not to mention out of all of my professors this semester he is the only one who is consistent with answering emails.

I'd have to say the worst thing about Edwin is the lack of actual graded assignments in his class. the chapter question sets are pretty much the only grades, aside from like 2 actual assignments, and the midterm and final, so you def need to make sure you do well on them. aside from that however, he teaches very clearly and is an overall nice guy

Lectures are boring and its impossible to tell which part is actually relevant to exam content. This course is about popular music??? its mostly a history of music class and a lot of time is spent discussing racism at the beginning. Don't take this course unless you have to. Textbook is not needed dont waste your money.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Goes off on tangents a lot. Since he doesn't take attendance, its. hard to want to go because you will feel like you're not learning.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Arrived late to every class the past two weeks. Gets upset when students try to leave on time when class ends, acts like a disrespectful high school teacher. Talks more about his life and unimportant things rather than the course material. He refuses to give a schedule for his class because people wouldn't show up, but the hall's still empty, bud.

the class itself is so boring and very dry. he is very passionate about the material but I wouldn't recommend taking the course if you don't need to. show up once in a while, look over the study guides and you should do okay. he doesn't care about laptops but freaks out about phones. very anal about little things.

Not an easy A, but easy enough B. Tests are based on class lectures, but if you miss class you'll do poorly. He does give you plenty of opportunities to boost your grade. Do all the extra credits and the on-line quizzes and homework assignments and you will end up with an A- (even if you missed many, many classes like me).

It's an easy A, but those who say to show up once a week and you'll pass is a lie. Every test (Mid term and Final) is on the topics he discusses in class. I honestly regret taking this class, it's a waste of time. You are better off taking Art History or a different music class other than his. I heard that MUS 101 is fun and easy as well.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Professor Milham knows quite a bit about his field of profession but his classes are just terrible. He is extremely arrogant and full of himself, and freaks out over the use of cell phones in class. Class is all lecture. Two tests, easy enough if you take some notes and do some googling.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: The "not show up and get an A" comments aren't exactly true. The midterm was based off of notes typed on his lectures which is pretty easy if you took notes, so show up and put up with him going off topic alot or contact a classmate and do the assignments/exams for an "A". Also you can use your laptops all you want, but he freaks out over phones.

History of rock and roll, more like the history of the united states before rock and roll. 14 weeks and not one lecture about rock and roll. just everything in between. also talks about his personal life and blackboard for more than half of the lecture. easy A and you don't even have to show up. and you get unlimited attempts at online quizzes.

Professor Milham tolerates absolutely not talking and no cell phone use. If he catches you doing either he'll tell you to leave the classroom. The course material is fairly easy. There are online quizzes which are easy (you have until the end of the semester to complete them) and the final and midterm are not bad as well. He lectures all class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: This guy is a hardo. He freaks out with cell phones and people talking. Would not recommend

Arguably the rudest teacher I have ever had. he's so full of himself and looks down upon every single student. He is also very bad at his profession, he has no idea how to teach and likes to spend class talking about random things like his car, his dog, or stuff that nobody cares about and has nothing to do with the course. the work is easy though

Take this class for an easy A. I showed up once a week, if that, and got a B+ only because I forgot about the syllabus assignment. Goes off on tangents and will get pissed but just stay off your phone, do the online work, and show up for the 2 tests

He was late to my class EVERY single day. Then he would get mad when people left after 15 minutes. He was late 45 minutes to my final!! He is clueless and cant even conduct in 3/4. The class is super easy, but not worth the headache. He is passionate but so much that he thinks that his thoughts are the only way. Would not take again.

Took his class online thinking it was going to be easy. He lets you do the work at your own pace, but demands it be submitted in his format. Guy is also pretty rude, and always wrote long e-mails. Too bad he took more time writing emails than grading. Theres 2 exams, 4 assignments, and 10 chapter questions. Dont google the test questions.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: The people who are complaining are people that either never showed up or didn't do the work. The professor is a hot and cold type of person, but I did find the topic interesting and yes he can be kind of rude. But if he really bothers you that much, show up once a week and keep up with the online work. I got an A by doing that.

Extremely mixed feelings about this professor. The class is definitely easy and he makes it that way because he wants everyone to do well. As a person, he's occasionally funny, other times he's just so rude, and he is unnecessarily crude and inappropriate in some discussions. Talks about the syllabus for 20 mins almost every class. Easy A though.

This class was so easy, I can't believe the other reviews on here! Your grade is composed of 10 quizzes all of which you have unlimited attempts, like 4 open response questions that you can do as many times as you need, exam 1, and the final exam! If you go to class once a week you'll get an A. Gives extra credit too!!

He is bluntly honest and speaks his mind, but i like that because he's honest. The class and test are easy if you actually pay attention. He does not take attendance but make sure you still study for test if you chose not to go for the classes and only the final. His homework were hard to understand but he lets you try to get a better grade on them
Like others have said, he's rude to students and very self centered. Likes to shove his opinions and political views into everything. Even when I agree with him, he still gets on my nerves.
He's not very specific about what he expects students to do, and when he gives feedback on failed/incomplete assignments, he is very vague about what you did wrong.

Professor Milham's class is easy to pass as long as you do your assignments and study for the test's. As some people mention on here, yes he can be rude at times when student's don't understand his instructions or are on their phone's in class, but I don't regret taking the class. Just make sure you meet all his standards & you will pass the class.
Boring!!!
Was really easy. But not clear at all. Instead he likes talking about himself most of the times. Attendance not required.

Not a good teacher. He's rude and arrogant. He teaches his opinion more than the truth or the curriculum. He's blatantly rac ist. I would not recommend him to anyone I know. Unfortunately, the class is the easiest A i've ever seen. Tough decision. Choose wisely.

He comes off very rude but it is an easy class. I skipped the majority of it and still managed to get a B. Every chapter homework assignment can be found on google so those are an easy 100. The only thing that sucked was that attendance was not mandatory (which is good) but to find out when a test is you have to go to class.

Prof. Milham was extremely disrespectful to students. He encouraged questions yet got angry if they weren't asked his way. His assignments were painfully easy, but if not completely perfectly to his unnecessarily strict rules were graded as a zero. His feedback was rude and horribly condescending.

Very annoying and I skipped every other class and still was able to get an A. Just make sure you make it to the class where he says what he will be putting on the test, do the assignments and quizzes online, and you will be fine.

want an easy A without learning anything? definitely take this class! just don't ever attend unless on test days. Do the 10 online assignments and you should be good for the tests. Teacher is useless, he shouldn't be teaching. also DO NOT buy the textbook because you never use it.

He is a very strict guy, but he is really not as some comments make him sound. You DO NOT need to go to class neither buy the textbook to pass this class. This is definitely an easy A. Take if you need to fill the requirements but don't really care about the class itself.

He REALLY isn't as bad as people crack him up to be! If you just pay attention and do the very little work he requires, you'll ace this course. He does have strict classroom policies, but you just need to respect him. He does frequently flip out on students, but that's just funny in my opinion.

Edwin has zero, zero. respect for any student that walks in his classroom, which is generally empty due to his overall class atmosphere--he calls students out for nothing, explains very little of his rambling lectures, and hardly thinks of anyone but himself. Joke of a class, Easy A.
Boring and annoying class

He yells at the end of every class for people to stop packing up even when the class is over. And most of what he talks about is irrelevant. I didn't attend half of the classes and still got A's on the tests.

The class itself was ridiculously easy, but he on the other hand, was a terrible teacher. He complained about everything and every time someone pulled out a phone in a class with over 200 students. Easy A, but a painful experience.

Point blank- cool class, BAD professor. Extremely rude for no reason, doesn't EVER write anything helpful for notes, incredibly boring lecture, talks about himself ALOT. Wicked easy class- no attendance required, all easy online assignments, but he himself made the experience really quite terrible.

He is boring..that wasn't a really a problem for me though. Just do all your online assignments and study them for your quizzes and you should be all set. Uses about 30mins of the class to talk about unrelated topics to what is being thought. slept a lot through his lectures but i still passed.
Just do his assessments and study them the night before the test.  And don't drop your iClicker, he hates that!

Talks about himself alll the time, doesnt explain anything at all, contradicts himself constantly, calls people out for texting all the time, doesnt give study guides for any of the tests and barely gives any notes, good class but take it with a different teacher
Likes to talk about himself and his opinions A LOT. Didn't really find him very interesting and started way farther back in history than necessary to get to actual rock music

Would honestly say a big part of why I did well was because I liked the professor. He made the classes simple, gave VERY good feedback, and made assignments easy to understand. A lot of assignments (Few, but big) would piggyback off each other, which I personally liked. Am activley trying to take this professor again, highly recommend.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Hilarious

reviews.comment: Super funny guy! He made the boring curriculum of HON101 feel more fun and engaging. Almost every assignment was a group project. He does not grade many things, so be definitely make sure the first few assignments are done well. He takes off points for attendance, so try to show up (and on time)!
Absolutely Awsome, He's a super nice guy, also an easy grader, he cares about his students and wants them to pass, just don't show up 20 min late to class and you will be good, highly recommend
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Hilarious--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Very funny, gives short-ish readings (they're interesting and not too long), participation is a grade, but he tells you the number, not too much homework, willing to work with the class
Funny, sometimes irritating because talks a lot when he tells us to work our papers and I can't focus. Papers are easy and straight forward show up and participate.
very personable if you just be nice and do the work you will get a good grade and the material is all on relevant and interesting things such an amazing class environment and not too hard
I absolutely love him; his lectures are hilarious. I only got a C+ because I couldn't wake up at 8 a.m. and attendance was 30% of our grade. Still, would recommend.

professor eli evans is a legend. his lectures always include “bad” jokes but it makes everyone giggle anyway. he loves to photoshop himself onto other celebrities too. one day i missed class and had to make up an assignment, but he said my email made him laugh so he gave me credit anyways. overall just a chill guy. just show up and do the work :)
Professor Evans is very easy. There is homework but it's very simple and easy to do. Take this class is your looking for an easy ENL 101 and 102 professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: This guy is the literal GOAT. I heard good things about him and decided to take him for my ENL 102 class and it was very easy and very fun. Eli is hilarious and the topics covered were so simple, this was unironically 2x easier than my ENL 101.
Seems to enjoy teaching, tries to make class enjoyable and is also one of the funniest professors I've ever had.

This was my favorite class of the semester.  Honors class was fun and we got to talk in the beginning about whatever.  Was always let out early.  I don't like talking much but it was required in this class but ended up being fun.  Group activities were actually enjoyable and had a lot of creative liberty to have fun and enjoy the project.

Overall not a bad teacher. Kind of annoying though, forces us into group work, forced to participate, awkward and unfunny humor. Just a boring class overall, dreaded going to his class but he is strict about attendance. Easy class, an easy A for sure, but could hardly keep myself from falling asleep in his class. Recommend if you want an A.

Class with Prof. Evans felt a lot like high school. Participation was forced, attendance was mandatory to pass the class, and almost every single day we had some forced form of partner work. It also felt like I was being talked to like I was a high schooler. Overall this class was not difficult for me, but also not enjoyable either.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Dr. Evans is awesome. His lectures get right to the point of what he's expecting, and keeps all the homework relevant to improving your writing and analytical skills. He understands his students and is usually flexible as long as you communicate. He has fair expectations based on how students do. Surprisingly little ambiguity which is another plus.

He tries to make class fun and is very hilarious. he tries to make us do assignments that relates to his students and tries to make sure we aren't doing anything that we don't want to do. he really does seem passionate about teaching and making sure we succeed. i will definitely be taking ENL102 next semester with him.

Eli Evans is easily the most entertaining professor I have ever had. He is friendly, hilarious, and cares about his students. He always emails his students and offers help for the homework or if anyone is having trouble with the reading. He has been one of my favorite professors ever and I would definitely take many more classes with him.

ELi is hands down the best professor I've had as a junior at Dartmouth High. SO funny, and makes the classes light and enjoyable. I'm a biology major and I've never had more fun than I did in this man's English 200 class. He is the bomb. If you want a professor that actually gives a care about his students, TAKE HIM. 10/10
He is an absolute legend.

Eli gives you plenty of feedback. The class involves lots of assignments, but most are straight forward and not too time consuming. He is willing to work with you on deadlines if necessary. Would take another class with him.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Took him for ENL 102, pretty funny, easy-going. Would take again

Eli is the bomb-dot-com. He is super funny and his lectures/assignments are straight forward and easy to get good grades on. Show up to class, do the work, and you will be fine. The best ENL class I have taken at this school. Worth the 8am time too!
Super funny and easy going. Willing to help you with deadlines. As your writing your papers, he's willing to edit and help you earn the grade you want.
Loved professor Evans, supper funny and nice. Gives homework but easy, super sarcastic, would definitely take again.
Eli Evens is a great professor that teaches well and is very funny.

Prof. Evans is hysterical! I really didn't enjoy the class itself but he is funny and that helped kept me engaged in the material. He will mark down every time you raise your hand to give you points so keep that in mind and the homework is fairly simple, nothing too crazy
Great professor. Interesting class. I highly recommend him. Also very funny.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Hilarious--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Lots of reading. It was just an ok class, the readings are off the cuff and not things I'd normally be interested in. Expectations on the discussion boards are high.

Hes a great professor that keeps class entertaining when he can tell it starts to get boring. Its very easy to create a great student-teacher bond. 10/10 would always again, I wish he taught more classes I needed to take.

Attendance is only mandatory for finding out what assignments you have for homework. Other than that, the class is laid back and interesting. Works in groups a lot, but overall a funny, all around professor. I'm taking another class of his next semester!
Amazing teacher, personally I blame the school for hating this class. In the 102 Class its hard to get a higher grade than what you received on the first major assignment.

Easy A if you show up to most classes and turn in your work on time. He doesn't give any tests or quizzes, only about 4-5 papers a semester and a few easy online assignments. Also willing to give extensions on paper. Lecture can be boring sometimes, and he also takes attendance.
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--Caring

reviews.comment: Awesome professor. Lectures can get kind of lengthy or boring at times, but he is a very understanding and kind professor. Makes awkward jokes and tries to be funny sometimes, but he is overall one of the best professors at UMD. Gives a fair amount of work but is always great about extensions and help, especially if you email him or struggle at all
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: He's awesome. A lot of writing. He makes class enjoyable with funny slideshows and jokes. Homework after every class & a lot of reading.

Actually dreaded Freshman English until I got this guy. Does a great job explaining material and helps the people who deserve it. Usually a long reading and journal that is due next class, but its college so it would be expected. This dude got a great sense of humor and swag too and anyone who would rate him poorly probably didn't try hard enough.

Started out very easy & simple class, lectures were basic & understandable. However halfway through the semester he changed teaching styles &  began making the questions on homework and exams difficult, especially since he only every assigned parts of books since most questions required knowledge of the entire book to answer. Would not take again.

He's a really cool guy. I went into the class hesitant, but quickly warmed up and grew to like him because he really tries and shows that he cares about us learning. He's good at what he does even if it was hard for me to adjust to his style of writing at first although ultimately I feel like I learned a lot.

I love him, besides having swag on point he is very kind as human being and as a professional. You have up to 6 absences before you get points off, which you would not miss. He is understandable if you have good reason for not doing your work he is always there for you, office hours, class time and even off-time. He is awesome!
One of the best english professors i have had throughout my career. Highly recommend him! Truly cares about his students too!
Great class. Would take again.
Dudes a homie, got mad swag too.

You're allowed up to 6 absences without it effecting your grade. Always willing to walk around and help. Sense of humor is based on puns and making fun of himself. Interesting man, taught at universities all over USA. Knows what he's talking about. Doesn't make class difficult. Very laid back and casual. Easy on the homework. Great guy!

Such a sweet professor!!! Provides free textbooks and responds to emails quickly. Hands out extra credit if you show interest in the class. It's pretty hard to fail with him, you really have to not care. I found his Political Science courses to be interesting and proposed information not often referenced.

Professor Kazumori, although confused with another professor at the university, is a caring and thoughtful professor. With my experience, he wants you to succeed. He has an accent, but he does his best to articulate himself. He also provides class materials due to students' financial limits. If patient and ambitious, you will succeed in his class.
Difficult class for me but she is very sweet and cares about her students. Make sure to pay attention cause you could get lost.
He is so sweet and so caring. The class is so easy, all the exams are repeated questions for the homework. He really cares about his students and if they are understanding the material.

he's so nice and truly cares about his students. he really wants the students in his class to do well and wants to make the class as easy as possible. he always asks students if they understand the material and does a great job reminding students about assignments due.

He is a great professor and he really cares! Yes, he does have an accent but so do have of the professors at this university. He has organized powerpoint lectures to follow along if you cannot understand. If he is going too fast or you cant understand him, just speak up.

Was very lenient with grading materials, understood if you were unable to make class due to obligations. Has a lot of slides, but it's not that bad in the grand scheme of things. Can talk to outside of class, even if not class related. midterms allow cheat sheets, no final for my class (Class was PSC 161, wouldn't give me option in ratings)

The professor showed me anonymized grades and there is no student who got B+ in the class. The professor provided a lecture note of about 100 slides each week. The professor did not teach PSC347 in the previous semesters. (A student has limited access to phone and emails because she has a job.)

Although Professor Kazumori is as nice as can be and is genuinely interested in the learning of his students, his class is poorly organized and managed. he does not use a textbook but does not provide enough in any lectures to accurately answer the homework questions. The way in which he teaches makes the process of learning impossible

I enjoyed the class-the guest speaker invited as well as the project requirement-  all relevant material. It was a fast moving class, so it was important to stay caught up. Professor Kazumori graded fairly and even offered extra credit in the beginning of this course. I would take another class led by Prof. Kazumori if given the opportunity!

I actually took this class as a winter session course and I think the below rating does not represent the views of the students who actually took the class. Professor Kazumori was organized, clearly knew all the material very well and was passionate about the subject. He was always helpful you just have to contact him and ask!
Very hard to understand but also nice, but seems to struggle to explain what he is teaching and often asks other students to explain for him, but has study groups and not a large workload at all.

There is the use of iClicker, which is an attendance app that is used for class. If you don't use it, you will have a bad attendance/participation grade. His exams are easy, and you do a practice exam before. He talks quite fast, but you get used to it and he's very nice, you can ask him to slow down. He gave us Mandarins after one of our exams,

His material is all covered in power points he shares. Tests are straightforward and he lets you take the online quizzes in groups. He wants to make sure everyone does well and asks throughout each lecture if people have questions. Everyone complains abt his accent but you can understand him and if you need him to clarify he will. Nice guy. easy

He really cares about making sure that students understand the material he is presenting and ensuring that the way he is presenting the material is effective. He uses Pearson quizzes online and two tests combined with a group project for the graded material in the class. His class isn't that difficult and he really cares about the students.

This professor knows what he is talking about when it comes to Economics. He is very helpful, and holds special study hours outside of class. If you do the homework and study for a few hours you will do good on the two midterms.
he's a good professor but is hard to understand, the tests are easy as long as you pay attention and the class is not to boring

Hes a pretty chill dude. But you cant understand a word he says. Hes pretty boring and i never paid attention in his class but i take macro now with a different professor and the materials easy so this shouldnt be hard. Easy to cheat its almost like he knows but he doesnt care. And end of semester he gives you champagne, sadly i missed it lit
Very hard to understand. Seems like he knows his stuff he just has trouble explaining it

Difficult to understand, and his lectures do not seem to make much sense. Uses convoluted examples that don't really help. Fairly regular homework (not difficult) and the tests were heavily scaled. Group project at the end of the semester.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: Really nice guy but I can't understand a word he says. Everything is online and when you ask him a question it just gets more confusing. He says the book is required but we NEVER used it. I basically needed to self teach myself or else I would have failed.

He is very easy and understanding. If you have an issue, he will instantly try to help. The exams are scantron and often with bonus. He likes to assign students into group for term projects. It can get boring in his class.

No textbooks but there are books you need a physical copy of. Also attendance isn't mandatory but there is a classwork portion of the grade with an in class assignment each day. No essays but the whole semester is group work, with short answer quizzes and then a big group project at the end.

Participation isn't mandatory, but there is a classwork portion of the grade with an assignment each class. The whole semester was group work with the same people, and most people just worked with whoever the sat near on the first day, so choose your spot carefully I guess. No essays but at the end of the semester there's a big group project.

Eric was a great professor! All we had to do was read sections of different books he assigned, have a class discussion about it, and eventually take a quiz. All quizzes were open note, and as long as your name was on the discussion sheet for your group, your participation and attendance were taken. The final group project was a breeze too.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Super chill guy. Work is relatively easy and attendance wasn't mandatory, but would still show up anyways. Honestly great class for first semester.

He's a little condescending but deep down. He's a good guy who cares about his students success. Technically attendance isn't mandatory but you get a discussion grade every class and if you don't go, you'll get a zero for the day. Class is on a scale of 1000 points and every day this is a loss of five. No essays only exams,

Great professor! Really cares about his students. Doing the group project at the end of the semester was a bit annoying because of all of the components - but there is zero to none homework in this class. The course texts were also awesome + the tv show we had to watch. I would definitely take him again!
A amazing teacher that is super clear on his rubrics and gives good feedback on assignments. class is so easy and he generally cares about his students.

This professor is so engaging. You can tell he really has a passion for literature and that shows in the way he teaches us. I went to his office hours to ask him so many questions. I was very pessimistic that I wouldn't like this class because i did not like literature but he has made the class discussions very engaging.
