very good professor and really good guy. I would recommend him to anyone.
Most understanding guy ever; allows resubmission of papers and his class is group project-based

Prof Casero is hands down one of the best professor in the English dept. His class consists of 4 major assignments, 1 exam and discussion papers. I missed like 3 or 4 discussions but it wasn't too much of a big deal because they were only worth 15 pts and the major assignments were worth more. The class is an easy A if you stay on top of your work.

Very respectful, friendly, and outgoing. Gives thorough and concise feedback. Just show up to class and participate every now and again and you will do great. One of the best professors I have had so far.

One of the best ENL professors, I highly recommend taking him for 200 courses! He is extremely helpful, gives great feedback on assignments and isn't a tough grader. Participation grade is given for every class but is attendance based, and there are a lot of optional writing days he does. Thoroughly enjoyed class discussions as well.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Professor Casero was the first Professor I took in college and he has been my favorite English teacher so far. If you participate often then you will do well in his class. He also really cares about his student succeeding. If you have questions, he is always willing to answer them and his lectures are also really fun and full of cat pictures!
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Amazing professor. Very resourceful if help is needed and very nice. Gives thoughtful and descriptive instructions. Would definitely recommend if you struggle in english.

The professor was great! You were able to redo any major assignments you got a bad grade on. Alternate assignment if something comes up (a week to complete from the day the class was). Class was just students talking but you don't have to if you don't want to. Student discussion assignment were you have to talk in front of class. 1 online test
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Casero is an amazing professor. I took his class second semester of my freshmen year in 2022 and I only got a C+ because I stopped going to class and wasn't motivated to go to any. Had nothing to do with his class. He understood that and helped me. The show we watched was a great show and he correlated it to class which was fun.

Professor Casero is amazing professer. You're learning literature that you need a professor like him. Lectures are fun and useful. Lots of reading and writing but don't forget it is literature class. Accept late homework and generously grader! Accountable and reliability!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Really good guy (albeit pretty awkward) who cares about his students. If you put in any amount of work you will do great. You basically don't have to attend a single class if you don't want to because he posts alternate assignments online. The classes are mostly just fairly informal discussions on whatever reading was assigned.

I took this course freshman or sophomore year but had to give this prof a review. His class was the best. I think We watched 3 films and wrote papers on the three films. He's so kind, helpful, and understanding. really wants students to do well. End of the semester there was a final group project that was a little tough

Professor Casero is such a nice guy. I took this course as a required credit, but his class was my highest grade and he was my favorite professor that semester. As long as you put a tiny bit of effort into doing the assignments it should be an easy A. Definitely try and get his classes if you can!!

An awesome teacher! Very understanding of mental health, and a genuinely nice teacher. Class is a perfect balance of challenging but also not breaking your brain. Took three classes with him, would highly recommend.

So helpful 24/7!! He knows exactly what he wants in his assignments and if you hit all of the requirements clearly you get an A. Has everything outlined in MyCourses week by week, tons of opportunities to make up work. Readily available in email, encourages going to his office hours for any questions on your papers. Wicked understanding & nice guy

Prof. Casero is an amazing prof and he cares a lot about his students succeeding. He lets you re do a lot of your assignments for a better grade, but the assignments are easy to begin with. With online classes all year he cut down the meetings from 3 days a week to 2, then to 1 day. I'm taking another one of his classes next semester.

Professor Casero is honestly the best. His class is fairly easy because he is extremely understanding and truly cares about his students. If you do all the assignments (not complicated), you will pass with an A. Although this semester was online (fall 2020), he made sure everything worked for everyone. I'm definitely taking his ENL1102 course!

One of the best ENL Professors I've ever had. If you ever happen to fall behind on work, you can easily let him know and make up the work for it and submit it late to get back into action. Eric is very understanding and a nice person to be around. I may have fallen behind when things went online, but I managed to get back up there and pass.

Mr. Casero is my all time favorite. I have taken 2 classes with him and I'm planning on taking another one for next semester. When people say he's a real gem, he really is. He makes sure no one is left behind. He's very understanding so make sure to talk to him if anything's going on with the class or making up assignments.

Dr. Casero is an amazing professor. Schedule appointments with him to review your work and you should be set. He genuinely cares about you and your opinions in essays won't affect your grade if they differ from his. Only gripe is that I had over a 100 in his class and I got an A instead of an A+. Doesn't affect GPA but I just wanted to have it.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Professor Casero is the best. He makes it a priority to keep his students engaged during his class. He emails back instantly and genuinely cares how you do in his class. He lets you make up missed classes as well as papers you did poorly on. 10/10 would take again!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Mr C is the real deal. Very nice man and cares about his students a lot. He gives 4 Major Assignments and also provides a writing samples for all of them which makes it easy to know what you have to do. He gives you extension on all your assignments and hes an easy grader. More importantly he makes the class interesting and hes a nice guy.
Cool guy. Due dates are very negotiable. Lots of reading random articles.

Professor Casero is easily one of the nicest men I have ever met, he is very understanding and is quick to accommodate to whatever needs you may have. That being said, it is fairly easy to skip his class however your grade will be affected. I took his controversy in film section and I really enjoyed it. 10/10 man.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring

reviews.comment: Dr. Casero is an amazing professor who wants his students to succeed. He's easy to get a hold of outside of class. He genuinely cares for his students. This is my second semester taking him. The workload is manageable. The exams and papers easy. Just attend and participate. He likes discussing the course materials. A great professor all around.

Professor Casero is an amazing professor who really cares if his students pass! I have taken him two times, and for both classes he has been an amazing teacher! The syllabus is very clear, and the only real work you have to do is the readings and occasional papers. Easy A!

Professor Casero is genuinely one of the nicest. He does take attendance but will always give you the opportunity to make up what you missed for credit. He only gives readings for home work and then eventually you will do 4 papers on them, very lenient if you need more time on them. No final. Liked him a lot super sweet, easy final presentation.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Caring

reviews.comment: Professor Casero is a very talented individual. He won't just hand you an A, but if you remotely try you should get one. You can tell he is very passionate about what he does. He is very clear on how he grades his papers, and will give you more time past a deadline no questions asked. Class was fun and I learned a lot at the same time.

Professor Casero was a really great teacher who obviously genuinely cared about the course and his students. He does everything he can to make sure students pass while still making sure they do the work. His classes are open discussion grades for participation/attendance, but allows 5. There are only 3 papers and the readings are not too difficult.

Awesome teacher. Didn't have a textbook but 5 different novels to read instead, not too much reading for each class. Going to class was helpful for the class discussions we had on the readings. Tough grader, but definitely gives you good feedback and lots of opportunities to improve it. Would definitely take again!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Took ENL 200 winter course online. VERY condensed 4 week course. LOTS of reading /some writing. Was a bit tough on grading but gave good feedback. Class consisted of two online discussion posts a week/ 1 4 question (short answers) quiz per week and 1 paper per week due. He does give extra credit writing assignments towards end. Would take him again
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: He was an amazing professor, very good english teacher, loved every moment of the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Would take again

reviews.comment: Professor Casero is awesome! Gives clear understanding of what is expected of you. Because it was honors, I imagine it was a little more difficult but still not hard. A lot of homework but due dates are usually flexible. As long as you stay on top of it and go to class, you'll do well. No tests, just essays that he willingly gives extensions on.

Dr. Casero is a professor I have taken twice. He is so simple, and the work he gives is just the right amount of challenging. He himself is a true gem. Any one-on-one meetings with him are so helpful and, for me at least, inspirational. He has truly helped me through my first year of college and has cared for my well being. Take his class.

I really enjoyed his ENL 101 & 102. Really caring professor. Readings were not bad and interesting. 5 projects, 2 are easy worksheets. VERY GOOD about making himself available. Very organized. Grades in a timely manner. Excellent w extensions!!Truly have nothing bad to say about him! Will def take him for future ENL classes.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Really caring professor and easy going. If you have an issue, you can talk to him about it and he will do his best to work with you. Easy grader as long as you do your work and put in effort. Lectures can get very boring but as long as you do your work and show up to class, easy A.

I took him my first semester of college, and decided to take him second semester too. He is a good teacher, but sometimes his class is so boring. If you don't go to class at all you will be sure to fail. But if you put in effort he won't let you fail! Definitely worth taking him, definitely helpful inside and out of class

Professor Casero is such an awesome professor! I've never enjoyed an English class as much as his, and he makes the novels more bearable to read. He makes sure everybody is on the same page and really wants all of his students to do well. He even let me use him as a reference for a job interview, which was really awesome.

Dr. Casero is hands down the best professor I have ever had, including all of my elementary, middle, and high school teachers. After assigning work, he will go to each and every student to make sure they understand 100% everything going on. His words exactly:"I will always grant an extension so long as you ask me in person." Amazing person overall.

Such an amazing professor! So sweet and caring , you can tell he really cares for his students. He always seems nervous, however he is very intelligent and the biggest sweetheart!! He is very helpful if you don't understand the material and there's a lot of class discussions, they are all interesting! I will take his class all four years if I can!!

An amazing professor, really takes the time to make sure that you know everything and does not want students to fail. Very clear on the assignments and if you need more help he has conferences that you can sign up for and he will help you. Best professor ever!

I love this professor! There are quite a few assignments but they are all bearable. Dr. Casero is so sweet and is ALWAYS willing to meet with you and help you out in any way. You will have some partner work and a total of 2 very easy, painless and short presentations. If you are a freshman, please take this class to get you acclimated to college!

Castro is a teacher that seems to be very nervous most of the time but he's very sincere and caring, it's actually really adorable. Homework was just to read certain passages. If I remember there were 2 essays and no final. He's a very easy teacher, take him! I could have had a higher grade, but I slacked. EASY A! He grades mostly on completion

He always made himself available, even outside of his office hours when needed. There are 4 essays, no tests/final. He is a pretty lenient grader and won't deduct points if your'e not the best writer, just whether or not you have the required content.  Overall, great professor, very organized, and is extremely knowledgeable in his subject area.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: He's actually a really great guy who genuinely cares about his students and wants to make class fun and interesting. Homework/Classwork is not really bad.  Really easy going and willing to work with you if you need extra time or extensions. Just take him if you want a solid A/B in ENL200, he's easy and friendly.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: Often goes on unnecessary tangents, for examples he yells at people and tells them to leave if he hears a cellphone goes off. Exams and assignments are online however

He is AWFUL! Crashes out over absolutely any noise, and he HATES phones. He would often spend half the class complaining about technology or people who ask him questions. All work is online, which is nice. The work can be done without the textbook, so don't waste your money.

Biggest crashout ever. Will take the time to embarrass you in front of the whole class for saying something quietly. If you even check your phone, expect an at least 5-minute session of him explaining how he is better than everyone. His lectures usually contain about 50% about himself rather than the music. Class is very easy tho
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Though he does go on tangents as mentioned in other reviews, gives very easy online quizzes and just 2 exams. Attendance not mandatory, but there are weekly lecture quizzes so it may be beneficial.  Offers opportunity for extra credit, I wish every intro class was as easy as this course.
He is an easy class, very creepy man. Hates the sounds of phones going off, hates to see phones out. He says things that made me very uncomftorable and would never take him again. But very easy!

Easy A class, I never showed up and managed a 101/110 on the final. He rambles ALOT and shames you for anything that is not revolving about him which is why I didn't show up. Exams are online at home and all the assignments are online at home with multiple tries. He will not answer questions if you have them he will direct you to the syllabus.

-Great at history telling.//
-Rambles: Starts class with 15-20 minutes of personal rants.//
-Often late: Starts late, freaks out when you want to leave "on-time".//
-Strict on phones: Will go off on a 5-min rant if one rings.//
-Knowledgeable but insufferable yet caring: Interesting lectures, but loves hearing himself talk.//

If you come in late, have your ringer on, or decide to have a quiet conversation in the back of the room disturbing nobody, he will stop class to shame you for no reason. Barely gets to the point in his lectures cuz he gets distracted talking abt god knows what and when he finally gets to his point, class is over but god forbid you pack up on time.

This class was made unnecessarily difficult by professor. He is extremely tedious about details in assignments and will give you a zero over it. Lectures would be more interesting if he didn't spend 80% of them complaining about literally everything, and half the time it is unrelated to course topics. Avoid this class if you can
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Don't take this professor if you're not prepared for a headache. In all of his assignments, if you have a tiny mistake he will make you resubmit them, and takes ages to post the new grade. The class itself was easy but he is very picky with his assignments. His emails are ridiculously long too. Too much trouble for a level 100 class.

This guy makes no sense. He talks about nothing and we don't even do anything in class. He is genuinely scary and screams at the class. He thinks he's funny and has a dry sarcastic sense of humor. He literally talks about the weirdest stuff and it's just odd. Don't take this professor.

The worst professor I have ever had. his class is easy but i was generally scared to be in his class. He is very rude and always spends the class complains about people that dont show up to class. he calls out people and will search for phones that he hears which gave me so much anxiety. Take his class but dont go if you know what is good for you.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: He talks about absolutely nothing. Waste of time.

Professor Milham's classes are pretty easier but the lectures are difficult because he does not show slides. He just pulls up a word document that reads no phone use and then lectures.  He has an extremely dry sense of humor and is extremely sarcastic but his class is easy. He assigns extra credit, too which helps your grade.

Class is really easy to get through if you do the online quizzes & 2 tests. Half the class doesn't show up because he calls out students and goes on the longest tangents. He'll go over the class time by 5 minutes and will call you out for packing up your stuff because he's not done talking. He's an acquired taste for sure, but survivable.
Class is super easy, I stopped going after a month since his lectures were pointless... Passed the class with an A everything is on quizlet
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Nice Professor very good at answering emails unlike a lot of Umass professors.

The assignments and tests in this class are easy, but I went probably once every 3 weeks because it was unbearable. It is all lectures, but the professor goes on random tangents or the same rants about Blackboard or cheating for like 3/4 of the class. He is also rude and will publicly embarass you for minutes at a time if you talk or use your phone

I like the guy and his sarcasm. The class is mad easy tho. All of the answers to the chapter set questions are all on quizlet. Went to class once and I ended up with a A+. Take this class if you want an easy A with credit.

No power points, notes, or recordings. Lecture only. doesn't take attendance but must go to pass. Assignments and test weighted almost equally. No phones during class he'll call you out. Only got through half course material. 10 quizzes with unlimited attempts based on textbook chapters, 1 midterm, and 3 part final, 1 part was extra credit,
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: For the Professor: Standard 'I'm a computer science/engineering/physicist expert' with a music major. He owns his classroom, so don't be an idiot. For the Class: Show up, take half a page of notes, use quizlet for chapter question sets, review notes and quizlet for tests, and you pass A+. If you struggle in this class, you shouldn't be in college.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Sends out miles long emails almost daily. Very condescending with his wording. Every email includes xx amount of students havent done this, xx amount of students have done this wrong, only 1 student did it correctly. Discussion posts are overly monitored, and he deletes your posts if he feels like they are redundant and you receive a 0

He is very passive-aggressive, almost rude with the way he speaks through email. If you make a mistake, prepare yourself for the passive-aggressive commentary. He assigns so much work, too, so try to be thorough but not too thorough because then he will make comments about you overanalyzing the assignment even though he sends pages for emails.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Very lecture heavy, is typically late to class almost each time anywhere from 5-25 mins. Gets distracted easy. Has a poor attitude at times . 1 test a week before finals. Do not need the book . 10 easy online quizzes. Easy A class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tests? Not many--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: I know it's a lecture class but she loves to yap, would do other classes hw in that class. Be sure to take notes on what she writes on the board. For exams review, she never finishes them in class, so go to office hours and work your way from bottom to top on the exam review she gives. Study for exams a week or couple days before.
Shes the best, lectures are so interesting, work is easy and reasonable

Prof Eckert takes the extra step in ensuring her student's are successful. She gives great feedback and the course expectations are clear. Grading and assignments are all pretty straight forward, although there is a decent amount of weekly homework, but late work is accepted. I definitely recommend taking a class with her.

While lecture-heavy Prof. Eckert keeps discussions topical and hilarious. I've taken a few classes with them now and never have I purchased a textbook. Weekly discussion boards, an exam or two, a presentation, and a paper are the usual layout for their syllabus'. Great at giving feedback and willing to give extra credit.
A lot of lecturing in this class but if you pay attention and participate you should do well. I thought the class was interesting and Professor Eckert really wants her students to succeed.

Professor Eckert is just amazing! Attendance is mandatory but she makes lectures very interesting and she always has positive energy. The work may seem like busy work at times but if you do the readings(which arent that long) its really easy to pass. Definitely hoping to take her class again in the future!

Easy class, she had weekly assignments which was based on reading to do, she had two online exams which you can use notes for, she allows you to turn in late as well, I also did the final wrong and she still accepted it and graded it as if it wasn't wrong. gets semi off topic but she will help you if you ask as well.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Expressive and personable. Gives students the opportunity to artistically express themselves by giving leniency on how assignments can be done. Greatly appreciates students who will engage her at her level and always looking to improve. A few chances for extra credit. A casual class format weekly assignments, an end paper, and a midterm/final.

Professor Eckert is an absolute sweetheart. The textbook is very small and super easy to read. As long as you do the assignments you should pass with flying colors. She is willing to help if you need it and you get multiple attempts on her quizzes. I suggest taking her over any other Ethics professor
Shes super chill, just take notes and listen to her lectures and you'll do fine. Shes pretty great.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Professor Eckert is an absolute delight and is probably the best professor that I have had at UMD so far. She made class materials very easy to access by posting PDF versions on the MyCourses, and she gave really good feedback on assignments. Class felt more like exploring a hobby than a college-level philosophy course.

She is an overall great professor. If you show up to class and take organized notes there will be no issues passing her exams. She will let you know what you need to know for the exam. She was engaging and interesting. She is easily reachable through email and after class. I would recommend taking her if you show up to class most of the time.

Dr. Eckert was one of my favorite professors! Her exams were easy if you went to class and paid attention you should be able to pass the class easily. She does not use PowerPoints so make sure you take notes. After taking her class the philosophy department tried to recruit me to be a philosophy major or minor. I loved her class so much! Take her!

This class is way too much work to be a general education course. Especially if taken in senior year. Professor is a nice person but her tests are annoying/specific and ask you to delve much deeper than the class lectures. Alot of the concepts are obscure in the course and confusing, try to avoid this class if you need to fit a requirement.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Eckert is truly an amazing lady. Shes very funny and sometimes goes on a rant and talks about things outside of philosophy. Shes an amazing professor and her exams are okay, study and youll pass. I would definitely recommend taking her

One of the most laid back professors. Lectures go off topic very frequently and she repeats the same information for like 2 classes straight so you are guaranteed to remember it even if you missed a class. Exams are mostly short answers and not to difficult if you listened in class. Offers an "upgrade" so you can make ur 2nd exam replace the first.

If you do not take down 100% of her notes, you can expect to not pass any test. They are not open notes and her tests are all short answer and she questions every single aspect of a subject. She is truly a nice and funny woman, and even with paying attention and attending a majority of the classes, it was still very difficult to pass a test.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious

reviews.comment: She is very nice and the class is easy. Attend class, take ALL her notes down. Tests are open notes. However, it is EXTREMELY hard trying to get in touch with her. She did NOT reply to ANY of my emails. She gives an actual final (not on the last day of class like most 101 elec.), very frustrating. My nursing finals should be my priority, not this.

She is the cutest and sweetest women ever. Open note test, take home test was part of the other test and the questions weren't hard at all. Go to class, take notes, and you're fine. She keeps your attention from her silliness. I would take philo 101 again if I could. Made me question a minor in philosophy. She knows her stuff!!!
If you go to class and take notes it's an easy A.  She makes material very interesting and easy to understand.

She is soo convinced her opinion on everything is correct that she refuses to listen to others opinions. very snobby and rude when e-mailing students back. unless you absoultely love PHL i would not take it! no powerpoints, she just goes on rants about random topics

Professor Eckert is upbeat, really into the material she teaches, and makes the exams as easy as possible by giving the exact material needed to know during lecture. Our class consisted of 4 exams and 3 homework assignments. This was an easy A elective for me and I surprisingly learned from it. Sometimes classes can be dull so bring coffee.
She is seriously by far the best professor i have ever had. She sincerely cares about her students, and is so down to earth. If you ever have the chance to take her DO IT! shes the BEST!!

I took plato and metaphysics  with her. It can be hard to pay attention in her class as it tends to go off into random discussions and be a bit boring but her classes are pretty easy. She is so passionate about philosophy and really cares about sharing her knowledge. I would recommend taking her classes.
By far one of my favorite teachers. If you are willing to participate, it will be an amazing course and an easy A!
she was awesome! her class was fun and exciting. the tests were very easy. fun class and i would recommend her a million times over.

One of the most passionate teachers I have had at UMD; not only about the subject matter, but also about being a teacher in general.She really cares about her students,&wants them to take something away from her lectures.She tries to give everyone a fair chance at a good grade. Everyone who gave her a bad review clearly is just CRAZY. Go Maureen:)

She loves what she teaches, you have to respect her for the amount of work she puts into it. This isn't a class for everyone, but give it a shot, you may like it! She doesn't really use the textbook, uses outside sources. She is very easy to talk to and get help from. She is there for the student, great professor! A+

Babbles, disorganized in the highest, does not encourage philosophy as a matter of interpretation but rather as a Take if you want to memorize thoughts of philosophers past, not to actually learn how to DO philosophy.
Decent prof, Kinda hot for her age, Gets really excited, Her tests are really easy if you just google her topics. if you can figure them out. Didnt use textbook at all

Very nice. Extremely easy to lose concentration on her lectures, and i don't even have ADD. Only a dog can hear her speak, every class her voice reaches a higher pitch. Overall, she is a good professor.

Honestly this teacher is amazing! she really knows her subject matter and constantly goes out of her way to accomindate her students. Any class you have with her is sure to be worth your time, If you ever get the chance to take her class do it!

She's really funny, makes weird noises during lectures. Probably a rough class to get through if you're not interested in the material, but if you are this is a great professor to have. Don't over think the projects! Tests weren't bad at all.

As a religious studies minor, I love REL classes, but REL 201 with Eckert made me hate life. She is absolutely crazy, and makes insane squealing nosing during lecture, which is her babbling. Chooses favorites in class, tests on obscure material, and gives redic. assignments.

This class is one of the most boring, useless classes I've ever taken. The teacher is full of herself and thinks all this stuff is the most important information in the world. She truly believes she is a philosopher and takes this stupid crap VERY seriously.
Unless your obsessed, dont take this class. I hated it. She is very involved in philosophy and has a snobby way about her.

Eckhert is amazing! She loves teaching and is open to class discussion on the topics. There are two tests (quite easy if you study), a midterm and final project (both very fun to do), and homework on WebCT (the only hard part of the class.) I would take another class with her. =)

accomplished scholar, clearly in love with the subject.  Enjoys teaching without distraction.  Clear expectations.  Pay attention and have no trouble.  Cut class or sleep through it and your grades may suffer. Hint: Read the Plato, everything relates back to the Plato.  You may actually learn something about life if you engage your mind here.

Prof. was good but this has got to be the most useless, worthless class in the history of college. Nice women, but she acts like this B.S is the most important stuff in the world when its really just that: B.S

At first I disliked Eckert- I thought she seemed pretentious, but she grew on me quickly. Her enthusiasm for ancient philosophy is great- she does a lot to relate very dry material to modern day life (she compared the Road to Larissa to Mapquest). Great class!
GreaT :)
Interesting class and interesting professor.  Good class for both philosophy majors and non-philosophy majors.

She definately knows her stuff! She acts as though philosophy is the most difficult subject ever, haha. She is a very nice and personable lady, though, and laughs are definately guaranteed if you take her class. If you're a philo major, minor, or if you're simply fufulling a humanities requirement, take her class!

SHE takes attendance every class. her lectures are so boring but you need to listen to do well on tests and papers. if you dont mind listening to boring lectures  and like taking notes you will do fine in the class. i fell asleep all the time. her tests are pretty easy if you study the study guide the papers kind of sucked. i got a b+

A little disconcerting the first time you have her, just because she is so enthusiastic about her subject, but she definitely knows her stuff and is very good at sharing that knowledge.  I cannot comment on her knowledge of the moderns as I have only had her for the ancients, but in that period she is definitely unbeatable.

She is AWESOME!!! Take her if you get the chance!  Engaging discussions and laughing are bound to happen in her classes, and the assignments are not that hard to do. She challanges people to actually think on thier feet and you will be a better thinker for taking her class.
she finds ways to make some of the assignments easier by allowing many of the assignments to be creative.
not happy with this class, she thinks too much of herself and is all puffy and proud

She is very interesting and knows the material well. Despite the fact that its a deep subject she explains it fairly easily and thoroughly. Asks for many smaller assignments rather than a few huge ones. One of my all time favorite philosophy teachers.

As said before, very energetic! For such an intense topic she explains it with ease. Lots of essays to write, but they're fairly short and fairly easy. First test was very confusing but second was a breeze. Gives extra credit assignments too!
Very enthusiastic, great sense of humour, very involved in what she teaches.  Always willing to help, and is always willing to accomodate her students.  Definitely taking more classes with her.
Excellent teacher, and in my opinion very attractive!
Very enthusiastic and genuinely interested in teaching the subject to students. I loved her energy and general attitude. Very likable person. I highly reccommend any PHL course with her.

Fantastic teacher, very enthusiastic and thrives on debate. She managed to teach all the material thoroughly without rushing the class or straying from her energetic, relaxed personality. Awesome teacher, take a class with her.
She is a great persom with a great personality. I would recomend her to anyone.
Evans is a great person, and I love having conversations with her. Her class can get a bit stressful but she is very open to working with you if you are having any issues.

This site made me scared to take her because of the reviews. However Im glad I took her African American Literature class. It changed my life , I have a new outlook an understanding of the world around me. Shes so sweet if you put in the work she will go above and beyond to help you. The best Professor I have had. Made me a better student.

Graduate student nearing the end of this long journey and I will say there was one bump in the road... this professor. Over two hours every week we talk; the book of the week. At first I would read the books, all 300 pages or so. Until I realized it was never content driven, just pander to her lens and she'll accept it. Not clear, no concrete work.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I only had Professor Evans once, but I would take her again in a heart beat. Her class was very difficult, but it was worth it, because I came out a much stronger writer and critical thinker. If you have an idea for a paper, she will help you to expand it. She is always willing to give feedback. She's also not afraid to give you a bad grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Good professor, very kind and easy to understand. I had not taken an upper level English before so it was hard for me to write in this style but it was a great class

Most disorganized online course I've taken. Only two of the four in our group project contributed, one who didn't even admitted she "dropped the ball" - which lowered our grade significantly, which, in turn, lowered my GPA. VERY tough grader. If anyone gets an A in this class, you are awesome, but don't count on it.

Professor Evans is one of the best professors in the English department, she is so incredibly bright and has a passion for literature unlike any other. She's here to help create successful critical thinkers-not to tolerate slackers. She is someone who wants you to think long and think hard, and she's made me such a better student because of it.
Just don't do it to yourself. English is my passion and she made this passion conditional. Boring. Cliche. Vague. Semi-inappropriate. Oh, and the highest she gives is A-. Do I need to go on?
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: For an online summer course, she gave us an overwhelming amount of material to read, 3 texts plus a ton of other materials to read. I worked so hard in this course, and she did not care about students GPA's. This course was all subjective and theory, basically no right or wrong answers, and the grading is based on what she believes is right. Awful!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Evans is one of the sweetest, understanding, and smartest instructors I've had at UMD. She's so into what she teaches which makes for really interesting lectures. She has no problems meeting outside of class if you don't understand something and she always pushes you to do your best. The workload is tough but if you really wanna learn I'd recommend
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: She's a lovely person and knows the subject well; class discussions are interesting. That said, this class has a HUGE and unreasonable workload. Her grading criteria are ambiguous, and you won't be able to read her handwritten comments on your work. If it's the only challenging class you're taking, you may survive it. Good luck and godspeed.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: I have taken many literature classes and always done well.  I was never sure what Prof. Evans was looking for, and never gave her what she wanted.  Feedback was specific to the assignment, so it didn't really help much on the next one.  Her grading on discussions was unnecessarily hard.
Great professor. As a Continuing Ed. student I learned a lot from this class. Dr.E is very helpful. Her expectations are high, but is it a college class.Lots of great reading.

A very kind, understanding professor.  The classwork was overwhelming for a summer semester, wish I had taken it during the regular year.  The format was confusing, but this was the first time this online format was used.

LOVED Dr. Evans.  She is a professor who makes students think.  Class discussions were great and she was very interested in what her students had to stay.  She does give a lot of work, but if you are someone who wants to learn, then you'll love it.  Don't take her if you're just trying to fill a requirement and don't want to invest time in it.
Her online women's studies class is so confusing, I am a good student regularly but I am missing assignments because it is so disorganized and I cant keep up.

I have never been in a class that is so disorganized before. I am taking her online women's studies class and I never know when anything is due. It is so confusing (and I do not mean the information) I just cannot keep up with what we are supposed to do. And there is group work in an online class, that doesn't make sense to me.

The most disorganized professor I think I've ever had. Due dates changed about every day. She may be better in person though--I had her for an online class. I cannot wait to get out of this class and never take her again.

Dr. Evans was the BEST English teacher I have ever had here, and I have a heavy workload as it is.  She listened to the class about our concerns and when we had a mutual agreement that we were going too fast, she slowed it down and kept our best interests in mind.  She is smart, funny, and very kind.  I would definitely recommend her.

Prof. Evans is extremely enthusiastic and really nice. Her class mostly consists of discussions but to her credit she keeps your attention. She is very helpful if you ask her but is a tough grader, so I suggest bringing her a draft before handing in any papers.

Very nice lady BUT impossible to understand why she grades papers the way she does.  You can't learn what to change to up your grade.  Class is as fun as it can be, but trying to learn from your "mistakes" won't happen.

Her grading is all over the place. I think she doesn't even know what answer she wants at times. The assignments and work are easy but she doesn't accept your ideas. In-class discussions involve her stringing you along on ideas and when you finally have a strong example she'll shoot it down and replace it with her ideas.

Great professor, loves the subject and will do anything to help you out. may seem like she is looking for one answer to everything but she really just wants you to dig into the text to support what you are saying. and she's adorable

She is a good teacher.  She has high expectations and loves class discussions.  She gets very excited and could talk for hours, which she will try to keep you late.  Make sure you ask for help on all of her papers.  Overall she is a great etacher and I recommend her highly.

A lot of people like this lady.  She disqualifies many interpretations of fiction as she believes her's is the only correct one.  Therefore when you're in the lab spending 20 hours on a paper about your own ideas, it doesnt matter.  If you do take her, make sure you cater to her views.  One class was enough for me.

Nicest lady, she can be very helpful but she is a hard grader and demands a lot. Clearly knows her stuff but when grading papers its like she expects us to know everything as well as she does which is impossible.

I took her for 2 separate courses in 1 semester, which i don't recommend to ANYONE! I read almost 20 novels between the 2. Every class is a discussion and participation is most of your grade. She's nice and helpful, but I didn't have time to meet with her outside of class. The work is overwhelming, so just be prepared!

Sure is the nicest and sweetest lady you could meet. However, I put more work in her class than my other 4 writting classes combined. Tons of work for a 200 level class. Be prepared to work hard when you take this class English Majors since this class is Mandatory. Most of the books you read are interesting though.
Extremely difficult, time-consuming class, but Prof. Evans knows her stuff and is very passionate about lit. the reading is at times overwhelming, but well worth it.
if you have her drop it like its hot

The work is overwleming. She treats the class as if it's the only one her students are taking, and the workload over midterms (3 papers) and finals (another 3 papers!) is rediculous. I would not take a class with this teacher unless you are willing to commit long hours to writing boring papers. The books she assigns are interesting though.

Work is very time consuming and there is a lot of reading required. All the work seems to piled on during mid term time, so be prepared to do a lot of writing. I do have to say that the books for the class were very interesting to read.

I have never dropped a class in my life and i had to drop this one. Prof. Evans is a sweet lady, but the class is impossible if you wanna have a life. If you only had one class the workload wouldn't be too bad. I wouldn't recommend this for full time students.

Prof Evans is really nice and friendly, however she is hard. She requires a lot of work but she wants you to do well. She is willing to help you at any time, but just be aware she does require a lot of reading from her students. The work is time-consuming. She is really passionate about what she teaches.
She sucks! Very unreasonable teacher and when she says something is ok she means its a C-. I will never take a class with this professor again. She really doesn't know how to teach.
The reading was overwhelming at times, but she's a fine teacher and very reasonable. The presentation topics she asked for were a little on the obscure side, though.
Professor Evans is awesome. She's very understanding, helpful, and extremely passionate about literature. Highly recommend her classes :)
No Comments

Firas is one of the best professors at the entire uni. He makes modern references that gets the lecture laughing, is down to earth, but still keeps you focused on practices that are important to real-world software engineering. He is caring, flexible, and teaches well. W simpsons fan
Was very good working with the Professor on my Masters project. Allowed for independent research with helpful suggestions.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Good professor.

Great professor, very funny guy and made the class fun. There were 2 midterms and the final being a group presentation and term paper. He made it very clear on what to expect on these and gave out multiple opportunities for extra credit. Overall made the class interesting, was easy to communicate with, and wanted his students to do well.
Senior Capstone is great with Professor Khatib. Make sure you are in class for the Kahoots, and please do your fair share of work in your group. Very easy class, just put time into your project!
Good professor, easy to follow lectures, and easy course load. Also a silly guy.
Great guy not too bad a class try to pay attention no grinding video games too much and you will be okay.
He can be funny but sometimes doesn't feel like his mind is focused on the class. He reads right off the slides verbatim.

Great professor. Good at explain his subject and makes class somehow fun and enjoyable to be there. Best part is that you actually do learn. You still need to read and its easy to fall behind but thankfully he is very fair and understanding professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: I really liked him as a professor, and he made a class that was extremely boring, actually somewhat interesting. Overall I'm glade I had him. It almost makes me want to take the game design classes that he has.
Funny yet demanding

One of the best professors in the CIS department next to Clinton Rogers. Makes class fun and makes you actually want to go. The topic of the class is kind of boring but he makes it so much more fun to learn and really cares about his students. Will always help you if needed and explains topics very well and makes sure you understand everything.
Made lectures fun and interesting. Wants you to do well and will be there for any help. Wish all professors were like this.
Awesome dude. Funny and really cares about what he does

EXCELLENT CIS professor. He is extremely helpful and shows great interest in the subject. He really takes his time to make sure students understand everything during his lectures. If you need help, he will be in his office for you. Definitely NOT a boring teacher. I wished all my CIS professors were like him.
Kinda "COOL" professor.  Teaches well and is always at his office when students need him..
She is genuinely kind and caring. Her class is not hard if you try, just don't fall behind. It was super easy and I actually had fun.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: such a sweetheart. if you do the assignments and show up to class you will pass with an A or higher. got 90 or higher on all of the MA's. she can get a little political tho. half of the class used ai for their assignments and she never noticed or just didn't say anything…..ironically the course was about ai..super nice i would take her class again
Fair was a great first-year teacher, very understanding, and if you keep up with the work, you will be completely fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Prof Fair is not bad but she did make a few students feel like that don't belong in her class a few times. She also gives a lot of hw so get prepared to do a lot. She also grades MA projects really strictly it's impossible to get an 95+ on those projects. But  overall the class might not be bad but the Prof Fair is not as nice.

She's such a sweetheart, she cares a lot about her students and their success, she'll always check in on you, see how you're doing. Her class is very hands on and interactive, she is a little loud but besides that- she's very sweet and very funny. Pleasure to have as a teacher.

We didn't have a textbook but we read two books that you have to get but they are fairly cheap. You have to actually read it if you are going to understand the material and her class was very easy and the way she teaches! You also have to think outside the box when it comes to material and not be so straightforward. A great professor overall!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: I went through a really tough time personally when I took this course. Professor Fair generously accommodated me, and I felt like she was in my corner, even though she scared the life out of me. Her assignments were really hard for me personally because they were about joining clubs and going to social events, which I struggle very badly with.

She did an absolutely amazing job at being the worst professor I have ever taken by far. She grades your writing based on if your opinion is similar to hers and not if it is actually good. (Write what you think she will like). She acts as if she is better than everyone. Other than that she is very kind, BUT DONT LET THAT FOOL YOU!!!!!!!!

professor fair is so sweet and kind. she is very clear on her expectations. if she notices that you are slacking, she reaches out to you with resources and sees if there's anything that she can do to help. it is clear that she wants her students to pass.

She's very sweet, she even emailed me at 5pm to help send me evidence for one of her assignments. She did make it important to come to class and she does a lot of group work but its very helpful. Can't really complain about her at all. Very clear instructions on everything too

Professor Fair was the most welcoming teacher I had my first semester. If you need to miss class for any reason, she will allow you the opportunity to make it up. She is extremely understanding, and I will be taking another class from her again due to her nature. Group work is fairly common, but never dreadful.

Prof. Fair is very easy if you're willing to put in the work. Homework is due every class period but she stated that she will grade most work (such as discussion posts) based on if you tried, not necessarily by the content/quality of your work. She is very accommodating but only if you're willing to come to every class and put in effort.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: She is very organized and interesting.

Professor Fair is by far one of my favorite professors on campus. She is well organized and builds upon each lesson to create the overall final assignment. There are three major assignments in both of her 101 & 102 classes, and no final exams. She tends to be slightly heavy on the reading, but it all connects. I wish I could take her all 4 years.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Amazing professor!!! She is so sweet and caring!! The first half of the semester is hw heavy but it all builds for an easy second half. She wants everyone to succeed and is willing to work with you in every way possible. 10/10 would recommend. p.s. I have never liked English but she is amazing!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Meghan Fair is hands down one of the best professors I've ever had. She's not super easy, but she helps you through the hard assignments. She is extremely accessible outside of class, and reaches out to students even after they've completed her class. Always trying to give feedback and help you along. You would have to try to fail her class.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring

reviews.comment: In an assignment, I briefly mentioned a personal trauma I've gone through. Professor Fair found me outside of class, asked to sit down and talk, and went on to ask me MULTIPLE extremely personal questions not only about my childhood, but about the specific details of the trauma I suffered. Extremely unprofessional and harmful towards students.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Awesome Professor! Lenient on due dates (passed stuff in two weeks late and still got good grades) Can only miss two lectures before it affects your grade.

Ever so recently, Fair has become my favorite teacher. Transitioning from ENL101, 102 is expected to be harder, and yes, it is harder, but Fair really alleviates that imo. I was in the honors section, and there were only 7 of us in that class. She really shows that she cares about her students, has a great personality, and is very respectful!

A lot of reading and busy work at the beginning of the semester but its all to make the class easier towards the end. Professor Fair very clearly cares about her students success outside and inside the classroom. A truly amazing professor.
She's an organized professor. ALTHOUGH, SHE ASSIGNS TOO MUCH HOMEWORK FOR NO REASON, WOULD NOT TAKE HER CLASS AGAIN.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Participation matters

reviews.comment: She was very nice and obviously cares about her students, but I also think she requires way too much from her students. She gives mountains of homework every night which proved to be too much to handle with my other courses. Her workload requires almost all of your time and attention making it impossible do all of the work for all of your classes.
Great teacher overall. She was one of the first people to grab my attention and keep me hooked the entire class on my first day of college.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Gives good feedback--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Professor Fair was one of the best English teachers Ive ever had, and thats saying a lot since I have actually had some very good English teachers. You can tell how much she loves what she does, and how hard she works on the class. If you do the work, and do it well you can most certainly get an A. Highly recommend taking her!
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: She's a queen. She's definitely not a professor where you can just do nothing and manage to get an A (imo) but she is so helpful and incredible. The class is challenging at times but she was always available for help and did her best to explain each paper. She's also has a great sense of humor and is super down to earth.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious

reviews.comment: Cares a lot about her students and the quality of their work, she really wants to help you write well and be proud of your work. I felt like this class was a little too hand-holdy for a college course, but some people might like that, especially freshmen.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Meghan is by far the best professor I've had in my 2 years at UMassD. She absolutely expects you to work hard for your grade, and she can be a bit homework heavy. However she gives you all the tools and opportunities to excel, and she is extremely accessible outside of class room hours. Her class sets a strong foundation for all undergrad writing.
She is extremely serious about her class. If you are not prepared for a mountain of work that coincides with quality, do not and i shall repeat, do not take this class.
Very unorganized, snobby. Not clear on directions

Professor Fair is the English teacher I've ever had. She is very understanding about the workload of other classes you are taking, and if you speak with her she will accommodate you. If you put in the effort and show up to all classes you will get an A. Honestly just a funny, good hearted person. I regret not taking her class for 102.

She is very helpful, but as noted above, she takes her class VERY seriously. She's a great teacher, she really cares about her students. Also, she'll always keep you laughing while staying on topic. She's wonderful.

She takes her classes very seriously and expects her students to do the work, even if you're not an English major. The way she constructs her classes are very useful and she promotes student-group work to help with assignments. She can be a tough grader, but she's ALWAYS available for extra help when you need her, just make an appointment.

Doesn't assign much homework, but when she does she expects really high quality work. Especially with essays she can be a tough grader. However you can tell she really cares about her job and is extremely helpful and available to students for questions. Somewhat of a difficult class, but I would still recommend her.

Class was easy but only 3 tests as a grade for the most part, which are all on the computer. There are discussion boards for a topic in the news for each class, which were super easy. The class isnt to bad, but he can be difficult to understand
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He just rambles all of class and loses track of what he's actually supposed to be talking about. He literally just reads his posted lecture notes for the entire lecture. Attendance is mandatory but shouldn't be because I could teach myself. Only 3 exams. Lowkey hard. Lowkey very annoying because if you don't do well you're screwed.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: His quizzes are extremely long and are based off of MULTIPLE chapters at once and the study guide he gives you is absolutely no help. I emailed him multiple times I never got a reply and had to speak to him after class multiple times in person and each time he brushed my concerns for my grade off multiple times. Switch out while you can.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Never and I mean never take his class. His lectures are very long and many readings are required for a basic class and your grades are based off of 3 tests and 3 tests only and if you slightly do bad on 1 quiz your grade is basically tanked for the rest of the semester and he does absolutely no extra credit assignments so you can't recover either.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: had him as my professor for my Pol 101 class and specifically requested for him to be my PSC 151 professor he's so relatable and just a good teacher and wont stretch the time out hes just a good guy with good moral's.
Grades depend heavily on three exams which have specific questions based on the book (usually based on random statistics)

I've had Prof. Fobanjong for three online classes in the last year - PSC101, PSC306, and PSC342. His courses mainly consist of one discussion board post and two replies, weekly quizzes, and three exams. I've found the course load in each of his classes to be generally manageable. He's a super easy going, friendly guy.

Grade only consists of 3 tests and attendance. He doesn't allow retakes or any way to boost your grade up. "you get what you get". when you reach out to him for help he just directs you to the tutoring center or if you aren't confident that you will pass he tells you to drop the course. I don't take him if you have any form of testing anxiety.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This is the hardest professor I've ever been through. Every chapter quiz and three exams, some questions aren't strick answer from the text. They try to tricky and confuse you that you will struggle like "both A and B" or  "None above." The grade mock how poor your English reading and understanding is!
Class was just a lecture and extremely boring but it was basically a study hall. He is very nice and passionate about what he is teaching but it is hard for him to engage the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Fobanjong is simple in his expectations. You're graded on 3 exams (90%) with the rest for participation. So, basically it's your exam average plus a whole letter grade (assuming you actively participate). It's a good deal, no surprises on tests. He reads straight from the word doc which is boring, but everything you need for the exams is there.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Love dr fobanjong, essay grading can be tough but overall this dude is funny,caring, and a wonderful professor. Ignore the haters about his accent, it adds to the comedy when he starts role playing mid lecture
amazing professor!

He knows a lot about the world of politics. The tests are hard but he curves the grade so no one fails unless you don't come to class. He never gives homework, the only grades are tests and attendance. He's caring and wants every student to pass. He also emails study guides before each test. There's a textbook but he gives you the notes in class.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Although he is a nice guy, he is impossible to understand. You are grades on four tests and they often include things he never said. You will pass but you will never be able to get a 100. Once he gave us the notes and I still didn't have all the answers to the questions. He's a nice guy, but if you prov him wrong he will go on a ten minute tangent
reviews.rating_tags: TEST HEAVY--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--Tests are tough

reviews.comment: I've had worse professors. He is hard to understand and for PSC 151 seems like he's more interested in convincing everyone to study comparative politics than he is actually teaching it. Grade was based on 4 test grades and nothing else. You HAVE to read your book. Extremely lecture heavy and EXTREMELY boring.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Great professor. Passionate. Really just wants students to learn the material. If you demonstrate that, he's very reasonable.

I've had Professor Fobanjong a few times over the course of my career at UMD and he's a great professor and a great guy! The classes are easy as long as you pay attention, and even if you don't he usually gives you a study guide so you can do well on the tests. Overall all of his classes were great and I learned a lot!
Really cool professor. Easiest B+ I ever got. Class drags a little but as long as you show your face most of the time and he knows your name he will cut you a lot of slack.

The class drags on and he is really hard to understand. He gives you really detailed study guides which makes you think the tests will be easy, but they're not because its pretty much a definite that if its on the study guide, its NOT on the test.

SUCH a nice guy!  he is a little hard to understand, but just ask to repeat it and he will.  2 exams, and usually a paper, but my class didn't have to do it.  we had to bring in a news story every day, no biggie.  the exams are tough, but not impossible.  show up & take notes, he gives you attendance points.  great prof.
Nice and enthusiastic, hard to understand and more often than not boring, but a good guy overall, I didn't get my community service sheet signed but he still gave me a B-
I love this guy, and if you don't understand what he says, just ask him to repeat, he won't mind it.  i take every poli sci that i can with him.  he's not that hard as long as u come to class.
He can be hard to understand sometimes with his broken english but he gets his points out there well with great examples. He has a good sense of humor and seems to enjoy what he does.
Easy class, but it is difficult to understand him @ times.

I recently took one of his classes and he seems to be more demanding from his students than in the past. It's possibly because of pressure from the department wanting him to be a harder grader since everyone seems to get good grades in his classes.
Awesome teacher....knows his stuff..test are easy as long as you study....no trick questions....
never understood a word he said.....
Awesome guy, knows his stuff, very helpful after class. VERY FAIR in grading. Just go to his class everyday, and be nice. He is very easy to get along with and a funny guy! And i'm a comp sci major.
excellent teacher..really knows his stuff

He might not be the best professor teaching in class but he cares for his students and if you have a problem he will take the time to help you. He also has a lot of knowledge he is willing to share with those who take the time to read the assigned reading

His class was fairly easy for a 300 level, but I still learend a lot more from it than many other classes.Just talk to him after class or during office hours if you have a problem. he's an easy grader on papers and provides good study guides for exams.

I had one of his seminars, he teaches it like a 101 and lectures the whole time.  He doesn't seem to understand the concept and importance of class discussions and seminars.  Also, it is his way or the highway when it comes to the right answer.
Knows his stuff.
No Comments
he makes no sense and has a super thick accent
very boring

I really enjoyed starting off my college career in this class. It was my first business class and it gave me useful information in laying the foundation of understanding all things business. The professor was approachable and helpful. When I had questions or wanted to know more, she patiently talked with me even well after class was over.

I was having a tough time in this class, especially with the lab work. Prof Gomes worked with me and encouraged me to get back on track. There are a lot of different assignments, but her syllabus/schedule is really detailed, so you know exactly what to expect. She gives real-life examples in class, which makes the material more interesting.

decent class does give a lot of homework and if you need help she won't help you just makes you talk to the student helper who wasn't very helpful but the work is doable and tests are all online open book. structured so if you just do the work you will pass fine.
Super easy homework and assignments. Easy pass!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: MIS 101 covers a lot of different areas of business. The class and professor helped me to figure out what I was most interested in choosing for my major. There are a lot of different types of assignments, so time management is really important so you don't fall behind. Professor is knowledgeable and really cares about her students.

Great class...fun even! The professor's lectures were interesting and relatable. The class & her teaching style helped me gain an inside track on organizational leadership. I learned how to improve on my own skills to better relate to people in a team setting. The personality assessment helped me to better understand my strengths/weaknesses.

I loved taking MIS with Prof. Fernandes. Every class was engaging and the professor was super kind and understanding to all students. You do have one group project in the class but that is the only small downside. I would highly recommend this professor!

Professor Fernandes is a very respectful and understanding professor. At times this class will challenge you with the amount of homework and the challenge of some of the assignments. Tests, quizzes, group presentations, are all part of this class.

She is a nice teacher and only gets mad if you use your phone in class. You will do fine in the class as long as you stay on top of the Charlton reading. I recommend checking the syllabus a lot because a lot of work is assigned in the beginning of the semester that you will forget about

As long as you stay on top of things like reading the Charlton Book, writing the reflection paper, and weekly assignments it is an easy class. I do not recommend taking it at 8am lol, but it is a great class, she is so sweet, gives great feedback, and just an overall gem!
I almost changed my major just so I could have her again. I absolutely LOVE her.
Great Teacher, Easy Introduction to Business
Super nice lady and willing to help out when there is any trouble. Exams were really easy.
Loved her!

Knowledgeable, easy to talk to, and offers good feedback. She'll go the extra mile, you just have to make a real effort. There's lots of different assignments (group project, lab work, 2 exams, a paper, etc). Lots of material to cover, but she goes over the main points and tries to make it interesting. Would definitely take a class with her again.

Professor is a great teacher. The course is note heavy but very informative. She has a good communication with students and public speaking. She explains things very well. I like the professor more so than the curriculum she has to teach.
Great class. Very Easy to understand and super easy grading.

This was the easiest and best class I've taken! She gives you homework weekly due every Sunday that is all! You get 1 book report, 2 exams, and 1 group project that you will have to present. All you need mostly is the book honestly, but she does take attendance. So I suggest go and sit and do nothing draw a picture just to get the attendance grade!
great class , very helpful, and wow she is wicked hot

The class is a requirement so it's pretty basic stuff, but definitely lots to know/remember. I liked that she was open to student perspective. Appealed to different kins of learners by using multiple choice tests, open book, essay, and project presentations. She made a routine class fun and interesting.

Ienjoyed this class, and found Mr. Fields to be a good professor, he was funny and was available after class if help was needed on the topic explained in class. He re-explained more difficult topics, and reviewed most readings posted in Canvas during class. Make sure to come prepared to take notes in class!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Fields is a great professor! Attendance was not mandatory, but you need to come to class to make sure you do well on the assignments. He is very understanding and hilarious. The class was full of great conversations, and his laugh is extremely contagious. Assignments were simple, just make sure you keep up with the readings

I liked Prof Fields as a person but disliked him as a professor. He gave us so many readings to do and based our whole grade on just a few assignments. I ended up dropping the class because I wasn't able to see what he was writing on the board most of the time but there were no more open seats in the class. I REALLY wanted to like this class. :(
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: professor fields is very rude, makes his introduction class feel way harder then it needs to be. just very unhelpful not a good teacher at all
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This professor is very unorganized, walks into to class and for 2 minutes you would know nothing of what's going on.Also the tests is  difficult since he does not give a study guide.
Very good lecturer and explains complicated material in a simple and fun way. You could probably get a better grade than me if you actually did the in class readings. Work load is pretty light.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Professor Fields seems overall like a good person, just not a good professor. Doesn't answer emails, only meets in person once a week (online communication feels a lot more important only being in class once a week), tons of writing, TONS of reading. Overall not super friendly and I feel that I did not learn much, just had a lot of stress.

where do i start. AVOID AT ALL COSTS. nice and genuine guy, but that doesn't matter in the long run. you must do every assignment or you will fail, and the assignments are analyzing heavy text from people in the 1400s. Be prepared for thick paperwork. For a 200 level class this was harder than my 2 500 level classes combined.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Fields rarely answered my emails and passing the course depending on if you did literally every assignment if you missed something you failed. group tutorials via zoom but no one in my group participated and I got penalized for it
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Group projects--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Prof Fields seems like a great guy however I took this class while I had a lot going on and didn't have time for it. Didn't do well in his class but he gives multiple attempts at homework, extra credit and extension of due dates. The class was also asynchronous which I didn't like but he seems to care about his students success.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: this class wouldn't have been as bad if it were in person i think. teaching yourself this material is difficult at best
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Tough grader

reviews.comment: i took this online during rona and he was such a hard grader for not being in person. he has strict guidelines for his one every few weeks required zoom meetings, which is stupid because were supposed to be fully remote. we did not use the textbook at all and the reading is a LOT. dont take if you have a choice. doesnt answer emails either
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: This class is heavily based on discussions and there are no exams. There are two projects and one group project and each one has a paper that corresponds with it. Not a hard class if you are willing to do work and he is a pretty laid back guy. Show up to class and ask your questions if you have any because he does not answer emails.
Can be kind of a hard class if you arent willing to put the work in but its a very interesting class and Professor Fields makes the concepts easy to understand.
AVOOOOOOOOOOOOOOID. He does not answer emails
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Fields is a good professor. The lectures and explanations are hard to follow sometimes, needing a little more research outside of class if your mind does not already grasp the fundamentals of philosophy. Will shut down going off topic or controversial ideas to stay on topic. He also puts up practice exams which really saved me. Just do the readings
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: There is a lot of homework but once you get the hang of it, it is easy. He uses a program called mindtap. IMO, he doesn't teach, this program "teaches" he sort of just moderates the online class, he isnt easy to get ahold of either. He is not responsive to emails, Final made no sense, had to leave 14 ?? blank. Like I said, its a program, not him.

PHL 101 is an easy class if you go and take notes (you don't even have to, as long as you listen) his tests were mostly the practice exams plus a few extra questions. It's not hard and he explains material ehh ok. No final or midterm, 2 group projects. He's really funny and understanding. Isn't too quick with getting grades back.

Very disorganized and doesn't follow the syllabus at all, he blames us for being off track because we ask questions. When giving lectures he's all over the place and seems to jump from one topic to another. This class has to be self taught if you want to pass. Don't recommend at all if you're taking his class for anything more than credit.
Great professor if you study and care about the material. Its not an easy A but its very interesting.

Had him for an online class in the Winter session. Very unorganized. Gives quizzes and tests, with a reasonable amount of reading. Discussion boards are graded pretty rough. If you ask for explanations of things you get them though. Might be better in person, but I don't recommend for an online class.

Fields class is an easy A if you put some effort. He dose take attendance but you can miss 3 classes without it effecting your  grade.  There are  really short 2 exams but they are the same exact questions as the practice exam.  The readings are short and online, a few easy one line quizzes, occasional  discussion board writings &  2 pretentions.

Professor Field's class is not one you take if you want an easy A. I took 2 classes with him and had to work hard to get an A- and an A. Many other students got grades that were B or below. If you are willing to work you will have a great experience. He is extremely intelligent and is willing to help students who help themselves.

Homework is actually read and graded and if you don't get better than 75% then it's a zero. He allows you to redo a couple homework assignments but it is difficult to get an A in his class.
