His exams are poorly set up. He will tell you the test is on chapters 1-3 and only chapter one is on it. So you never know what chapter it will be on.

Fields is an amazing professor and insanely brilliant. The negative comments about him are simply due to the fact that he has high expectations of his students. Hence, if your taking his class be ready to rise to that level. If you do you will learn incredible amounts from him.

fields was the absolute WORST professor I have EVER had! Make sure you do NOT take him!!!!..... everyone in the class was failing and the average quiz score was a 30% and the amount of reading is more like a 300 level!
Is not clear in expectations. Laughs at students who do not answer questions the way he would. Very rude and degrading. Every class he goes off topic.  Hands down the worst teacher I've ever had.
Worst professor I've ever had at UMD.  Comes off as condescending. He has these mannerisms (scratching head, chin)that make him look like hes trying to emulate Plato or Aristotle. Avoid him.

really hard. all about memorization. gets off track a lot. isn't very helpful. horrible at getting back to you. if you take this class, you must put a lot of time to the side or you will fail. everything is based on iclicker tests/quizzes. these are always extremely difficult.
Great Professor, Epistemology is an extremely easy and interesting class, riiiiiiiight?

He is a nice guy. He expects you to do your work, pay attention, and study if you want a good grade. He doesn't clarify difficult points at all. Never attempts to make information interesting. SNOOZE FEST!

He comes off as a very intimidating Professor from all the horror stories everyone tells. He honestly is not that bad, he expects you to do your work and study which really is not too much to ask for. Tests are just re-iterating class notes. Do the work and study and you'll be fine. He is really clear and helpful and also funny in a crude way.

Excellent professor. I was concerned having learned that he was "evil," but the truth is he expects you to TRY, and for some people that is simply asking too much. If you are a serious student and ask questions, you will be fine.

This class is deff not an intro course with him. He is awful on tests grading and quizes are useless. He isn't good at helping and laughs when you answer questions wrong... ddddooooo not take for intro!!!!

This was an extremely hard course. Show up to every class, there are iclicker quizzes. Tests are hard, no matter how much you study. It is probably impossible to get an A in his class. He is a harsh grader as well. Look around at the beginning of the semester, the class won't even be half as full by the time finals roll around.
Warning! Most challenging course I've had. Defiantly shouldn't qualify as just a 101. Pick a different prof.
Studied hard for exams and attended class everyday and still had to drop the class because I wasn't going to pass. Much to difficult to be a 101 class

I have taken Prof Fields for PHL 101, Metaphysics, and Ethics.  I will be taking him again for PHL seminar and theory of knowledge. Contrary to popular beleif, he isn't impossible.  He is challenging but fair.  Show up, take GOOD notes, and study appropriately and you will do well. I haven't gotten less than an A in any of his class so far.
He says the word "Right?!" after every sentence lol.
Readings are rough and tests are tough, but he warns you in the syllabus. As a guy, he's cool and helpful, has a weird laugh and has a twisted sense of humor but this class was good
Cool guy

Tough teacher with tough material. Even when you sincerely try the exams and material are too out-there to understand and explain how HE wants on the written part of exams. So if you have a general idea but don't know the key words he wants to see, good luck. Definitely study.

The class is hard if you have a hard time remembering things. He gives good notes and the iClicker quizzes are open notebook and he lets you ask people for help. The exams are pretty hard but if you write as much as you know, you'll do okay. He does have a funny laugh but he's a cool guy. I'd take the class again.

attedance is mandatory, u have to buy an iclicker for a quiz everday thats how he takes attedance. lots of theories and definitions to remember, if u study youll do fine on the exams, they are straight forward. 3 exams, no final. lots of readings and philosophers, useful class though. he has a weird laugh, but not a bad professor

I advise anyone who's taking this class to READ THE MATERIAL. If you read what is assigned, you will do well. It's a lot. You're tested on your ability to recall what you read. Even if you understand it, you'll do poorly if you don't read. I thought I was doing horribly, but I started reading at the end and got an A. He drops 2 quizzes and 1 test.

Wicked hard professor. make sure you do really well on the online quizzes because they will save your grade. also do really well on group project. Tests are wicked hard. you have to study and have to go to class.

Basically, you have to study for you life!!!! Have study groups and I gurantee you will get an A. Other than that... you cannot lack off in his class. I had him for ethics and my friends and I all studied together and all of us got A's.

Fields is a great teacher, but for a 101 class this was extremely difficult. Tests were in 2 parts, all writing, and were very hard. Don't recommend for a 101 class but would recommend for a higher level class, really knows his stuff.

Nice guy, but i hate the way he teaches. His lectures could be interesting if you are passionate in philosophy. I had to dropp the class because the exams and quizzes were too hard! He takes really long on giving tests grades back.  He also takes long on answering e-mails. If you aren't into PHL don't take this course, it will be pointless.

Very passionate about the subject matter, but the way he administers quizzes and exams is absurdly difficult for a history of philosophy class. You'll enjoy the lectures if you like philosophy discussions, but the quizzes are super hard. All complaints aside, he's a nice guy who is very helpful when he can be.

dont ever take theroies of the mind.  i thought i could do it after all im pretty smart, some say i try to hard but when it come to this class with this teacher...i feel like im getting no where.  The professor is a jerk off. the first day of class i thought he was a graduate student.  Tests at HARD. i studied all night and still failed. DONT TAKE
love him. seriously.

I absolutely hate the subject matter, but I will say that Fields is a nice and interesting professor. I just wish I felt like I was learning something. In his class information goes in one ear and out the other; even though, I'm paying attention and taking a lot of notes.

the only thing we had to do in this class was write one 3-5 page paper that we worked on periodically from start to finish of class, he kept it interesting even though he never tested on any of the subject matter

At first he seemed like he was awful.  Study the study guides he provides and you will be okay.  I wouldnt recommend taking this class if you have an option but it was ok.  He was pretty nice towards the end of the sememster and he ended up being pretty lenient with grades.  All in all he is ok.
Pretty ill teacher. He is really fun and never lets the class get boring. I think he is the best philosohpy techer in Umass.

awesome teacher, most people find the subject uninteresting but if you can stand philosophy you definately want to take this teacher. He assigns a group project which is kind of a hassle but it isn't that bad
worst teacher ever
No Comments

She's a nice person. Very chill about things. Show up on days where she's presenting something new, but I don't think she really cares. Also she didn't care what time we leaved the class. Just get you're work done.

Disorganized, so many technical issues.  I will say as long as you pay attention, take notes, and do the homework the class is incredibly easy.  Quizzes open note, they tell you what to take notes on.  People saying we never learnt stuff on the quiz just don't pay attention or do homework.  CITE FOR THE 5 SENTANCE QUESTIONS, they forgot to tell us

Felt very unorganized.  I feel like I learnt more from students at some points.  She is a nice person, just not my favorite professor.  Our class had to teach her some things, I taught her photoshop things even though that class was my first time using it...

ARH102 was a shared class between Anna Dempsey & Laura. Laura is a little difficult to get a hold of in a timely manner, and there was a lot of disorganization with this class before break. The tests are very hard, with a study guide that didn't seem to help. I struggled a lot for the first two-ish months of class before they took feedback from us

Laura was one of two professors who taught this class and I thought she did an amazing job. She listens to feedback from her students and takes it into consideration to improve the class. She makes the lectures as interesting as they can be in art history and makes sure students are prepared and understand the information.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Laura helped me through school, and accepted that I may have different struggles in class with my mental illness. She provides step-by-step examples, and offers help in and out of class.

Laura is a really sweet person and a very dedicated teacher. However, don't let that fool you into thinking you don't have to work hard. She can be a tough grader, but you can always redo assignments, so its really not that big a deal.
Very layback, fun, and simple to the point assignments. Learn a good amount.

Laura is one of the rare professors who is amazing at what she does, but is still able to teach and consider the experience level of her students.  She cares about typography (moreover, GOOD typography) and expects you to care just as much about the work you do.  If you don't try, she'll know -- and then you're in trouble.
Laura is a great design teacher and knows what she is talking about and makes class very enjoyable to be in. She also pushes your  work to make it the best that it can be.

Teachers in a relaxed and non-stressful environment.  Lenient but to a healthy degree, allows you to put extra time into improving the quality of your work without slacking off. You feel at ease around her.
No Comments
Dr. Fisher is extremely supportive and caring. He will go the extra mile to help students. Physics is a difficult class by nature. Be prepared to work hard.

Great professor and overall great guy. Gives tons of feedback and will let you make test corrections to earn back half the points you lost. Make sure you pay attention in lecture and his explanations will help tremendously. Do not be afraid to ask questions either, as he will gladly help and give you a detailed yet simple explanation.

This man deserves a raise,....I have no idea what they pay him but he deserves it. He is the best and easiest professor you will ever have. He will help you any time anyway and its one of the classes that is one in a lifetime.

Professor Fisher is a very talented lecturer and has incredible experience in his field. He allows you to do corrections on your tests for half credit but he also gives similar review tests prior to any exams. The lack of hands on application during powerpoint lectures made the homework a lot more herculean than it had to. Otherwise, a great class.
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Sooo the struggle is real, right? I was really stressed out about this class but the prof was great. Extra creds, hw online. Labs are easy and boost your grade.

honestly I did not want to major in science. i was a music major. but his classes were so good that i decided to major in science. he also works with his students outside the classroom. spends lots f time teachibg you how to become a real scientist. i am so glad he is at umd.
the best prof at UMD I have had. Cares a lot about students and i very generous with his time outside the classroom.

Bob really is a fantastic Prof when going through his Astrophysics pipeline. It is his main area of research, and the shared interest is definitely a plus. This is a class that you take only if you want to take it. And because of this, everyone in the class wants to be here. Only runs every 2 years, so get in on it ASAP.

Very caring professor and a good lecturer. He does a very good job making sure you learn the material and helping everyone, for a 100 person class. Would defiantly recommend him and he is one of the best professors in the physics department.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Would take again

reviews.comment: had to take physics because of my engineering major this instructor was great
AFAIK the best physics prof here. You get to redo tests and make up one lab. Peace out.

His lectures are long and boring, but if you go to him for extra help he is helpful. The iclicker questions count for more then they should and are worded weird. He allows test corrections for half credit back which is great.  The homework counts about the same as labs which isn't good because the online homework penalizes you for wrong answers.
This class was really helpful to me. I am glad I took it.
Professor Fisher is one who cares a lot about his students. I like working with him.

He is a great teacher who makes it easy to learn. Pay attention on the clicker questions and take them seriously because they are an easy way to boost your grade. The class was so easy the only reason to go is to answer the clicker questions. He allows test corrections which helps a lot. Also, do homework! It helps you prepare for the tests.
Look your gonna have to take this course, and this was the best prof to take it from. Show up, do the clicker thing, do your homework. Great prof that got me ready for my other classes.

Great teacher. The material can be tough and he embraces the idea of preparing you for the real world. He wants you to understand and look towards physics with a critical eye but this generally means that you're going to have to work hard and truly  learn the material and connect the dots. I spent many hours studying outside of class.

Worst professor I've had at the University.  He knows his stuff but doesn't really like to share.  Barely teaches. You end up paying to take the class in which you just end up teaching yourself anyways.  Uses online homework that doesn't tell you the answers unless you get it 100% correct.  Not my choice for a professor. Would not recommend.
Pretty good, it's not gonna be a cakewalk though. Really into in-class clicker quizzes.
great teacher take him if you have the choice

If you have taken physics you will likely be fine, but if you are a first timer stay away. Way to much WHY and not enough HOW. A lot of self teaching which is very hard when you have no background in the subject. Also, a lot of... What do you think is the answer to this hard question? No, wrong, guess again, nope, try again. Please just teach us!
great teAcherre
God bless respected prof for helping me towards my new career
good class
Enjoyed the class. Not easy to get A or B, but overall very good.
Worst professor I've ever had in my entire life. doesn't seem to know what he's talking about. Drowns on and on about nothing during lectures.
Excellent teacher. VERY knowledgeable. He knows about almost everything in the world you can imagine. I highly recommend his classes.
nice professor. Knows what he's  talkin about and is very kind. I love the images... I reend th class
great teacher and individual. cares about students' success.
Dr. Fisher is a really boring prof and a horrible teacher. Classes are painful. Lots of pretty pictures but no content.  i leanred nothing.
this was a pretty cool class about the sky n stuff. not too much work. prof was engagd.

Is clearly interested and well educated on the topic. Homework assignments count for more than tests and they are all online and pretty straight forward. Test content and questions are very similar to homework assignments which is great. Boring subject for me personally but decent professor.
Rob Fisher is great! Edge of my seat every class. He's extremely smart but is able to make physics comprehensible for those less familiar. Enthusiastic and helpful if approached!

This is my fourth semester. This professor is the 2nd worst I have ever had. He is very nice but the class is incredibly boring to the point where you are just spacing out when he speaks. It is a daily powerpoint lecture with no class interaction. Absolutely awful. The book was a waste also.
Dr. Fisher is the worst professor I have ever had.

Professor Fisher is  very good professor. As a college student you have the responsibility to do your part and that means study, do the homework and go to him or tutoring if you need extra help. His office hours are very helpful and class time he goes out of his way to make sure you understand the material. If you do your part you will do well.
I don't like this class
Horrible class. Awful professor.
Dont Spend money on textbooks Instead spend Time talking with Robert Fisher
He is the Best professor I had till now.
Patient, Very Intelligent - No one word can describe him.

This class was like taking high school physics all over again. I thought it was going to be more challenging. If you want an easy A - Dr. Fisher is the way to go!!! His PowerPoint lectures are lame, especially the owl that's always at the beginning. The whole "Discussion Time" and clickers were counterproductive. I didn't learn anything new.

he is an amazing professor, outstanding educator with deep expertise in high-performance computing. His classes are well-structured, starting with theory and followed by hands-on labs. He and the TA offer great support, encourage one-on-one discussions, and provide excellent feedback. 

Kudos to his indepth knowledge and teaching style!

Amazing lectures!! This class was probably the only class where I learned how to code instead of being given code to copy and paste or learn on my own. The 2.5 hour lectures felt easy to digest because of the way he set up each class. The first part of the class was lecture with examples, then the second part of class was lab.

I loved this class with Professor Field! It was 3 hours long, but he was able to split it up so he isn't lecturing the whole time. Lectures were engaging and easy to follow. Homework/labs were tough at times, but the midterm and final exam were much easier.

The best professor I have had at UMass Dartmouth!!! Professor Field is one of the most intelligent and accessible professors that I have met, and his class (Scientific Computing) is both interesting and fair, covering relevant and useful material. He also is very encouraging of undergraduates and helps them obtain outside opportunities as well.
The best professor I have ever met. Very well designed course.
Very knowledgeable and professional.  If you work for it, you'll receive excellent training.

Students often joked it was the blind leading the blind, and they weren't wrong. She didn't seem to know the material, got angry if anyone stood up, and asked questions no one could answer, including her. Every class felt like a trap exams are 60% of the grade. She loves her sticky notes. I'd generally prefer to sleep for eternity than attend again
one of the worst teachers, which makes it one of UMD's finest. if you watch easy theory instead then you can pass the exams with ease.
no good, would not recommend but at least you don't have to attend every class
She is genuinely one of the WORST! professors on campus; I'd rather be taught by the janitorial staff than by her.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The most terrible professor I've encountered throughout my college career.

Read the reviews from her other position with this one and you should get a clear picture. She should be miles away from any academic institution that values its reputation and cares for its student body.

The tests are worth way more than they should be (I have finals that are worth less than 1 test in this class). She also had no idea what she was teaching the entire time. Almost everything is written on the board, so good luck looking back on anything if you miss a class. She has activities in class, but will yell if you try to help others on it.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She's a terrible professor—unclear, unprepared, and often incorrect. She assigned activities without explanations, relied on students to fix her mistakes, and stopped using slides after three weeks. When a student helped others, she scolded them instead of teaching. Even I wouldn't wish this class upon my worst enemy. Save yourself!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Did not understand the concepts well enough to teach it, very disorganized and hard to follow.

Very unqualified for this position. Almost every lecture was reading off of the notes that were from the previous instructor of the class. The homeworks were also from the previous instructor. Poor communication with students as there was no emails or announcements used at all for communication. Overall very unprofessional and poorly taught.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Terrible professor. Listen to the reviews because most of them are accurate and aren't exaggerating. Consider taking any other professor available over this professor.

So. Much. Work. But the professor was great, and I was super interested in the topic of the course. He uses no slides / online tools at all - so that can be a bit frustrating and makes attendance very important.

The professor rambles and doesn't know how to use the online side of his course, which is crucial. If the school requires online resources, it should ensure teachers are properly trained. Putting topics on exams we have not covered is not a quirky thing to laugh about when discussing your disappointment in your grades. Avoid this Professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
he's fine...
HOW DID NOBODY MAKE THAT JOKE!
but seriously, unlike most people, i found him to be a great professor who always kept a simile on his face even when nobody was answering his questions or when grades were lower that expected. many people acted like he was unfair but if you pay attention, do work on time, you're all set. pretty standard.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I took DSC 101 with him, avoid at all costs. He rarely knew what he was doing, admitting he relearned it the night before. For one of the homework assignments I asked for help and he said he had no idea and to ask one of the other students. Midterm did not relate to in-class work in any way. Stay away

Go to EVERY lecture and when he calls your name to answer a question it is OKAY to say that you do not know or don't understand (don't sit there silently awkwardly because he will NOT move on to the next person). Also, turn on email notifications for Cengage so you don't miss any assignments. If your class has a PAL tutor meet with them, it helps.

Never had this bad of an experience with a professor before. Very passive aggressive and unwilling to help both in and out of the class. Don't waste you energy and time on this professor. Tried teaching myself and going to tutoring but wasn't successful. Hope Dana finds purpose in his work in the future and doesn't just show up for a paycheck.

Fine is an awful professor! I have had him for two classes and regret both (Pre Calc and Intro to Data Science). He never follows the textbook, gives you tests and quizzes on topics that were never covered, and gives you homework on subjects that won't be covered until the upcoming weeks.

Avoid him at all costs if you value your time and grade!
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: If you are truly an enlightened student who doesn't care about your grade and just wants to learn as much as possible, he's your man. Otherwise, avoid.

Professor Fine is my worst professor I've had at UMD so far. The tests looked nothing like the notes and the textbook so I was thrown off guard every time. His class is my only non-A grade. AVOID HIM AT ALL COSTS!!

This class is crazy hard. One of the things I hated about this class was that there were no notes you basically had to teach yourself the whole semester all the notes were example problems that he makes students do in class by spinning a wheel on his phone and choosing a random kid and if you don't know the answer you automatically get embarrassed.

The professor expects you to teach yourself the topic and then publicly embarrasses you if you don't understand. We use our hour and 15 minutes of class time to do about 10 example problems that are not what is on the test. The homework is 70+ questions long. The book only teaches you the concepts and not how to apply it.

After having him for MTH181 and barely scraping by, I have no intention of taking another course of his. The exams are HARD. The review doesn't help. Instead of seeing if you've grasped the concepts/techniques and know how to use them, he quite literally makes 80% of the exam a test of whether you remember every word of his dull lectures. AVOID.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Professor Fine is alright I guess as far as teachers go. His big problem is that he posted all of the lectures online and then did review in class. The way I got through the class was by teaching myself how to do the work from the homework as I did the homework. I might as well have bought the textbook and done self study.

Dana Fine is very good for a remote teaching environment. He gives a reasonable amount of homework, he works on participation, which most professors don't do, and his exams are fairly balanced. As someone who is inconsistent and gets bad grades left and right, I am able to consistently get good grades. I recommend taking him for discrete math.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Forced us to have our cameras on and recorded lectures but never posted them class was mandatory. The only good thing he has ever done was not make us use honorlock.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: This professor sucks, i did bad on the first exam and good on the second and he accused me of cheating. He likes to try to fail students and see them struggle. He will not do review sessions or give you review guides. Avoid this professor at all costs even if that means dropping out of school. He sucks.

Best math professor I have had so far at UMass Dartmouth! Professor Fine moves through the material at an appropriate pace, gives fair and balanced exams, and lectures and answers questions in a clear, organized manner. If you are a decently good student, I would definitely recommend Professor Fine for Discrete Math.

Despite taking an on-campus course, all was taught online using a $200 online software. My grade was brought down because of group quizzes, GROUP QUIZZES, many students would argue and change answers after going to turn in the quiz.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: To myself, I am very good at math. That class made me not being able to learn anything from him because the professor does not know how to teach that class. The homework assignments had nothing relevant to the textbooks/lectures I have read. He does scale exams, though. This professor is not a recommendation if you are a math major like I am.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: THIS CLASS WAS IMPOSSIBLE FOR ME. I AM USUALLY GOOD AT MATH BUT I COULD NOT UNDERSTAND ANYTHING HE TALKED ABOUT IN CLASS. NONE OF THE HOMEWORK ASSIGNMENTS HAD ANYTHING TO DO WITH THE LECTURES HE GAVE. UNLESS YOU'RE REQUIRED TO TAKE MTH 148, I WOULD AVOID HIM AT ALL COSTS. GOOD LUCK

PLEASEEE AVOID  MTH148 UNLESS YOU ARE REALLY GOOD AT ALGEBRA. MY CLASS SIZE WENT FROM 30 THE FIRST DAY TO LIKE 15 AFTER 3 WEEKS. A LOT OF PEOPLE HAD TO WITHDRAW. HE IS A NICE PERSON BUT THE WAY HE TEACHES IS TERRIBLE.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: In class, you do problems he puts on the board and he calls names off randomly. You  do all of your learning at home watching videos he made as lectures. Homework is on Pearson, the worst thing ever invented for students. As long as you give it effort and study for the tests it's not that bad. He bumped up my last test grade to give me Dean's List!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: The best math professor at Umassd. If you are serious about learning higher math, this man will whip you into shape. It won't be easy!

If you wanna know how you can discover new math, take seriously what this guy has to say. Not for faint-hearted; don't do math if you are that faint-hearted, take music. He could've made it more friendly picture of math like V I Arnold does.

Where do I begin with my "Fine" friend? On the contrary, this man is by no means even close to the word "Fine." Prepare for his class, oh yes prepare.... To  want to avoid the school for the sake of your sanity. This man is a ring leader in a math circus of misery, set on starving his students of any good grades to promote your performance. Just NO

Terrible Class had to withdraw because it was obvious I wouldn't pass. Doesn't really teach more or less goes over the homework questions that no one can figure out, group quizzes were only given 15 minutes but they were more like an hour worth of work. Most of everyone I talked to was also expecting to fail. Would NOT take again.

All quizzes are "group quizzes" where he makes you count off randomly and puts you in random groups. Most likely you will do all your groups work and they will do credit for it. All homework AND lectures are online. He does not teach. Smart guy knows his math stuff but is mad at teaching it.
he gives a group quiz once a week that's not hard . he scales exams . hard but you just need to do the practice work.

Terrible professor. Expects students to learn ALL material OUTSIDE of class and covers review in classes. Tests are ridiculously difficult and are on material not covered in the review or curriculum. He assumes students understand him and speeds through material. Would not take another class with him again.

Worst math teacher I've had. Doesn't lecture at all makes you learn everything on your own. Just goes over problems in class and doesn't help much when the answer is wrong. If I hadn't already taken the class in high school I would not be able to understand anything that is going on. Gives online lectures that don't work and homework is too easy.

As someone who loves mathematics and has done well in all my math classes prior as an engineer, I can comfortably say this is the worst math professor I've ever had. DO not take, his tests are ridiculous, forgets to add graded points in the total score, has absolutely no understanding for his students and is overall very weird and a horrible person

Dr. Fine is a brilliant individual, and knows his stuff extremely well.  Within this brilliance lies his biggest downfall as a teacher for those who are bad at math or those who only believe they are good at math, he teaches as if his students understand things as easily as him. For me, this is how I prefer to learn, but this is not for everyone.
Just an overall horrible teacher.

He's a terrible teacher, he doesnt teach during his class time and makes you watch lectures online that are sometimes longer than the class period. when you ask for help or a question he does not answer it, he just asks you a question in return and returns to what he is doing. I had to teach myself the course and was hard because of my class load.

Mr. Fine is probably one of the worst math professor  I probably had in my whole educational life. He forces you to understand what he understands and I am obviously not on the same level of education in mathematics has he is. He doesn't see that you don't get it, and his explanations are very difficult to understand.
Do not take this mans class. Unbelievably unfair. He told us 50% of the kids in our class will fail Calc. Cannot teach. Just don't man.

This teacher is awful! I am good at math and I need calculus for my major. I decided to take precalc as a review before taking calculus. He does not teach, just puts questions on the board and asks people to answer them (seriously that's all you do the entire class). He gives group quizzes that are totally irrelevant.

I'm good at math, I'm good at math! but not when I took it with this guy. He is absolutely terrible, he does not explain himself or teach at all! HIs test have nothing to do with the material and he actually told my class that we aren't going to make anything of ourselves in the future!

One of the worst math teachers I've ever had in my entire life. I am now a senior in engineering... back when I took his precalc course he told me that I should drop my major... Please don't take this mans course... AT ALL COSTS

He makes up his quizzes 5 minutes before class and pulls the questions out of thin air (that may or may not have anything to do with the current topic). His tests are tedious and dense. He does not give you the solutions or make any attempt to explain his tests/quizzes. He has this evil little elf grin that he will display at any misfortune.

Unless you do not care about your GPA, do not take this dunce. He does not give out any homework, which would be cool if his test and quizzes didn't consist of material you will never see by reading the textbook or going to his lectures. Another words, good luck doing well if you do not devote your life to whatever he is "teaching".

Absolutely the worst professor I have ever had during my two years in college. He does not know how to teach, he just speeds through the material during his lectures, and does not provide any help when a student doesn't get something. Every week or so you will get a test or a quiz, which will include stuff not covered in his notes or the textbook.

Good teacher, expect to put I'm a lot of time and effort into the class or you won't do well.  He is very fair and grades based on effort and overall understanding of the subject. Those that are complaining just didn't put the effort in
Brilliant individual, knows his stuff. He's a difficult professor, you'd have to study hard, do the homework because they help and go to his office hours otherwise it'll will be really difficult.

Can't believe the reviews on here! I was scared going in. He doesn't move on until you understand the material. Scaled every test we had. Quizzes suck, because you have to do them in groups, it lowered my grade. Don't buy the textbook, just the online access code, because the ebook is there if you need it (which you won't).
knows what he's taking about but doesn't teach at all
sucks as a teacher but knows his stuff.

Don't do it, it's a trap.. If you plan on passing this class you better be very very good at whatever he is "teaching" because he doesn't do a good job "teaching" at all. He is all over the place with his notes and doesn't use practical examples. Hopefully you never have to take him, but if you do, good luck. You'll need it.

If you want fail, this class is for you. If you want a difficult class, this is for you. If you want to get 10 points off for not making your answer clear, this is for you. He's sucks at explaining and teaching. But other than 90% of the cons, he is nice.

AVOID THIS TEACHER. I studied the entire section of the book that he said his exam was gonna be on and none of it was on the exam. needles to say I failed and there is no way to redeem myself. He is boring and cannot teach to save his life he expects you to just know the math without being taught.

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS!I took AP calc in high school and got an A year-round but I managed to get a B- in his class. The class started with 35 and by the end only around 12 students remained. On the up-side, the homework is optional but his quizzes and tests are hard. His lectures are useless, I didn't go to half, I ended up reading the book.

If you do well in his class, you definitely know your stuff. He knows his calculus but doesnt really know how to teach it. When I had trouble he helped me and I understood afterwards. Doesn't use book but it would help if you did problems out of it yourself. group quizzes every friday, 4 tests, no final, hw is extra credit. I wouldnt take him again

DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS. You can tell he knows exactly what he is talking about but he has no clue how to be an effective teacher. Makes everything 10x confusing then it actually is. gives quizzes even tho he did not teach a section. Your better off using the book to teach yourself. In fact dont buy book you only need it for hw that he doesnt count.

STAY AWAY!!! He is by far the worst professor I have ever taken. I am an A/B student and he gave me a C I attended all the classes and did all the HW. Quizzes and Test are impossible he actually tells the class that he makes them impossible...WTF! If you want your GPA to hit rock bottom take him. If you as a question he ask "what do you think?"

Do not take class with Dana Fine.  Just don't do it.  He assumes you know much more than you learned in 101. He barely answers questions, and if he does, he acts superior.  Constantly late and flustered. Entire class failed first test and he said "happens to me every year, you guys have to try harder"

He gives you like 40 calculus problems due in two days. He doesn't help you with any questions or concerns, he ignores my emails, his tests are nothing like what he teaches in class. My class started off with about 30 people and there was about 10 left in the end. Don't trust him.

He teaches very technically and with lots of theorems.  His tests are way harder than the stuff he gives you for hw.  As long as you follow along in class, though, you should do well.  He is more than willing to help you out and scales grades accordingingly.  You just have to get past the first test and you should do fine.

Fine is by far the worst professor ! Start of class 60 students now maybe 10. he doesnt teach he puts a problem on the board and asks you what to do.i dont know you tell me.he an ass.i went for help and it was like pulling teeth for him to get him to do problems for me he is awfull! if you ask questions he doesnt answer it.he beats around the bush

This is the worst teacher I think I've ever had in my life.  He does about 1 problem in class, and can only do one because no one in the class understands how to do any of it.  The problems in class aren't anything like the tests and somewhat similar to the homework, so you basically don't learn anything at all.  WORST TEACHER EVER DONT TAKE HIM!!!

By far the worst teacher on the face of the planet. THE WORST. i really hope that this critique will steer you away from taking his class. At no point this year did he teach me anything about math. each class consisted of him saying how "stupid" my class was. HORRIBLE HORRIBLE HORRIBLE is all i can say about him.

horrible teacher. his test questions make no sense, he talks in circles. the whole class fails tests and he takes no blame. does not want to help you no matter how hard you try. the only way to get a good grade is if your a genious, avoid him!!

He teaches and teaches and teaches...But he loves and he loves and he loves... He will figure it out some day. oh yes he will figure out the one thing he loves the most...when he figures it out we will all be doomed.he will be the one who laughs last. He will touch it..of yes he's gonna touch it.

Was confused from the first day. Didn't understand a single topic he tried to teach. Only math majors could understand how he taught - do not take unless you are a math major. Did not correlate the HW to the text, the online program he told us we needed, or the notes in class. I withdrew even though I had a decent grade because it wouldn't stand.
worst teacher in the world, never take him
worst teacher every, teaches like its harvard

(CALC 3)I would not recommend Professor Fine for a non math major.  He is a mathematician above all else and teaches to mathematicians.  More than half the class withdrew from class, and I managed to pull off a B-, he is generally a nice guy and wants to see everyone succeed, GO TO CLASS!!!!
attend class then go to his office hours, he clears everything up in his office hours.  dont expect to get a good grade if you blow off class...

This class was a bad joke. I waived Calc. 1 because I've always done well in math and easily got a 5 on the AP test,  and I felt absolutely lost 90% of the time in this class. Our class started as 20-25 kids, when I withdrew on the last day there were 5 left. If you spend your free time enjoying Calculus and not having a life, you might like him.

Proffessor Fine is a very rigid man.He spends most of the year building up hatered for his students.Once in his office I asked for help .I left that ****ing office with equations clawed into my face, neck and chest .He hates his students.he has to go to Cambodia for three months a year to torture monkys and diddle boys just to take out aggression
No Comments

Extreamly difficult professor. Wants to teach theory, not applications.  Always willing to explain, but doesnt help often.  Scaled test about 7-12 pts and gave 50-60% "easy" questions on the test.  2/3 of class withdrew, no one remaining in the class failed. Well prepared = study entire book throughly.  Come to class, he will remember whos there.

He is a little eccentric, but he will amaze you with his intelegence. He told me that if he could do it all again he would become a geometric brakedancer. but its too late now on account of his faulty back. Thats when I said "don't ever stop being who you are". Then, he took me into his arms and said " you are magic man and I love you"I woke up thr

also mth 451 and mth 421  He is by no means an easy teacher and if you are just taking math for a gen ed get someone else. But if you like the subject and want to get a really good and comprehensive understanding he is a great teacher just don't be afraid to go to him during his office hours to get help.
Nothing "fine" about him.
This guy is on a power trip.  He gets a kick out of flunking people. Avoid him!!!!

Tough teacher, hard class to pass.  I'm usually good at math but struggled with this class.  He's a really nice guy and easy to approach with questions.  Just be prepared to work hard if you take him.

Terrible teacher. His primary goal is to flunk/weed out and then his secondary goal is to teach. I have always done well in math, but even with hard studying, doing all the homework, and going to tutors, I ended up withdrawing to avoid an F.  He considers giving a C a huge deal. AVOID HIM!!!

Do not take Fine if you just want to fulfill a 100-level requirement; but if you really love mathematics, then he is definitely in the top three of professors I have had.   He is hard and he goes fast, but he really knows his stuff and he shows a real love for the subject.

This teacher does not care about his students. His mostly comes to class with no book.  He went to Europe and did not even tell his department head.  He is horrible.  Anyone who thinks he is a good teacher needs therapy.

Awful teacher,more than half the class was failing and all he had to say to us was that the withdrawl period would be ending soon.I went to him for help several times and his response was to 'read the book' and that was it. I put majority of my time into this class, went to tutoring and still ended up having to withdraw to aviod an F.

This was the WORST teacher I've EVER had! I am a hard worker; always did my homework, and went to tutoring two times a week. Nothing helped. He does not clearly explain how to do the work, and is unwilling to bend his teaching style to benefit the class, which had an average of a D+. More than half of us failed. AVOID THIS TEACHER!

An extremely difficult professor, Prof. Fine gives work and quizzes before you actually learn the material well. Most of your learning will be done outside of the classroom, alone or with a tutor. His homework exercises are difficult and his quizzes require memorizing information. If you put the effort in, a lot, you will do fine.
prof. fine is a terrible teacher, he goes throught the material so fast and doesn't explain things clearly. He starts out the class by saying most of you are goin to fail. now there's encouragment
he is hard but if you pay attention you'll pass.
I hate math, but I got through it well.  I think he taught well enough for those who were willing to put effort into it.
tells what and why instead of just why. lazy people don't like him.
He doesn't go over any of the problems for homework and when you ask him a question about the homework he doesn't anwer you clearly.
this guy is the worst teacher ever...he goes to fast...he doesnt help...and its impossible to get a good grade...do NOT take a class with him...

Terrible professor.  I've never got a grade that bad in math before.  The whole class has a C or D average and he really d.n. scale that much.  The book is awful.  Only ones who do well go to his office and suck up and still dn know what they're doing.
No Comments
No Comments
I had this guy 3 years ago and I still think he is the worst out there
His class is no joke.
Goes way to fast even if the WHOLE class doesn't understand.
No Comments

Zack is a great professor. He genuinely cares about your growth as an artist and gives good feedback. Crit days are often long, but personally, I don't mind. As long as you show effort, you'll get a decent grade.

Zack is an incredibly devoted illustrator and professor. He will be your biggest cheerleader if you show him that you are willing to put the work in. He gives fantastic critique, and has worked as an illustator for 10+ years- those critiques mean something. If you're not willing to put the work in, don't bother with Zack's classes.

Great professor!. He has so much great advice and has taught me so many things about digital art! The class is 6 hours, but we get 45 min lunch breaks and get out early almost everyday. It's once a week so 3 tardies = absence.  1 unexcused absence and after that you go down a letter grade, but its only once a week. Go to this class, you'll learn!
Zack is a great professor! He's a tough grader and critiques can be long days. He wants the best for his students and his classes are great.

A difficult teacher to work with. He has bad ways of critiquing that put extra stress on students and makes the whole experience unnecessarily unpleasant. We are paying thousands of dollars to be here and the class was dismissed early which took away from work time. He is hard to reach and don't expect him to reply to your emails in the same day.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: The absolute worst professor I have ever had, consistently alienates students and plays favorites. Get ready for horrible critiques that last far too long and are not helpful with a heaping serving of passive aggression to top it off. There is no empathy for students and circumstances. Be prepared to learn how to hate your art and art in general.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: NY Times bestselling author, a magician with a pen, a master of both words AND art. Much like Moses carried the 10 commandments, Professor Giallongo will carry you over the threshold of inspiration. So have no fear ladies and gentlemen, Mr. Zack is Aces.
i like Vikram

ECE170 was mostly an easy class, but the professor gave off rude vibes to the point where he felt unapproachable and was heavy on the accent. Sometimes things were explained thoroughly, but other times the slides were rushed through, leaving confusion on specific topics. The exams are open notes, but there is little to no scaling.
Heavy on the accent, do you like passing? Don't take him.
Beware! Avoid at at all costs. Liar and cheat.

This professor just cost me my masters by failing me unfairly. He hides the online tests within the course and only keeps them open a short time. He edited the syllabus after and his comments to me when I appealed the grade. He counted extra credit against me and lied. Even in his lectures you can hear his unclear grading practices.

The average of our exam was 48. He scaled it 12 points, so the average was a 60. An exam should not be below passing after scaling. This means a majority of students will probably not even reach a B. He does offer some extra credit, but you should not have to take an entire extra certification exam and prep 20+ hrs for it to barely see a B-.
This class was a bit harder than his other class i took with him but overall it was not to bad  but I normally do well with tests and exams,
The class was not to hard only a few chapters were really tough but overall if you do his quizzes and pass the exams you should do fine.

Took FIN 650 online. Half of the material in the book is not what is given for assignments. It's as if the book gives you a 2nd grade example, then the homework is a 10th grade question and you have no information to use as a guide. Prepare to use google… A LOT. Professor is no help when you ask questions either. Exams video proctored. Do not take!

I find him rude. He doesn't want to answer questions that he deems simple. I had a question about the group project and he asked me in front of other students how did I make it into FIN 650 without taking the foundation courses. Also, in his group projects, he expected a group to discuss BETA something that was never taught in detail. Hard to watch

This professor is very organized and accessible.
His lectures are very good and exams are fair.

This class was challenging, but not really as bad as some other reviews imply. Prof. Vasudevan is more than fair in grading and offers partial credit if you show your work ( Notes of Excel). I'm not a finance major, but I didn't think the material was that bad. Just stay on top of it and you'll do just fine. I would recommend taking his class.

Literally puts 1-2 questions from the material on the test. the other 98% of the test is random stuff you never heard of or things you were never shown how to calculate in a specific manner. Im convinced everyone is doing poorly because last year the curve was crazy(about 1.5 letter grade increase). this course is literally one big curveball!!!

One of the best professors I ever had. Uses current examples to better understanding, exams are fair if you study for them, gives extra credit opportunities. You can tell he's really knowledgeable. Replies fast when e-mailed and takes his time to help students.

100% would recommend.

One of the best finance professors at UMD.  He is very knowledgeable and does a very good class.
However he is not very flexible, does not extend due dates and not much extra credit.

His classes were dull and boring. He kept talking and didn't stop for questions and expected you to understand the material his way. The tests were almost the complete opposite from the study guide and to my knowledge, he didn't give partial credit.
Professor Gopala's course is pretty simple. His exams however, are very difficult but he offers partial credit for the work you show. Not an easy A by any stretch.
AWFUL!!!!

I have had two classes with this professor. He does not teach the classes and I have been having to teach myself which has been overwhelming.  He is quick to respond to emails but they are not helpful.  Rather than help he suggests I use a study site or tutor.  He will not offer a live zoom or offer zoom office hours.
Great Professor. His course is straight forward and he was very helpful and easy to get in touch with when I was having difficulty.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Terrible at teaching and test review does not match up with the test. Assignments have wrong due dates also. Avoid if you can.

many options to learn info through his recorded classes, videos, weekly work and online work. professor is knowledgeable and available. tests are difficult, timed and video proctored. you can study the work and materials for each week and still not do well on the test. be sure to complete all other assignments.

Very fast paced class, you'll self teach. Lectures are rushed, but he makes himself available for questions & offers partial credit if you submit your work for tests. Don't bother reading the book-waste of time since none of the graded material is based on readings, but you may have to buy for online access. Nice guy, not so great teacher IMO.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I love Professor Vasudevan! He's a great teacher at UMassD. He is very knowledgeable in the world of Finance and is more than happy to help his students excel in his class. He's very quick to respond to your emails. I highly recommend him!

International finance with Prof. Vasudevan is extremely test/quiz heavy and not an easy class. His tests have very few problems on them 5-7 per test/quiz, so if you get a couple wrong you will get a very bad grade. Everything is open answer and you must show work, very little partial credit given. Overall, Id recommend taking this with a diff prof
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I took Professor Vsaudevan's class during Fall 2018. Excellent professor, very organized and high quality

Awful at answering emails, and does not connect with students. Class is very boring and also does not teach test and homework content well. Gives quizzes randomly on Saturdays and doesn't accommodate if your busy.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Dont take his class, very sheep minded professor. Doesnt teach you about real world finance at all.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Won't really learn anything, but it will be easy to pass. Gives example tests that are exactly like the test. Allows a cheat sheet so you can just have your cheat sheet be the sample exam. Online homework - easy. Nice guy, and tries his hardest to make you understand.
good professor, who taught a great class in International Finance. Clear lectures and regular assignments. Sample exam was quite similar to actual. Also gives extra credit for Bloomberg.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Prof. vasudevan is an excellent professor. He is always readily available to assist students . If you want to learn corporate finance and apply what you learn in class to real life Harvard Business School cases, than Prof. vasu is the professor to have.
Very good professor perhaps the best online class I have had at UMD.

Worst class ever taken in undergrad or grad school. Instructor totally disengaged. Class 100% self taught. Though instructor included some video lecture, they did nothing to teach the content. Asked a question (via email) for clarification on a topic, was told to look it up online.

I really liked this professor. While I am not a Finance fan, he made it bearable. Truly caring, hands-on professor who wants you to succeed and provides ample questions and resources to help you. No reason not to do well with all his help. He is a rarity and the only reason I dropped him 1 star is as his accent could be hard to follow at times.
Professor Vasudevan is very organized, lecture videos are great and he is very quick to respond to email.  He does not let you retake assignments past the due date.  Good if you are willing to work.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Very nice guy and will bump your grade. Very hard classes, no help in office hours-went to another professor.

It's a blessing that BlackBoard offers a professor to do nothing more than monitor student participation. Get ready to teach yourself finance; the poor videos provided by Vasu don't explain much. Class is graded by test score, so make sure to keep doing the homework (which mirrors the tests). Buy the book, you'll need it.
Professor Vasu is very good. Black Board is very well set up and he is also  very responsive to emails.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Gopala is a very good professor. Very organized materials posted on BB. He has online video lectures which are short and very good. He is also willing to regrade the online exam for you.  Overall much better than the usual UMD online course.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I took International Finance with Professor Vasu.  He is very organized and posts all material on Blackboard. First exam was tough.  Second exam was online and the third is optional.  Overall very helpful professor. He is tough on you if you slack and dont do assignments etc.

Vague and not very helpful.  When emailing, CC the department chair and dean to get a response. Though I got a good grade, a lot of time and work went into it.  He used  confusing  taped-PPT (with no help or formulations given).   Take if you already know finance well; another totally self-taught course!  I suggest seeing him often in office hours.
Very good class.  You have to be careful to complete the assignments on time.  Good luck.
I found Vasu to be one of the best professors at UMASS.  Very organized,  helpful and willing to manually regrade your exams for online courses. Would take his courses again.

Hard assignments. Thinks having test reviews in the classroom is OK for an ONLINE CLASS where students are not local to the campus. Won't provide help and will chastise you if you dare point out that in-person reviews make NO SENSE for an online only class. AVOID. Worst prof. I had at UMASS.

I took professor Vasudevan's class during fall 2014. He is a lot better than most faculty at UMASSD.  MYCOURSES is very well organized and responds to student emails quickly even during weekends. Course has a lot of assignments and they help learning.

Took his FIN 650 online class while in the MBA program at UMass Dartmouth. The entire class is outsourced to McGraw-Hill Connect, Vasudevan never makes an appearance. He answers emails four or five days later unless you CC the dean. Take his class if you are already a finance expert and just want to take some automated online quizzes for credit.

I took it as an online class. The biggest issue was the timeliness of communication. He would not respond to all the questions of the email, and I would have to re-ask several times. The questions I had was mostly related to the syllabus or assignments. The assignments were posted late and did not totally match to the syllabus. Not good for online

Professor Vasu is a very intelligent and knowledgeable professor.  This is a really good class if you are motivated and can work consistently.  You can get atleast a B+ if you do the assignments and the quizzes (online).

He is a good professor but he grades harder than most. you don't spend a lot of time in class.  Class time is evenly split between mcgraw hill assignments and in class. you will have to teach yourself some of the material because only one class a week is a lecture.

Vasudevan has a bad rep when it comes to his classes, but you can't argue that he knows his stuff and does a very good job at teaching his information. You'll no doubt walk out of the class smarter in finance, but don't expect a cake-walk. When it comes to his exams, he doesn't play. His exams will seriously challenge you but is straight-forward.
Professor Vasudevan is one of the best professors in the MBA program.  The course is perhaps the most challenging MBA course. Not for slackers.Very good knowledge on the financial industry etc.
Professor Vasu is one of the best teachers in the College of Business. Mycourses is very organized.  He makes use of McGrawHill Connect for Assignments and Exams. Good Course for FIN 312
One of the best professors at UMASS. He is strict but if you do the work you will get a good grade.  He does not give makeups for exams or quizzes.Take him if you are organized.
Good Guy. Very interested in students and passionate about teaching. Fair grader.
Professor Vasu is good at Finance. When compared to other MBA professor he is quite strict and demanding. Take him if you really want to learn finance and are willing to work.

I really liked him as a person and as a professor. I took the class during the intercession and it was difficult but did a great job taking the time to answer questions and help us out when we needed it. The tests were difficult (and online) and sometimes there were questions that we did not cover in class so that was a bit difficult.
Vasu is a very good professor. He likes to help students and work with them. He can also get upset at students who do not want to learn.Take him if you want to learn good stuff.

This professor is horrible, he has no communication skills, Mycourses is a mess, he needs to organize it better and structure his class, which it isn't don't expect to get much out of this class. Everything I learned in it was from the internet looking things up, not class, horrible teacher.

Thank god for Google. I learned more from the internet than this useless professor. What a waste of time it is taking this class. Its a shame to because I am a finance major and I am doing well in my other classes, not this one, take someone else if you can!!!!!

1) Homework, quizes and tests are never on material covered in class  2)When you ask him to answer questions about homework, he doesn't even know how to do it, and gets it wrong. 3) Horrible communicator, he can't convey class practical ideas on how to solve the question. Questions, formulas and often tests aren't related to textbook.
Vasu is the best teacher I had at UMASS. Good website, very clear lectures.  He needs to be nicer to students!

Don't bother talking to the guy. He will ignore you because he feels he is so much better than us. Other than that and his very thick accent, the class isn't bad. Gives sample quizzes and tests online before you take the real ones and they are word for word what the real ones are except he changes a few numbers. Easy class

He has a very good scale on his grades. Slides and lecture are useless. he has a indian accent, not so bad though. He doesnt teach basics, he goes really fast, he demands you use a specific calc and won't support your regular TI-83, he posts exam and quiz practice, multi choice are impossible, most practice problems make no sense,just a bad teacher
Very good professor with a deep knowledge of finance. Don't expect him to teach you the basic stuff like how to solve an equation or such.

Professor is very intelligent and teaches high quality stuff.  He needs to organize WebCT better (most teachers dont have one).   You also need to be prepared for cases or he will get you. Not an easy guy but very good.

Very disorganized. WEBCT is a mess and he doesnt post everything on there on time including exams! The tests have multiple choice but sometimes dont even have the correct answer in there.. its like he doesnt even know how to do it himself. I wouldnt recommend any of his classes. If you want to learn something worthwhile go to another prof!!
deep voice + mumbules + monotone + heavy indian accent = what the heck is this guy talking about

I am not sure how people are saying he is extremely helpful and easy and WebCT is helpful..  I had him for Seminar and it was miserable. I passed with a B but he is NOT helpful during office hours, WebCT ends up getting cluttered and messy because he doesnt delete things or forgets to put them on, Tests are extremely hard hence the scale
Professor Vasudevan is one of the best professors I have had at UMASSD.  Very organized and helpful guy. His WEBCT is fantastic.

this man wanted to try to teach me finance without a pencil and paper. calculator only. i tried to argue that if you hit the wrong button on your calculator unknowingly, you have to rely on trial and error to come up with the correct answer. i'd never hire a financial advisor who didn't own a pen and paper. he's a bad teacher.
He is the most organized professor that I have seen at UMASS. Posts lectures on webct, gives out sample exams, great syllabus and very fair grading

Great teacher, willing to help , cares about his students, class is alittle tough at first but gets easier as you go on, tests are easy if you study the review test, smart guy and funny at times. one of the better finance teachers at umass

it's a hard class, just hard material, but he is a great guy, scales insainly, but dont get me wrong, if you had a hard time with accounting you'll have a hard time with this material, if your a math person you'll do fine. if you had to take this class with anyone, take it with him
I thought he was a very funny guy. Tests are good. Quizzes were better. easy to talk if you have a problem
His course is pretty easy, his exams come straight from reviews, his classes are horribly boring though and he is very susceptible to brown nosing, so get in with him and ur set...
Very organized class. He is the best!

He gives sample assignments/quizes/tests online before each real one.  They're practically the same so the tests aren't surprising and they're fairly easy.  The only work to be done is 3 assignments/quizes/tests.

Dr. Glowny is a wonderful professor. This course is hard but she does her best to make it understandable for everyone. She is always available for questions and is very quick to respond to emails. Dr. Glowny will provide you will great knowledge, learn what you can from here!

Dr. Glowny is one of the most caring professors at UMD. She genuinely wants her students to succeed and goes out of her way to support us throughout the semester. Even when the material is challenging, she takes the time to break it down, answer questions, and make it easier to understand. She is truly one of the best professors at UMD!!

Dr. Glowny is disorganized, very insecure (which is very surprising as a professor), asks for feedback to know “what works” and what helps us learn but never implemented any of it until very late into the semester. And she's a racist! Racially criminalized me in one of the classes! BEWARE!You're on your own in this course with her so please study!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Glowny cares a lot about her students. It is very apparent she wants each and every student to succeed. Unfortunately, her lectures become very confusing as often she rambles far past what we need to know. I fin that in lectures between the rambling and everything else it's hard to absorb the content.

Dr. Glowny cares so deeply about each and every of her students. She presents the material clearly and will always explain further if anyone has questions. I took her for 270 as well, and will be taking every future class she teaches!

Lots of material to cover but makes the lecture interesting and encourages questions. Truly cares for her students and listens to feedback. She also provides additional resources for exams to help you do better. She is the best nursing professor I have had yet and will be taking her next semester.

Knowledgeable in women's studies and philosophy, but is not set to teach in a digital age. Often spent the first chunk of class trying to figure out computer issues which wasted a lot of time. Dense in depth homework is required but she does not give in depth feedback in return, often just things like "good" or "well put."

Met on Zoom, scoffed at because she didn't agree w. my idea for paper. Arrogant attitude.Outrageous reading/citing for hw. Would never take her course again. Ruined near perfect GPA. Condescending responses to emails. Never answers initial question. In depth hw that are not graded but mando. Grades few assignmt's that are worth alot on final grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: I had Prof Gardner for WGS307 Ecofeminism. The course was creative, engaging, & interactive. We created our own blogs that we posted to weekly. There was a good amount of reading but the topics were so incredibly interesting. She was terrific w/her feedback! The course was well organized & expectations/assignments were very clear.
Very tough grader for a 101 class, grade is determined heavily by discussion boards and tests are scored out of 20 making it very easy to lose points. I would choose an easier professor over her.

Tough grader but is flexible with deadlines if there is any issues with internet and wifi. Doesn't lecture in class at all. Talks at the class without any lecture slides. Reads off of a word document as a lecture then goes right into group work for every class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I love this class (ecofeminsm). She is down to earth and the class is so interesting. The other students are great too so it's been an awesome semester. I wish I could take dr. Garndner again but I am graduating.

I haven't seen an HON301 review on here, but she's great. She is willing to help me with any problem I'm having even if it doesn't have to do with honors. Very clear grading criteria and all of the discussions posts lead up to the two final assignments for the class.

Took her freshman year & I thought I was going to die. Failed my first paper but after crying to her got a re-write & pulled a B. she's a little intimidating has very high expectations. Assigns a lot of readings & be ready to discuss all class. Final big presentation not only on content but speech and body language. CHALLENGING  but doable

not sure what happened to her between all of the good comments and this past semester, but it was something. Not helpful, condescending, rude, and her lessons were thrown together at the last minute. Half of grades are based on journals, which have minimal to do with the course and were not explained well. made mistakes when finalizing grades too.

Do not take this class if you are not interested in learning Race, Gender and Sexuality through a Philosophical lens. I was unaware of the cross listing and probably wouldn't have taken it if I had known. Heavy reading (very difficult) and discussion based class. Very tough grader. I am an A student, and I got a C in this class. Not an easy A!

I have always gotten A's on writing papers for other gen ed requirements as a bio major. For some reason I never meet her standards for writing papers and got by with C's and D's. There's also group discussions in every class which most students hated.If you're not interested in philosophy, dont take this class.

This class was extremely difficult for me.  This is the first class I will not receive an A in.  So safe to safe she's ruined my great GPA.  Unless you are a whiz at philosophy, don't take her.  She does not offer any extra credit either.
Great class & professor! 2 papers, no test! EASY A
Very stimulating, always had something interesting to say. She was very nice and helpful, the work load wasn't too bad.

Advanced Ethics was my second class with Cathy and I enjoyed it. She is a great lady and knows her stuff. The readings are interesting and she does a great job dissecting them in class. It's hard not to get involved with mandatory readings and online discussions.

I took Professor Gardner's class as a freshman, so I was nervous that a 300-level would be over my head, but it was well worth the risk. History of Feminist Thought was interesting and thought provoking. She gives a lot of leeway in terms of grading, so as long as you actually read something you should escape wtih a B.
Professor Gardner is fantastic. There is a lot of reading, but it is well worth it and you actually learn something.

she is easy, funny & understanding. there were NO exams. 3 papers, kinda tough grader, but if you work with her you'll do fine. gives an extra credit assignmnt to replace a low grade. doesnt take attendance but there are 4 days where you have an open book journal assigmnt which is easy and given a grade from 1 - 3. she wants you to do well.

VERY FUINNY TEACHER! Her humor is wonderful, and any college kid can identify with it. She makes all the topics covered very fun to learn about. She is also always willing to stay after class to help. The class was VERY easy too. We had to watch "High Noon" and do a repor on that, do an OPEN BOOK midterm, simple journal reports, and a final.

Best professor I have ever had. She is an amazing lady.  I would love to sit her down and just pick her brain for a day.  She is very clear it terms of lecturing and what she expects.  She is always willing to help, encourages class discussion, and shines a bright light on difficult issues.  Warning to crazy note takers; notes are disorganized.

Prof Gardner is an aweseome professor. She knows her stuff about feminist theory. She made the class really interesting and always respected our viewpoints. Her assignments were not bad at all. Two Short Papers and a final paper 8-10 pages. A good amount of reading though. I recommend her highly!!
Great prof. Classes were interesting and she's always around if you need extra help. Highly reccommended.
No Comments
She is so inteligent!
awesome teacher
She is great!
fabulous professor.
easy class, just keep up with the work, she has a great sense of humor and teaches in a fun and interesting way.

Nice lady. She's little weird. She's funny and teaches a fun class.  The assigned readings were interesting but not too long.  If you're a casual fan of ethics and willing to do a little reading, take this class.  Enjoyable class.

Readings she assigned were interesting. Decent grader, so long as you write in your journal and keep up with it.  Participate in her class and you will get more out of it.  Couldn't shake the "if you saw a baby drowning on the side of the road, would you stop and save it" example
She is an extremely nice and sweet woman, but I just was not interested in philosophy, which is not her fault. She is very helpful, but prepared to have difficult readings. Great teacher!

She is an awesome prof.  she's funny and intelligent and fair.  her accent is brittish (for thoes that were wondering). i took her "medieval philosophy" class and i loved it.  i'd take her again if i could.  amazing teacher

Awsome teacher, I've had her twice so far and if i could take her again I would.  A lot of people say shes hard but thats just cause there lazy.  She doesn't ask for much and if you put in alittle extra effort you'll def. get an A.

she has attendance policies that change throughtout the semester and if you are absent and send your homework in she wont accept it, yet she will allow someone who missed the ay of the test to take it.
She is a horrible person.  Rude and insults you. Don't take her class. She is also hard to understand..some kind of accent.
She is just plain mean.
No Comments
No Comments
No Comments

Professor Gelmi is AMAZING! She is by far the best prof at UMassD. She is very silly, and she truly does care about her students and their well-being. Her class on monsters is super interesting and she is very knowledgeable. She does have high expectations, but she is always offering additional office hours, and she gives very thorough feedback!

SOOO nice! Class is very interesting and engaging even for someone who isn't an English major. She really knows how to get a class talking with one another. Monster class is so cool and she's just so enthusiastic but genuine. Super willing to help and is always offering office hours. Also gives great feedback.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Best professor ever.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: A true gem!  I would highly recommend it, even for a free elective.  She is compassionate and funny and encourages student participation and feedback.  I wish I could have taken her more than once in college!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: MY GOAT. very understanding teacher with interesting lectures. Her class is amazing and the topics she covers are interesting especially with her approach of teaching it. Please take her 258 class on Monsters if given the chance.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Excellent professor, she is so compassionate and caring towards all her students. She wants you to succeed in her class. Very in depth class discussions that make the class interesting.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Funny and giddy prof. Lots of work. Quiz on readings every week, midterm, group project, final project, final paper and final exam. Makes class more fun if you participate and read. lots of work but its an upper level english class so its expected. Cares about students and is flexible, offers extra credit
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Attendance is not mandatory but does count toward participation grade. She offers extra credit and easy ways to bump your grade, as well as extensions if needed. Super helpful class discussions that will help you answer all of her quiz questions. Lot of reading, but it's a literature course.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Professor Gelmi is a great person and caring to her students. However, she expects a lot out of students' essays and grades them pretty harshly. If ENL 200 is a university requirement for you and you are looking for an easy A, I would not take this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Professor Gelmi is organized and ready to teach. She provides clear instructions and top notch reading lists. She gives you timely feedback. You can learn a lot from her if you put in the effort.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Get ready to read
