reviews.comment: She is the best. I took Horror Literature and she makes the lectures so fun to listen to. As long as you read the books or read spark notes (cuz that's what I did) you'll do fine. The quizzes r super easy. You won't fail the class even if you're a bad writer.

Professor Gelmi was one of my favorite teacher at Umass. Her bubbly, outgoing and fun personality, mixed with her passion and knowledge for what she was teaching, made for a great class. She cares deeply for each of her students and loves watching them succeed.

Professor Gelmi was one of my favorite teachers at Umass. Her bubbly, outgoing, and fun personality, mixed with her passion and knowledge of what she was teaching, made for a great class. She cares deeply for each of her students loves watching them succeed.
Professor Gelmi really believes in her students and genuinely wants them to succeed. She makes studying English very interesting.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Inspirational

reviews.comment: She's the best professor I had all year, so funny and sweet and kind. I would absolutely recommend her class to anyone, but be mindful that there is a good amount of reading to do (four books for the semester). Almost entirely discussion-based, three papers are the only written homework assignments, everything else is just reading assignments.

Her class was great! She makes sure everyone understands the assigned readings before starting the class. She loves questions! Go to her office hours! She is very easy to talk to and gives great feedback. She assigns a blog project, 3 short papers, a final paper and 4 quizzes. The blog project is super easy and based on the theme of the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Caring

reviews.comment: She is literally the best professor i've had so far. She has such a cute, quirky personality and the discussions she has with the class are so fun. She assigns really interesting books to read, gives 4 quizzes, a blog project, and 3 short essays. Not a hard grader at all and willing to help you as much as you need it. Definitely take her class!!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Professor Gelmi is the best professor I've had my whole time at umass. I never actually write on ratemyprofessor but honestly she's just that great. The discussions she holds in class make it so you wouldn't even have to read the material, but it does help so you can contribute in class. Would definitely love to have her as a professor again.

She is an amazing professor.  She gives wonderful feedback on essays, and is always available to help with essays as well.  The amount of work is very reasonable, as is the time she gives to complete said work.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Amazing professor.  Her class was the only reason why I showed up to campus on some days.  Super nice and understanding.  If you're not the greatest writer she is always available for help.    Her class was a blast and incredibly interesting, and she has a quirky sense of humor.  Wish I could take her for more classes.

Gelmi is one of the best English teachers I've ever had! 3 papers for the class, but she was willing to meet during office hours and give feedback on rough drafts. In class participation / attendance matters. All the books were interesting and the assignments weren't too difficult (topic was dystopian novels). Definitely take ENL200 with her
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: I've taken Professor Gelmi two semesters in a row for two different ENL200's. Hands down favorite professor / teacher / instructor I've ever had for any grade of education I've taken. Can't say enough how much I love this woman

Professor Gelmi's awesome. She really knows her stuff and makes class interesting. Going to her office hours helps when you have papers cause they can be pretty hard. She's nice, helpful and funny too. Attendence policy is pretty strict though.

One of the nicest, and most friendly professors I have had at UMass D so far. She's incredibly smart and really cares about her students. However, expect a bit of reading and somewhat difficult writing assignments. However, I feel she has made me a better writer so the hard work is worth it.

Dr Gelmi is definitely one of the most supportive professors at UMD. I went to her office hours to discuss most of the big assignments and she was beyond helpful every time. I'm a decent writer so she really pushed me to be an even better one. Writing assignments are manageable and only 2 tests that are pretty straightforward. highly recommend!

She is an awesome professor. She will help you with whatever you need and is always willing to give feedback. She can be a tough grader for the essays, but it's an English class so expect it. An A easy obtainable though with how helpful she is and how the class is set up. I took the horror class and I do recommend it.

Really great teacher! One of my favorites here so far. She is very funny, and makes the material very interesting. You do need to do all the reading and show up to class for full points. But there are only a few papers and two tests the whole semester. Overall she is amazing and I HIGHLY recommend her!

She's great, she's funny, I loved her class. She assigns a lot of reading (I believe 5 books for the semester), but all were good reads. There are writing assignments, all of which are pretty straightforward.   If you do ever need help, she's easy to get a hold of and easy to talk to. I don't have anything negative to say!  Really fun class!

Lots of reading but once you get in the habit youll be fine. Class discussions after every reading, you dont need to participate. Go to class and take notes on discussions and youll get a good great. three small essays 1-2 pages. midterm - take home. final - in class. Gives good feed back. Great teacher - would take again!
She was legit my favorite teacher ever. I'm not a fan of English classes, but she made it 100% worth taking. I'd take another class with her any day.

She's a great teacher and is really into what she teaches but I wish she would easy up with the strict attendance! After 3 absences and tardies she takes a 13 of your grade off. Also, don't always need the book. I sparknoted my way through the class and papers! The papers are pretty easy and short overall, I would recommend her class!
She is extremely helpful and stupendously interested in the subject she teaches.  The class is fun and interesting even when the reading is lackluster, though the reading is rarely boring.
A lot of reading, but she's a great teacher! She gives good feedback on papers, and has flexible office hours. Best teacher in the English Department, by far! Take any class taught by her if you can!
A lot of reading, but an amazing teacher! She gives good feedback on papers, and has flexible office hours if needed. I'd take any other course in English if she taught it!

There definitely is a lot of reading. However, she makes ALL these books interesting and fun! She jokes around, is clear about her passion towards being an English Professor, and is such an understanding person. She helps you with any question you have, and guides you in the right direction. By far my favorite professor so far in this semester!

This wasn't the first time I took a class from Dr. Kul. His class population is usually divided in two groups: Ones who's there to get credit, and ones who's there to learn. Those who are there to learn usually get A's because he leads class discussions only one group cares to listen. Hopefully I can cross paths with him again.
Ok so contrary to your personal beliefs he makes it easy to pass the class but since ours was in a 100+ person lecture hall it was difficult to pay attention and be engaged
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: his jokes don't be hittin
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: the grading is 70% exams with 3 exams and 30% homework. He takes forever to grade
Great professor.
Enough Said, one of the best professors on campus, easy.

Prof Kul did a great job in keeping the class enjoyable. CIS273 is a highly technical class that includes three advanced topics. Yet, his lectures were always informative, based on discussions and interactivity, with lots of opportunities to ask questions. For good grades, you need to attend and take notes because questions will come from lectures.
Absurd night and day difference in difficult between the first and second half of the semester. Professor was oblivious that he left most of the class lost.

I would highly recommend taking Prof Kul. There is not much homework and it is not very difficult while doing it. The labs are very easy and most of the time the TA will give you the answer. Study for the midterms and have a notecard to help you. The class can get very boring but you will do fine in this class if you do the HW.

Prof. Kul is very driven about cybersecurity. If you want to learn this field, he is the right person. If you want a course to get an easy grade, he isn't the right professor for you. I thought this course would be an easy pass, but I have learned so much because his passion for cybersecurity is contagious. Office hours are very helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Test heavy--Accessible outside class--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: He takes databases and cyber security VERY seriously. But these things made the class more interesting for me because I honestly didn't know there was that much to know about databases. He also was a good research mentor for me, his deadlines and scrum-style management kept me on track and we banged out a paper much faster than usual.

I took Digital Forensics under him. He made us work for it. He gave us good feedback. After a long time a professor who's reliable from the learning perspective. He makes you work for your grade really. He's organized and dedicated to his work. We can learn what's commitment from him.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: You won't get a feedback from him on anything basically. Grades projects at the very last, how will students know if the project is up to the mark? He will scare you for every thing. The course could have been really good if he didn't scare people off and if he gave proper feedback for the project performance.

Professor is very qualified. From day 1 he was scaring off students about how rigorous and difficult this course is. I believe there were better ways to make students serious about the course than to scare students from the start. His lectures are good but he wants class participation. Doesn't get into some topics unless you ask about them.

She's a very nice and understanding professor! But the workload is HEAVY. Not so much the reading aspect but just everything else on top of that. I took this class for credits and instantly regretted it. She also takes SO LONG to grade major assignments, and honestly she's a pretty tough grader. Just a warning!!!
This professor was very good.

Had her for ENL 220, we read four book which was hard since I'm a slow reader. Before or after almost every class there's a homework, but usually just a reflection that takes no more than 20 minutes. As long as you go to class and keep up with the reading, you'll be fine

She is nice but gives way more work than she needs to for a YA fantasy class.  I think it's insane that we have multiple assignments a week on top of readings and major assignment deadlines. I've been given less work in my 300 and 400 levels, definitely skip its just not worth it at all.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: she is really nice but she does not clearly explain your major assignments and takes a really long time to grade them. There are homework assignments due before every class that usually take a while. The readings that she gives for homework are 15+ pages long.

Dr.Howe is super nice and passionate about the subject- its clear she wants everyone to succeed! However it is a LOT of reading. If you are not a fast reader or dislike the topic, you may have a hard time. She's very easy to reach and helpful if you run into issues or need an extension.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Caring--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: She's very nice & you can tell she is someone who is passionate about reading & writing, & she cares about what students have to say during in-class discussions. However, she assigns WAY too much work. She will assign more work than your major classes or related classes. I would not take her class again because the workload is actually insane.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: I was not a fan of Prof Howe. For the first half of the year, her class was easy, but then she would randomly pile up a ton of work and she was hardly around. She has a lot of bias in her teaching. If you need to take ENL200 I would suggest there are better professors to have.

Prof. Howe is a caring professor who is always willing to help out her students. I found the topics covered in the course to be quite repetitive, with an emphasis on gender. Overall, her class was boring but its quite easy. I would recommend her.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: medium class, if you do the work you will pass. easy A as long as you come to class 3 MA's and a couple of homework and the work is due before class time. she helps if you need it. TAKE HER I RECOMMEND
Easy class overall, always taking about gender identity. I feel like if you don't pick something about gender to write about for your essays you can't get the highest grade.

I feel that this class helped me develop critical thinking skills and analytical skills far more than most English classes I'd taken prior. She's also incredibly easy to reach outside of class if you have any concerns, and there is a clear objective. Fantastic professor. Also knows how to properly use mycourses to keep grades and updates constant!
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Best English teacher at umd very nice  but her grading is lowkey confusing when I come the essay but overall good professor take it.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Accessible outside class--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Great professor and class. Take Disney Lit if its an option, easy and fun.

Took Disney in Literature w/ Prof. Howe and it was awesome! She is also very nice and funny! She's a bit of a harsh grader when it comes to essays, but if you keep up with your homework and other assignments it won't affect you too much! 10/10 would take other classes with her again!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Hilarious--So many papers

reviews.comment: Take her Disney course! She's the best! Many assignments to earn a good grade. Splits class to online and in person. Online must do readings, quiz, and annotations for Tues and on Thurs, watch a Disney movie and show up to class to talk about it. 3 papers and 1 final she made optional. So understanding, flexible with extensions, really cares!

I loved having her as a professor. I took her Fairytales and Adaptations class and actually enjoyed the readings. She has great discussions with the class and makes sure everyone gets a chance to share their thoughts. If you like fairytales, Disney, or just children's literature in general, her class will be really enjoyable.

Professor Howe is nice. She does not give much feedback on any drafts. If you want help from her you must go to her office hours. This class is heavily based on group activities and peer reviews. She normally accepts late work (up to a week late) and if you need extended time for an assignment she will give it without penalty if you ask in advance.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Amazing professor! As long as you pay attention in this class you will pass. She provides office hours the entire semester. She will help you edit your essays to protection. Would definitely reccomend her for any English class. She has a great personality too.

Lectures are very dull, splits the class into random groups a lot. Pretty much every class she finds a way to inject her opinions on women, gender roles and sexuality into the lecture, even though the course has nothing to do with it. Makes sense as she is a Children's Literature and Woman and Gender Studies major. Avoid this professor.

Professor Howe is amazing. I wish I could take all my english classes with her. Although she gives lots to read she is very understanding. She gives extensions on her papers. She also gives 4 skips. Which for a Tuesday Thursday class I think thats pretty fair, She is very approachable. Also gives great feedback and is always there to help, JUST ASK
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Shes awesome. Attendance is kind of mandatory. She gives you 4 skips until it starts hurting your grade. Her class is fun, she always talks about memes and Harry Potter. Funny stuff. She is a overall amazing teacher and I chose to have her next semester as well. I defiantly suggest taking her class��

If you want an easy A, don't take this class. I've received all A's on my essays in college so far, but on my first essay with Howe, I received a B-. She always wants you to use quotes, even if she doesn't say so, but she did allow me to rewrite a paper since she was not clear with the grading criteria. Participation is a part of your grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Professor Howe is a original professor. She cares about her work and wants her class to get as much out of the lesson as possible, a great choice. She is accessible and very understanding if you need to speak with her.

I'm having her for a third time and I'm so excited. Engaging class topics, easy essay assignments, small quizzes on the required reading (but you can manage with skipping some). 6 excused absences before they count against you. Very relaxed, but still gets everything done. Come in with a good attitude and you won't be disappointed.

I took her Magical Worlds Lit class and it was amazingly fun. The course is VERY reading heavy (around 100-150 pages per class) but the novels were of genres I'd read on my own free time! In-class lectures are discussion based. Don't take her class if you want an 'easy A'. All books are listed on the syllabus and she provides most of the material!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Not impressed, the class itself was fun and interesting but grades are based on a small amout of assingments and the grading of these are unnecesarrily tough. It makes it difficult to do well. I am an English Major, taking 3 writing foundation courses and yet she gave me one of the worst grades I've ever recieved on a paper.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: For English 101, you can't go wrong. Very explicit as far as what is expected and reminds students when stuff is due every class. There is a fair amount of "process work" which is basically just small tasks that lead up to an essay, but no biggie. She is quick to help and fix papers (drafts) which is a huge plus in my eyes.

Amazing professor in the sense that she is respectful to her students, understanding, and loves to engage with  the class. The down part is that she assigns tedious amounts of homework readings every class. Also prepare to read 200+ page books over the course of a week for each novel. Overall, most people pass with at least a B-
Requires group project worth 25% of grade for an ONLINE, ASYNCHRONOUS, SELF-PACED course, which automatically makes it not asynchronous and not self-paced!

Prof. Han was amazing! Super funny and actually knows his stuff. Homework and quizzes were manageable, and he gives study guides for exams which help a lot. Be sure to pay attention during his lectures because a lot of material is covered. Easily my favorite class last semester. Highly recommend taking him!

This was a tough class, so much homework and the group project was brutal. just be ready to started off reading the book and the lecture setup he has, but I quickly shifted to just skimming everything it just takes to much time the way he has it setup. its passable but the class just has information overload.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Having a group project in an online class be worth 25% of your grade is certainly a decision.

Prepare for in-person exams with a cheat sheet. Wiley Plus is used for homework and quizzes. Three exams are worth about 60 percent of your final grade not cumulative. Extra credit is given as well as practice exams and study guidance. Nice person, and lectures are ok.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Han is a good teacher but his classes are tough. He curves his tests, is easy to reach out to and there is a big group project. If you don't participate in the project or don't do the work, you will fail. He is the only professor who teaches ACT 444 at UMassD currently so you won't have any other options.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Professor Han is a solid choice for ACT 311, and styles the class for a CPA Exam prep. He makes the ACT 311 course harder than it needs to be, as he is very lecture heavy and a considerable amount of outside class time for reading and preparation was needed for me to attain a B+. 3 tests, HW and Quiz assignments for each of the 11 chapters.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Standard teach yourself professor but at least his lectures arent boring... oh wait. Dude gets a 3 because he allows a printed cheat sheet for exams. I covered that ish in size 5 font
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Han is truly invested in helping his students. When the course was in person, he always cracked jokes and got people laughing. Once the course went online (COVID19), he filmed YouTube videos for six chapters of content in a single week. Always accessible via email. For intermediate accounting, he makes it very manageable.

Had a hard time with class, but genuinely felt the prof was doing everything he could to explain the material. You. Have. To. Study. I probably put 4 hours a week outside of class into studying and once I had read the material the lectures were far easier to follow. Also really enjoy his lectures especially when they go off topic.

I really enjoyed this class. Exams aren't weighted extremely high so it is easier to salvage your grade/ is helpful if you are a bad test taker. Exams are based on the study guide, if you want to get a good grade I suggest taking notes on the powerpoints and notesby yourself, going over the problems in the slide, reviewing the homework and quizzes.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Han is one of my least favorite professors and I always regret taking him. It's my second class with him. His instructions are unclear, I never retain anything from his lectures, testing/grading is always weird, hard to understand, never goes over homework or tests, only take if you're good with google/self-teaching. Avoid if possible.

Professor Han is a good guy. He gives helpful study guides and tells you exactly what you need to know for the exam. He goes over hw in class sometimes / if asked. He takes attendance daily,  usually even before class starts. He gives quizzes every week, however you never fail. The lowest you can get on the quiz is a 7/10 even if you get all wrong
Good guy. Easy to follow and explains the concepts well
One of the worst professors I've ever had, first test had class average of 60, and he wouldn't scale them. His lecture are not helpful and his exams are extremely difficult and unclear

If you are taking his class and need this class as a graduation requirement I genuinely advise you withdraw from the class now, save your gpa the plummet, and take this class again in the future with a different professor. Literally ANYONE but him. He doesn't teach. You're lucky if he's even able to answer your one question in that one class period
Supposedly a "completely different professor" during office hours, which is great and all but he can't do that for every single student. Avoid at all costs, lest your gpa plummet.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The only people who I saw succeed in this class were people who have already taken Calc 1 and people who cheated on every test. don't expect the stuff your learning to be on homeworks. He provides practice sheets before every test, but he loosely follows them. His teaching style is odd and forces you to learn everything on your own time or you fail
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: In my experience with his calc 2 class, he was remarkable at making sure he was clear to everyone in the class about his teachings. Huff singled people out or walked around the room to make sure of this. at first it seemed uncomfortable, until I realized it's great use. I learned a lot, and I give him credit as my most effective math teacher.
So random, doesnt teach one topic at a time, he mixed it all together. Very messy. Barely explains the concepts.

For Gods sake never take this man!! He would laugh every time I didn't know how to do a problem. He dosent really teach he gives one easy example then puts more difficult problems on the board. He calls you up by row and tells you to solve the problems. His exams are SOMETIMES similar to the practice ones he gives. hope this helps .
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: So I obviously have an unpopular opinion of this man. He is very intimidating the first couple weeks and isn't the best professor. However, if you go to office hours and make it known that you want to do well, he's awesome. Tests are heavy but if you have a good math background you're solid.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: He speaks softly and to the board, he will give the thrum and expect you to understand and apply it. Often makes tables go to the board and solve problems, wich is nice but often with little instruction than do the problem #X on the board. His tests are tough, and you have to study even if you took ap calc in high school.  Stay on top of the homework, and turn them in a chapter by chapter. He nor his aid tells you when homework is due. All homework is hand-written. and you will not receive it back.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Professor Huff knows his material how ever you do not learn much. He is scattered all over the place which makes it confusing at the beginning when homework is due or where we are in the material. He does not teach, all he does is explain a few problems and makes you go up to the board and solve them.

Lecture method was poor. A topic would be vaguely introduced and then a group of 30 students would be left to gather around a few whiteboards with fewer markers/erasers  and even less feedback or direction. Homework is collected and and returned with very little feedback MUCH later. Exams were graded very slowly and were scrutinized. Killed me.

Professor Huff was a solid professor; nothing great, nothing bad. Most of his test have extra credit, homework is basically graded for completion, and he is a great "teacher". Can be a tad confusing sometimes but if you ask him to explain or go more in-depth he will.  Homework Average: 100 Test Average: 83 Final Grade: B

I enjoyed having Professor Huff. He teaches a little fast but if you can keep up, you will do fine. You pretty much pass the homework if you do it. His test are a little hard but he does extra credit (10 points worth) and the finals were EASY. He may not seem helpful but if you ask for it and go to his office hours, he is. MTH 153 & 154 grade: B-

This professor will bore you into the oblivion and ask you why you're not engaged after. She was good at getting us to meet and interact with each other. The homework is excessive given that it's an introductory course for college. Her grading is very strict. She asks for a lot from students and complains when students are fatigued.

Work Overload used 2 College Ruled notebooks by the end of her class completely just jams chapters and expects you to remember almost 20 pages of straight notes every 2 weeks in person exams that uses trick questions and wording she doesn't really want you to pass she wants to make this class as difficult as possible would never take her again

Professor hartnett was a good professor. These other reviews are true, she is a tough grader and takes points off easily, but she gives you all the material needed to get good grades on tests and class work. Her class works are like mini quizzes. She won't let you make them up if you miss them. Review the slides in depth before exams!!!

Shes a very nice person, but the way she grades is odd. she always finds a reason to not give you full points. She often uses phrases like "which of the following EXCEPT" or "which is FALSE" in the quizzes to throw you off. One person used chatgpt to complete their assignment and we all had to do in-person quzzies for the rest of the semester.

I am normally a student who gets good grades so to experience how difficult this teacher was to pass was honestly not the best experience when I took this course. Her graded is very different from typical professors, so coming into her course was a big game changer, I don't suggest taking this course if expecting a good outcome.

Extremely tough grader, assignments are required to be printed out and handed in class when due. She took points off my assignment because I handed in the paper assignment at 1:02 instead of 1:00 to use the bathroom. Very strict and nit picky with grading. Grades on grammar and spelling also. Would NOT recommend taking her class.

Although a very nice woman, she's extremely difficult to pass. I am a student who averages A's-B's, so imagine my surprise when I get a grade back from her and I got a low 68. Like hello? Although sweet, she will not really help you and expects you to understand the material no matter your condition. Take this class with someone else if you can.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: she is a very nice lady who i've talked to multiple times. however, she will give you no credit if you missed an activity. the course is very test heavy and you must read the textbook to do good on the test. doesn't really seem to care about the students and how well they do.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: When I spoke one on one with her, she was a friendly woman that I didn't mind talking to. But she grades very hard especially for a CAS class, and she is so hard on her students. The class was easy, but she was very critiquing and rude. She would call out students for going to the bathroom with their phones. Like bruh it's my phone tho??? Like what

Shes very knowledgeable but strict. If you are able to enjoy the content the workload won't seem as demanding. Shes able to explain things clearly and really encourages questions. Overall, she is a good teacher to have in your back pocket.
Her tests are rigged and designed to make you fail, I've never studied so hard for a test in my life only to end up with a C.
she doesn't help at all. your grade is based solely on tests so if you dont do well, you will fail. she is probably the worst professor ive had lol
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She doesn't consider students' outside responsibilities and lacks empathy as a psychology professor. Her exams are based on her views, and most of the class struggled/failed. Instead of taking accountability, she blamed us. She's rigid and unprofessional—I'd recommend avoiding her.

So sweet and passionate! Doesn't give homeowork but she posts chapter guides. They're not required though. They're very long but SO helpful. 4 exams, she drops the lowest one. So much extra credit you can do. She does do class activities that you can't make up but they're worth 3 points. There's also a quiz at the start and end of the semester.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Patricia Harriet is a very disrespectful professor and very difficult. She enjoys arguing back with her students, and isn't very helpful.  She has favorite students. I will not be taking any of these professors' classes again. I would advise you not to take any classes of her unless you want to have a very difficult semester.

I had to take Dr. Hartnett LifeSpan and Human Development course and as someone with ADHD she was one of the worst professors I have EVER had. She gives no room for better grades and gives no homework. The handouts she does give are sparse and she gives you less than 5 minutes to complete them. The tests are worded so poorly. I would NOT take this.

Incredibly passionate and intelligent professor. my favorite professor in terms of ability and what I believe matters to a student. She told us, her human lifespan class, that it was her favorite to teach, which may be responsible for the passion she had when teaching it. I have not taken her other classes. I recommend for all. show up.

I wouldn't say she is the most memorable prof, but she did her job well. PSY 220 wasn't too hard with her, and you just had to put work into the class. Many people who complain about her did not do the work. Overall the class was alright and PSY 220 was actually useful for real-life stuff like parenting. Lectures are a little boring sometimes.

she is a hard professor, tests are on paper and in class, she doesn't take attendance but she does give class work and she uses that to give you grades. she has 4 tests only one of them is open notes and a fill-in-the-blank quiz. She does give chapter guides and lecture notes YOU HAVE TO WORK HARD FOR YOUR GRADE. STUDYY
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Attendance is not mandatory but you will struggle if you skip a lot. Filling out chapter guides on time with lectures will also help, but class is still pretty difficult. Exams are pretty hard as well. She is a nice professor, just not very helpful.

We need to cut her some slack! Yes, she is an EXTREMELY tough grader, like a Ph.D. level tough grader, her feedback is awfully harsh, but she just wants her students to be the best they can be. I do agree that non-majors should avoid her but she is extremely knowledgeable. I do have empathy for Dr. Hartnett and appreciate her.

Worst teacher ever. Gives extra classes on the timings of other class. She thinks we only take her class. No matter how much effort you put in and study still going to fail. She makes tests at the Ph.D. level much more challenging than what she teaches in her class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: DO THE CHAPTER GUIDES! These are not graded but they do help a lot for exam prep because she includes information not on the notes. She is only intimidating in class, outside the classroom she is very understanding and nice to talk to. You do have to put in work to do well in her class.
Prof. Hartnett picks favorites. Get on her good side fast. She notices who doesn't show up for class.

Hard professor. Grading is very to the syllabus and she does not care what anyone has going on in their life outside of her class. If you dont go to class, you will not pass. Tests are based on the very long chapter guides she gives you. So if you dont do those, youre not passing the tests. Get the book for the guides. Study very ahead for tests.

This was one of the hardest classes I have taken in all 4 years I have been in umass. She is incredibly strict on her grading. If you are not good with tests plan on getting a low grade. Studying and study guides takes 10 hours+. Not worth it for a 201 class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Lacks sympathy for students having outside of school issues. Does not know how to answer emails. Not helpful at all

She treats her students like adults, is very kind, will offer feedback on assignments before they are due, and grading is divided pretty equally. I've heard students call her rude, but the worst I've seen is that she's a tough grader.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: She is very clear about expectations, and she really tries to help people. She gives outlines for each chapter so you only need to know info she asks about on the chapter guides, which is more effort on her and easier on us. Tests are easy as long as you study, and guides dont even take that long to complete. Very nice and friendly.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: She really doesnt care. I really dont understand people who become professors if they do not care about the success of the student. If I could give her a -0 I would Ive gone to this ladies office hours stayed late studying and still some how failed as most of the class did. She dosent allow you to make up assignments or retake them either

Patrice Hartnett is the WORST educator I have ever taken during my academic experience. Not helpful at all. Very irksome. She called me out for switching my camera off just to go to the bathroom. When I have asked for help, her reaction is very condescending. She expects you to just promise information easily when everyone learns differently

Dr. Hartnett isn't that bad. She tries to scare you in the beginning by saying her class is hard to pass , but she's actually a really intelligent and kind person. The way her class is structured really motivates you to put the effort in and work for it. Just make sure you read the textbook for her tests DONT JUST rely on info from the lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Never again.... Has no sympathy for her students and what they have going on outside of school.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: She is an expert on developmental Psychology but a horrible educator. She is not very approachable & lacks emotional regulation skills. Her behavior is unprofessional towards students & her grading is emotionally influenced rather than merit-based. These behaviors make her incompatible as an educator with many students & their learning needs.
She ruins your life , Her grading is terrible , Doesnt really teach  kinda rude

I have not had this much of a problem in a psychology course every in my life. I never understood what she was trying to go on about, she threw so much material at you all at once. The class average failed every exam. Would tell you to feel free to ask any questions, but when you do, you would receive a response that would make you feel dumb.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Avoid Professor at all cost. She grades all in class assignments. You can receive a zero on the assignment even if you showed up to class and attempted the work. Her test are heavy on book material. She does not give any study guides except printed handouts. She has no reviews. She speaks way to fast when lecturing. She's boring and not personal.

Usually I would never take a professor with such bad reviews but was forced to for my minor and let me say I can confirm all of the worst are true. Someone like her should not be an educator. Her exams are way too difficult for what the class is, you can study for hours and still receive a poor grade.

Hartnett is not as bad as everyone makes her out to be. She is nice but is a tough grader. I'm a psych major and I studied hard for tests but never received higher than a 78. She does give extra credit and the online quizzes are easy. She gives two finals in one final period, which stressed me out a lot. I would not recommend to nonmajors.

Patrice Hartnett is a smart woman who is extremely knowledgeable in her field. That being said, she is the worst professor I have ever dealt with. Her initial grading criteria is clear, but she rarely sticks to the rubric given. Her feedback is not well explained and very condescending. Avoid her if you can.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Patrice Hartnett is one of the most difficult and condescending professor I've ever met. She gives you unclear directions and takes points off if you don't follow them. Her comments on your work will make you feel like you're dumb. She has absolutely no empathy towards her students. she plays favoritism and is very deceiving. Shouldn't be teaching.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Extremely hard grader. you'll never get 100 on anything. tests are ridiculously hard and your not allowed to use your laptop or go to the bathroom.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I truly believe I was being set up to fail this whole class. You could study for hours on end and still end up with a 40 on a test. She is very rude and you would need to be her to even get a 90 on a test. This class felt like a 400 or 500 level course and has no right being taught by her. NEVER take a class with her.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Professor Harnett is very rude with comments inside and outside the classroom. For example constantly says in class you should know this from psych 101. Not everyone is a psych major or maybe people dont remember cause they took the class THREE YEARS AGO, gives tons of material and tests are impossible you can study for hours for a 50.

Patrice is a very wonderful person, however a hard grader. She basically tells you on the first day of classes that her class is hard to pass. She gives you material to study but it doesnt always seem relevant to the exams. You have online quizzes due each week and make sure you do them because thatll help your grade. Be prepared to study hard.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: THIS IS THE WORST PROFESSOR DO NOT TAKE HER!!!!!! Her exams have nothing to do with what you learn in class! She is available for extra help with writing assignments but never explains anything or what to change, and grades assignments too harshly! Also she gave us 2 finals in one day She really should just not teach at all Everyone I took this
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Shes very specific on what she wants done by her students so make sure you do every single thing in the class the way she wants it. Talking points are a big thing to do and the papers are easy as long as you follow the guidelines she gives.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: This is NOT an easy 101 class. If youre just in the class to fill a requirement, you wont pass. This is great if youre interested in psych but if this isnt a subject youre passionate about, good luck. Tests are hard and specific, iclicker questions are graded, attendance is graded, and LOTS of material.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: If you go to class, pay attention and study the day before, you will be fine. She doesn't post her lecture slides until 2 days before the test. Maybe opened the book once. She does take attendance and can be tough, but the class overall was extremely easy. She gave one extra credit assignment and the studies are an easy A. No laptops in class
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--EXTRA CREDIT--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: VERY DIFFICULT teacher. When the test comes you'll need to have studied for hours to even come close to passing. Grades very hard on writing assignments.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Do not be late! Study guides are extremely long almost 150 questions for each test and she only chooses 3 from each study guide for the exam. Cannot use laptop and freaks about seeing a cell phone. A online quiz for each chapter and 3 difficultly graded writing assignments.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: She is a professor who will blame her students for not doing well on tests, thinks it has nothing to do with her. She gives very long study guides and your test will have almost nothing on it in regards to what you have to study. She is rude, but relatively knowledgeable. I had to take her again because she was the only one teaching this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Took PSY 101 with her. Her tests, iClicker questions, attendance, and mandatory participation in a psych research study were only components of grade. Speaking of her tests... They're HARD! Get ready to study for hours on end to walk in and get a C. 60 straight up MC. Don't take this class if you're looking for something to sleep though. Nice lady.
reviews.rating_tags: TEST HEAVY--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Doctor Hartnett doesn't give any grades away for free, but if you study and read directions closely you will pass. She is very specific on grading policy. Do the study guides, study her handouts, and go to class and you'll do fine. She can have a real attitude sometimes though, usually when classroom policy is breached.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring--Tests are tough

reviews.comment: Absolutely loved her class, she made it very enjoyable. It wasn't overly difficult, but it was as challenging, as it should be. Her explanations are clear and the study guides she gives are extremely helpful.

So very nice. Expected to attend class. Hard grader on reading assignments that she assigns. The test questions really make you think, they are not your average only one answer is correct questions, they are ones where you need to pick the BEST choice. She is very friendly and willing to work with those who need extra help.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Prof. Hartnett is clearly passionate about what she does, which makes class more enjoyable. She states clearly what she wants from you in projects, but that being said she is a very tough grader if you don't have what she wants. She gives a lot of extra credit in class, so don't skip! She makes you work for your grade, but it's worth the hard work
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Horrible.  She words her tests difficult. You have to have a front and back thorough knowledge of the topics to do well on test by not getting tricked. Makes those with computers sit in the very last two rows. If you have a computer anywhere else than those rows she will complain. Attendence is taken with about 5 i clicker questions per class.

This was the second class I've taken with her and I would do it again if I could. She makes you work for every single inch in her class which leaves you feeling more self satisfied by the end of it. You can't blink during her lectures or else you'll miss crucial info, but this is easy because her passion for the material is palpable.

Dr. Hartnett is one of the nicest teachers I have ever had. She is a tough grader, but she is very clear on what she expects. She genuinely wants her students to do well in her class. She made the class interesting and gave us all of the material you will see on the tests. Do the chapter guides and take notes in class and you will be golden.

Dr. Hartnett is a nice person, her tests are tough and a little tricky. When it comes to the Late Adulthood Project her comments tend to be on the rude side. She's very strict with her classroom rules, but she tries to make the class enjoyable.

Love love love this professor! You will learn so much from her lectures. She can be tough; absolutely no cell phone use (she will call you out) and her tests are hard but do her study guides and you'll do fine. No homework,  2 short essays, last test is open notes. Talk to her one on one and she is incredibly helpful and so sweet.
Best professor I have ever had. Hardest grader but she cares so deeply for her students and wants everyone to do well and reach their full potential.

Awesome professor.  Although her grading can be tough, everything is graded clearly and with a lot of comments that allow you to learn from any mistakes. She is very professional and clear on instructions and grading criteria (usually a rubric that she hands out to students with the assignment).

Dr hartnett is a very sweet kind woman and genuinely cares about her students. However she is a terrible professor her teaching style is terrible claiming to be teaching a mastery course and to not worry about grades but grades so difficult that it makes it impossible to do well in her classes.  I would avoid her if at all possible

I absolutely adore Dr. Hartnett. I was enrolled in her Lifespan class and I wish I could retake it. Don't get me wrong- there is a lot of work involved to get a good grade. Showing up is key, and so is doing her chapter guides. She will help as long as you do your part. I cannot wait to hopefully take more classes with her, i loved her!

Class attendance doesn't count, but don't show up and you won't pass. She gives extra credit in class which makes a huge difference in your grade. She gives a chapter guide for you to fill in and test questions come right from it.  Need to study for tests to pass. You need the book. go to class, take notes, and study. you'll be fine.

Hartnett was an great PSY 101 professor, she was tough but it's because she wants you (as a psy major) to know your stuff. She writes out all the notes for you but she's a tough grader. Wouldn't recommend if you're looking for an easy A, she makes you work for it
worked my butt off to barely pass, her tests are TOUGH, expect to study for 3 hours to get a 70

Tests are based off lecture but words questions very difficult and makes them tricky, i got the same grades on tests and reading the book made no difference in my scores, must pay attention to everything she says

FOR ANYONE LOOKING FOR A BASIC ELECTIVE STAY AWAY. Yes, she is very approachable and willing to help if you talk to/email her for help, but her TESTS are VERY HARD. Pure 60 MC in 50 mins. Tests averaged between 68-72. Book is not mandatory but can help a lot if you put the time to reading. If she thinks you're not listening, her attitude unleashes

She is awesomeee!!! Not a lot of professors show genuine interest in students but she does. She is super helpful in everythingggggggg. Her exams and grades may be hard but as long as you study, do the study guides, and show up for class for extra credit, you will get the grade you deserve! I 100% recommend her! :D

Really nice, but class is really hard. You have to go to class, and sometimes she gives EC opportunities that REALLY end up helping your final grade. Test Q's are really ambiguous, but do good on the project & you should be fine. She is always willing to help, so take advantage & see her in office hours for extra help & help on the project- 2 parts

Hartnett is a sweet heart she cares a lot about students and though she is a hard grader she gives you a chance to pass assignments in early for corrections. She is always available for help and uses great techniques to explain difficult concepts to the class. Only thing that annoyed me was her intensity over spelling but overall she is great.

She's nice and everything but her class is very hard. She crammed the entire book to lecture even it's impossible for 1 semester. I never had below B on PSY and now, I got C- on this class. You have to buy a book and do all study guides in order to get good grades. She's a hard grader as well. I'm not a psych major but I took it to get better GPA.
Psychology 101 is a boring mess of a class.  But Hartnett isn't a bad person.  She's nice, but her grading system is random, her lessons can be dull, and she's kind of airy or distant at times.
The class is hard but the teacher is amazing. She grades hard and you have to follow directions carefully but she will work hard to help you understand the material.

In terms of teaching ability, probably the greatest teacher I've ever had. She gives incredible examples that make the material really understandable. It is a lot of work, but all her test questions are from the study guides so if you do them you should do fine. People say she's difficult but really she just expects effort from her students.
She is a great Professor! If you are not lazy and you do the work, you will get good grades in her class. she gives 4 tests and a lot of extra credit. I recommend her.

Professor Hartnett teaches lifespan more in depth that most professor. She admits the first day that he class in hard and that she expects a lot from her class since this class is for psych majors and you have to go to graduate school. She really believes she is helping you get ready for graduate school. Great professor after that

She is a wonderful teacher. I learned so much about lifespan development. Attendance is important as she takes many test Qs from class discussion. You definitely have to work hard for your grade, but it is fulfilling and worth it!! I would take her for every psych course if I could!

She is by far one of the best professors at UMD. I didn't really like PSY, but she made me grow to like the class. 10 quizzes only takes best 8 and 4 tests and only takes best 3 tests. HIGHLY RECOMMEND

Loved this class! Dr. Hartnett is extremely knowledgeable in this subject and can answer almost any question you throw at her. She gives funny examples and uses props when explaining to engage the class. Definitely not an instant A, you'll work for your grade- but it's worth it!

Professor Hartnett is great!! Class is challenging but as long as you make show up and take notes you can get a good grade. Gives study guides for each chapter. Some tests are open notes. She is always willing to answer questions and help out. Overall great class!

Good professor. She knew what she was talking about, and tried to make things as interesting as possible. Don't really need to buy the book. Take good notes in class and you'll be fine. Drops the lowest grades, its an easy A. Has mood swings alot, and isn't very friendly, but overall a good professor.

Dr. Hartnett is one of the best professors i've had.  She goes into great depth with the material and know's what she is talking about.  I would definitely recommend her to anyone interested in learning developmental psych.  She has high standards, but taking this course was very rewarding.

She is a horrible teacher. She is rude to everyone and always has an attitude. She is not approachable at all. She does not try to help the students out at all. She is an extremely tough grader on projects and papers too. I would NEVER take a class with her again it was miserable.

Her class can be hard, but she does make everything interesting, and you need to READ! Read the Chapters & fill out the Chapter Guides.  Other than that really pay attention to her lectures, everything she goes over will be on the tests.  She knows what she's talking about, so pay attention!

Took her for 101 and 220. Not the easiest professor by a long shot but she knows her stuff. You have to go to class and pay attention as well as read the chapters to pass. The chapter guides will save you when it comes time to study, so do not neglect them. She enjoys the subject and is very good at teaching it, but to pass, you have to learn it.

I know alot of people think shes difficult, but if you show up, pay attention, take good notes, and do the chapter guides you'll do fine.  She explains everything thoroughly and you know what you have to do from the start.  Do your work and you'll be ok.

Condescending of students, confusing, unhelpful! Even if you do study, good luck on the tests; they're really hard. i-Clicker questions in EVERY class and she does not give credit even if majority of the class gets the question wrong. Second time taking psych, and this was more confusing than before even knowing some material going into the course!

she made everything so much harder than it actually was. it was like high school material but her explanations just made everything so much more confusing. her tests are rediculously hard for a 101 class, esp when most of the kids are only in their as a non-major elective.

I had her for pysch 101 and she makes the class so much harder than it has to be. You barely use the book except for studying, but when i did study i got lower grades on the tests than when i didn't. The tests are really hard and she gives you tons of information. Don't take her if you don't have to.

test are much harder than they should be. she got too frustrated with the lecture hall. most people were not doing well because of her ridiculous tests. but in the end she scaled up alot so i did fine.

Hated going to class. she is probly a good teacher in a small class but not in a large class. she is excited about the materal but she made me not like psyc when  used to before. go to class everything she sas is on the test EVERYTHING. has hard tests

Her tests are really easy if you study. Attendance is mandatory but she doesn't count it. She counts clicker questions only if you get them right. She includes questions from the book even if she doesn't go over it, and she will keep you until the very last minute.

Strict w/ attendance but shes a pretty great teacher. I learned a lot which kept me wanting to attend my 8am class despite the time. DO THE CHAPTER GUIDES, theyll save your exam grades, I didnt and couldve had an A. Easy/interesting project & short papers. Fair & usually pleasant in class if youre not late or absent too much. I recommend overall.

She knows her stuff and really gets into it. You can tell she loves developmental psych. Very helpful and sweet. Go to her office hours and she'll love you. Do your work and you'll get a good grade.Tests aren't difficult if you do the chapter guides and show up to class.

This child psychology class was overall interesting. Her tests can be somewhat difficult if you do not put the time in to do the chapter guides or read the book. She does take attendance but in her syllabus it says its not mandatory but to do well you should go. Overall good class but for an elective.. kind of tedious.

She keeps teaching till last second. She teaches you quite a few things that are not on the class. Why make attendance mandatory if you could just use the chapter guides(must spend lots of time to fill in) and pass the test?

She is very nice usually, but watch out when she's angry! Class is NOT easy. Mostly because questions are worded badly and are not clear. (Situations where there could honestly be more then one answer - things like that.) And she really likes to take off points on HW! You must go to class even though it is "not" mandatory. Would not take her again.

You will need the books. Do the chapter guides. They'll seem tedious, but she takes test material from them. The case studies are somewhat interesting. She's definitely a mom, but she tries not to be biased. Overall, not too bad.

She is a very tough teacher, she does give hard tests, but if you take the notes well those will help. DO NOT WORK WITH ANYONE ELSE ON ASSIGNMENTS!!! She will give you a zero for working with other people on assignments. She is helpful when you ask for it, but do not take her class expecting an easy grade, you need to work for it!
Very boring class. She takes attendance too. She'll keep you until the final second every class. Tests are pretty hard. Try to get into a different class if you can.

She gives many small assignments and her test are somewhat hard. You need to read the book and do the chapterguides in order to do well on the exams. Make sure you take notes down too. She also curves at the end of the semester. Sometimes she can be boring but the material toward the end of the semester is interesting.

Really nice professor. If you go to class you'll do great. The notes she gives during her lectures are veryyy helpful. She does take attendance. I didn't use the book; there's only one assignment where you'll "need" it but if you don't go to class you might need it. The class is interesting and it's really pretty easy.
cute, but would not recommend

I never leave reviews but when I saw Hasanoglu had a low rating, I had to. If you attend class, listen, think and treat him like a human being you will pass easily. His class is more about thinking through ideas and problems rather than memorizing facts. He is very laid back, funny and will treat you like an equal. Best class I took last semester.

He sounds exactly like Archer's voice actor (H. Jon Benjamin). Like, it's uncanny. Easy enough class (I took PHL 110 online) but he has questions that are sometimes tricky and seem intended to trip you up rather than prove that you read the material. YouTube videos for lectures are removed on the day of quizzes--a strange tactic to prevent cheating

Prof Hasanoglu himself is funny but its honestly a bit much for a classroom environment. He didnt teach much of anything in the beginning of the semester but once we had our first exam, it consisted of material that was not taught enough in class. Would not take him, do not recommend. instead of trying to be funny he just actually teach.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Do not take this class. Grading is based on exams, papers, attendance, and participation. There were about 5 total assignments this semester. Your whole grade slips if you don't do well on one. Exam content is more difficult than what is reviewed during the lectures. The professor is not very communicative outside of class.

He is a really nice professor who cares for his students and really focuses on lectures and that is where you learn all the information you will need throughout the semester. We had about 5 assignments all semester including 3 papers, a midterm, and a final.

professor hasanoglu is very unique, and i think that makes him a hit or miss. he is funny, but be warned that if you slack off your grade will suffer. attending lecture is the only way to pass class, and the tests are more difficult than expected. it is important to participate and be engaged, otherwise you will not understand the material.

whatever you do AVOID HER CLASS. she seems like a super sweet woman but she clearly states on the first day of class to drop if you can't understand her well but doesn't take into consideration to speak a little slower so everyone can actually understand her. she doesn't even teach properly. your grade is also only the four exams you take that sem

the worst prof ive ever had. if u need it for a requirement, suck it up but u will hate every second. lectures are just her belittling you and wanting you to be wrong. i learned nothing from this class, lectures are pointless- there really is no information in them and just trick questions. study the study guides and youll pass but classes are ruff
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Just don't take this class she doesn't know what she's talking about the exams are literally different from the readings and her notes, avoid this class spare yourself that headache i had to drop that class because it would've affected my gpa and im a freshmen watch out
thought this class would be easy but the professor made the class difficult.

This was the worst professor I've ever had. She has no idea how to do her job, constantly shows bias, and creates a toxic environment. It feels like students run the class while she's loud and consistently wrong. I honestly don't know how she got or kept this position. If you value your sanity, avoid this class at all costs.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: teacher is so terrilble. everything you are required to know for exams is not taught during class, but you have to watch hours of videos and readings on your own and it is not reviewed in class. She is mean and loves to embarrass you

Horrible teacher who asks for opinions but then says your wrong no matter what I had to answer a question about if the word 'no' actually meant no and I said that it does mean no and she literally said "no means keep trying or yes" do not take her !

she does have an accent but i personally do not think its that bif of a deal like some people say. Shes kind of fresh ngl, especially if she notices you are not participating or doing other work not from class. lots of reading (i didnt do it tho) and 4 exams made ur grade (lowkey cheated cause it was online on mycourses and so did half the class)

Her grading scale is very test-heavy. She has a strong accent and will tell you to drop if it bothers you - I wish I did and encourage you all do. Participation is only 5% of your grade, the tests are worth 95%.

Not as bad as people say. Sometimes lectures do get into awkward subjects, she is definitely a tough grader and there is a ton of reading to do for exams, but if you see her after class, she will help you lots. It really seems like she wants her students to succeed and pass. I recommend her class but not all the hate is unfounded.

Honestly, she's not quite as bad as some of these reviews say, the class isn't that difficult but its graded very heavily on the tests so if you're not a good test taker it's probably not for you. I wouldn't recommend this class but some of the hate is blown out of proportion

This was by far the worst professor I have ever had! She consistently made students uncomfortable and was beyond disrespectful. She enjoyed humiliating her students and in my honest opinion deserves to be fired for her poor teaching skills, lack of communication, and unorganized syllabus, lectures, and study guides. DO NOT TAKE A CLASS WITH HUAYHUA
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: This professor was a difficult class,even though there was no mandatory assignments except for group work presentations, and exams.The exams are on the reading and videos, and the study guide given for each exam will be your saving grace to pass this course. Its important to go to offices hours when you're struggling, because she will help, kindly.
way too difficult for an intro class, shes always on the offensive. too much weight on tests and way too much content for at home that lecture seems pointless.

reviews.comment: Professor Huayhua sets an absurdly high standard to meet for an introductory class. I would not take her class ever again. I was taking this for a credit and is very hard to pass her class as she assings hours or reading and documentaries to watch every week. She yells at students when we don't know the answer. Horrible professor.

One of those teachers who thinks everything revolves around her class.If you spend as much time on this class as she expects you to your other classes will suffer. I suggest only taking this class if you've already gotten your important classes out of the way. Not exactly a class you look forward to.

Unorganized, disrespectful, and just a mean person. Loves to criticize everything. She tells us we aren't paying attention, will tell people to leave the classroom, will come over while in groups and tell us we aren't doing work. Her work outrageous for a 100 level. 3 hours of videos 30 pages of reading with 20 questions every week is way too much.

reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: One of those teachers who will ask the class if they understand and takes our silence as a yes when in actuality everyone is so confused that they dont even know what they dont know
no bueno.

Assigned a project worth 20% of our grade and didnt go over it at all and only gave a very unorganized and contradictory rubric. When most of the class did the assignment wrong, she blamed us for "not paying attention". i honestly surprised she hasnt been fired yet because every student who has had her thinks she is a horrible and rude teacher.

The guidelines say not to claim a teacher shows bias or favoritism but....

also she is very unorganized, inefficient, doesnt explain her assignments then gets mad at the class for it
She assigned tons of papers and videos every week but didnt really teach anything herself. She is also stubborn and just not a good teacher

This is perhaps the worst teacher I've ever had. She is unorganized, hostile towards her students, she has high expectations but doesnt teach anything, and she doesnt explain anything. Everyone in the class had expressed how lost they were, and by the end of the year most of the class was failing. She blamed us for this, or course.

Very rude and unhelpful toward students. Doesn't actually teach the class, you have to teach each other based on your readings. Very unorganized. Class lacks structure. Everyone leaves confused and frustrated. Assigns a lot of readings/documentaries, so if you don't have much time in your schedule for excess work, I would not recommend.

reviews.comment: Horrible teacher, tried to email her about my grade and work and she is not cooperative or helpful.
Horrible teacher, tried to email her about my grade and work and s

worst teacher ever. She would contradict herself. She's also harsh, doesn't assist pupils improve, and doesn't clarify projects. When we told her we were struggling, she blamed the class for being nasty.

This was by far the worse professor I have ever had. Professor Huayhua doesn't upload on my courses she is insulting, loud, and disrespectful and enjoys degrading her students in front of everyone. This professor constantly gives out random grades and does not give feedback and she is never prepared. You will end up reporting her to title 9.

seems like a nice lady but she's a terrible professor. Pretty much have to teach yourself everything as her lectures are confusing and she can be hard to understand sometimes with her strong accent. Only grades are the 4 exams which are actually not very hard but are long. She can be kind of rude sometimes and argues with students occasionally.

reviews.comment: She does not like answering questions, usually responding with "it is very clear if you do the reading" which I did. She always sounds like she is yelling and calls people out in the middle of class. Confusing explanation of topics, she left it largely up to the students to teach themselves and each other. Not a good class, not an accessible prof.

The bad reviews are those who did not take the time to understand Professor Huayhua. While her accent does make for a difficult time understanding sometimes, her lectures are very engaging, and her tests are based off of lectures/materials. If you can keep up with your own course load.. you can get an A in this class.

Very nice lady, awful Professor. Always shows up to class late and although she is passionate about anthropology, her lectures are rushed and unprepared and consist of very unorganized activities. Tests are based off of stacks of readings and films, so you must do the readings and homework to do well. If you hate reading, dont take this class.
