reviews.comment: The material in this class was interesting, but I definitely do not recommend this professor if you dislike heavy reading oriented courses, as you will often have to read long documents or watch 40+ minute documentaries nightly.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: There is a lot of reading, documentaries, and worksheets due  every week. There is no textbook but she posts reading assignments on my courses. The material in this class is very interesting, but there is a lot of information. She is a tough grader and there are only 3 exams but there are many quizzes that add up to count as a 4th.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: This class is actually super easy as long as you answer everything liberal leaning you will be fine. Just overall this lady is awful. She is just mean and will try to be embarrassing and condescending in front of everyone. Easily thee worst teacher I've ever had.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: DROP THIS CLASS. Her class is a total snooze fest. She lectures from the beginning to the end of class. Her accent is very thick, often making her difficult to understand. She does not accept late work, and she often comes across as condescending when you ask her to clarify on anything. I should've dropped her class when I had the chance.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I had to take this class as an elective. She knows what she's teaching well and she really tries to make her students understand the class, however, she has a very thick accent and is hard to understand. No homework but lots of long readings and videos are required to read and watch so you can pass the quizzes. Overall, she's alright.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Dr. Huayhua is extremely passionate about anthropology and wants her students to succeed. I appreciated that she told seemingly uncaring students that they should not come to class. During the last class, she gave us the opportunity to advise her on how to improve the course during future semesters. Overall, I learned a lot from this course.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Although you can tell that she is passionate about teaching, her thick accent is hard to understand. Her lectures are extremely fast and confusing and she does not post them online. One article required for every class, about 30-45 pages a week. The quizzes and tests are difficult but not impossible. My 400 level classes are easier than this class.

Must put in a lot of work to do well in her class. I did not like her at first, but she grew on me by the end of the semester. Highly recommend going to her office hours for help. DO the readings or you will struggle in class. Research paper is a large portion of your grades for the class. One midterm and one final.

I've taken Prof Huayhua twice, both for electives. Her thick accent is hard to follow, and she means well but her class can be difficult. She's a tough grader, and makes you work for her grade. Taking notes/ attendance doesn't matter, and she grades based off her opinion. If you like to read 30+ per class and re-write her thoughts, then take her

He is an okay professor but he doesn't provide any resources so if you miss a day you are going to be severely behind unless you can self teach. He also didn't post any grades for the entire semester so your grade is going to be a mystery because there is no syllabus provided so you can't calculate your own grades.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This professor expects you to already know everything he is teaching. I found myself really confused the majority of the time because he was teaching something I had never seen before without explaining how to do it or where students went wrong. His reviews for the test never help with the exam either. I suggest switching the professor.

I would say that Huff is a 4/5 professor for the average student. If you are taking him, understand that your participation in class will heavily determine how you do. Do the problems in class. I participated heavily and was able to get an A. This is not a slideshow class, you must attend and be present and take notes. Not as bad as people say IMO.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He is a kind but getting towards the age of retirement, especially with his quality of teaching. He explained many of the concepts extremely briefly and would ask students to replicate it on whiteboards right after. Over half my class had to withdraw. I would recommend looking for someone else unless you are able to self-teach well.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He knows a lot about the subject but he couldn't teach it very well. He is alright during office hours, but not during class time. We were a week behind where we should've been, and were taking tests with content we had yet to learn. The TA was a savior for me. Also, Huff doesn't read emails. I do not recommend.

Horrible Teacher. Probably around 80-85 years old, so should be retired. Constant mistakes while writing topics on the board, and doesn't use numbers, its all theory. I have him at 8 am and he always shows up late, but when you show up even 30 seconds after him, he asks you why you were late. Doesn't enter grades so you cant tell how you're doing.

I'm in Pre Calculus currently and all he does is shuffle into the classroom and starts writing on the board. He then barely explains the topic and calls us up by rows to solve the problems on “slates”. The homework doesn't follow the lectures or exams and I've been having to learn it myself. Overall, difficult, but the TA is making it bearable.

Melvin Huff is my goat. He legit gave me infinite chances to get a better grade. He never scaled because he never had to everyone always did so well. I wish he taught more classes. He makes lots of funny jokes and really made calc 1 and 2 enjoyable. Let him boost your gpa you will be so happy you took his class.

GO TO CLASS.
DO FANTASTIC ON FINAL.

that's all he will grade you on and that just it. but he's a good guy deep inside.

Though he was able to walk us through the topics, he wasn't answering his emails. Often we would be brought up to solve an equation on the board and some students weren't able to understand the problem. Other than not responding to emails, the only way we would be able to communicate with him is during/after class and during his office hours.

While I had him as a professor, I didn't like him; he had messy handwriting, was very soft-spoken, and had boring lectures. Once I had other Calc professors, I regretted disliking him so much during his class. Most Calc professors have bad writing and boring classes, but at least Huff makes the effort to help you understand the material.

If you want to learn math and have a good laugh at the same time, take a course with Professor Huff. He's the funniest guy I've ever met and has a natural talent for making people laugh. His jokes and stories are the highlight of every class.

I had the pleasure of taking a course with Professor Huff, and I have to say, he is an amazing teacher. He has a gift for explaining difficult concepts in a way that makes them easy to understand, and he always takes the time to ensure that his students are on the right track.

If you're looking for a math professor who is both knowledgeable and approachable, look no further than Professor Huff. He is an excellent teacher who goes out of his way to make sure his students understand the material. He is also very friendly and easy to talk to, which makes the learning experience all the more enjoyable.

Professor Huff is an outstanding educator who truly cares about his students' success. He creates a positive and engaging learning environment that makes math fun and accessible. I would recommend him to anyone looking for a top-notch math professor.

I took two courses with Professor Huff, and both were amazing. He has a talent for breaking down difficult concepts into manageable pieces and explaining them in a way that makes sense. He is also very approachable and always willing to answer questions and provide extra help.

Professor Huff is hands down the best math professor I've ever had. He is incredibly knowledgeable about the subject matter and has a real passion for teaching. He makes even the most complex concepts easy to understand, and he always go the extra mile to help their students succeed.

Overall, I highly recommend Professor Huff to anyone looking for a math professor who is both knowledgeable and approachable. He made calculus fun and accessible, and I feel confident that I have a solid foundation in the subject thanks to his excellent teaching.

Professor Huff honestly changed my life. His passion for mathematics made me realize there is something more to it that I did not realize, so I am now taking a math major, and really enjoy learning the intricacies of this tough subject. Take him if you want to be inspired, he makes the tests easy, and scales as needed.
He is a great professor, wants all his students to succeed! I really enjoyed this class
Really enjoyable class! I would love to take him again, he was very engaging throughout, and grades exams fairly, weighing them and everything!
I really enjoyed his class, Huff is really caring about his students, and ensures everyone makes it with a good grade!
Greatest professor ever! I hope I can get him again next semester
This guy is the best professor you will take! My only complaint was that the class was just a little too easy but I still enjoyed his lectures.
I love this guy! His class was amazing I would love to take it again.
Huff is a funny professor, his lectures were great, and I really understood the material he gave to us.
Professor Huff is an excellent professor, I enjoyed his lectures thoroughly, and he made himself available outside of office hours. He scaled this semester which was amazing!
He is a great professor, a very structured class, he scales the tests and makes it very easy to pass. Take if you want an easy A in calculus.

Lectures tend to be scattered, as he seems to do it all off the top of his head with minor supplement from loose papers. Handwriting is occasionally illegible. Class persistently averaged 40-70% test grades which he does not scale. Does not give extra credit.

Huff knows his math. And he can teach that pure math. My main issue with him is that he doesn't do anything else. Huff is bad at communicating work due dates, grading criteria are iffy, and if you get lost in class it's usually up to you to catch up. Stay on top class and you'll be fine, but if you fall behind or want to learn more, it's up to you.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Huff is ruthless. If you don't have a strong math background the class is so much harder. He is really bad at using tech but he's nice. I never missed class and I still almost failed. If you don't understand, ask questions or your grade will suffer. The tests are worth a lot and no retakes or extra credit. I wouldn't take him again even if paid.
Hes a funny guy

His way of "teaching" is highly disorderly and extremely hard to follow, especially if it's your first time seeing the material. He doesn't warn you about when homework is due during class, leaving you to find out yourself if you're falling behind or not. His writings on the board are messy and all over the place. Teaches at a fast pace as well.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Prof. Huff is a sweet guy, and he's really nice, especially if you're struggling in the class. If this is your first introduction to calculus, you may not fare well unless you took it prior to umass.
Doesn't enter ANY GRADES

reviews.comment: I took calc in high school and had an incredibly hard time with this class. He does not put grades in, does not answer his emails, and teaches things in a way that doesn't make sense. So many people in my class ended up dropping bc they could not pass. 10/10 do not recommend.

If you've taken calculus in high-school this is an easy A because the tests don't go too far beyond the basic concepts, and all the homework is open from day one. If you haven't taken calculus, it will be very very hard. Only reason he doesn't get a 1/5 is because he is chill and you can get an A without BS if you know what you're doing.

reviews.comment: So, contrary to most reviews I didn't mind the Huffster too much. I recommend him.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: This class definitely not for everyone. Huff's lectures can be confusing and teaches in mass, either ask a lot of questions or learn it on your own. reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: ugh. your grade is practically made up of 4 tests (including the final) so if you flunk it, that's it. he's participation heavy. goes down the row and asks questions and has rows go up to the board. he mumbles and write downs his notes then erases them too fast for you to write down. i'd rather take my high school teacher's AP Calc AB class again
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: 83% of the class failed the most recent exam, he blamed everyone, said there would be no scaling, and said "withdraw or work harder" That alone says enough.

I am convinced this teacher wants his students to fail. Poor and confusing explanations of the material, denies you all resources during tests, left with absolutely nothing. As if that wasn't the worst of it, he purposely puts questions on the test that confuse you, his tests arent to see understanding. Take the class to lose your scholarships.

Where to begin with this one... 1) Mumbles during explanations, makes a lot of mistakes that STUDENTS correct 2) No calculator, formula sheets, or notes on tests 3) Confusing explanations, I've heard my classmates say "WTF he calls this teaching?" 4) No extra credit 5) In tandem with 4 and 2, tests are 85% of your grade, meaning high chance of fail

reviews.comment: Prof Huff is an awesome professor. He genuinely wants you to pass and will give points back if you meet to go over the exam. He pushes back due dates if you ask him, too. Take advantage of office hours, and participate because it will only help YOU. Calc is a touch class because of the material. Huff is here to help.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: This was the worse Math Professor I have ever had. On the first day of the class he didn't even introduce himself just jumped right into "teaching". He makes you go up to the board in rows to answer questions. He doesn't talk about homework at all or won't tell you went anything is due. The tests are extremely hard no calculator allowed.

Huff is my FAVORITE. From making really bad jokes to dressing up in class- you name it all. He's an amazing teacher if you have a good understanding with math. Being in his class makes you learn a lot. Beware because he does make people go up to the board in rows to do examples but it's a good way to learn because you're actually learning stuff.

reviews.comment: Great Professor, has a great way of being able to interact with every kid in the class which is tough to do when its a room full of 45 students.

reviews.comment: This man is the worst professor I have ever taken. Between arriving 20 minutes late to class, to not responding to emails, to mumbling and pointing at random scribbles on the board, his class is impossible. Also he assigns mad amounts of homework without actually telling you there is homework. If you have him, beware and try to switch ASAP.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Huff is a great teacher and very down to earth. He states that if you do not know something on homework "I probably did not teach it to you so do not do it" He tries to keep students engaged and seems to do a really good job doing so. His tests count for most of his course but says that he wants to see overall growth and cares about it.
Professor is amazing. I received a D in calc 1 and took him for Calc 2 and got a B. Most kids will fail his exams, just don't be one of them. Take him if you have the option too.
He mumbles and points at his scribbles on the board. Does examples and won't explain anything
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: It was really a hard class to pass he lectures doesn't even makes sense he jumps from one subject to another and don't give many examples which would make sense and he gives out heavy amount of HW and don't even grade him self and has his TA do it for him. I work never take his class useless it mandatory.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: His tests are tough and he doesn't allow calculators on them. His homework is very unorganized and you don't know when anything is due . The only good thing is that you can pass in late homework for full credit, but it can be hard to make up if you get behind on it.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: His lectures can be very convoluted and confusing, especially if you haven't taken any AB/BC Calculus in high-school. I found myself teaching myself most of the material because his explanations were horrible. Switch out of his class if you can.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: This is the worst math professor I have ever had. The man doesn't know his own syllabus and doesn't pay attention to what homework is assigned. He teaches in groups and gets everyone to the board to do questions no one else can see. He assigns homework that has nothing to do with the lectures. You have to drill him for answers. Avoid at all costs.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: If your a good math student and you work really hard you should get an A, otherwise it's a bad idea.  Homework is only worth 10 percent and the test are a whopping 90% which makes this class really bad for anyone that isn't good at math and doesn't work very hard. He's a good professor, don't be afraid to ask questions.

I have taken calc before and I would pay money to have my H.S. teacher again. He was not particularly clear but would help if you asked. Tests are worth 90% of your grade and there are only 4 including the final, which is cumulative. The tests are hard. Homework is done on paper and you will get 90% if you do all problems. Due day after the test.
If you do not have a strong Calculus base before taking his class, you are in trouble.
Homework every night, all collected at once the class after the test.
Hes great if your good at math, if not beware

Took him for calc 1 and did fine. Thought he was a good dude so i took him again for calc 2. Literally had 4 or 5 grades all semester. All but one of them being exams. Gives really tough tests and no extra credit or assignments to help balance out your grades. Now i'm a semester behind because of poor teaching. GOOD GUY, POOR PROFESSOR.
Challenging professo. Be prepared and ready for a tough semester.
good teacher. allways late to class

THe man is a living legend. Proper style, knows his stuff, and he cares if his students do as well. Make an effort to see him after class if you have any problems. He will ask you to go to the board and do problems; give it your best effort and he will help you complete the problem. Has a great sense of humor as well. Would recommend!
i rate 8/8

First the nice. real nice guy, tries to help and enjoys teaching. THE BAD. His grading policy makes no sense partial work full work, wrong or right answer doesn't matter he's all over the place. lectures are decent. super disorganized. if you can self teach calc, take him. else beware

Melvyn Huff is witty and has a good sense of humor. Through his lecturing you can tell he enjoys teaching the theory behind calculus, and this makes the class more interesting, plus its nice to have a math teacher who doesn't have an accent. His exams and quizzes can be difficult but its worth it to take his class.

Some people like him, others can't stand how he teaches. I loved the class and think he's a phenomenal instructor. Willing to help you understand. Homework re-enforces the lectures. Review sessions prepare you for the exams. Gives you as much time as needed for the exams, so if you try hard, learn the material, and take your time, you'll do well.

I had Huff for Calc II and came into his class after a really rough time with Calc I and left his class with a MUCH better understanding. He really truly cares about his students and if you show initiative and make him see that you want to pass, he will go out his way to help you. One of the nicest teacher's I've ever met. HIGHLY recommended!

Entered class with a genuine interest in calculus. I really enjoyed this class. He does lecture rather formally but this worked well for me, while it didn't for others. I think he's a great teacher. Cares about students, good sense of humor, shows high interest in his subject. Challenging and rewarding for engineers. Should be lecturing at MIT.

Dr. Huff is a funny and friendly professor, and is always wiling to help. He offers a lot of ways to keep your grade from plummeting. The one thing that really brings him down is how bad he is at explaining things. He's way too formal with the math and I was often lost in what he was trying to explain.

Prof. Huff is a great change of pace. After taking a few math classes here, it's really refreshing to finally have a professor who not only understands your questions and tries to help, but one who speaks English. Do the homework and actually study for tests and you'll do great in his class.
He is an awesome teacher. His teaching method is not the best but you catch up really fast. Do your homework and go to his office hours and youll be good.

very enthusiastic teacher, who actually knows and cares about his students. I recommend taking his class if possible, he will help anyone who asks and if he knows you are trying will give you the necessary help.

I had Professor Huff for Calc I and Calc II. Calc I was terrible. I didn't understand his teaching method. Second semester, I understood and it's much easier now. Don't write down every example, watch sometimes. Tests are hard but he offers stdents who get below a 70 to get ten more points up to 70. Easy quizzes. Lots of homework. Wants you to pass

Prof. Huff is a great guy and really wants to help. Hes one of the best 1v1 tutors and is funny. He teaches a very roundabout way to do things and teaches theory's behind the math which is more confusing than helpful. All in all good class and will take again.

The engineering section of calc II are a little hectic. Huge class size and the material isn't easy. Huff is a great teacher who really lectures well. If you need help go to his office hours. He is a great one on one tutor in his office hours.

Class was rather annoying, most information you can gain from someone else or in the book. He's never clear and goes on about a subject for a long time. He is quite willing to help but his availability is quite limited. "Enter at your own risk."
Just put in the effort and show him you are trying.  Seek help and he will reward you with a good grade.

I wouldn't say that Huff was my worst Calc professor so far, but he could show some improvement. He was hard to follow at times, but if you asked him anything he would really help you out. At times he did lack control of the classroom at times, which was distracting. Overall, I would look to take a class with him in the future.
Absolute worst math teacher I've ever had. Run as far away as you can. Nothing he teaches makes any sense. I passed by the skin on my teeth. Teaches everything in a complete roundabout way.

Very hard class. He's a nice guy and will always help you out with any questions. Very smart but hard to understand him. If you can avoid him do it, but it's not impossible he his pretty generous on tests.

Avoid at ALL costs. He is all over the place when teaching. Goes from one concept to another without even telling the class what he is teaching. In short don't take a class with him. I don't doubt he knows what he is saying, he just doesn't know how to convey that info to the class.
Do not understand anything he teaches. Possibly the most difficult class you will take

The amount of reading for this class was definitely overkill, but otherwise no complaints! She's really chill, and is willing to help out students after class. Discussions in class are also very productive, and help a lot with the essays. Never gives a hundred though.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Seems like she'd be chill outside of class but she does not give af about ur gpa. She never gives 100s, one of those teachers who thinks her 3 credit class should be evaluated like a NASA entrance exam. Dont take unless ur lookin to drop your 3.6 to a 3.4
This teacher is extremely unorganized, NEVER RESPONDS TO EMAILS!!!

I don't understand how a professor can relate every single reading to themselves- Prof. Harrison often goes on tangent's which makes the entire material confusing. Accuses you of using AI when you don't meanwhile they use AI to discover AI which is completely unreliable in itself. Steer clear from this woman unless you just wanna be given 0's.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: do urself some service and avoid her if u can. Will accuse you of using AI even if you didn't (she uses ai detectors to grade work lol, ironic right?). Material is not hard AT ALL, but somehow manages to go on 50 min tangents about things that has nothing to do w/literacy. Some professors shouldn't be allowed to teach and this prof is one of them.

She is a pretty easy grader. I had her for two different classes last semester. If you want to take an English class where English is not talked about as much but her political views are, TAKE THIS CLASS!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Do not take her! She will only talk about herself and not even the books that YOU paid for…you will sit there for the 75mins and think why would you ever choose this class…and she will grade everything last second.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: The best professor ever. She made us get a couple books; you can find them on those free download sites, but if you do buy them, they're cheap. I have not enjoyed an English class since 8th grade, and she brought my love out again. The feedback she gives on papers is phenomenal; her lectures are amazing, she's funny, and she was meant to teach.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Prof. Harrison was really nice. You will have to read quite a bit but as long as you stay on top of what you have due that week you'll get an easy A.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: I liked going to this class. There were two big papers and before every class, you are supposed to submit a journal on the assigned reading for the class. The topic of my class was suburbia in the 1970's. Participation isn't required for the class but it's a big part. I'd take this class again and it was an easy A in my opinion.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Great teacher. Should have failed the class but she gave me a second chance and motivated me to do better

Incredibly disorganized professor. Her grading is hard and all over the place. Our class topic was on family drama but we did group presentations on something completely unrelated. She doesn't reply to emails and when she does they make no sense at all. Required to read 7 different books for class, all of which you have to buy. Avoid her if you can

I love Prof. Harrison! I have chosen to take her twice to satisfy my English requirement, and there's a reason for that. You are never left feeling overwhelmed, and she truly cares about her students.She is not the best at answering emails. Visit her during office hours if you think you need to clarify anything. Take her if you can!
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She hardly replied emails and was a very tough grader. If you are not a literature person, do not take this class. You would be alone. Even when you explain that yoou are struggling in the class. Also, once she thinks you are a bad writer she keeps on failing you. I literally wrote one of my papers with a tutor and still got a low grad
Genuinely cares about students and wants them to succeed. Easy A as long as you show up and do the assignments. She gives loving and comforting grandmother vibes :)

Prof. Harrison is the absolute BEST.  Although she may not completely reachable via email, she is so understanding, and cares about her students.  Go to her office hours, talk to her after class - it will be worth it as she gives solid feedback on any draft and/or final drafts.  A complete hoot!  I'll take her again in a heartbeat!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Hilarious

reviews.comment: I love prof Harrison. She is so kind and sweet always laughing and smiling. You can tell she cares abt her students outside of class too. Very reasonable with work and grading. I wish I could take her again!!! Miss her already

only three grades end up making up your entire grade for the semester, two of them essays, there aren't opportunities to improve any of them. She also has a tendency to call on students who aren't raising their hands. The only good things I can say are there are no tests, and in class you can just sit and listen as long as you don't get called on.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
This woman is very tough at grading. She also doesnt reply my emails! I have really suffered in this class. She doesnt even give extra credit. If you have never done literature before. DO NOT TAKE HER CLASS. Because she would make you hate your life.
Tracy, If you are reading this. you have finished me
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: There are weekly reading assignments. I did 4/15 required but by doing very well on 2 essays that are together 60% of the grade I was able to attend class and get away with a C+. The readings are 20-50 pages of a book due by each class with a quick write-up solely on your opinion, not summarizing. 4+ books need to be purchased or found.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Hilarious

reviews.comment: I took her class on suburbia, which didn't have textbooks per say, she had us buy several novels to read for the class! I really enjoyed the class. The grading was based on the weekly journals (which if you submitted on time, you got credit), and 2 longer essays! the essays were fairly easy to write because the subject material was interesting!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Hilarious--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: shes great. she really cares about her students and applies things to real-world situations. there is a journal due every class and a lot of reading but honestly, it's not too bad. there are two papers and she's a super easy grader. do the journals, read, and attend the class you'll do great. take her if you can!

Professor Harrison was a hoot. She sucks at getting a hold of I don't think that she has even replied to a single email I sent. She does bring politics into the class but as someone who likes politics I don't mind. She is really fun to listen to and she's very kind. I would take her again def one of my favorite teachers.

Very nice lady! Hard to get in touch with, but if you are actively trying she will own up to that and work with you, so no worries just be patient and respectful. LOT of assigned reading and you may have to purchase like 2-3 novels, online copies can be found for free and she assigns some text as well. Class discussion is very helpful!
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Get ready to read and participate. However she is easy going and her work is pretty much organized and doable! Shes one of the best English professors in this school. Come to class, submit your journal entries on time and turn in your essay and you will pass.

She's a great professor. Although, she is really unorganized. If you missed any handouts, prompts and even readings you will need to ask a friend because she does not post none of them on mycourses even though on the syllabus she says she does. Disappointed.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Sweet teacher, but a decent amount of writing for a 200 class. An assignment is due before the start of nearly every class (I had her MWF), which is a lot considering most students are taking 4 other classes as well

Tracy Harrison is a wonderful teacher. She gives you good advices on what to and not to do in writing. If you go to her office hours she will help you in any way she can, all while being a fun person.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Profs like Tracy are why I attend school.  She has a monotone/dry sense of humor. Her sarcasm is so on point, I want her to be my mom. So easy going. An absolute peach. Passionate about literature but not a weirdo about "symbolism". Simple plot analysis/discussion.

She is great and so sweet! She understands English is not everyones main concern and is very understanding. Only two papers assigned, fair grader, pretty lenient. Always willing to help you, wants you to do well! Easy reading journal you must stay on top of, and easy 25% of your grade. I took another class with her because I liked her so much!
Professor Harrison is great! Class was always fun, and the assignments were interesting and never felt like a waste of my time.
Really nice professor, makes an effort to know everyone, funny, her lectures keep you interested. You can get away with skipping a couple of the books, but not all.
She's such a sweet woman. The bulk of your grade is the journal entries, and two essays, and they're both simple. Take her if you can, and really get to know her.
Fun class, simple assignments, and Prof. Harrison is the sweetest person! Constantly made effort to engage students in the material. Would definitely recommend!

This is my second class with her and she is a great teacher. She's very lenient and is really helpful if you don't understand what you're doing. She makes class is enjoyable and doesn't make you do unnecessary things that other classes have to do. Take her for anything you can!
Professor Harrison is the best! She is intelligent, funny, and always makes class interesting. She doesn't take any bull, though. Love this woman!
She was a good teacher, very helpful and understanding. Her class wasn't too hard even though it was an honors level. Work load isn't too bad. Overall, great person, pretty good class.
pretty great. i love her. she really cares about her students and i liked being in her class.

A great professor! She is very helpful and cares about her students. You'll have plenty of off-topic discussions that keep class interesting. Readings are fair and you'll have plenty of time for essays. I took her twice and hope I can take her again.

Harrison is a really nice professor.  She breaks down the assignments a lot.  This helps you get all the concepts of a paper correct, but it does make the class boring as well (same stuff over and over).  However, if you put effort into the course, it should be a breeze.

Professor Harrison makes things pretty easy for ENL 101.  Yes, there is quite a bit of homework but that's simply because she breaks everything down to assure we get each aspect mastered (lots of paraphrasing, summarizing, etc).  I recommend her for anyone who's will to keep up with the assignments for a good grade.  It's not that hard!

Very helpful and tries to get everyone involved in class. Not hard just a lot of reading. So if you don't like what you're reading you may not like the class. Also depends on what class you have her for. world lit is boring.

She's awesome and tries really hard to open people's eyes to new ideas. Most people who take her classes aren't English majors (at least in my experience) so no one really seems interested so she really does try to make the class appeal to everyone. She's also very approachable.
Tracy Harrison is the WORST teacher ever! She gives homework and doesn't explain how is it to be done. Also she gives way too much homework Also you attendance is based on your homework.

She is a nice teacher, but gives way too much homework, and the homework doesn't help your grade, it will only hurt your grade if you don't do it. Same thing with attendance. Papers are difficult to write.

Tracy Harrisson is the worst teacher in the history of our universe. I came into this class expecting to learn different styles of writing..I came out knowing less about writing anything. If your taking Eng 102, avoid this Prof. at all costs and your life wont be as miserable as mine.
Knowledgeable and interesting
Not understanding, terrible with technology, monotone lecturer. Crashing out. Good lord.

TAKE HIM HE IS THE BEST, tests are for each chapter which is online, and no class for the day, he gives 3 papers to do but they are easy, His slides are exactly what's is in the book so you do not have to get the book if you don't want too. highly recommend. Most uncomplicated professor I've had so far

I have nothing nice to say about Holden. He is my least favorite professor on campus. He is very monotone and talks very quiet where you cannot hear him. He is very rude if he suspects you are not paying attention. I would never take any of his classes again which is sad because I love Psy. Everyone that asks me about him I tell them do not take.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Professor Holden's lectures are very dry and quite boring, but the material is kind of interesting. Prepare to do a lot of reading to understand what he is talking about. Good guy as a person, gave us his cell to text him instead of emailing him, and also give extra credit for attendance and participation

He is a nice professor. Only graded on 4 tests and one extra credit paper. Usually scales tests if most grades are low. Quizlets are helpful for reviewing for tests. You don't need to buy the book as powerpoints have all of the information. I recommend taking detailed notes.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The class was based solely on exams and though the lectures were helpful all of the material was in the textbook and needed to be reviewed before the exams regardless. Get ready to read.

Psych 101 was a usual 101, except there was like a daily quiz on the chapter. It was bad if you actually read it, just kinda easy to make mistakes. Or if you miss a class you forfeit the grade. Overall easy to understand, and honestly pretty chill.
reviews.rating_tags: Test heavy--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: The class itself is worth taking especially with Prof. Holden. He applies real life situations to diagnosis/ disorders. You HAVE to buy the DSM-5 because it is used for in class case studies and tests. Theres 3 tests all open book which are based on 1 case study and a PowerPoint project. Take him if you can!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He is great professor and I have taken him more than once. He can be a little bit dry at times but the material is basically a review of stats just applied to different types of psychologically testing. There are 5 short tests, last one was a take home & allows makeups. One easy class project/paper, emails his PowerPoints & offers extra credit!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Lecture heavy--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Kevin Holden is one of the nest professors in the PSY department! I would totally take him again. He explains things to you have an understanding of them, he is available after class or during office hours if you need extra help! He allows you to retake his exams if you have done badly but these are harder than the first exam he gives! So study!!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Holden is THE man! lectures can be a bit dry but very specific of his expectations on the exams. Give you all the info you need to pass and offers extra credit several times through the semester and offers makeup exams to improve your grade. Would take his class again and again.

His lectures are dry but the material is also dry so it's not his fault. Study the lecture slides and you'll get an A. Some homework assignments but they are so easy. One essay, not bad. Take him if you want to get a good grade.

Not a very hard class or professor if you pay attention to lectures and take good notes. Doesn't really write things down, so you need to either type or write fast in order to keep up. Tests were all multiple choice, information right from the lectures. Fairly easy final paper. Easy grader, lets you retake exams. Nice professor, easy going.

Holden is SO monotone, but a nice guy. You can always retake an exam to try to do better on it, however the material is really confusing & he goes really fast. Def recommend bringing your laptop because he doesn't always repeat things. 5 exams and 1 paper for a "class project" that was really easy. extra credit options too. overall great guy though
Great professor. Makes time to meet with students before and after class. His instruction is very clear. One of the best professors I've ever had.
AWESOME GUY. GREAT TEACHER. BEST OF THE BEST.
Very funny, keeps things moving, interesting guy.

Dr. Holden is a great professor. There are times when he is hard to follow, but if you keep up on the required reading you will do fine. He brings in various tools/examples to help you learn about psychological testing (the WAIS, Mental Status exams, etc. I did not need to re-take any of my exams but he has allowed students who didn't do well on hi

Not a difficult class at all.  He is more than willing to help you.  The presentation we had to do was probably the hardest part of the class.  Multiple choice tests, not hard to do if you pay attention and do the readings.

I took him a few semesters ago. He is very monotone, lectures are straight to the point and informative but not very engrossing. Very intelligent in his subject and the course itself is easy if you attend classes and can pay attention to his speaking.

Professor Holden, Is my first psychological professor and from my experience he is very amusing and helps us do things we cant figure out.Professor Holden is very nice, and his class is very interesting. i would definately recomend him to any colleuges or any one coming into college.                                                     P.S.- He is e
I took two classes with this guy. Counseling was the best of the classes I took.  Fun & hands-on. I definitely got more out of this class than any other one I've taken.
he is awesome,nice,and extremely helpful, he tries to be funny sometimes, he's not very funny, but i give him effort for trying.overall great teacher.and agian he is very helpful
I love this guy.  He truly knows his stuff and is capable of expressing the information clearly.  He is not going to feed you information so work hard and come to class prepared.

Seriously do NOT recommend.  I love psychology but he makes it miserable.  low ...monotone voice makes you dread class.  tests are very hard and the questions are confusing.  Just talks for the class..no powerpoint.  nice guy but difficult class and it didn't have to be.
Class is pretty hard. you study forever and then still get low grades. He isn't clear and class is extremely boring. Would not recommend.

Personally, he is a horrible teacher. I had to withdraw from his class. He is very boring and his lectures don't keep my interested. The tests are tricky because he'll ask a question that is one sentence in the book. I studied three hours on one test and got a 52.
Initial impression was that he was dry, boring, and not very nice. He's grown on me over the semester, though. Not very organized in lecture presentation. Can seem imtimidating.

This professor connects management concepts to real-world situations, which makes the material more engaging and easier to understand. They encourage participation, critical thinking, and teamwork while creating a supportive learning environment.

He is such a sweet guy and an awesome professor. I love his management 312 class and had fun learning about business law. You get 3 absences and there is a check in code every class before it starts. He doesn't like side chatter so pay attention or go on your laptop. But I loved Bobby he is very sweet and cares about his students.

I took Professor Hazelton for my first semester here and he will do anything in his power to help when needed.  Office hours for extra help and is always engaging with the class. This Professor is a Class Act
Attendance is mandatory and everything is done online. Classes are usually lectures and he does talk a lot. Class is not difficult, discussion posts due weekly, and he grades very lightly.
talks too much and always makes people feel uncomfortable by calling you out in front of the class. Too many papers and assignments that are very time consuming. Do NOT take him.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--So many papers--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Absolutely the best professor i've ever had! Professor Hazelton genuinely cares about his students inside and outside of class. He makes a hard subject easy to understand and makes it enjoyable.
Very nice guy, really cares for his students and makes the class enjoyable. 10/10 would take again.

Very understanding professor with only 1-2 assignments a week. He will talk all lecture but will try to have you guys engage. And you also get 5 unexcused absences and attendance is taken online with a code.
Very engaging and informative class. You are always well prepared for every assignment given. Highly recommend!
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Basically high school, easy but you'll learn more about the teacher than the history

Totally disorganized. It takes over a month for anything to be graded. Prepare to watch her struggle with power points for 20 minutes every class. She assigns a group activity which isn't in the syllabus, pairing you up with randos. Half your grade is based on the last 2 days. Instead of her class, just watch Schindler's List. You'll learn more.

I truly love this porfessor I learned so much about the Holocaust and she is so beyond sweet and caring! she knows her information and truly loves teaching. I recommend getting to know her outside of class she is one of a kind! would take again

Offenberger is a great professor who makes the class very light and fun. All assignments are relatively easy while she also provides extra credit opportunity. She cares about her students and gives them the best chance to succeed and do well. Definitely should take her.

If you wish for a fascinating, respectful and well managed course, I would highly recommend Professor Offenberger. She respects student opinions and always makes it clear what work is necessary in order to have it be up to her standard. Would Highly Recommend to any Historian.

Dr. Offenberger is an amazing professor. If you love history she is the professor for you and youll do great in her class. She is very passionate about what she teaches. If you have questions ask her after class she will NOT answer emails but that is the only problem with her. Her classes are easy and she grades generously. final papers are long.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Amazing lectures --Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She is not accessible outside of class and never answers emails. She is rude, only cares about expressing her ideas, and doesn't allow students to ask questions during lectures. She is very unorganized and doesn't follow her syllabus. She also doesn't provide study guides for exams.

Professor. Offenberger was a fantastic professor. She encourages students to collaborate on tests, projects, and discussion boards. In addition, she loves answering any question by email or message. All she asks from her students is for them to be engaged and participate in daily activities.

If you get a chance, take 356 with her. I learned more in this class than I did in my entire business degree program. She's an amazing lecturer, and the course readings are actually interesting. There is a lot of writing involved (two written exams and a 10 page paper), but if you're willing to try you'll probably do really well and learn a lot.

Professor Offenberger is probably one of the most unorganized professors I have met. She is nice overall, but does not care about a student outside the classroom. Also not worth emailing her, just go up to her. She also will give you a mid term with no review but a study guide with it and introduce the final paper at the same time. Not Recommend.

I had Dr. Offenberger for History 101 & 102. I have to say that, she's is the best Professor I ever had for the history department. As long as you show up and put in a great effort to learn, you'll do fine! She does make class fun and is very passionate with western history. She is responsive to email and one of the nicest Professor there is here
This professor cares about her students and the material. If you communicate with her and show interest she will have your back. She is my favorite professor in the history department by far.

Professor Offenberger really brings meaning to her classes as she is passionate about what she teaches. If you participate in class and put your best foot forward early, she will take time to help you understand somewhere you may be struggling.

Professor Offenberger is overall a more hands off professor when it came to HIST101, some of the lectures would appear on tests and some were useless. She gives you 33% for just showing up to take both the final and the midterm, which is nice but really shows how easy it is to pass. She won't reply to emails for weeks. Not bad, but not the best.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Lecture heavy--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Shes the goat! TAKE HERE AT ANY LEVEL

Dr Offenberger is a genuine gem of the UMD History department. I've taken her 3 semesters in a row & she has blown me away every time. Some of her rules might be a little whack if you're not dedicated but don't let that steer you away. She is so kind and brings incredible insight to every discussion. She is the reason I changed my major. Must take!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Professor Offenberger is open to feedback and works well with the class. There is a good amount of work but it is manageable so long as you stay on top of it.

As long as you show her that your doing the work necessary to pass she will help you out. 2 test and 1 essay. honestly as long as your in class taking notes you will pass the 2 exams with flying colors. essay however the earlier the better. just dont be lazy and you can get an easy A or if your like me and dont focus on the essay/reading easy b-b+
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Caring

reviews.comment: The only thing I did not like about her class was the fact that she was disorganized, and wasn't good at communicating when she was going to be in class or not. We weren't even able to email her either. Other than that her class is pretty easy and she takes attendance everyday so make sure you show up because that counts towards your grade too.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Knows the information she is teaching but doesn't seem to care about you as a student outside of the classroom. Goes really fast. Kind of just get a rude vibe.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Cares and passionate about her subject and the students. Loves her dog Frenchy. Her smile is amazing as is personality

Prof. Offenberger is very nice and her class was pretty easy, but she was very disorganized. For the midterm, we used the wrong scantron sheet, so we never really got a grade on it. And after busting my behind on the 5 page final paper, I never received a grade. I emailed her, got nothing in return. Don't expect much but to fill your requirement

Sweet teacher, I personally was not a fan of the no laptop rule and think it's a decent amount of work for a 200 level class. There are multiple large papers (only allowing books as sources), a midterm and a final. And a group assignment and a couple of other smaller assignments.

Loves her classes and the students inputs and learning what the subject matter is. You get to know Frenchy her dog and about her family. She is easy to speak to and learn from plus her personality is a charm.

Professor Offenberger is extremely passionate about this subject which makes lectures very interesting. The class is made up of a midterm, a final, a final paper and several other mini writing assignments. She mostly grades on effort so if you are trying hard you should do well. There is A LOT of reading required for this course so be prepared!

I took her for Western Civ 101, and she randomly would make the class online. But she isn't a very hard teachers, she gave one essay and an end of the year research paper. She had the midterm be open notes and the final is online. Not necessarily a bad prof, but she kind of treats the class like children. 1-on-1 she's super sweet though!
I am not a fan of history and have never been good at it, but Dr. Offenberger made such a difference in my interest. She makes the topic interesting and she really cares about her students.

Professor Offenberger is really nice. As long as you come to class you will be fine. Participation is most of your grade. There are minor homework assignments. The midterm and final are easy if you study! The final paper is graded fairly easy and is a short paper (5 pages max) Do not use your phone in class- she will be mad! Extra credit available!

She's an amazing professor that actually cares about her student passing and learning, she is very friendly and deffintly will give you a second chance if you show that you care about the class, show up and actually participate. The matrerial itself is easy and if you pay attention in class you'll get an easy A.

Professor Offenberger is probably one of the friendliest professors I know. I've taken HST 101 and HST 102 with her and she's never once lost my attention. What she requires for course work is reasonable and stated in the syllabus. She truly cares about her students and makes class interesting and relevant for students.

She is so beautiful! I loved her class. She says you need the book to pass but I read no more than a few chapters and did good. No presentations. She takes random attendance and expects you to be in class. There was only a few homework assignments, a mid term and a final. Lectures were dry but she loves her job so they weren't horrible. Take notes!

Pretty good professor, interesting topic material, and not too many papers. Some things to note though: she is very particular instructions she gives for papers FOLLOW THEM, she does not post notes online and sometimes it is hard to keep up with power points (but you still need to take good notes), and her midterm was pretty hard.
Very passionate and enthusiastic about what she teaches. However, a bit more organization and clarity would help.

Very knowledgeable on history and her powerpoints are well put together. If you ever have any questions she will do her best to answer. Sometimes with homework and reading material she was not clear when they were due.

Probably one of the best professors I have ever had at UMD. She loves teaching and shows her enthusiasm every day. Extremely nice, few things get her mad (texting in class, being rude, calling her by her first name). Her only weak point is she isn't very good at responding to emails. I highly recommend taking her. Also intelligent and beautiful.

Best professor I've taken. Makes everything very clear and all lectures are very relevant. Didn't need the book that much because she taught everything in class that was on the tests. Wrote a lot of papers but most of them were short except for the final. Easy to follow along and easy to retain the information. Take her.

Dr. Offenberger is new to being a prof but has tons of potential to be exceptional. Her lectures can be difficult to take notes and the writing assignments are very opinion-based. Her enthusiasm makes the lectures enjoyable and attendance isn't 100% mandatory. Reading is a must but the books she assigns are very short and enjoyable. Would recommend

Enthusiastic professor, willing to go out of her way to help students. Always stayed after class to answer questions, and was very interested in everything students had to say. Not the most organized class, but we only had to worry about the midterm, the final, one presentation and one paper.

She is the most amazing professor. She is very passionate about the class and is very interested in everyone's opinions. I have never had such a helpful professor. It is minimal homework but she expects people to participate in class and do the readings. There is a midterm, paper and final. Plus she is gorgeous!

I took HIST 101 with Professor Offenbergrr. She is a really nice lady. Never got concrete homework with her and only had a midterm, 2 papers, and final. Even though you take notes all class, she makes it interesting.
great porfessor nice guy class was easy not to much work I liked the layout of each week very clear and helpful feedback take this class for a credit :)
He's very charming and a nice person

The easiest classes you will ever take. When learning in his classes he makes it simple Movies, Readings, power points. You learn a lot and its easy to understand. As long as you do the work and read and watch his films you will do great. Try in the DBs and take time on quizes and you will do great. Main grades are final, book review and DBs.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Ironfield is a gift to students. He knows his stuff, expects you to apply yourself, follow the directions, and gives a hefty amount of information in multiple formats (PPT, Movies, Readings) But, he clearly states his grading criteria, expectations are simple to follow. Do the reading, the work, follow directions, and you'll do great!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: AWESOME PROFESSOR! TAKE HIM!!! Easiest class you can take at Umass. Super understandable format and grading criteria, and everything is online, including exams. Huge GPA booster as you can easily get an A in this class if you follow assignment instructions and turn work in on time. Never used the textbook, hardly even went to class either. Take him
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--So many papers

reviews.comment: He gave us barely any homework, just a few discussion posts and a paper. Two tests and they are both online.

Professor Ironfield is so passionate about teaching. His class is lecture heavy and he can talk a bit much but the workload isn't bad. You have a few discussion boards literally like three and a final paper. Plus two online tests just do the study guide and honestly, you should pass it's pretty easy.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I don't know how his class is in person, but this was the easiest online course I took during the entire pandemic. Each week there was a discussion post based on videos along with three exams that you could find the answers online to. One quick four page paper that he grades easily. Easy A.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I took two 7-week classes. The information was super relevant to the course and the books he has you read are awesome. But! There is a TON of reading which makes it a little difficult to keep up with the lecture. If you are taking his class because you like history, then youre good. If not then maybe stay away. Grading isnt bad just show you read
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Very nice and respectable man. Personally I wont be taking him again because I dont need any HST classes. No need to go to class as he doesnt take attendance... but you learn more when you do obviously. Lectures every class, gives a few tests. Midterm/Final and one 5 page paper.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Horrible class. This professor talks for the entire 50 minutes about absolutely nothing. There are 2 tests and a final, and a paper. The tests aren't hard but you have to study on your own and not rely on the notes he gives.

Probably one of the worst professors I've ever had. Hand writing is terrible, notes are not useful in the slightest, and doesn't update MyCourses. I literally almost fall asleep in his class every day. Do not recommend taking this class. Only gives 3 tests but the power points online will be the only thing that helps with the exams.
Amazing lectures, but its still US history. You show up, you learn. Did not study and passed. Do NOT buy the textbook. Waste of money. Never even took the shrink wrap off.
reviews.rating_tags: GRADED BY FEW THINGS--Amazing lectures--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: I love history but I almost fell asleep every class, I really don't know how people who don't like history survived.  He talks very monotone, and his hand writing is horrible.    Class is 3 tests (the last one is the final but it's the same length as the first two) and two incredibly boring 4 page papers.  He seems nice but that's about it.

God awful teacher. He writes on the board in writing you can't read, doesn't update my courses, takes an entire year to get grades back to you, you need to go to class and get the notes but you literally cannot read his hand writing, and he has the most annoying voice ever, I used everything I  learned in highschool to pass. Awful awful class.

Professor Ironfield is such a nice guy. He tries to make the lectures more realistic and not as though he's reading write from his notes. Even if you aren't huge into history, it's a good class and it never got too boring. 2 papers and 3 tests for the entire semester, but none of it was too difficult as long as you go to class and take notes.

Have taken 3 classes with him. 2 (in class) and 1 (on line) all 3 A's. As far as his in class courses. Have seen people write his tests are hard because the info is hard to find. What? How about try note taking. Before class he writes names/places he plans to discuss. Write those down and when he mentions them write down the bullet points he gives

He is a nice guy however, he was no help at all if you go to his office hours. I went to his hours all the time for extra help because I could not pass any of the test (2 tests) he gave. He was no help and my grade suffered because of it. We barely used the text book. He was not clear during lectures and is a tough grader. No electronics in class.
Took an online class with him. As long as you pass the work in on time was very easy. Good class to take if you want to do well. Workload was very reasonable.
So unbelievably easy. Gives you a copy of the exam as the study guide. Only 2 very short papers and 3 tests total. Easy A if you need a history class

No electronics allowed in class, so don't bring a laptop to take notes. No eating or drinking, so don't come hungry. His handwriting is chicken scratch, some friends and I would play jeopardy trying to figure out what he wrote. If you take a class with him, I give you the best of luck!

I like him, he is pretty straight forward with what he expects out of his students. The study guides are the exams. He had us buy books but we never used them once so don't buy the book. The first month or so he took attendance but then he stopped. If you like using a laptop to take notes don't take this class, he doesn't allow electronics in class

This class was not really hard it's just he's very picky he wants all the details. You really can't miss class. When he talks you have to write it down fast because he doesn't write anything on the board. And he gets very boring, I almost fell asleep in that class so many times

No hw. 4 very easy quizzes, just read the chpts assigned. 3 tests including the final, just study and you'll do fine, they're not very hard. 2 papers on which he was very vague with the instructions. Hands things back extremely late or not at all which is very frustrating. Starts out the year taking attendance, putters out later on. Good Prof/class

He is a good professor. You will have 1 research paper, a few quizzes as well as a few tests. You get study guides for tests with all the questions, and the quizzes he tells you what documents to read for the quiz. Easy A, especially if you love history like me

I am not a history person but found interest in the topic.  He is a bit boring with lectures but does engage the class in thought & discussion.  He knows his stuff and is a wealth of knowledge.  2 Papers.  Midterm & Final.  Give study guides.

Knows a lot about his field but is horrible at explanations. Lectures on and on about nothing useful then makes you struggle to find what he wants on the test. Takes forever to pass back tests. Long, painful class!

Boring Class esp if you have him in the AM... Gives a study guide but not all the answers to it, finding some of those essay questions can be difficult...Lastly, he takes foever to give your grades back, overall i give this class average at best

He was great. Exams are easy because he gives you a study guide and if you do well on the first paper you don't have to do the second one. A couple of cakewalk easy quizzes too. Shows a few interesting videos and will help you if you ask him. I hope to take another class with him in the future.
He is a very knowledgeable professor. He loves what he does. Just do your book reports and study for the 2 exams. I'd take his courses again in a heart beat.

Average. His classes are straight lecture and you sit and take notes the whole time. His tests are easy because he tells you exactly what he wants on the study guide. 1 midterm and final and 2 papers. No need for the textbook at all.

I really enjoyed this class and the professor is great. Just do the readings and take notes. that's all there is too it. He's fair and willing to help out. the study guides are the exams so make sure you study what is on them. I would def. take another class with him if I could.

He's a passionate lecturer, which is rare at this school. Friendly, easy quizzes, but he can be a stickler on tests so watch out. Lectures are for the most part boring, but attendance is mandatory most of the time and he tries to keep students involved. Overall a good teacher and a good guy.

had him for 115,116 do not buy the book he is all you need. gives quizes which are not bad but here is the thing class before the test stay after and ask him to go over the study guide tells you exactly what you need to know such an easy A if you can memorize what he wants you to know. funny guy can be a bit borning but knows his stuff

He doesn't use anything he teaches you in class on quizzes or exams. Everything is in the book. Going to class is useless but you have to because he takes attendance very seriously. Goes off topic very easily. Even through all of this, he's an easy A. Recommended.

He is very passionate about the history of the US. He takes attendance but does so less as the semester goes on. Make sure to go to the class before the test to review the material. The test are easy if you study and is a great class to take for a GEN ED. The only problem is that he takes forever to get the test grades back. 3 test 1 book review.

prof. ironfield is a aswome prof. go to class take notes and you;ll be fine go to class before test and stay after he goes over everything he wants for the test so easy but you do learn alot really enjoyed taking him

He takes attendance, but did so less and less as the semester went on. His lectures were boring, but I recommend going to most of his classes. He gives study guides for his 2 exams, and for the final with all the info you'll need. One book report due during midterm. Overall, an easy class. Take his class if you can.
does anyone know if you need both books or just the jungle? as far as i can tell the notes are the most important part of the class. does anyone know?

3 test on lectures 1 on a novel   gives review sheets before all tests and will answer all questions for you if you ask nicely.  allowed to miss 6 classes.  great lecturer but subject isnt always exciting. he loves history and sarcastically pretends we do to. I recommend him but please do not abuse his kindness and ruin it for everyone else.
Easy course, his jokes are horrible... Took this course for a GPA booster, and it worked
good teacher   grades sort of hard on his essay tests but overall covers all material you need and i like him. hes usually in a good mood in class

he is actually enthusiastic about history, but i just dont like the class.  he thought he'd cool and added an extra essay to the last test and, go figure, everybody ran out of time.  also his nasally voice at 8am is painful to the ears, literally.  this semester he also is doing an extra exam on a book not covered in class, which is great.

His class is very easy. All you have to do is take notes and take 4 exams. Plus, before each exam he gives you a piece of paper that has the material from the test and goes over the answers. Simply study this and you'll get a great grade. Had one book test, but that easy. Overall and easy A class.
Easiest class at UMD as long as you go to the review class before the test...He gives out the answers.  Sucha a breeze but great guy.  Also allowes 6 abscences : )

Very friendly professor, always willing to be helpful. Lectures are interesting, but if you just write down what's on the board, you're going to be sad come test time. Use what's on the board as a guide for your notes, and it'll be almost impossible to fail. He might be my favorite professor. :)

He's kind of boring.  He tries to be funny, but it isn't.  He does give study guides for all exams.  Get ready to write A LOT for the exams, they are all essays.  Know your stuff and attend class to know what he's looking for.
Great teacher very knowledgable. If you attend class and keep up with reading tests are a breeze. Has a good sense of humor and can keep lectures interesting. Great history teacher.
No Comments

Valova is extremely disagreeable and can be swayed by "shop talk". If you're willing to bash the school or talk about how much she's paid to do very little, you might win her favor. It's clear that she's more focused on being petty than providing an education. My experience was beyond frustrating, and I would strongly recommend avoiding her courses
Very helpful with any questions. You definitely get out what you put into this class.

If you get at least a B+ in her class you automatically get a 100 on the final so you should really try as hard as possible because it is a free class if you do the work. Homework is tough but don't cheat because you will most likely get caught and they will give you a zero with no hesitation.

her class was enjoyable but a little difficult since i've had no coding experience beforehand. she is very willing to explain concepts if you are confused and speak up about them. she has 8 exams in total (but they're all online) and they're practically every other week so you constantly feel busy. homeworks are a big part of your grade too

Valova has very clear and loud opinions on matter where she has no expertise in. She would consistently say C++ was easy but didnt know basic standard library functions such as sqrt() and continued to argue with the class even after bringing up the cpp reference documents. This woman is only here to waste your time and the Universitys money.
Reads off the slides like nobody's business. Takes credit for thesis without knowing the full meaning of the title. A sassy, master grifter.

Keeps her senior design course very organized. There will be a ton of documentation (think a 10-15 page document every 1-2 weeks) on top of the actual project. Projects span a wide array and are typically interesting. Great professor, very accessible and will do her best to keep you and your group on track for graduation.
Class is difficult but rewarding. You will learn more in her classes than any other. But you will have to work for it. She will also help you with anything you need, be it this class or any other.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Caring

reviews.comment: Do not let her good CIS190 class, her personality, or other students who like her fool you about her other courses. Grades might as well be made up for both semesters as the criteria is never clear (you can pass easily, but your grade might as well be RNG), and things are poorly handled in general. My experience was frustrating and I stand by it.

Second time she ever taught Senior Design and she did an awesome job. Really made the class more organized; forced everyone to have the project requirements met by the end of the fall semester which definitely allowed for more polished projects. She also actually checked in with the teams to measure progress which apparently Bergandy never did.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects--Hilarious

reviews.comment: She's the only Valova in the department. That's a good and bad thing. What bugged me the most is that in 362 she went on tangents every lecture and we learned nothing. In 499, we had a midterm on a single lecture. Makes no sense to me. Great person though.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Makes a lot of jokes. Will assign homeworks one after the other with about a day break in between (might put them online before the due date of the previous). Gives about 10 days for homeworks, be ready.
Great lectures, great material. Heavy workload though so best prepare your angus
One of my favorite professors in the CIS department. Workload is intimidating but she's super helpful.
Get on her good side and you will get a good grade. Visit her office often. Team work matters. Become one of  her favorite and you will be fine. You do learn.
Great teacher.  PAy attention and you'll pass.
She really cares.

Definitely one of the most intense workloads I have ever had but it was very rewarding. I never thought I would know C language like I do now. She was very funny and enjoyable to have as a professor and I would definitely take her again. Don't let the workload scare you, you will be happy you took this class.

If you don't clown around and you actually take her class seriously, you will leave her class with a crystal clear understanding of the C language.  She is tough at times and does assign quite a bit of work but the work is rewarding.  Go see her in her office if you need to.  She is hilarious.  I'll definitely take another class with her

Attendance not mandatory, but if you haven't already used C a lot, don't expect to pass. Often answers question in a roundabout manner. Also a pretty large workload, but it is manageable. Other than that, she really is a great professor. If she seems to not like you, it's cause you don't show up and slack off when you  do. Just do the work :P

Very straightforward, no nonsense class. Effective teacher and her labs are awesome, challenging and you progress quite quickly. If you do the work you will get a lot out of this class and you will do well. Her tests are very fair and cover exactly what she covers in class.
Excellent professor. Clearly explains the materials and gives several examples if you don't understand. Lets you skip the final if you did well in everything. Do recommend.

Great professor who makes you want to come to class. Her tests are a bit on the harder side, but if you pay attention it shouldn't be a problem. I will admit that some of her assignments don't really make sense (for example a linear problem that she forces you to do recursively) but overall a fun class. Don't bother buying  the books wedont use em.

Solid professor and adviser all around.  Need help?  You can get it from her without complaint.  Those that say she takes favorites are the ones that never show up to class and never build a relationship with.  Subsequently they do poorly because they don't do *any* work and know nothing about the material.  Take if you can.

Random simple quizzes keep you coming to class, but she rewards with clear, concise, and very helpful teaching methods. She's also not afraid to go off topic if progress in the class allows for some fun, including playing whatever game you're playing on your laptop. (She said I was boring because I didn't play games, haha.)

Yeah, she plays favorites a bit; she likes to poke fun at people. But honestly, if you have any sense of humor whatsoever, her classes should be a breeze. Workload is on the higher end, but very manageable. If you need any help, her and Clint (assuming he is still there) are GREAT teachers. I don't say that much...
Actualy, she failed me because i didnt do the work, inspite of being on her good side. Dont give false hope to students. Do the work, youll pass.
Plays favorites.  This means you get on her bad side you don't pass.

Great teacher. Pretty large workload, but it's still managable. She is definitely willing to help if you just go find her and ask. Grading on tests/quizes are pretty objective, and straightforward... projects and demonstrations tend to be more lax. Just do the work and you should be fine.
