Definetly plays favorites. Knows her stuff, but can't always explain it to the students.
No Comments

Pretty hot.I second that. But, she defianetely favors some students over others. Very difficult class. If you are buddies with her, you will pass the class with a higher grade than expected. Otherwise, expect no help.
Excellent professor. One of the best I've ever had.
Excellent teacher. Always willing to help. I think shes pretty cute too.
She was unfair in grading. Likes suck-ups. Favors certain students and unfair to others. Not objective in grading.

Funny.  Stay on her good side or she will make a fool out of you in class.  She does give alot of work but it is graded fairly and she is willing to give more time if needed.  I liked her, but I didn't like the class or the material.

OK, not the greatest but not the worst. She overloads on work, but i have to admit, she is a very fair teacher in grading. As long as you are on her good side, you will pass. Grades DO NOT matter, as its same for the rest of the department.
She assigns a lot of work, but you also get a lot out of her classes.  She also seems to always be in her office to help you out.
Good professor and has sense of humor.
good teacher.

Tries to make her lectures interesting for us, which is always a plus. Never falling asleep in a class is very helpful when you want to get a good grade. Alot of people say that she gives too much work but i think thats bull****, only because most people

Professor Valova knows what she teaches.  Sometimes students find she has a tendency to overload students with too much detail - rather than having them understand core concepts well enough to go on to advanced concepts.  It does not hurt that she is HOT.

Next to Kattan,I think she's the best teacher in the department.Although she is hard as sh** she is extreamly fair.She helps people who are lost too.Plus she has a great sense of humor.Make sure you do the work and come to class.

Stop whining about this teacher being bad, just because its not Kattan and you have to actually work to get a decent grade. This is one of the few teachers who will do anything to help a student understand a concept.
She is pretty much useless
Great teacher, expects alot but you will definetly learn something.
Tough teacher, doesn't seem like she cares too much about teaching. Gives a lot of work (thinking that its only her class you are taking). Tough on grading.
I hated this class, but she did make you learn.  She was tough and the work was tough.  But she really expected alot out of her students.  I never took her again, i didn't like her.

She's is very helpful.  She's more than willing to take time out of her busy schedule to help you make sure you get a concept.  She's very approachable.  She needs to be cloned and replace professors like Eberbach!

Excellent, she ensures that the entire class if following her lectures and not just dosing off or staring into space.  She is engaging and still enthusiastic about the material she teaches.  A good example for other professors in the department to follow.

Valova is the only professor who is able to both keep the class awake and teach us something. I give her all the credit in the world for shooting down the stupid kids that think their smart/funny/good looking.

All CIS majors should take 360 with her, its really the only class I've learned anything in.  She is very difficult, but teaches everything clearly and knows a lot about what she's teaching.  She is also very helpful outside of class.

Difficult class but you actually learn something in there.  Also, she's more than happy to make fun of the inevitable stupid comments and equally unfunny jokes that many CIS majors feel the need to make, which makes the class quite entertaining
hard as ****, but entertaining.
No Comments

The only assignments for the entire class are 4 tests each worth 25%. She's a great professor, very nice. She also does no classes on Friday, which I loved. Her lectures are easy to follow, the material covered in her PowerPoints are included in her tests. She makes it a very easy class, just pay attention, watch the documentaries and take notes.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Got a zero on a 100pt assignment that was worth 25% of my final grade because I missed the deadline by three hours the deadline was set to 5pm when she said in class it was 11:59pm I emailed her if i could at least get some credit even if it was late She didn't even respond to my email. when asked about it she said "I just have to live my life"

She was a horrible professor who really just taught by showing the class documentaries. I was considering about minoring in sociology/ anthropology but having her as a professor was a terrible experience. The crazy thing is I put in time to study and go to every lecture  and her tests had a lot of things that weren't on the chapters or from lecture

Coming from a hard working and determined college student, please do yourself a favor and do not take Isabella Rodriguez for any sociology courses. I took her SOA 111  course to fulfil the 4C requirement for UMD. She does provide online text book chapters that she claims will be "all the information we need to pass her exams". WRONG!

Also cross-listed as ANT 350. The class wasn't exactly what I had expected to learn about going in, but she is a great professor. Show up for class and participate, do the readings, and you will be fine. The tests are also easy, she gives you study guides beforehand.

I took her last semeter for a gender course (Spring 2010) and she is awesome!  She is not difficult at all, she just wants to know that you care about the course and do the readings.  She loves when you show interest in the subject and class.  I would HIGHLY reccomend her, shes's great and easy!

She is terrible with interacting with students, she doesn't understand that students are not just machines and even though she been late to class manyyyyy times she doesn't care if your late once. Honestly her teaching is really bad she just reads off a PowerPoint and ask random questions. She doesn't grade anything at all everything is online.

Professor Rodrigues was a temporary professor for SOA 101, and in the first class, she expressed she did not want to teach it and this showed throughout the semester. The class wasn't hard per se, but her teaching style was awful and the tests did not make any sense. It was like reading something that was translated into 40 languages.

She is a really nice person. She definitely is passionate about the subject and I felt I actually learned things that I did not know before I took the class. It is graded off 4 exams, which are easy if you complete the assigned readings and documentaries.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This class is easy to pass when the only thing graded are the 4 exams and online. her lecture is just her reading off the slides that she post on my courses. if you show up and study for the exams you can easily pass
Very disorganized and unclear-- do the readings because the content covered in class is not ever on the texts

Professor Rodrigues is a very nice professor but her lectures just consist of her reading over the presentations you are supposed to do for homework. I wouldnt even call this homework since it isnt graded. You can easily never show up to this class and still get a B in the class it is that easy.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria--Caring

reviews.comment: This was one of my first classes so take this with a grain of salt: Your grade is entirely reliant on 4 tests. They are mostly multiple-choice and aren't too difficult with knowledge from lectures/readings as well as a bit of common sense. Interesting content; didn't enjoy the class discussions though. Professor was passionate about the subject.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: She's not an easy A but she's not too difficult either. She has some interesting things to say. Sometimes it's hard to understand what she wants in an assignment but if you just go with it and try your best it's not bad at all. Lots of reading

The first day of class I wasn't sure how I felt about her because she seemed tough, but then I realized she's actually very nice. Lots of class discussion. Lots of reading for sure. Makes class interesting and makes you feel comfortable. I think she liked me lol.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Has hw but its just to watch videos (i think, never did it). Must come to class to take notes. Notes are easy. Tests are decent. If you get below a C on the test well then... Very passionate and knowledgeable about anthropology. I was intimidated of her the first few classes, and wanted to drop it, but im glad i didnt. Take Her
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Great Professor but you should be ready to read journals in detail and be comfortable with lots of class discussion. Fair to those who put in the work but you won't get away with being a good multiple-choice test taker.

Go to class and youll do good. Shes very much interested in the subject matter.  Class consists of discussions. Interesting Class! Requires books but you dont have to follow assigned readings to pass. Three small papers and a take home final. Small power point. Doesnt give out A's but its an easy B+ if you put in minimal effort.

I took this class to fullfill a gen-ed. There was no book for this class. It was entirely based of off her lectures. The problem is she never starts class and almost never finuihes a lecture. When she lectures she is difficult to understand and goes on uneeded tangents. The class is a joke and an easy A however because its only graded on MC tests.

Fair grader, and always makes the power points WAY more interesting than they actually are (sometimes they're dry, but this is an intro class to its expected). She is very intelligent, and a very good lecturer - I'd definitely take her again.

This professor is AWFUL. She is rude and does NOT give clear explanations about what it is that she wants. I don't know what grading system she uses but she REALLY needs to work on it. DO NOT TAKE HER!!!!!!!

Do not take her! She is unorganized and rude. She is not helpful to her students and does not clearly state what is expected in terms of assignments. She is very hard grader and it is often seen as unfair. Those who liked her are completely off. She is knowledgeable in the field of ANT but is an awful instructor. Do not take her!!

She is awful. Hands down the worst professor I have ever had in my three years. Her voice is like nails on a chalk bord not something anyone wants to hear at nine am. She spends 25 minutes taking attendance and runs over her allotted time every class. Only take this class if you want a head ache and have time and money to waste.

I love her! I've had her for two classes and would take her for a third, if I could.  You learn A LOT in her classes, and she is really enthusiastic about Anthropology (not to mention she's really smart).  People say she's tough, but she is not.  Do the readings and go to class and you'll do fine.  I'd highly recommend her to anyone!

Unless you're and anthro major, dont listen to any one who gave this prof a good rating. She is extremely smart for her profession, but she is extremely rude. When she speaks to people she is very unprofessional and sometimes offensive. I cant count how muchclass time was wasted by her yelling at another student like her own child. avoid if u can

the students that don't like this professor are the ones that think they shouldn't have to do readings, go to class, and generally give a**** about school.  Rodrigues is brilliant, but only take a class with her if you are willing to do the work and get engaged in class.  She really knows her stuff, and you can get a lot out of class.buy the books

DO NOT TAKE HER!!! not organized. have no idea what you are graded on. thinks all college kids drink and skip class. SO BORING. keeps class going for longer then given time. complains. no one liked her in my class. i get along with all my prof. she was awful. i will NEVER take a class with her again. HAVE to go to class or grade will suffer.
very nice do the work and u cant go wrong

This professor is absolutley brilliant. She is also one the nicest and most interesting people to talk to. her brilliance and intelligence is infinite and I suggest that everybody take her if they want to learn alot about cultures and socities

Professor Rodrigues does have favorites, because I was definitely one of them.  Whenever I was very late to class she still greeted me with a huge smile.  I received A's in both classes I had with her but I had to work hard and cover absolutely everything about the topic.  I found the classes to be very interesting as well.

I had her for two classes and I think she is a really great teacher.  I do agree that she does have favorites, luckily I was one of them which might be why I liked her so much.  She does demand a lot of reading, but doesn't really test you on it, she just asks questions in class and can put you on the spot.

One of the most helpful and understanding profs at UMass. Yes, she takes attendance, but that's the way it should be - eliminates all the low-lifes that can't get their butt to class. Do the readings, go to class, actually put some effort into your papers, participate, and you'll do fine.

Once you get past the accent, she is a funny and personable lady. She knows alot about the subject she teaches, but tries to con you into becoming and anthro major ("OR minor", if she's not convinced!). Isn't very clear about the topics discussed, but... just work REALLY hard on the projects, you'll be okay!

I agree with the comment about how I think we're doing her research for her. Class wasn't so much about the Anthropology of Women as it was the anthropology of people-she-does-post-grad-research-on. Still, the class and the readings were very interesting and I would take her again.

Prof Rodriques is really nice and helpful. Her class was interesting. Her assignments were somewhat tedious. For our final project we had to interview a Brazilian, and it had nothing to do with global women. We are basically doing her research. She is a hard grader, she really likes to give out Bs, it's hard to get an A in her class.
she is very nice but her assignments are very hard and time consuming. also takes attendance and marks you down for absences which i think is silly in college. overall would not recommend the class.
hard teacher

Nice lady, but by the gods does she demand a lot of work!  My class had to do a project at the end of the year where we had to interview a Brazilian person, tape it, transcribe it, write a paper on it, then present it in class!  Ugh!  Finding a Brazilian is harder than it looks.  This is on top of the half-dozen of 5 page papers you'll write.

Top ten worst teachers I have had. Does not explain things well. Too much work. Compares you to BU students. lets us out late daily. Very demanding and thinks we are all in love with antro. Too much for me.Does not explain work or go it when completed. Has BS classes for when she is away.AVOID!!

Always shows up late to class then thinks she can keep us late. Way too much work and demading projects. Most annoying voice I have ever heard and she picks favorites. Compares us to Brown student snad makes you feel stupid if you don't understand the assignment. This class is an absolute joke!
i thought she was great. a bit of reading BUT WORTH it

While an interesting topic, this teacher demands and expects SO much of your time all the while comparing you to Brown students. Complains about no secretary- daily...Never responds to e-mails and assumes we're all Anthro. majors or minors. She's not terrible, but could be a lot better.
No Comments

She is a nice professor but if you have a demanding major to begin with, her class may not be for you. You have to read from 4 books at once which is time consuming. Her exams can be confusing, esp. the way she words her questions. Overall though a good professor who makes the class enjoyable.
there is a lot of reading 4 books. but the class is enjoyable and can be fun.

Professor Izadi is one of the best professors in all of marketing. Just three exams, weekly group assignments, and a presentation at the end of the semester. All easy. I most definitely would take her again!
Professor Izadi is my favorite professor. She is very nice and does a great job with the content she is teaching. There are three exams and quizzes and a group project. All are pretty easy.
Overall, Professor Izadi is very helpful in learning the concepts of the course. As long as you show up and pay attention, you should be able to learn the topics and do well in the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Group projects--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Professor Izadi was incredibly helpful both in and out of class in teaching the subject matter. She is available for help when needed and keeps the class relevant and interesting. The group projects also helped me learn the material better through practical research and use of the concepts. Overall, she was a strong professor I would recommend.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Group projects--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: she is very nice, I give her that. But when it comes to group project, what she asks you to do make no sense, the exams are horrible.

Group projects and online quizzes make up the workload of entire semester, make sure you choose your group members well. Peer evaluations severely effect your grade. Professor also grades harshly on presentations and group papers. You have to work for it and make sure everything is on par. Quizzes can be very tricky too.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Professor Izadi is always helpful and provides real world examples in the class to help explain the various topics. She gives useful feedback throughout the group projects which also helped me understand the overall topics in real world context.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Group projects

reviews.comment: Anoosha is a very sweet and understanding professor. The class was very unconventional with a heavy focus on group work which was very engaging and interesting. This is a 400 level class, the students complaining at this point should understand that professors have expectations and standards that we as students should meet. Great class overall!
Nice prof, valuable class. great choice if you want to learn something. Not suitable for people who're looking for easy pass courses.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Anoosha loves group projects, but she also loves you. Her exams were a little hard to understand at times so you do need to study from your notes. Make sure you pick a solid team for your group project.

Professor Izadi, is a very nice and caring person. Her accent is very understanding. But her class was very unorganized in many ways. Her group project assignments were so confusing and what she was asking for was so unclear. Her exams are a bit horrible because some of the questions made no sense. You have to ask a lot of questions to understand

Really nice guy, but not a very good teacher. In a 102 class he expects the students to have a very good understanding of the subject week 2. The class wasn't hard except the fact that this professor is pretty difficult to understand and doesn't speak very loudly or clearly. If you're looking for a class to fill up a credit I wouldn't advise him.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He is a very good professor and makes sure his students understands the material. He gave my class the option of a take home final and a few quizzes we take home too. He does weekly DB's; which are easy points. The class material was a little dry but he tries his best to make it fun. High recommend taking him, especially if you need the credits.

Such a cute man, really passionate about policy studies. Class was not super interesting, but it was filled with tons of important things everyone should know. Two quizzes, a midterm, and final all multiple choice. Only homework is a discussion forum once a week. As long as you show up to class you'll get an easy A and a lot of useful info.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: She is beyond helpful and accommodating. If you put in the time, you will get an A in this class. She is very friendly and was always willing to assist outside of class. I can't imagine anyone regretting taking her class. From my experience, there is no one better.

Professor Isherwood was nothing but fair. The homework and quizzes she gives do not have a huge effect on your grade. But in order to do well on the exams, you need to take very good notes and do the homework. Our class didn't do so well so she let us do test corrections in class and at home. Easy A if you actually try. Easy C if you don't.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: She likes math a lot. Don't make eye contact. Ask her about her math scarf
reviews.rating_tags: CARES ABOUT STUDENTS--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--SO MANY PAPERS

reviews.comment: She is a super nice person who is always in the tutoring center to help anyone out. Her tests can be difficult if you do not study every piece of material given to you.

She is extremely helpful and will give you multiple hours a day for help on a worksheet or on homework. However, her tests are based on the worksheets she gives out but are mixed into a slurry of who knows what. Very hard to do well in this class.
John Rahme cares a lot about his student's success, he's a very fair and understanding person. Makes sure to give relevant homework before Exams. Would recommend taking if possible.

Extremely frustrating class. Lectures are hard to follow and unclear, making it difficult to understand concepts. Nothing is recorded or posted on Canvas, so good luck getting information. Office hours were unhelpful, emails often went unanswered. Students had to teach themselves the material. Will ignore students to prove his given point. Beware!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Genuinely one of the worst professor I've had in 3 years of college. Prof. Rahme is the epitome of what makes a bad professor. He possesses a unique deficiency in teaching ability that will make you question if you are in the depths of Hell described in Dante's Inferno. The fact that I am paying for his course is an abhorrent misuse of money.
very confusing and hard to follow lectures. exams and quizzes are alright

I love this diva, take a shot every time she mentions how hot Brad Pitt is. Very understanding the class is hard and there are difficult concepts however she does a great job explaining everything. One thing I hated was that we had to do so much reading like a reading per class, usually averaging 20 pages though some were shorter. Still the goat!
Professor Mulnix's Intro to Philosophy is a breeze as long as you do the readings, pay attention, and ask questions. Great professor who is funny and passionate about philosophy!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: For an introduction to ethics class it was the hardest thing ever. I had to take this for my major so i couldn't drop it. So much reading you have to do and she grades like you hurt her family. She doesn't take attendance but she does group work and you can't make it up. Tests are heavily essay based. RUN.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Graded off online quizzes, classwork, and 2 exams. Mulnix is very well versed in philosophy and it shows in her lectures. She is always available to help and explain things in depth so it is easily understood. Going to class is key for understanding the concepts, but attendance itself isn't graded.

Super kind but class is kind of difficult. Grades are 25% groupwork 25% annotations and 25% for each of 2 exams which are handwritten and kind of difficult. Tests are long as well. I recommend her but there is a lot of work in the course.

Gives reading assignments and quizzes are only 4 questions. No homework so each quiz is crucial in order to pass. The readings are old and hard to comprehend and her questions are not straightforward in order to confuse you.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Mulnix is a great professor. She really got to know us by learning our names and our interests. She is great at getting us to think and create our own ideas. I like how she lectures. She's engaging and keep us interested. She clearly knows what she's talking about and she's very passionate.

Very passionate lectures you can tell she really likes philosophy, kindve a tough grader on written portions of tests but in class work and quizes are easy (look at powerpoints!) veryyy heavy dense reading though, but if you want generally easy gpa boost this is good class, attendance not mandatory but wf usually had groupwork you can only do irl
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This class is very very tough. The two tests given in this class are very difficult and a big part of your final grade. You have to retain a lot of information to pass this class. If that is your weak point, I would not recommend. I wish I had dropped this class when I had the chance.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Profesor Mulnix is a great profesor but i wont lie her class is hard theres alot of reading and 14 quizzes which tbf isnt bad. The bad part is her grading there is 2 exams, together 60% of your grade you need to pass both and its memory based but too much to memorize 7 readings 50 questions and quotes from readings for the exams. Dont recomend.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Her lectures are always great. A lot of reading but no textbook required. The homework is very easy. PAL sessions really suck and I always dread going to them. I would honestly rather listen to her talk for hours. Kind of weird grading for open responses on exams, but the exam multiple choice and homework is easy.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Mulnix's class is no walk in the park - her tough grading and dense readings can be a real challenge. But with unstuck study and consistent effort, you can survive her course. Just be ready to put in the work.

Not one to review anything, but avoid Mulnix. I was told a note card was allowed during tests--not the case.-30 points if an assignment is submitted a second past the due date. 1-2 quizzes a week, in class assignments you get a 0 for if you miss the class no matter what. Still hasnt graded final exam/submitted final grades that were due yesterday.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: She's okay as a person, but her classes are so so boring. She assigns a lot of reading with quizzes every week and her tests are way too difficult for an intro class. She says attendance isn't mandatory for a grade but if you don't go you'll probably miss some in class assignment that you can't make up.

If youre looking for credits and a simple course this is the last professor that you should take. Im a business student and had to fill a requirement so I decided on the last open class PHY 101, it was the last open class for many reasons. The professor posts at least one assignment per week but expect two as well as in class work. DONT TAKE THIS!!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: TOUGH GRADER. Her tests are extremely long and sometimes feel impossible -- I would be surprised to see if anyone ever got an 100 on a test of hers. Also, I was .2 off of an A.. she wouldn't round. Her class is very lecture heavy and she almost discourages discussions. VERY material heavy and focuses more on the readings than the actual content
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I didn't want to take this class because of the reviews, but I had no choice because I needed the credits and it's the only class that fit with my schedule. If you need an easy A... DO NOT TAKE A COURSE WITH THIS PROFESSOR. She is very harsh with her grading and takes philosophy very seriously. She's passionate about philosophy and very nitpicky!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: if you hate reading and tests don't take this class. the work is hard even though she says its easy
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Heavy, dense, and LONG reading material. Be careful if you disagree with her--acts like her opinions are fact and will be rude to you in front of everyone if you have different views. She was quite contradictory at times during her lectures, often switching up if you pointed out any flaws in the material. Interesting class, but all over the place.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Mulnix is a funny and kind person, but is also a little all over the place. She always waits until last minute to upload assignment instructions and grades. If you enjoy philosophy youll enjoy the course, but its not easy, she grades tough, and you have to read a lot of dense philosophical papers. All the work is at the end of the sem, so good luck
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She is one of my favorite professors. You're going to have to work in her class, but she makes the material as interesting as possible.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Mulnix was an amazing professor. I am not a huge philosophical person, but her class was really enjoyable and she made sure we thoroughly understood the material. There is lots of reading but the assignments themselves are pretty easy and she genuinely cares about her students.
Very tough grader but she's a great professor. She makes you work for on it but overall she is lenient. Do not take this class if you are looking for an easy A as it is not your typical 101 class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Probably the hardest 101 class I have ever taken but honestly she is not a bad professor. Get ready to read and write and I would not say it is an easy A. I got an A but I had to work for it but anyone can pull it off if they're willing to put the work in. She is nice and gives great feedback and I would take her again.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Great professor very energetic and passionate!! I always enjoyed coming to class. She really cares about students and is always available outside of class. Class was organized and grading was fair. Would take again and will recommend to others.

Took this class because I was interested- mistake! Teaches the books she wrote which are confusing if you've never taken a philosophy class before. So much reading. The 2 papers, she explained very clearly... I even went to office hours to ask more specific questions and still did very poor on them though I worked extremely hard. Disappointed.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Everyone rates Mulnix's 101 as confusing but that is because philosophy is confusing. She does a good job breaking it down and provides a lot for students (like giving test questions weeks in advance). If you show up to class, participate, and study you will have no problem passing. If anything take it for Mulnix herself, she is hilarious!

I took PHL101 freshmen year with Prof. Mulnix and enjoyed it so much I decided to take her 340 course. Grading is based on weekly questions about books written by Mulnix, two major essays, & a presentation (you dont have to stand in front of class), and few extra credits. Its a lot of work, but Mulnix is hilarious, inspiring, & very knowledgeable.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Hilarious

reviews.comment: This is pretty simple. Go to class, read the material, do your homework and you will not only pass, but excel in class. It is amazing how hard this is for some students to grasp.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Inspirational

reviews.comment: This was the hardest 101 class Ive ever taken. Theres readings and quizzes due before every single class, and tons of group work. The readings are super long and confusing, but she really breaks it down for you. Also an electronic free class, so her lectures feel like theyre 4 hours long.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: She really is not that hard of a grader, philosophy is an awful topic in general but she gives you four free absences and the test questions are given ahead of time. If you study you will pass and all the quizzes are online.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Stay away if possible. Very confusing professor when lecturing and found extremely difficult for a basic philosophy 101 course.

She has a lot of energy, which will help keep you awake even though it is generally a boring class. BUT, this is not an easy PHL101 class. If you genuinely want to learn and put in more effort than usual to get an A, then she is perfect. But she's a tough grader, lots of group work, and homework

If you are genuinely interested in learning, expanding your mind, and having interesting conversations, she's the professor you want. Reading is heavy/important but you could probably pass with a B or C without reading if you take good notes and participate. She is extremely down to earth, funny, and is VERY into lecturing through discussions.
Took Philosophy thesis class with her. I learned a lot. Was able to write my theis
I loved her class. She was full of energy, engaging, and clear. Her powerpoints are super helpful for studying for the six tests she gives. She learns your name and gets to know you.

She is a great professor. She's funny and helpful, and it's great because of the fact she wrote the textbooks so it's very consistent with her tests and lectures. She is a good teacher, but this was a very hard class. Very time consuming. Two reading assignments due for each class, one journal entry  each week, 3 exams, and 2 papers.

This class was tough. There are a lot of tests and group work so prepare to work hard. Those are the only things that count towards your grade. There are readings for homework but they are not necessary to do. She makes detailed power points about all the readings which is more helpful to study than the actual readings.

FYI this is not the easy 101 class you are looking for! Possible to get an A (I did) but it requires a lot of reading and understanding toughlong reading passages. All exams are in class essays only. She gives you the inclass essay prompt beforehand but you have to memorize everything you want to write no cheat papers.

I like Professor Mulnix's personality. Tons of energy. She's entertaining and funny. I was never bored. The course was pretty easy too. You get the test questions on the first day of the semester. As long as you pay attention in class and take notes, it's not hard to get a good grade. Plus I learned a lot. I'd take another class with her again.

Takes PHL 101 way too seriously. Exams are essays and she's not a lenient grader. Unless you know every word of every reading you won't get a good grade on her exams. And she takes attendance AND her homework is tough. Wish I dropped it when I had the chance
She takes 101 way too seriously. Completely turned off from philosophy because of this class.

This professor is amazing with her ability to thoroughly explain the material. She will use the entire class time to explain the readings and to make sure everyone understands. However, her homework is unrealistic for a 101 course. She's one of those professors who thinks her class is the most important class and is unrelenting with the readings.

This is an INTRO course! No need to make us write a million essays on dense materials! She is taking this class way too seriously and we are all there for a gen ed requirement. I don't suggest this class unless you love philosophy and love to write obscene amount of papers.

I had intro with Mulnix. She is super smart but laid back in her teaching style. Class is challenging but it's not hard to get an A if you do the work. I decided to become a major after taking her class. Then I was bummed when I found out she isn't teaching this semester. Hoping to take at least one more of her classes again before I graduate.
DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS! EXTREMELY CONFUSING. I had to drop this class, she is horrible!

I did not enjoy this teacher.  She can be quite condescending and patronizing if you do not agree with her specific philosophical view.  Honors 101 is one of the most ridiculous classes I have ever taken.  Mulnix didn't work for me.

I loved the active part of this class, but the lectures and papers were really hard. You have to write a three page paper every week and the material is really hard to grasp. You have to attend class. She is also a really hard grader.

Super enthusiastic in lectures. Lots of insightful and entertaining things to say. The class was very well organized. Exams are blue book and study-heavy, but she gives out various possible prompts beforehand, making the actual studying straightforward. Just the right amount of homework between classes (short reading and a 1 page paper).
Very nice and very helpful. Sometimes the material can be convoluted but that is not really her fault, Fridays are all on MyCourses and no textbooks. Overall a great class!

Really helpful. Easy to understand. The exams were long answer essays that required a lot of studying. She uses a lot of real life examples to underline her points. Anything you dont understand she is more than willng to help you. ALOT of reading is done for her class...theyre not too long, but theyre relly difficult to understand..

You must take at least one Mulnix class before you graduate. Take them all if you can. I have taken 5 of them. You do work pretty hard but you learn a ton of philosophy. Plus she is super smart and is funny.  Class is actually enjoyable to go to, which is good because its required.

I am taking Happiness-best class I have had at UMD.  I love that she teaches stuff that actually matters and doesn't dumb it down, but really wants to teach us the true complexity.  Always available for help if you ask, and gives very thorough notes that make sense of tough readings. I recommend her for anyone who wants to actually learn philosophy

Took twice, enjoyed neither class (modern phl + special topics). Hard test format (study 9 essay questions, get tested on random 2). You will view countless powerpoints slides, and if you ask for clarity, you'll receive little. She has fans because she is a goofball, not because she is a good teacher. Classes require memory, not real philosophy.
The professor is nice and the class is straightforward. No surprises. Prof knows her stuff, you will learn something. Would recommend.

I'd steer clear of a professor who is only what she has read.  No open mind for dialog.  Then demands adherence to tests that are very old and quite flawed in concept.  Do Not Take her Class if you want to brilliant.

Does know her stuff but, BEWARE. She expects you to have studied the material as many years as she has. She's had years to "internalize" the material, I think she often forgets that she wasn't born with knowledge and that she had to learn it at one point too. She's a very tough grader.

Good course, mostly because of the professor. The material was hard, but Professor Mulnix was always willing to answer all of my questions whenever I had them (which was a lot!) She seemed to really care how I did in the class. Plus she was actually interested in the material. I would definitely recommend taking her class.

This professor is hilarious and fun! I have never enjoyed philosophy but she made the discussions interesting! BUT, when it comes to doing a professor's JOB she failed. She barely gave any papers back, so if someone did something wrong on one they were doing it all year without even knowing. Grades too hard for the amount of help provided (none)!!

Teaches her take on philosophy as if some kind of ritual adoration for simpler minds of our predecessors.  Not palatable, the class.  Her personality, though, attempts to be real, except that philosophers don't deal with reality, but with conjecture.
Took as an online summer course. DO NOT TAKE. Course was horrible and completely hard. I withdrew half way because it was impossible to understand and her tests were too hard.

She's a great professor, very nice, very enthusiastic. Sometimes her lectures are a little unclear, so definitely try and pay attention. Go to class, keep up with the readings and take advantage of extra help and you'll do great!
Great teacher. Recommend course. Loved the class
Mulnix was awesome and wore funny t-shirts. The class was thought-provoking and entertaining.

She comes into class everyday with a smile. She gives hw that takes a lot of time to do. She makes the material sound easy but its very hard to comprehend. Her lectures got really boring sometimes too! Dreaded going to her class at times. but i guess overall it was alright. attendance in mandatory, so don't miss her classes. her exams are hard!
Professor Mulnix is a really good teacher. She always bring a smile to class and makes the material interesting. The class ends up being pretty easy becuase you get to drop the lowest exam.
Professor Mulnix is a really good teacher. She always bring a smile to class and makes the material interesting. The class ends up being pretty easy becuase you get to drop the lowest exam.
Mulnix is really awesome. She is extremely inspiring and it is very clear that she is very intelligent regarding her subjects. And she is ridiculously good at teaching.

Although the work was not difficult, there was a lot of reading. I purchased the book on the syllabus, but we didn't use it; she provided all the reading material online. She does not reply promptly to e-mails.  Worth taking the class.
Professor Mulnix is the best professor and thats in all possible worlds!!!

Nice, young woman. Very pleasant and enthusiastic about subject.  Copious busy-type homework along with quite a bit of reading required to accomplish homework.  No tests but big group project final (group projects generally suck).  Do the work, show up, get good grade.  Not rocket science.  Native English Speaker.

She is so hot, it is hard to listen what she says, but when you manage to take your concentration of her girl next door, sweet and sexy image, she is Ivy League brilliant.  She is a midwest darling who is fun, energetic and just plain fantastic.  I would highly recomend this class.
dr genius is for real. she is the coolst professor i know.
No Comments
Mulnix is entertaining as everyone says but she has all these ratings only cuz she had a bet with her hubby who teaches here too i guess and she had us rate her during class.
Dr. Mulnix deserves a high five!
Very enthusiastic, she loves this class and is never fails on trying to make it interesting. At least a B if you do the homework and essays.
Jen Mulnix is the most enthusiastic teacher I have ever had, but she's so great! Definitely take her class!!!
Great job!!! Your class is awesome!
A great Professor!!!
fun class, easy to follow, no tests just homework and papers. Mulnix is extremely hyper and draws fantastic pictures.
Mulnix is a good professor.
professor mulnix is one of the most helpful professors i ever had.  she is always willing to help during office hours when you are stuck on a question
Professor Mulnix is very excited about what she has to teach. Her papers are easy and some times lets you out of class early.

She is my favorite professor so far at UMD! So funny and happy! always wants to make sure her class is having a good time, but at the same time manages to get all of the material in class covered! So easy to talk to, and will always be there for outside help if you need it. Simply the best!
Very informative
Profesor Mulnix is rad.
Mulnix is such a good teacher, I am willing to look past my usual distaste for the midwest.
It's a very fun class. I highly recommend it!
Jen makes you work superhard & theres something due every day, but I'm taking every class w/her b/c she goes out of her way to help & is so down to earth & fun
Mulnix is pretty much amazing. The philosophy t-shirts are HILARIOUS!
Never understood PHL until Mulnix I wish other teachers would be like her!
MULNIX ROCKS

A great professor.  She explains concepts really well, the reading is pretty easy.  Only one test, no paper, one giant group project.  If you study for the test, it'll be easy.  This ethics class was great and I highly recommend it.

Clearly some people who write these reviews dont get philosophy, because Mulnix is the best professor I have had here at UMass.  It also doesnt hurt that I enjoy going to class just to look at her. Wa-wa-wee-waa!
It has only been two weeks of class, but she is SMOKIN!!!!
lots of little work that just gets in the way....
Bodhisattva Mulnix is funny and really really smart (but can't draw pictures to save her life) I'd definitely take one of her classes again. Mu!
loved the cartwheels. mulnix rocks!
awesome prof
My favorite class last semester!

This is the worst/hardest class I've ever taken. She is impossible and it so critical of every assignment I did. I worked hard on a paper and she wrote me a page long explanation of how bad it was. Do not take this class. It is AWFUL!!!

The class made me think more about life situations. I liked the group work and the online assignments were fun. The reading was kind of hard. Mulnix tried to relate with things going on with us, which helped. And the drawings and transparencies were good.  Overall she was a nice prof.
the class wasn't easy but the grading was fair. plus the prof was enthusiastic and funny. i also liked the philosophy tshirts.

Professor Mulnix made this class worth it.  She is funny and that made a hard class more interesting.  The material is tough, but she is always willing to help each student understand.  She really seems to care about each student.  I would recommend her to anyone.

Such a bad class. No matter what you say or write, its wrong. Assigns a term paper and grades them very hard. it is impossible to get a good grade. Lectures are boring and tests are impossible. I wouldn't take her again.
No Comments
Confusing.  Not fun!
this class was so hard and confusing

Dr. Bowen is a great professor and super easy to talk to. She really cares about her students and explains things in a way that actually makes sense. I'm really glad I took her class and would definitely recommend her.

Dr. Bowen was my favorite professor this semester. I learned so much in her class, and she was always kind and willing to help. She truly cares about her students and has a real passion for teaching. I would highly recommend taking her class.

If there's one thing I would recommend to future students, it's to take her class! She makes the material easy to understand. Her exams are based off of her lectures, so pay attention. She doesn't try to trick you. She truly wants the best for you!
Pick her for an instructor if you can. She's amazing! She will make such an impact on your experience at UMD!

Dr. Bowen was by far my favorite professor this semester! I learned so much in her class, and she always took the time to answer my questions without ever making me feel like a burden. Her passion for teaching really shows, and it's something you don't see very often. You'd be missing out if you didn't take her class!

Dr. Bowen is seriously one of the best professors you'll have. She actually cares about her students and makes tough material way easier to understand. She's always willing to help and makes class engaging, so you actually want to learn. Taking her class is a really great experience.

Dr. Bowen is amazing! If you get the option take her class, DO IT. She is such a funny and kind professor. Her class is engaging and she always presents the material in a clearly. She is always there if you need help whether it be her class or another class. She is the professor that will make an impact on your life. Listen to everything she says!
The best instructor ever!! You would be so lucky to get her as an instructor! Take advantage of everything she teaches you, she's going to make you a better person and nurse!

Dr. Bowen is the BEST professor! She genuinely cares about each of her students. She explains all the material clearly and is always willing to help with other material when needed. Her dedication and support makes such a difference in your learning. Her lectures and powerpoints are amazing! If you have the chance to take her class, do it!!

Start strong, so you're not stressing for the final. Test, quizzes and HW heavy due to nursing curriculum not her as a prof though. Love her story dumping moments. Content gets heavy as the time goes by and be sure to study. She is a clinicals prof and has just started lecturing. Shockingly this class was harder than 212 . Overall great prof.

Prof. Bowen is by far the best professor ive had so far during my 3 years at umass. She makes you feel good about what you do. she motivates you and encourages you to do and be your best!! love her youll be lucky to have her !!

Unlike most toxic professors we've had, who are always wishing you failure, Prof. Bowen is a kind young woman who wants his students to succeed. She's not just a kind person, but her explanations of the material are detailed, and she's very knowledgeable. Moreover, just because shes sweet does not imply you can fool her. She'll evaluate you fairly.

Professor Bowen is extremely compassionate and skilled. Clinical with her was always fun, and full of challenges that she would help us work through. She gives the best feedback, is easy to get along with, and genuinely cares about her students. It's hard to find professors who want to help you do well in nursing, she is the exception.

THE GOAT. There are a ton of assignments but they are all easy and take between 5-10 minutes. She also doesn't do essays. Instead, she will ask around 4-5 open response questions and you have to respond to them. Very easy and very good professor.
Just a great professor

This is not a class you want to be taking especially if english is not your thing. There is at least 3-5 assignments through each week. She is a tough grader and wants you to know stronger words for assignments even if you can't understand the class. You will not got feedback on assignment's, only the big ones which still takes her weeks to grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Such a great professor. The work is a bit heavy but super easy! lectures are easy and she is very helpful! a lot of homework so beware but that's also what is heavily graded at 40% 2 papers and both at 30%. also gives extra credit on final paper!
love her so easy shes the best

Professor Kim is very sweet and she cares about making the class enjoyable for students. Her lectures and instructions for assignments are very clear and she takes the time to help students as well. Very easy going class and she doesn't care much about attendance. The class basically consisted of 3 major assignments and easy in-class assignments.

Yuni is so sweet, one of the most caring professors here, she really values student success. Sometimes directions get a little confusing, but she is always willing to go over them and help you in or out of class. Very personable and considerate, definitely would take her class again.
Me say good
W professor ngll

Dr. Kim is an amazing professor she is so nice and caring her class consists of 2 essays (midterm/final) 
(easy grader) posting group discussion boards each class and 3 multiple choice reading quizzes(which are not too hard if you read) and a group presentation (based on one of the readings of your choice) she gives easy bonus points as well

Professor Karim is nice and respectful but not a strong instructor. The class is very lecture heavy and boring, notes move way too fast to follow, and lectures do not match homework, exams, or labs. Questions often add confusion. Labs were brutal with little help. Homework is time consuming but graded leniently. Avoid if possible.
Great guy, but seems to struggle with getting students to be able to comprehend the lecture material.

Professor Karim is a respectful and kind individual, and I appreciate his professionalism throughout the semester. However I found it challenging to follow the course material due to a lack of clear explanations during lectures.

Dr. Karim knows his stuff but he goes through the lectures at light speed and if you miss one thing you are behind a mile in his lecture and it is better to read the book before class and just use him to review what you already read about. His exams were similar to the quiz which were easier than the homework, and Pearson sucked bad.

This is for ECE 201 (did not know how to put it in). Professor Karim is nice and tries his best. But the content in ECE 201 is just too vast. His teaching style did not translate and I felt like I wasted my time coming to lectures. If you are an Ele. Engineer, go ahead. If you are like me, a bioengineer forced to be an Ele. engineer, worth to wait

The hardest part about this class was the math. He often goes on tangents, but is very kind. We had a textbook but it was offered through Pearson and so was the homework- which you could attempt as many times as you want. Doesn't do a lot of exam prep. I would not take this class again, BUT I did enjoy my time in it.
Great professor

Professor Karim is very nice but most of the learning in this class you do on your own. It's hard to keep up with him in class and the lectures do not match the labs whatsoever. If you want to pass a test you better study in a group because the material in his lectures and homework is not useful. He does let you use cheat sheets on exams though.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Although his lectures aren't "mandatory", try not to miss any. He writes incredibly fast, so if you miss something you might be stuck in mud for a bit. The labs were not great. The exam recitation was held the evening before the exam on Monday. On a SUNDAY. The exam wasn't even finished by the professor until after recitation. Textbook is needed.

He wrote problems on paper, not finish, then loose them for the next lecture. His exams made no sense and werent related to anything he taught. He would have the TA's hold recitation sessions on SUNDAYS to prepare us for the exams but WOULDNT provide them with the exam because it wasn't finished even tho the exam is on MONDAY. The labs also sucked.

He doesn't produce lecture slides, instead he writes on pieces of paper so you have to scramble to write down all the material before he flips the page. However, the problems on the paper aren't correct the majority of the time or he crosses out half the stuff so you cant understand it. The material isn't related to the labs, homework, or exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Karim is a very good teacher, that can sometimes be a little bit difficult to understand but that isn't a big deal. I would strongly recommend him because the tests are not there to be confusing, they are there to make sure you understand what you are doing, and you get a lot of points for just attempting each problem. He's a great prof.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: If you're looking to have a virtue signaling professor that will take every opportunity that she manifests to tell you about her lived experiences, lecture the class on opinions and disregard the scientific method, then try to radicalize a class without explaining the ramifications that will happen, boy do I have a professor for you.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I personally could not stand this teacher. The course itself in my opinion was really easy and it's the same assignments every week. But the way that this teacher grades and critiques students makes the class so difficult. It's like she will critique something that is your opinion and won't help you out whatsoever if you're confused with a topic.

I truly believe this woman gets joy out of berating and borderline bullying students. She is so unbelievably rude in her discussion posts. She wont try to help you understand, she will just berate and shame you for not understanding. Do not take her classes
I would HIGHLY recommend finding a different professor. The only one who is allowed to make mistakes and be human in her course is her. Very scary experience

Great SOA professor, had her for health and healing, learned a lot and did really well in the class. Most of her lectures tied in with other classes and I feel like she was great at listening to students and our concerns.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: i dislike her very much
Professor wack.

Was a great class to be apart of, learned to communicate with classmates and learn from one another. The grading criteria is straight forward if you read the material you will succeed. She is very set on correcting you and making sure you are taking in the readings which Is a plus! If you like creative criticism this is the class for you.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Very rude and disrespectful, shares only her opinion not real facts. do not recommend!

Maybe I lucked out on having a class who speaks up during lectures, because if you actually participate in the course, Prof. Caldera really isn't that bad. You just need to have an open mind and be willing to literally read maybe 3 things a week. Quizzes are open note and online + the only real grades you get are participation, midterm, and final.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Her class is only basShe gave me an alternative final because I was going to be away for finals week. She gave me 6 short essay questions requiring a min of 750 words EACH. She told me only to use the powerpoints for my essays then failed me cause I didn't site the powerpoints (HOW DO YOU SITE A POWERPOINT). She is very rude and argumentative.
She is very rude. She made me anxious every time I stepped foot into the class.

Caldera is all over the place, let me just say if you have the opportunity to take any other professor, do it. She is incredibly biased in her lectures and provides opinions as opposed to facts. She doesn't accept any challenges from students, and only pushes her own opinionated agenda. If you want to learn anything, do not take her classes.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Worst professor I've ever had. Rude, condescending, picky, contrarian, unprofessional, disrespectful. She swears in discussion boards and contradicts herself. She only wants to push her own understanding of the content, and does not want to be challenged. No lectures provided for an online class. TONS of reading required.

This teacher is unbelievable. The bookstore says materials not posted for this course but yet on the first day of class she tells us there is 5 required books and we already need to start reading one. She is extremely rude and grades unfairly. Will be filing a complaint on her if she keeps it up.

Extremely condescending and difficult to work with. Being that its a covid filled semester my personal schedule conflicted with my school schedule but I didn't let it interfere with y work. Professor caldera was privy to my personal issues but disregarded them when it came time to grade. She blind sided me to say the least, avoid her at all costs.

For my 1st semester as a transfer student transferring from community college this professor, in particular, was extremely rude very difficult she doesn't like being questioned about her logic or expertise she swears a lot & she discouraged many students makes read you read a lot of lectures & heavy course works as well having so many study guides
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Would never take her again.

This class should really of been a 300 level class and not an intro course. The amount of reading that was mandatory (its on your tests) was way too much. She does give study guides but there super long but helpful. Her exams are very strongly worded and you'll get things wrong because of this. Lectures were interesting.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Honestly, for an introduction level course, she made it unnecessarily difficult. For starters, she is condescending and a nitpicky grader. She called me a middle schooler simply because I didn't capitalize one word. Her course load is a challenge and I spent about 90 dollars (would have been a lot more) on books that weren't all necessary.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Professor sometimes lecture wrong information, and strays off topic to insert own opinion
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Very vague rubrics. Give very unhelpful feedback. Not one to give any mercy to student, and she is a total airhead. She was quite rude in online discussion boards.

She's an okay teacher. Attendance is mandatory with the REEF I-clicker an if you don't have it she will fail you. The work load was a lot but she does give extra time if need be. Not a class I would take again.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Awful professor. Very disrespectful when talking to students. Favors students. She goes as far as swearing when answering students' answers to discussions posts. Lots of reading and her questions are very vague. She expects you to be a mind reader when answering questions. Do not take her!!! She's RUDE.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Students must take a proactive approach with professors. They differ in teaching style, personality. If a student finds the class boring or frustrating it is up to you to make the best of your situation. College is about challenging ourselves,professors. If something bothers or intrigues you Speak Up. Feisty,opinionated,disorganized but passionat
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Do not take a class with her as a professor!! She's very rude and disrespectful.
Took Ant 111 over Maymester. Lots of reading, but I did all the work (skimmed through two of the books) and received an A-. Shes not that bad.

She loves to talk about politics so much and is one of those people who doesn't really listen to any dissenting opinions. There was one incident where she argued with a student about raising the minimum wage. She'll go on liberal rants that often have nothing to do with Sociology.   Also, the amount of reading is ridiculous for an intro class.

Introductory level class felt like a senior class. The reading is unbelievable you can't get anything right in her discussion boards and she's rude. She claims to never receive my extra credit papers and gave me a final grade without them.  I received a D+ with three other A's for the semester I have never received a D+ in my life.

Honestly the WORST professor I have ever taken. She is so unbelievably rude. She is the worst grader ever. She told me to retake the class again when I learn how to follow instructions. I literally cannot stand her. Don't take this class with her. Find somewhere else to take it.
Worst professor i have ever had. Cant even think about that class without becoming upset.

Tests are hard. Expects readings to be mandatory for exams. Concepts are hard to grasp from the book (its like a fieldwork book) it sucks. pulls examples out of the book from nowhere and expects you to answer them . Horrible class but does offer extra credit and is a nice teacher. Helpful on class assignments, not lecture

"Dr." Caldera is a joke of a professor. She tried to screw me out of my grade in this class by claiming that she only had 3 of 10 papers that I submitted. This is due to her old age. She told me to email her the papers and then forgot about them and tried to tell me she didn't accept emailed papers. She also sits there and does nothing each class.
This was the second time I have taken a class with her, the first time was okay, this time not so much. She is rude, unprofessional, inconsistent, and inadequate. DO NOT TAKE
This professor was unclear rude in posts and her assignments were incredibly unclear, her political agenda was heavily promoted in this class.

Rude with comments to posts, grading assignments and emails. Entry level classes are held at an upper level criteria. Books upon books must be read in order to pass. Questions are confusing and worded in a manner you may not understand what she is looking for. I would not take another class with her.
Second time I've had this professor. She is my favorite because her classes are so interesting.  The reading is interesting too. You can get a good grade if you just do the work.  No problem.

I disagree with the comments about the professor's politics. In my class she was fine with people disagreeing with her. She was good at getting us to think about political events that affect our lives. I learned a lot.

Great professor.  Well organized and gave lectures with slides. I went to her office hours and she helped me enormously. Our class was large so some students didn't ask questions but she is helpful if you ask.
Bad choice of textbook, lack of organization and poor explanation of material; not respectful towards students in the class, and unreasonable in her expectations in regard to class level.

Awful professor. Only teachers her own political views, and teaches Communist theory as gospel. Class is insanely easy, even if you don't do the readings, as long as you answer every question with her awful bias. Avoid this professor if you don't want to want to hit things every morning.

She is an okay professor, but she has a bias state of mind, which makes her class annoying. Shes really harsh with her approach to topics, putting more emphasis on her opinions than the readings at hand. Online Tests always help to raise your grade but a  lot of readings necessary in order to pass the major exams.

I took this class to raise my grade but it did not. he instructor is nice and helpful. On final exam, she only used the economics part not psychology or kinship part. Economics is hard for me. Take this class if you like economics.

My worst professor so far. I had A's and B's in every class until her. It's not that the material is hard, it's that she makes up trick questions on the tests. She's un helpful. In e-mails she doesn't always answer questions correctly. She's rude and over opinionated. Does not care about students. I dislike her very much. Don't take her class.
Awesome Professor! I enjoyed waking up for her 8AMS. Favorite class all semester! Do all of the work and you can easily get a good grade.
Fascinating lectures about other cultures but also about our own society.  Prof was very interested in students' point of view.  Tests were hard if you didn't study.
Great professor and lecturer. Lots of reading, but manageable. Very understanding and helpful. I would take her again.
Liked this professor a lot.  I had to take this class as a requirement but didn't think I would like it.  But it was great.  I  found out it was very iteresting.

This professor was really interesting. She made me care more then I thought about weird cultures and things I didn't know about. She loves cats so they are on every slide which makes her awesome. :) Her tests are easy enough if you study and I really enjoyed her class.
Have taken two classes from this professor because her perspective is to make students think.  I learn a lot.

Great professor! She is funny, inspiring and always available for help. The class was challenging and I learned a lot. She makes a all topics in the course interesting. The slides were helpful. Anyone who is willing to work hard and add a little to particpation will do well and enjoy her classes like I do.
Probably the worst professor ive ever had. She makes stuff up. Very rude. Stay far away!!!!!
Awful professor. So rude
Second time I had this prof.  She is intersting and fair.  She helps anybody who asks.  I got a lot out of it.

Great teacher even if the course was not that interesting.  She encouraged participation from everyone.  Always asked if anyone had questions and reviewed everything as often as neeeded.  I would recommend her definitely.

EXCELLENT TEACHER.  Really cares about her students and helps them through this course which requires a lot of work. She knows her stuff.  Her slides make following the material easier than understanding the readings.  Although I didn't like the course that much, she made it okay.

Loved this class.  Teacher was enthusiastic and funny.  Listened to students and encouraged arguing. The reading was interesting.  She uses slides and discussion questions to help us study for exams.  Highly recommend.
Great teacher.  Knows her stuff.  I found the course fascinating.  Although it was a huge class she tried to involve everybody  in discussions. The tests were hard.
Professor doesnt know what she is doing and grades every student differently. She has her favorites in each class. do not take her

This prof is awful. She is rude and doesn't know what she is talking about. If you are not one of her favorites in class she refuses to help you or answer emails. Her lectures are filled with false information. Avoid taking her at all costs.

Terrible teacher, she doesn't have any compassion with people, definitely has favorites in her classes.  She graded students differently, in one test on the same answer some students got it wrong and some right! She's ridiculous and has an attitude almost all the time! I would not recommend her at all!
Nice person but not that great as a professor

Didn't seem like she had much experience with teaching. Her notes weren't great and she "disagreed" with half the things in the book. Didn't have much knowledge outside of her "area of expertise". Her tests were difficult for a 100 level class, as the questions were unclear and it seemed there was more than 1 answer to a lot of them.

Very nice person, great clarity, very helpful, and good text. She is willing to go back to previous slides if she is going too fast and she also asks people if they have any questions. Her tests are a little difficult because the multiple choice answers are VERY precise, but she was willing to re-adjust them and drop the grade from first test.

Nice lady but not a great teacher. She tries but her classes are extremely dull and she often doesn't know the answers to the students questions. She reads the whole class from notes. I could learn it by myself more productively.
Ok teacher.  Classes are boring, I could teach myself the material

instead of learning about society and how it works throughout different aspects of history she decides to teach an extreme version of identity politics with extremely clear biases. I'd like to learn about how societies rise to power and how they fall from power thank you very much! what a scam. I wonder why she doesn't teach that. seems important.

I've never almost fallen asleep and played more clash royale more than sociology 101 with her. She's going to send so many readings and videos and you shouldn't even bother opening them. Just show up to lecture, ignore the whole thing, do work in other classes, or whatever. You can skip three classes until you get points taken off your grade.
She is very sweet, exams are all the quizzes put together. Quizzes are online. You will be working in groups the whole semester. Try to take this class with a group of friends, 4-6 friends.

Did not like this class. Most of the time, I was lost. She goes too fast. Assigns group work 3 minutes before class ends. Assigns us group projects and made us present to the whole class (About 100 students), which I found very unnecessary, given that this is a 1-hour class. Not very technology savvy.
This prof is very nice but she gives you so much work that u wanna kys

If you are taking this class as an elective, it is not HORRIBLE- but I would avoid. It is a huge lecture, but you are expected to do group work. Professor is a bit annoying and unrealistic with the assignments. Group presentation at end of semester in front of HUGE lecture class. TA is also a bit annoying, but I would rather take her instead.

Professor Kulick is extremely opinionated. She makes her political and personal views and moral very known throughout the class. If you don't agree with her be cautious of speaking out. Rather than learning about sociology we learned what she believes is right or politically correct. Easy class to take as long as you don't have a differing opinion.

If you have another option please take the other option. She doesn't know how to teach. I didn't understand the class at all literally played the snake game all class. She puts too much information on a slide and moves too quickly. Doesn't give time to work on group projects until couple days before. Lowkey don't take this class.

Stupid easy class, the questions on the midterm and final are straight from previous quizzes and even if you dont do those their super obvious. This is a great gpa booster class I basically just played roblox whole lecture.

It's an easy class, but she is a horrible professor. This class is more about feelings than facts. Quizzes and tests are ridiculous, easiest way to pass them is to answer the question like a normal, intelligent person, then select the complete opposite answer. If you're taking Sociology as a major, you should really re-evaluate your life choices!

This is by far one of the most easiest class you could ever take. She gives you one mini quiz every week (online) and it's so easy because the answers are right on the slideshow she shares. Yes, community service is mandatory for people with lots of absences. Tbh this is the class which has probably boosted my gpa. It's impossible failing.

Has very strong opinions about lots of topics in her class and don't disagree. Makes you complete 6 hours of community service or else you lose a strong portion of your grade. I personally had a tough time listening to her lectures because she makes everything a racial issue.

Horrible grading system, takes forever to reply to emails. Made our entire class do 6 hours of community service as a requirement to pass, not the worst thing but barely anyone did it. Huge rambler, very corny. All assignments can be done without listening to a single lecture or reading a single article given. If you can stand to listen to her, A+.

not great but not terrible; chose a good group for my project so that went well; show up for class; a lot of interesting convos; mini quizzes every Friday but they're super easy & directly from readings and notes; also READ THE READINGS SHE GIVES! they're super important because a lot of exam questions are from the reading directly
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Shitty profesor . Her grading criteria is terrible . She would admit on messing up grades . I got placed into a random group for a dumb  group project . Very unfair profesor . Barley replies to emails . And if shes this I hope you become a better teacher because your not profesional. Your weird and dull .
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Thought this class was ok. Her grading criteria was odd, highest you could get was a 95 on a lot of assignments but all of them were out of 100 which brought down average. Class was sometimes disorganized and wished we did more activities so there wasn't so much lecturing. Also, felt more like a current events class, but overall was easy class.
I didnt show up for class, she posts notes online. Passed the class with a 94%

shes an average professor. her class wasn't hard but it was extremely dry. she's an easy grader but personally she just wasn't for me. the class consists of weekly quizzes, lots of reading, a midterm, final & a group project. she rarely answers her emails promptly & when she does, it's not helpful at all. overall average. take if u want an easy A.

Because of COVID, she didn't make attendance mandatory which was nice. I hated being in class though because I found it to be very boring. This class is just a bunch of readings and the midterm and final were both on those. I definitely recommend keeping up with the readings and quizzes. This class wasn't hard I just didn't find it interesting.

The class was super easy, there were weekly quizzes, a group paper due at the end of the semester, and the midterm and final. There are readings that are helpful to the quizzes, but she usually goes over them in class with a powerpoint. Taking notes definitely helped.

Prof. Kulick is ok. Her lectures can be boring and dull but her class as a whole is very easy. The only grades you really receive in the class are weekly quizzes (which are extremely easy and you really only need to show up to pass them) and the group project which is also ridiculously easy. She's nice but her teaching can be monotonous.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Group projects--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: she's nice and responds to emails quickly, but I really did not enjoy her teaching style. For a semester group project, she's extremely unhelpful. Copy and pastes the same feedback to every person in the class, doesn't give much guidance on how to work effectively as a group, and is a bit uninspiring. very nice person, just not my personal fav
