She always takes time to meet outside of class and is very helpful. 339 has a semester long webcase, weekly posts. a lot of work so pick a topic you actually want to spend 4 months on. I did learn a lot, much more than SOA101. Interesting topic and i learned a lot
- Four major assignments - Weekly reading responses - major project at buttonwood zoo (teach children about climate change) Socially awkward.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: I love Kulick, I think she gives excellent feedback and opportunity for growth. She was helpful when I asked questions and is overall, so kind!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: prof Kulick was very helpful accessible outside of class. group paper and powerpoint, midterm and final and very easy iclicker quiz make up the grade. She even gave a take-home online open book midterm if you didn't do well on the test. takes the best grade out of the two. She really cares about students and wants them to succeed.

take this for an easy A. she says shell have pop quizzes but we only took 3 throughout the semester through clickers. midterm & final are ridiculously easy if you read the readings since her questions are straightforward.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--GROUP PROJECTS--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: Easy class, attendance is always taken. Kulick is helpful but she takes a while to grade so it can be frustrating.
Easy, interesting class, but she makes it so much more complicated than it needs to be. She's a little frustrating and not the best teacher, but if you show up and do your work its easy enough.

Kulick really cares for her students. There is a good amount of work she requires you to do. Readings, discussions, group work, etc... She wants you to succeed and is willing to help her students get an A. Attendance is mandatory but shes accommodating if you have a legitimate excuse. I'd definitely take her again

I love her!! I was so worried because before I had to take research methods I heard from almost everyone that it is so hard and so much work, but I can honestly say that it is absolutely NOT true with her, she is so understanding and breaks things down so easily it can be an easy A! love her!!

I thought she was very nice and is very open to help students, she does get aggravated with students who do not participate. She sometimes goes to fast but if you let her know she will slow down and re-explain.

She was one of my best math professors. Although the topic is very hard, she tried to make it simple by doing many examples and games. She also provided extra credit opportunities, which helped a lot to my grade. 
She is also welcoming and friendly during office hours.

Literally one of the worst professors i've ever had. The entire semester she teaches you on excel but the exams are on paper so she crams formulas in the week before the exams. I was also assigned in a group project where my team member did NOTHING so I tried to speak up and said it was unfair and she basically said too bad. just terrible 0/10

Extremely rude and shows little to no accountability. Rarely attaches required course work, and blames students for this. Will get verbally aggressive with students who do not raise their hand. If you raise your hand multiple times in class you also receive rude remarks. Announces sudden online lectures that are mandatory with very short notice.

The homework is brutal and it's even worse because she dosen't even teach it. What she does teach dosen't make sense and the class is so painstakingly boring that it was hard to even keep my eyes open let alone pay attention. This class absolutely tanked my GPA and I have to retake it. DO NOT TAKE.

Professor Leili is amazing. It is definitely a hard class, so you will have to put the work in and study independently. She talks fast but don't be afraid to ask her to slow down or to explain again. She is ALWAYS willing to help you understand the concept better. Overall my entire class had a good experience with her, fun and good professor.

One of the worst teachers I have ever had in my opinion. She does not teach for the students at all. She just says things aloud in hopes you understand, and when you dont- she moves on. No hands on learning. Just hopes you know what you're doing. Go in for tutoring is the final hope if you have her. Good Luck.

Literally the most irresponsible professor I have ever had. Submission links aren't up when they should be and she's completely inaccessible outside of class. Please do not ever take this professor's courses you will despise every second of it.
Goes way to fast and gives no help when asking for it.

Whenever someone asks her to explain something again she seems annoyed. Teaches way too fast. Doesnt help you through it. Went to her office hours as well and she seemed annoyed that I did not understand the material. Way too much homework. Even assigned homework on finals week. I couldn't tell you one thing I learned in this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Avoid taking this professor at all costs.The unproffesionalism & unorganized behavior will kill grades. At the bare minimum she understands she's rough so she gives overly impossible extra credit assignments.Homework will take hours if you want to do it correctly. If you are looking to waste your time & learn nothing this is the professor for you
It is clear that what she's teaching seems very simple to her so she goes through problems really quickly because she believes them to be common sense.

terrible, terrible, TERRIBLE! she is unorganized and doesn't care about her students. she seems to start to improve, then takes 3 steps backwards. she doesn't explain things so students can understand the material, she never responds to emails, and doesn't have clear grading criteria.she never really can answer a question your asking. #NEVERTAKEHER

If you take Business Stats I urge you to steer clear of Professor Leili. Her lectures are always a mess,with unclear explanations of concepts and material.She will often throw random classwork that has the weight of a quiz with no warning and no make up. She doesnt send out any reminders for exams either. Do not take her class.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He was a lovely professor. I had him for CAS 103, he was very good at being clear when he was grading and he was very good at responding. He was VERY lenient when it came to deadlines and was very forgiving. I would recommend him for anyone taking CAS 103. I will say he did treat us like we didn't know basic things sometimes.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Really good professor, you can tell he tries really hard and cares a lot about his students. Very accommodating and will give you a great grade if you show that you cared and put in effort. Daily attendance is taken that requires you answer a question when your name is called from the roster.. easy questions though

Prof. Lamperti is very clear in his expectations out of this course. He is very nice & understanding. This is your sign to take this class for an easy A!! As long as you show effort & do your work, you will easily get an A. No tests, just tickets to class, 3 easy Unit assignments (= the sum of your grade)- 1 being building a resume. Easy stuff.

Noah Lamperti is a graduate student-professor who is great! He knows what its like to be a student and genuinely cares about if you pass or fail. Teaching a sophomore class is difficult and he does it well. The work is explained before it is due and he always will remind the class about what is due. Great class, easy work, would take again!
Hard to understand and if you miss even 1 out of 3 of the journals, you will most likely fail the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Nice lady, not a good teacher. You will be teaching yourself the material

she is such a sweet professor and really cares for her students. she gives slide shows for every week. You are required to do a ticket to class before each week but they are relatively easy. She is not a tough grader either. Love her
Unrealistic expectations out of all my professors. Does not explain assignments clearly

This class is VERY test heavy. If you are someone who struggles to learn content do not take her, because you will be teaching yourself. VERY heavy grader. While other professors were removing questions she was counting them against our grade. Takes a very long time to email back. Rubrics are not clear and will call you out in the middle of class.

I think she really wants you to succeed and is a sweetheart and she gave her all to make sure I understood the material for exams. HOWEVER she is a super hard grader and is very hard to understand, she also will explain things wrong then tell u to forget it. Overall she is nice but I don't know if id want her again but if I HAD to I wouldn't be mad
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: She forgot about ALL of our meetings for our last one I had to go and personally find her. For the exams since she didn't make it she really couldn't answer any of the question. Her result go to is she can't answer a question is that "it was probably mentioned in lecture". She told one student they were "knowledge less". Wish I could write more :(

Yall this is an updated version!!! DO NOT TAKE HER. if you have no other option, take the class a different semester. she is not organized and the rubrics are not clear. she is a terrible grader and does not know what she is talking about. if you absolutely have to take her, the class is passible if you teach yourself the content.

Awful. No organization. Gives 4 assignments a week and doesn't tell you about half of them. I was sick and she didn't answer my email until 2 weeks later.She does not know what she is doing 90% of the time and doesn't explain anything including her exams.she does not know how to spell, so be prepared for your exams to not make any sense
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Professor LeSeure is an amazing professor who is always trying to find ways to make her class more exciting and approachable. You will HAVE to put in the time with the before class assignments and there is a group presentation near the end of the course. I have had to miss class a few times and she is very lenient and understanding.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: LeSeure is such a sweet professor who genuinely cares about your success. All you have to do is try and submit the work on time and you will get an A in the class. The only issue I've found is that she isn't the best at responding to emails and sometimes there are communication issues when it comes to the work itself. Ask if you need clarification.
Professor LeSeure is such a kind person. This class is very easy, and if you're ever confused she always answers emails in detail. Love her.
Best teacher ever!! sweetest professor and will make your day amazing!

If there is any way you can avoid her then do it. She was new Spring semester last year and she is already disliked by many. She is very smart, however she is very rude about giving feedback and has no problem calling you out in front of your peers when she disagrees with you. I feel that she is very unapproachable.

I had him this Fall 2025 semester as well as last Spring 2025 for another 300 level (upper division) course for undergrad studies. I'll be having him again for a third semester because he's a kind, considerate, not too much stress Professor! 
He gives great feedback, and is willing to help you out if you advocate for yourself. 10/10 recommended.

Mandatory attendance and the only grades are a few papers. You have between 1-3 weeks to write a paper, peer review, and submit, then the cycle begins again. Fun class and great lecturer, couldn't recommend a better professor.

Super chill and down to earth, super passionate about writing and giving feedback to students while not being a super tough grader. His classes are fun and thought provoking and encourage honest student engagement. If you're looking for a class that isn't super stressful but you'll still learn something, this is it.

This professor is great and comes with great ideas when it comes to writing. He is always willing to help with projects and easy to understand. He explains everything correctly and I would 100% take his class again. 

P.S: why cant more professor's be like him...

Amazing professor I definitely recommend taking a class with him. There are readings in the first month or so of class and discussion posts. The main two assignments are 8+ page autobiographical essays, there are some other assignments sprinkled in the beginning. Very funny prof and very accommodating!
Dude is awesome. Very engaging (which I feel is tough to come by), doesn't give a ridiculous work load at all, nice and down to earth guy.

Loved this class. I'm not big into sports but this class let me explore a different writing style, and you can tell Prof. Mann is invested in the readings and class discussions. Make sure to keep up with the assignments, though. They get to be long and require going out and interviewing people.

If you have the chance to take class with professor Mann then take it! Although this class was almost 3 hours long he made it enjoyable and interesting. Your grade is based on participation, homework and 3 papers, which he gives amazing feedback on. If you want to become a stronger writer then definitely take his class.

Lucas Mann is one of the most amazing professors I've had throughout my 4 years at UMASS. He's very personable! He also wants to see you succeed in his course. He loves creativity and the more creative you are the more likely you are to pass! Don't hold back in his class he wants everyone to speak up and give their opinions on the work!
He was an excellent professor. Covered all the bases, is great with students, and it very attractive.
Great teacher, easy grader and easy on the eyes

Professor Mann is great. He is an easy grader, gives very useful feedback on papers and class is really enjoyable. However 360 isn't for shy/quiet people as its centered around comedy/satire and there's a 5-7 minute stand up routine at the end of the semester. I'd definitely take him again for his other classes though!
Helpful. Not a lot of homework. 4 papers throughout semester. One presentation. Easy grader. Best Teacher!

Dr. Ling Lin is one of the most knowledgeable accounting professors I've had. Her lecture style uses handwritten notes instead of slides, but she explains everything in great detail and answers all questions. ACT312 requires studying, but you'll learn a lot if you put in the work. Exams are fair and reflect what's taught in class. Would take again!

nice person. HW due dates are flexible. but in-class lectures are insanely confusing she switches from her lecture notes to the textbook constantly. handwriting is not the best, talks very fast, constantly tells you to refer to the texbook. when you answer a question wrong in class she makes u feel like you're slow. no extra credit.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: This was a breath of fresh air after taking a different professor for ACT 311. Professor Ling makes this class very simple by hosting attendance optional lectures, having homework/quizzes for each chapter, and the exams are similar to the quizzes but with different numbers.
shes is really nice! The homework, quizzes and test are online. She couldn't make this class any easier and she does a great job explaining things in detail.

She doesn't seem interested in teaching. I ask her questions on the chapter as I'm not understanding, and she refers me to either the text book or her notes. I schedule time with her to discuss problems I am having and in the call she will refer me to her notes or text book. I'm scheduling time with her because I don't understand them to no avail.

I thought this class was super easy, DB due once a week, a few homework assignments due before each quiz/exam (3 quizzes and exams total). 99% of the time I was able to find the answer to her discussion board topic right in the textbook. Definitely not a hard class by any means.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: One of the hardest working Professor I ever saw. She really takes her time to explain concepts. I disagree with the low rating scores because her lectures are very detailed. I learned so much from her. I myself recognize the amount of effort she puts in her lectures. Great job indeed. Enjoy the process of hard work and you will get far!PERSEVERENCE

I don't get the negative reviews about Professor Lin. She is really helpful and cares about her students and provides online office hours before exams. I am taking ACT 312 in the Fall with her! Study her study guides and the quizzes and you should be fine!
Prof. Ling is Great. I don't get the reviews says she don't know anything. She is an accounting machine. It is Intermediate accounting you have to put your effort in this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Lin is great! Those who say she isn't a good teacher or doesn't know the material do not know what you're talking about. Yes, lectures were a little boring sometimes but accounting isn't always fun. As long as you study her notes, go to class regularly, and do all your homework assignments, you should easily get an A. Take Lin Ling!

Took this professor for ACT 311 online. Course was well organized and to the point. Grading is based on homework that is due 3 times per semester, 3 quizzes and 3 exams. Course uses Wiley Plus, straight-forward -read, do homework, quiz, exam, repeat, no fluff. I'll be taking ACT 312 with this professor next semester.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Awful all around. While lecturing she covers up her notes on the projector which are already prewritten in chicken-scratch handwriting. Asks you for the answer before explaining how to do it. Won't put lecture notes online. Everyone in my class has complained to her but she makes no adjustments in her teaching style. SHOULD NOT BE TEACHING.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Professor Lin is an Accounting machine! She knows the material inside and out. I consider myself fortunate that I had her for ACT 311 & ACT312.  If you attend class, participate, nd do the reading and homework assignments, you will do fine. She prepares you well before tests, gives condensed notes on each chapter, & allows a cheat sheet for tests
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: My issue with this teacher lies entirely in her teaching style. What you learn in class doesn't translate to homework/quizzes/tests. She allows a full page front and back cheat sheet for each test so it's not hard to do well on the tests but she's always asking students to answer each other's questions. Questioning if she knows the material herself

Lin Ling is awesome, I don't know what some of you are talking about. I had her 2 semesters in a row and had As. Yes her accent is kinda different but you can understand her. Print the Chapter notes and follow along. Attendance is not even mandatory,but you  need it. She treats everyone with respect and is always there to help you if you need it.

She has to be one of the worst teachers I have had since entering college. She has a thick accent and pretty bad hand writing. Be prepared to learn more outside the classroom.  I would not recommend this class unless you have prior knowledge of the subject.
Ling is the best, hands down. Clear and has standard expectations. She is so funny and sweet, also always willing to help. If you try and participate in class she'll love you.
She was a very nice teacher, but hard to understand, she spoke really fast, but did scale the grades
She gives the best notes and goes over everything well.  She gives extensive study guides so you never have to guess what's on the exam.  I would take her again.

She is the best accounting professor I've ever had.  She wants everyone to do well.  Accounting is a hard subject, but she condenses the textbook into clear notes and makes sure everyone who cares is on the same page. She wants you to do well, organizes helpful activities, and cares.  A+

She is a smart. Gives you printable notes with all the important info, and goes over them in class so you need to go. The tests can be challenging but you'll do alright if you keep up with the hw and try on the quizzes.

I really liked Prof. Lin as a person. She's very funny and easy to talk to. My only issue is that sometimes I got confused with the way she worded things and explained them in class. But her class is structured really well and she pre-posts notes online, so if you miss a class you can stay caught up.
She is a great professor,very helpful.She does have an accent,but it didn't interfere with the understanding of the subject.I would def. take another class with her again.

best accounting teacher at the school. very helpful, quick to respond to emails. easier to understand than these reviews say. Some students who didn't like accounting took it out on her. highly reccommend
I thought she was great. Her accent is tough to understand at first but once you get used to it you'll be fine. Shes very helpful and genuinely cares about her students.

Although her accent isnt that hard to understand, the class was very boring. Attendance mandatory and expects students to participate every class. Tests are nt dificult as long as you actually bring a review sheet with you. Overall, she is a decent professor.
Fabulous teacher! Do all the homework and ask questions. She is reasonable and will always answer emails! I am an accounting major because of her!

It looks like the people who took her for 211 had a hard time. If you are taking her for 311/312, it's an easy class because you have a feel for accounting and the reviews/quizes/homework are sometimes ON THE TEST so they are extremely helpful. I would avoid her if you've never taken an ACT course before. She tends to cover material a little fast

Accent NOT overly difficult to understand.  Attendance not reqd. Class consists of explanation/review of notes posted prior to classes. Will review HW if necessary. Quiz b4 each test, drops lowest quiz grade. Study guide given b4 each test. Test Q's based DIRECTLY from study guide. Will scale tests, gives extra credit. Wish she taught more classes!
Great Teacher! choose over Chan Du for Accounting! Don't let the name fool you, her accent really isn't that bad. there are others that are much worse.

Ling Lin isn't thaaat bad of a teacher...granted you can't really understand her or read her hand writing she still cares about the students. If you want a scale on the tests just twist her arm a lil. just pay attention and you'll do well. other wise steer clear

DONT TAKE THIS CLASS! My ACT 211 class was really easy but Ling is so hard to understand. Her lectures are so boring and the tests are way different than the material she teaches. She expects everyone to know so much, when the only reason we were all in the class was for gen eds.

She is an amazing teacher. I had her for ACT 211 311 and 312 and she is by far the best actg professor at UMass. Her accent isn't bad at all if you bother to pay attention. The course is hard but she doesn't make up the exams or quizzes it is a course administrator that does this. If you actually want to learn accounting you should take her.
One of the worst decisions I've ever made in my life. If you have to take accounting and she is your last resort, you're better off waiting for another semester.. TRUST ME

I would definitely say do not take this class unless you have to. Her accent is so strong that it seems as though she can't really speak english. She also goes over the notes really fast so you can't even try and understand what she is saying. You basically get a 100 on the homework if you give some sort of effort. Taught myself everything.

As a 311 student i enjoyed the class. The course felt like 80% review, but new material was covered at the speed of light. Her Notes are useful summaries of each chapter. if you don't get something in the notes, you can find detailed examples in the book, or you can visit during office hours and she is EXTREMELY helpful 1 on 1.

She has a hardcore accent and doesnt explain anything. Everyone in my class had issues with her. Somehow I ended up with a B but I think its because I agreed to sell my soul to the devil if I somehow passed the class. My test scores were all C- or lower. I still don't know how I got a B.

She doesnt explain anything. And i have to agree with MIKE AMATO, this class is in chinese. You will def. need the book which happens to be VERY EXPENSIVE. Her tests are very hard. She's not a fair grader. And her hw's are very long and she doesnt EVER give the answers. Dont TAKE THIS CLASS unless you WANT to fail. :)

CLASS IS TAUGHT IN CHINESE. Not convinced she speaks English. the class is in chinese. if you know chinese then u'll still need some previous accounting knowledge to pass.  Test are horrible and u better have a book bc you will learn from the book. Not willing to help. Sincerely Mike Amato
RUN AWAY FAST. If you have previous accounting knowledge thats the only way you'll achieve a good grade. Straight by the book standards, no scaling, no bonus points and absolutely nothing learned.

This teacher is straight out horrible. you can not understand what she is saying and moves way to fast. one student once told her she didnt get it and she got ridiculed in the middle of class. in a class of 60 people almost 20 had to drop it. take harrington
class was very difficult and she was often very hard to understand. if you do the homework and quizzes it brings your grade up alot. overall not a bad professor and willing to help you.

She was a good professor but the lectures can be long. Take notes because some things can be on tests. The class was fairly easy, but she can sometimes go off topic and that can make everything all a little confusing

Best professor I've had, very clear in person lectures as well as online lectures that you can review. Provides a lot of practice problems and worksheets to make sure material is understood. Her lectures are extremely helpful. As long as you are paying attention in lectures you should be good.

I had Prof Lawton for GenChem2 and she makes chemistry easy to understand. She supplements her lec's w/ prerecorded videos. Watch the videos take notes and be prepared for class. Review notes, go over topics, and do practice problems/worksheets over and over until you understand them. Go to PAL sessions!
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I took AP Chem so a lot of Chemistry 1 and the beginning of Chemistry 2 was review for me. Overall I thought she explained the material well, answered any questions, and prepared her students for the exams. Chemistry in general is a difficult subject, so as long as you do the homework she gives and attend lectures you will be prepared for the tests

Best professor I have had so far. Her grading criteria is clear, she gives concise feedback, and generously grades quizzes. If this is your first time taking a chemistry class, have no fear. Dr. Lawton has got your back with both online and in person lectures. So long as you attend the lectures and participate, you are good to go.

Dr. Lawton is a great professor. Her lectures weren't the most interesting but I think it didn't help that the topics she was covering were things I already knew. Overall a great professor, she also responded quickly to any emails I would send her if there was an issue with the homework.

The class followed a very basic format that worked well. Flipped classroom where notes were due at the start of the week. She rehashed during class. She gives clear examples and was a good lecturer despite feeling a little disinterested and boring sometimes.
This class was a lecture and I didn't really learn anything but as long as you do your work you will pass. Her classes were really boring and she didn't take attendance till the end

It's chem so it will be hard. But Lawton is one of the more forgiving chem professors at UMass. Her lecture videos are to the point, but zoom is basically meaningless as she just repeats the videos. There are weekly quizzes and hw, you will do a lot outside of class. Her quizzes and exams are not difficult if you study, but put time into studying

Professor Lawton was definitely one of my favorite professors this semester. She is SO understanding and willing to work with students who have conflicting schedules. Her content and lectures are clear, concise, and easy to follow (although the content of the course was tough.) Would 100% recommend!
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: She is very understanding, responds to email quickly. Her lecture notes help you understand the concepts clearly and she breaks them into sections making learning flexible to your schedule.
Weekly assignments due on Sunday (like 10 questions max but usually around 5), two major essays, and a science project. She's funny and flexible.

Weekly hw assignments due on Sunday (like 10 questions max), two major essays, and a science project. Easy class and she can be funny. Just show up to lectures and do your work and you will most definitely pass.

(This class was online) She's a good teacher, I emailed her for feedback and she is happy to look over anything for you. This class is so easy, I recommend this class as an easy "A", as long as you get your work done your fine! We didn't take any test, you have weekly hw, 2 essays, and a science project.

She made everything very clear in her descriptions and made notes easy and simple to take. There were a fair amount of quizzes but out of the 8 taken 5 count. Make sure your homework is done or your grade will suffer greatly. Attending class is also something that you should make a priority because notes are very useful and helpful for the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Very good at teaching chemistry concepts. She does not have an accent that is difficult to understand like other professors, making the material more tolerable. Assignments are important for your grade but mostly paying attention during lecture is key. Very nice and helpful, I would take her again.
GIVES NOTES TO STUDY FOR EXAMS AND THEY MATCH QUESTIONS. COVERS ALL SUBJECTS AS PRESENTED ON TESTS. CARES ABOUT EVERY STUDENT AND WILLING TO HELP YOU SUCCEED IN HER CLASS.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: She gives an informative lecture and for those who do not understand Chem well, she has many tricks to help you learn. Homework assignments once a week and quizes once a week. Tests every 3-4 weeks. She puts a printed out version of notes on her blackboard page and goes through and does them in class. takes only the top 5 quiz grades for your grade, and test grades. The textbook is NOT used at all.
Lawton is great outside class, but lecturing is not her wheelhouse. Only take lawton if you have a lot of chem background knowldge

Let me be clear that I dont know chem.  "Why a bad rating if you dont know chem?" I went into this class with a positive attitude. I was gona work hard, sit in front, and not sleep(I did all that). I ended up with a C-. Lawton is great outside class, but lecturing is not her wheelhouse. Only take lawton if you have a lot of chem background knowldge

Don't expect to get a good grade if you don't put in the work outside of class. She expects you to read over the chapter before each lecture so you aren't clueless when she goes over examples. Put in your fair share of work outside of class and it will be easy!

This class with Lawton was pretty much my high school chemistry class, just less clear. It seemed like she skipped around quite a bit and even though I knew what I was doing, sometimes it felt like her lectures made things more confusing. HW is done online weekly and tends to be long. Would not reccomend if this is your first chemistry class.
he skips class and doesn't have his own syllabus posted he uses someone else's

The professor gives pretty good feedback and is chill. The class is pretty easy overall, but it felt like I learned nothing from this class. It was very disorganized to the point where you have no idea what your grade is until the very end of the semester.

Lowkey felt that he was rude and favored the boys in the class. He skipped class a bunch and never gave any real instructions on assignments and then graded super harshly. I'm fine with doing a monologue as a final but I'm not an actor and idk what he was expecting from us. Definitely skip him and take anyone else if you can.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Horrible professor, waste of my time. He showed up maybe 4 times. He was caught cutting class. I paid money for a class and he had us make tik toks. If you don't mind doing nothing and not being taught, go for it.

You're only graded on a meeting at midterm and the final project. The actual class periods didn't feel valuable. I had to figure out a lot on my own like formatting. He was kind and gave helpful critique and suggestions in the midterm 1-on-1, but the syllabus was basically useless and wasn't followed at all.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This professor literally cancelled class for 40% of the semester. We were in a theater class and only did one monologue which was our final. I didn't actually do any work until finals week and that's cray. Didn't learn a thing, but he did the bare minimum, so he gets a 2 instead of a 1. I was honestly on the fence between a 1 and a 2, but he chill.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: fair
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: After a month long battle with anyone who cared to ignore me, my playwriting professor, Morgan J. Peters, is failing me when he cancelled class over eleven times before taking a 'medical leave'. He assigned multiple assignments in the last week that had not been mentioned when he FAILED TO UPLOAD THE ONLY GRADED ASSIGNMENT UNTIL THE LAST WEEK.

If you show up and participate you'll get an A. It was more learning about how to do spoken word than the study of it. He canceled a lot of classes due to health issues. You have to read your own poetry and works by other people in front of the class. A portfolio of your own works is due at the end and you have to write and recite a poem for final.

Take this class! Super fun and really amazing to hear other peoples' stories and work. This class really brought me out of my comfort zone and made me a better writer. I made lots of cool friends and the professor is awesome! A must for anyone that even kinda likes poetry.

Professor keeps it brutally real and unapologetically honest. His lectures may touch on many subjects, but they are all related and make you think. If you're taking his word class, stay on top of your work, one writing a week!!!! Go to class too, you may not see him take attendance, but he knows, trust me. Best prof. on campus.

PLEASE take with caution! Great, personable guy with a lot of character and a lot experience in the film realm. But, I almost wish the course was as jam-packed and exciting as it appears to be. There's a syllabus to abide by, but he never really honored or followed, leaving this free-for-all kind of feeling. You never really know how you're doing.

The misconception about this class is that it is easy, when in reality, it is just an independent class. He teaches in a way where he directs you, but you do the research on your own. He is very knowledgeable. Attendance was not required, however he did do random extra credit attendance points. Class consists of poems and a portfolio of 13+ poems
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Disorganized and rude. Easy class, but I wouldn't take another with him.

If you take this class get ready to write! AS you have to write 25 pages of poetry for the semester. also 14 poem analysis papers. These can be on anything topic of your choice. He is very flexible and is a great teacher.
I FREAKING LOVE MOGAN PETERS, ONLY BIG THING IS THE PORTFOLIO BUT JUST DO THAT AND YOU WILL PASS. PLEASE TAKE HIM.

Mwalim is a very smart man with a long impressive resume. His class was fun and he was a pretty helpfulcool dude. However, your whole grade is based on a compilation of works (about half of which are assigned and half on topics of your choice) making up a final portfolio. The end product is 80 or so pages submitted by email. Stay ahead or headache
Pretty cool class. Learned things about black history that didn't know existed. I also took his African American studies 101 class and enjoyed it.

I wouldn't say I necessarily learned anything about literature in this class... but he really is a laid back professor. The only assignments is a portfolio due at the end of the semester. Other than that class can range from lectures on black history, music, etc. Nice guy, show up for class and participate and you will do fine.

I have notlearned a single thing in the class which irritates me because I am paying money for it. He show up late a lot or won't show up at all. All class has been is students getting up and telling stories. Grade based on participation and portfolio (13 papers)& final story. I wouldn't recommend anyone who takes pride in their education. Easy A.

Professor Peters is awesome. He's very intelligent and extremely helpful. Personally, I wouldn't mind if he spoke about African Americans. It doesn't bother me AT ALL. I would definitely recommend him.
EASY!!
No test, quizzes, or book use, just a Portfolio at the end of the semester. Go to class because he takes notice. Bring in work you've done and participate in class and you'll be fine.

guy is an absolute joke. if you want to take a black history class then take this. it isnt an english course. all he does is talk about music and why black people are responsible for everything but dont get credit for it. he sweats a lot and thinks his own jokes are hilarious. take him if you want to learn more about african american music.
Professor Peters is awesome there are two books assigned but you read it at your leisure. The only thing that is due is a portfolio at the end of the semester. But he is awesome definetly take him.

He cancels class without notice half the time. When there is class the lectures are disjointed and often unrelated. He likes to show-and-tell his own work and promote himself...a little too egotistical at times. On evaluation day he told the class to 'be nice' because 'I'll treat you the way you treat me'. To evaluate him honestly I came here.

Black Folklore: Class was pointless to attend. The only grade was a portfolio due on the last day of class and a presentation toward the end of the semester. We had to write a folktale and recite it to the class. Very easy class. I didn't do any work, did my portfolio and folktale a week or 2 before they were due and got a B.
Mwalim is a great teacher. Take his class, you won't regret it. (He's also a very talented musician and filmmaker.)

TERRIBLE TEACHER. I would not recommend that anyone ever take his class. he uses facebook to tell you something, then accuses you of spreading rumors about what HE wrote. He is ALL over the place when it comes to teaching. one of the most unprofessional teachers i've seen in my entire time at college. DONT TAKE HIS CLASSES!!

Let's just say he isn't the least bit helpful. I took this class excited to learn about filmmaking and well, learned how to fend for myself instead. He never said one piece of information that was helpful. And to make it worse, he told us he wasn't going to teach us how to edit. Told us to use a tutorial instead. What a teacher! NOT RECOMMENDED!

A complete waste of my time. I was looking forward to taking his literature course and found a rude obnoxious individual. He was always late or just didn't show up. There was no rhyme or reason to the class. The final portfolio was the only grade and I doubt that he even knew who I was or read my portfolio. Don't waste your time!

Mwalim is great! I think many of the negative comments were from users who are unfamiliar with a teacher who expects a little more from students reading a book and taking a test. His class is always interesting and while he does jump, it's all connected. He pretty much only teaches creative-type classes so learn to think outside the box and do well

His lectures were random and disconnected.I bought 3 books I didn't need.It was a lit class gone bad-more like African American Studies class.He changed his explanation of our portfolio papers way too many times and I had to keep going back re-doing what I already did.All that work! I WAS ahead, but I had to redo everything.He was also always late

I would dissuade anyone with any interest in anything theatrical from even considering this course or this professor. He shows very little respect for students or his position by showing up twenty or more minutes late if at all, but punishes students for the same behavior. His grading has no discernible criteria other than his obvious like or disli

Grr.  Took Oral Interpretation and basically did everything that he asked, in the way he asked us to do it, in the time specified, but he couldn't remember anything (despite claiming to take mental notes) and had no clue the entire time.  We sang about the P Funk and unless you had friends, it was a miserable time.
I got an A- but never did any work.  I don't think he knew my name. He pretended he saw my work two times though.    Pretty sad.
I made three auditions using what I learned in his class. Simply put, he knows his stuff.
This teacher is bomb...no tests/exams...lecture class, interesting stories, papers aren't due until the end of the semester. Highly recommended

"Outrageously poor" would put it sweetly.  Completely devoid of lesson plans, punctuality, unique ideas, respect for students, respect for theatrical peers, or any sense that what he was doing was helping ANYONE learn ANYTHING.  Disjointed, disappointing, and a shame to the otherwise reputable dept.

It's one thing to come to class an average of 20 minutes late wearing a too-small Insane Clown Posse t-shirt... it's another to give out the grading scheme during the class and being a black supremacist. Ninja, please.

The single most WORST professor I've ever had in the English Department at Umass Dartmouth.  He was always late to class.  He never knew what he was talking about. You can, however get an easy A, if you play stupid.
really cheap..
Good class, fair grader.  Participation is a must, but you are made well aware of expectations.
Great class & fair grading, if you put in the work.

I really enjoyed his classes. Took two of them and learned a great deal on the subject as well as about my management style. I found his assignments to be thought provoking and allowed for career and experience reflection.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Professor Maher is challenging in that the concepts taught in class are to be applied to current business issues. This also makes it more interesting than regurgitating business facts back to him. Fair grader with comments specific to your work. Apply yourself and do the work and you will do great. I would take his class again definitely.
I love Professor Maher! His class consists of discussion board posts and movie analysis, but if you put your time and effort into it, it will pay off! No quizzes or tests.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This class was a seven week class so there was a lot of reading in a short period of time. Each week was an in-depth discussion post, but there were no papers. Do a well thought out post each week and you will do well.

Did not provide a single due date in the syllabus. Only graded on 10 discussion boards and 3 mini papers. The first mini paper he provides you with a topic, the next two you have to pick yourself with absolutely no description as to what you are supposed to do. He is very rude. Class would have been easy if feedback and due dates were given..

I really liked this professor. Makes you work in terms of reading but still a reasonable and considerate professor. Insightful and passionate about his work, I learned a lot in his class. The only thing stopping me from giving him a full 5 stars was that he could be disorganized at times. Other than that, he was great!

This was my second class with Prof. Maher.  and this was a four week online class.  He assigns a lot of reading, articles, lectures, watch movies and then write about each along with the usual discussion board.  Most everything is opinion or experience based so there are no wrong answers.  Just do all the assignments and you'll be fine.  No tests!

Syllabus was unclear in regards to due dates. Wrote quite a few papers.  Sometimes assignments were vague on syllabus and required clarification. Very little feedback.  Unsure how to interpret some of the comments he made in his annoncements while clarifying his assigned homework, meaning somethings could be misinterpreted as rude.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Eight week online class.   One discussion board and one short essay each week.  He eliminated our final paper because the entire class was "so good".  Replies to all emails and gives decent feedback to each assignment.
She sets clear expectations from the beginning and genuinely wants to make sure you know what becoming a nurse is about. Very supportive if things come up and you need an extension.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Ill be fair, pinto is very passionate about what she teaches, if your good student and try she will like you, but she will also still nitpick and get rlly mad if you are not paying 100% attention to her which is little exhausting, grades very strict, exams are so so easy though basically common sense, dont buy the books you dont need em

seeing the bad reviews really discouraged me when I had this professor freshman year, but Pinto is extremely kind and passionate about nursing. If you have her freshman year I promise she is the best. She does assign readings but her homework only takes up to an hour which is what should be expected for a college level course, study study study.
actually terrible

Pinto is extremely kind and cares about her students. Regarding the bad reviews, she tries to make class as painless as possible and I have only ever seen her get "sassy" when people are loudly chatting or LITERALLY BURPING OUT LOUD while she teaches. I missed quite a few assignments and have an A- still so... Amazing professor, would take again.

Easy easy class i only got a B because I forgot to submit two in class assignments and thats like 20% of the grade. Shes pretty sassy and willl call you out for not paying attention or talking for two seconds shes just kinda intense.

shes a very hard grader and will nitpick certain things about your essays like no other english profesor ive ever had did; lots of points would be taken off for annotation, and sources. she also would be somewhat passive agressive in class and there were lots of essays assigned with readings and group projects. be prepared to read and write.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: I think her grading is unfair. She treats students as peers because she compares you to herself when giving feedback. She needs to learn how to teach. Moreover, she never grades homework on time because she wants to find a lot of mistakes in your homework so that you won't get good grades.
There is a lot of homework it sucks but I expected it for an accounting class. She is fun a little odd though. Make sure you pay attention cause you will get confused.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Great professor! Offers extra credit and hands out notes for each chapter. She is very engaging and doesn't just read off of slides all class. She clearly cares about what she is doing and wants you to do well. Showing up, paying attention, and doing the smart book will help you to do well.

Nancy is very passionate about her job, you will notice once you take her class. Homework is long and hard, but she is a good teacher who is willing to answer/teach every question you may have. Attendance is mandatory and definitely important for the exams, she provides study guides for every exam, however you will need to study a lot by yourself.

At first I didnt like her but she actually explains accounting concepts very well so showing up to class helps. She'll drop your 2 lowest grade assignments. Very easy to reach. However the HW she assigns on connect will take you hours to complete also her exams are on paper so this is class you need to prepare for to pass.

She hammers you with work to the point that it is obscene. Nobody can be expected to complete the weekly assignments she requires while working/living/taking other classes. Unrealistic workload that seems more like a test to see if you'll quit more than anything. Busy work should be left in high school, not an MBA program.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Professor Osborne is a great professor. Accounting is a tough subject and it requires more work than other classes. Her tests are difficult, but you will pass if you go to class and keep up with all the assignments. I realize other students have had a different experience with her, but she is more than willing to help students who show initiative!

This has been by far the worst course experience I have ever had at the University. I am finishing up sophomore year, and I have never seen a professor degrade, yell, and call out students until her. I went to her class in fear every time. I recommend taking a different professor as accounting is already a hard subject to learn.

She hasn't improved since 211, she has stopped lecturing completely and only does worksheets during class time, that takes days to go over. In order to pass her class you need to be able to teach yourself and if you don't understand it you may as well change majors. If she wasn't the only one who teaches this class I wouldn't have taken her.

May be one of the worst professors I have ever had. She does not know how to teach and doesn't care about her students. Tests are extremely difficult and the homework takes hours to complete, if you need to take this class take a different professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Don't be afraid to take this class with her! Barely any lectures. Class time is mostly spent doing group work that is NOT graded. Homework and quizzes every week on connect and tests were online. No big projects. Be prepared to do work in class. She loves when you ask questions and is super nice, one of my favorite professors!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: As nice of a person she is this class is very hard, tests are very difficult and you can find better professors, other accounting ones provide cheat sheets for tests and quizzes while she doesn't, as hard as the class already is, having a cheat sheet helps out a lot which she didn't provide, good person, but very hard class that you can get better.
good professor lots of homework.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: The Taxation class is a bit tough because of the change in tax law every year. Professor Osborne does a good job of explaining how taxes are calculated, but in the real world all of these things are calculated for you. The class has 3 exams and is structured upon chapter problems and quizzes which take a considerable amount of time to complete.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: She is not very accessible out of class, and you can tell that she doesn't want to talk to you. ALWAYS BE ON TIME She spends 15 minutes of class time berating the students who come in late or not at all. If you've never taken accounting before DON'T TAKE HER! save your GPA. I barely passed because of the extra credit and tutoring I had to take!
The way this professor explains is very confusing, I had tutoring all semester for her.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment:
I had osborne for act 211 and 212. 

She is not very kind and for me personally created,  unpleasant classroom atmosphere both due to her rude attitude and hubris towards course material knowledge.

She often showed up to class late but hounds students when they are late. 

She NEVER responded to any emails I ever sent.

Expect lots of homework.

The amount of work this professor gives is horrendous. Each assignment takes hours to complete. Her grading criteria for discussion boards is ridiculous. She is definitely one of those professors who acts like her class is the only one you're taking that semester. I would never take one of her classes again.

I enjoy suffering in this class. I think Professor Osborne is awesome and her lectures are a rude awakening for students to be accountable for what they learn. I LOVE THE PAIN, I LOVE THE HARD WORK!!! I LOVE IT! BRING IT!!!! I WILL NEVER STAND DOWN!!! THANK YOU PROFESSOR OSBORNE FOR CHANGING MY MINDSET!! GRIND IN PAIN ! Reap the FRUITS OF YOUR WORK
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Inspirational--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: her work is extremely time-consuming and she does not give a good explanation on how to do it. i would avoid her.

Be sure you show up to your zoom class on time because she makes a big deal about it if someone shows up late. Sometimes it takes her a while to start the class, but she blames it on the students instead. She does post her notes online which is super helpful. If you have Chegg Premium, you can find everything for this course on there. Lots of hw.

This is a very tough class but worth it. She gives an extra credit option to boost your score up to 9 points, which brought my grade from a B to an A. Tests are very hard and you need to study and read every single chapter. She cares very much about her students and is easy to get in touch with. I would take another course from her.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: she is the worst, gives a lot of questions on the exams and not enough time to complete it, on the exam if she sees most students get good grades, she just assumes that everyone understands and she makes it harder on the next one. she assigned class exercise and only go over some of them. Just don't take her

she is the worst, all of her tests are so hard, she gives you a lot of questions on the exam to complete in one hour. also gives you in-class exercise and go over some of the exercises she wants and does not want to give you the answers.
Very difficult class. 3 test, weekly hw and quizzes, 2-3 tax research assignments, and tax return final project. Gives you cheat sheets but exams are still difficult.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: This class is extremely difficult with the amount of work that is given. For example, in one week you have to do McGraw Learnsmart + Discussion + Quiz + Problems and it takes a copious amount of time.

Professor Osborne is amazing! She knows her students at a personal level which is rare for a school with 60+ students per classroom. She wants you to learn and is willing to help with homework assignments outside of class. She does one lecture heavy day and the others we do practice sheets. Prepares you well for the exams. Just attend class.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: I really enjoyed her class. She really wants to help her students learn. I know a lot of people really didn't like her, but in my class people barely showed up or walked in late and were expecting an A in the class. This is a class that you have to work for that A. I'll be taking her again for ACT 411.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Scatterbrained, confusing, and just tough to learn with extremely tough assignments. Dont know what possessed me to take her twice. Simple accounting can somewhat make sense with her but as it gets more complex she confuses. Shes prone to frequent errors in calculations and numbers. Attendance matters. Dont get caught cheating
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: Lecture heavy classes with group work. Cares about subject, however is not good at teaching it/asking questions. Text book is very expensive. Exams are very different than class. Expects you to learn through trial and error in a group, rather than explaining the material well. Dry lecture, gets distracted easily.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Obviously very passionate about what she teaches, but overall not a great professor. Lectures are pretty boring and it's very homework heavy. Show up to class and read the book is really the only way to do well. Taught myself a lot, unfortunately.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Professor Osborne is at best an alright professor. In order to pass her class, you'll need to show up, pay attention and take notes. I took this course 1st semester of my sophomore year and it was challenging. Most students in my class ended up with a C or B while quite a few students ended with Ds or even Fs. Do all the HW and study for each test.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: If you have any hopes of passing this class, buy the book. Professor says that all you need are the slides, but her slides have minimal information. She is very bad at responding to in-class questions and emails. Your best bet would be to steer clear.
I would not recommend this course unless you are prepared to sacrifice all other commitments.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Lectures were interesting and he is willing to help you if you put in the work. Lots of reading, but overall it was a great class.
This professor is incredibly rude and dismissive of students. He barely teaches and when a student has a question about the topics he expresses disregard and mockery.

Attendance is taken sometimes. Nulty is very knowledgable and structured in his teaching and provides thorough review before quizzes and exams. He is very responsive and is able to answer questions. If he sees you are putting the work in, he will equally put that same effort into helping you.

Professor Nulty taught this course in a manner not appropriate to the level of difficulty expected and with student failure seemingly in mind. Course content topics were unverifiable and structured poorly in an irrelevant syllabus. Engagement was discouraged in a degrading manner. Not worth it if you already have a difficult course load.

Genuinely rude for no reason. Work is hard for no reason, it shouldn't be hard but he makes it hard. Lots of quizzes worth nothing and exams worth everything. Does not want students to excel. Talks to students like they are babies/very little respect for people/makes a fool out of students who do not understand. Would never take his class again.
You basically have to teach yourself through the textbook. He's not helfpul at all and kind of rude. I genuinely think if I had a different professor I would've got an A in the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: He only grades quizzes and exams. Nothing ever makes sense. He just basically degrades us the entire class. He said he expects nothing better than C's.

You are expected to read a lot and take notes on it and then in lecture he goes over the reading and you can ask/talk about it with him in the class. For tests you just had to explain the person in the readings argument in your own words thoroughly. If you are confused he is very available to talk to offering tons of office hours.

I did not take PHL101, I took PHL 110 and PHL 350. Philosophy classes are never "easy" so if you are taking any philosophy class expecting an easy A, just don't even take it. Both classes I took with him were good if you tried. He is hilarious and makes class discussions engaging. Exams only, but it's not that bad. Def take his class

Reading his reviews when picking out classes was extremely concerning but after having him as a professor it really isn't bad. He is textbook heavy and you do have to read and come to class if you want to pass. He tells you exactly what to expect there are no surprises at all. Gives 2 weeks before tests to study with the guide and will help.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Now that I am a law student, I fully appreciate the pain this professor put me through. His closed book essay exams, which annoyed me to my core are now the standard by which I am graded for law school. The irony of my entire course grade in law being contingent on one essay exam for the semester is too rich. If you are a pre-law student, TAKE HIM.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: do not take him. you either will get a rlly good grade or fail. personally i had to drop

Actually took PHL 110 Principle of Critical Thinking with Professor Nulty, but wasn't an option for review. There is a lot of good information about argument validity that will help you in some other classes. Class lectures really helped me understand the material better and Nulty was more than will to help during his office hours.

Take advantage of the resources he gives you. If you meet with him outside of class he will tutor you. His expectation is just that you read the book and participate in class, not big asks for a college student. His lectures are great and he is reasonable. He gives what will be on exams 2 weeks ahead of time. Also gives essay questions ahead oftime
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: DO NOT TAKE THIS PROFESSOR. ALL YOU DO IS READ, TAKE NOTES, AND TAKE TESTS. YOU NEVER ANY OTHER HOMEWORK. VERY HARD TO BRING YOUR GRADE UP.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Honestly do not recommend his class. Why is he grading so hard? This is an INTRO Ethics course! His discussion posts are graded heavily, and he does the most.

Philosophy is very interesting though Nulty made intro to ethics excruciatingly devastating. I met him for office hours at 8am once to get better clarification and understanding; he belittled me and i left crying. He is insensitive and lacks empathy. Dont take this guy. A challenge is encouraged in college, but this guy is absolutely terrible.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: It was painful, but dang, I came out the other side better. Now I'm obsessed with Kafka, Dostoevsky and Camus. I took away one star simply for the mental anguish that accompanies his classes. If you want to be challenged, go. Philosophy isn't for everyone, therefore don't blame the teacher if what you learn or don't understand upsets you.
Absolutely horrible

Just no. People all have their own ways of teaching but this is asinine. Lectures at times were interesting, I have to admit that much, for however aimless they were. And the grading? Have standards, fine. There are plenty of cool Professors who can be difficult and respectable. This is not that kind of Professor. 

"Torture" would be charitable.

Content not that difficult; actually is interesting, but he managed to over-complicate and overwhelm. The student is expected to parrot theory in graded handwritten essays "in your own words." In "other words", you must "word" things "his way". If you understand the material, it doesn't matter. He will torture you if he doesn't like you. Period.
WARNING: Extremely demanding. Every class is treated as it were a 400 level class. Only philosophy majors should attempt his class. Avoid him if you have a full-time job and family.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--So many papers--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Lots of papers with research. High expectation.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Amazing! Great lectures, makes time for students, he will even do meditation with you. He does have some drawbacks, he's a tough grader and the courses are paper/essay heavy. But, all exam questions are given 2 weeks in advance and he will personally check them and give advice. Take advantage of him and you will do well.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Test heavy with only open response questions. Expectations are high, tough grader. Accessable for office hours. Test questions are given ahead of time. Lectures are interesting and if you miss them you are behind. Class content is directly taken from the reading books. Learned a lot and changed my perspective on the world.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Absolutely terrible I withdrew from the class a good way through. 14 page papers were required to get an A. Just stay away from this guy who thinks he's way smarter than everyone else.

Easily one of the hardest classes I have ever taken. Also one of the toughest graders with a weird grading system. If you don't put a ton of work and detail into your exams and discussion posts, you'll get a B or B- AT BEST. Wasn't accommodating to people if they missed exams, so if you miss one you automatically get a 0. Attendance isn't taken.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: This can be a difficult class if you are not willing to do your part. Professor Nulty is completely open to helping you understand the material, but he will not babysit you like some other professors do. If you need help, you must reach out and explain. He is one of the most intelligent professors I have come across and always willing to help.

Super tough grader for everything. When he grades he just tells you whats wrong but doesn't explain how to fix it. Expects everyone to go to online office hours, which is unreasonable especially for online students. Wish I withdrew from this class when I could've.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I started out hating this course but after reaching out to Prof. Nulty, he was willing to help me understand the course material and check my test answers. The content in the course is very interesting but there is a lot of reading and writing required for the course. He's always willing to help his students and his explanations are helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Tim must think he's an award winning Harvard philosopher. He is in fact a mid level intellect who thinks his understanding of philosophy supersedes the ability of the average moron. Gave me a bad grade because I didn't use quotes from the book I couldn't get my hands on (Used class materials to the fullest). He then told me he was being generous.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--So many papers--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Don't get me wrong - he's a difficult professor. Expects a lot of his students. That being said, if you actually put in the effort and show interest, he will want you to succeed. He's tough and strict, but isn't the type of teacher that wants his students to fail. Don't take if you're not willing to put in the effort. Test & homework heavy.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This class was awful! I took in the middle of COVID. He only graded us based off exams which were unreasonably hard for a graduation requirement class. He is also incredibly difficult to get in touch with outside of class. Avoid this class and professor at all costs there are much easier people that teach it. Just wait a semester to get your credit
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things--So many papers

reviews.comment: I didn't choose to take this class, it was chosen by my advisor. i did not like this class at all. the professor gave way too much work thinking we have no life outside of school. he gave us a week do to everything. we had weekly discussion board posts with 4 exams. those exams are 5 questions with 4-6 paragraphs per question. i do not recommend.

This class is based on 4 exams and weekly discussion boards. His exams are 5 questions to be answered with 4-6 paragraphs and you have a week. My first exam was 4800 words and my second was 5500. You have to get the book and do detailed analysis for every writing and discussion board. Use outline questions, answer them, use them for exams!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Took during COVID. 4 tests worth 20% of grade each. Expects essays out of discussion posts and grades super tough. You wont do well if you dont read bc everything that is on tests is direct from the book and not online. He's a nice guy but grades way too hard for a class that is an elective for some just learning PHL. If you can find something else
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Lectures are very important, make sure you go. Prof. is very easy to understand and gives a lot of analogies for it to make sense for you. Keeps the topic interesting. Tough grader but gives feedback instead of just labeling wrong.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: If you get stuck in a class with him the only way to pass it is to go to his office hours and listen in class. He gives us the exam questions a week before and expects us to come to office hours. Please don't skip on class, the readings and avail office hours. It was not a hard class, just memorization and a bit of effort.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Best professor Ive had. Not a class to take if youre looking for an easy A, but if you put in the work (and read!) youll do well. His lectures are entertaining, almost like a conversation. He really wants you to learn deeply; he doesnt teach shallow, simplified stuff. Hes passionate about the material. Usually 3 tests and an essay (7 pages)
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Will not let you pass the class if you don't come to class, despite his lectures being word-for-word what is written in the readings. If you go to Clark, don't take his class. Despite not going to class, I did the readings and answered the exams exactly the same as someone else who did go to class and our grades where different.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Nulty is overall a really good guy. He is very willing to help you understand the material if you are struggling, and expects excellence in analysis. It is imperative that you do the class reading, otherwise you will be lost, and will not do well on exams. He is very straight forward in grading and what he expects from his students.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Nulty is an excellent Professor. He is passionate in what he does and he creates interesting lectures that left me wanting to hear more. The class is going to be difficult at first, but will get easier as you go on. Sure, he makes the exams difficult, but he is one of the most fair professors I have ever had.

I took this philosophy course to learn and gain knowledge. However, this professor is relies on loads of difficult reading, to which he unpacks in one class, and quickly moves on to the next. My biggest disappointment is that there is no room for independent thought. Ironic. The class is about memorization not about learning.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Professor with an elevated sense of self-importance; he believes his way and word is essential to students, even though his lectures are almost verbatim the same thing as the readings in the book. For good grades on the exam, you are expected to memorize the readings and copy the chapters down. No room for independent thought; ironic for philo
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Amazing professor. If you actually have any interest in philosophy he will have a burning heart to help you succeed in the course. But he is really hard on people that take the easy way out like cheating or taking answers from other students. But he really burns for his craft even though he can sometimes not realize the stuff is complex.

By far the worst Professor Ive ever had. Please do yourself a favor and choose a different class. He doesnt even Thoroughly explain the material he is teaching he expects you to already know the material somehow. Definitely dont take this class if you dont like reading cause you wont pass.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: I withdrew from his class because I was interested in actually learning ethics/arguments as a skill rather than just memorizing copious amounts of information to SEEM smart. There are no class discussions and he never checks to see if you actually understand the information before he's moving on. Just get out, there are better professors to have.
reviews.rating_tags: TEST HEAVY--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Professor Nulty was a very difficult teacher at first but he is a genuine guy. he makes you work for the work and its becomes really easy to do after a while. If you do not meet with him for help or show up to classes its going to be difficult but i highly recommend this class. Most engaging class I've ever been in!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Inspirational

reviews.comment: A complete and utter nut job! Would not release my grades to me and then tried failing me on an exam because he "didn't like the adjectives I chose." I ended up having to withdraw from his class and I still need to report him to the dean of students. He has been the worst professor I've encountered at UMassD.

Nulty is one of the best lecturers on campus. His class is interesting and thought provoking, and he is unbelievably intelligent. Attendance doesn't directly effect grades, but miss class and be prepared to bomb your weekly quizzes. I found his grading to be inconsistent, but there's no little to no written homework & he drops the lowest quiz grade
A lot of reading. A quiz every week. For a 101 class, he expects a lot from students.

Professor Nulty is an amazing professor and gives such clear and awesome lectures, but the quizzes and tests he gives are super hard. As long as you read and go to class you should do well, and he is very open to meeting outside of class to clarify anything. It's a difficult class, but I think his lectures make it worth it.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: I'm an art student so I took his class as one of my mandatory classes that I need to graduate but after having him, I would love to have him again! He actually gets the class to laugh and goes through a really interesting subject with clarity and ease. Not the class to take if you just want an 'easy A' but its amazing and easy if you actually care!
Amazing professor. Without a doubt my all time favorite class
Quite literally the best teacher I've ever had. I wish he taught more classes at Clark

Prof. Nulty gives a good introduction to college philosophy, but is limited by the scope of his syllabus and unlikely to give outside reading even if students show interest. Fair grader and absolutely hilarious!

Definitely NOT an easy grader. His exams are extremely DIFFICULT.  Good professor but for a 101 class, he shouldn't expect perfection from his students. It should be an introduction class for people with zero knowledge prior to this class who NEED guidance. I am not saying he should baby the student but instead acknowledge when a student is trying

Not only is he an awesome dude in general, but the guy knows his stuff! Dr. Nulty is great at teaching the subject, integrating an awesome sense of humor and weird but funny examples in his lectures. It helped me learn and he does well at giving good feedback and making sure you get the material. A+

This class was fantastic! It gave me a solid foundation for future classes! I literally passed 3 subsequent philosophy classes because of the rock solid foundation I got in this class! Awesome professor! Pay attention and it will make your entire academic career that much easier. Many classes (not just philosophy) are based on these principles! )

Learned SO much in this class! Do a little bit of reading and look at Nulty's comments/study notes.(he basically gives you the answers for the exam ahead of time) Best online course I've taken so far.

You will have to read a lot, and considering that the material isn't easy-obviously, its philosophy, you will have to invest time into your homework. Honestly, if you read and actually TRY than the class wont be difficult. If you're worried about having to read for a college level course you should probably ask yourself why you're in college.
