VERY hard class. Would not recommend taking an online class with this prof! What he posts is very difficult to understanding and he does not help when you have questions. I had a 4.0 GPA before this class.

Nulty should clone himself and teach every class at UMD. Smart, funny, helpful, cares about students. Type of prof. who challenges people and can change your whole life perspective. And for those of you who complain about the reading, you shouldn't be in college if you are going to moan about having to do work to pass a class. seriously ridiculous.

This class is crazy hard. You have to read or you won't pass. His sarcasm is a little bit annoying and not cool sometimes. The final and midterm consist of a lot of studying and reading so  don't take this class if you're lazy on reading. Overall cute guy sometimes

I am a business student that's not the strongest in philosophy. Was able to get a B in this class. Def a challenge but didn't have to exert myself too much for the B. Easily one of the best professors I have ever had at UMD, so interesting. This is everything a college course SHOULD be wish UMD had more of this.

Professor Nulty is by far the best professor at UMD I have had. If you are interested in Philosophy, take every class you can with him. His classes are challenging and you have to work hard for good grades but you will learn so much. The guy is so brilliant and knows everything about philosophy.

Extremely difficult professor, but well worth it if you can pass his classes. He weeds out the lazy students in the beginning. He only wants people who are serious. You have to do the readings EVERY night, usually 2 or 3 times over in order to pass his random quizzes. Midterm and final are HARD but def. possible to get an A if you study hard enough
Professor Nulty looks specifically attractive when wearing glasses.  In all possible worlds.

Awesome Prof! so helpful. have to go to class and read every night. tests are out of the random but clear if you read and pay attention. TAKE NOTES. exams are based on notes and he helps if you have questions.

although he might be the hottest teacher on this campus , he is HARD ! you MUST read in order to even know anything about this course, he gives alot of work and his tests are hard. you deffinatly have to study. but he is so awesome nad interesting and VERY helpful if you need help with anything. i reccomend him, youll learn alot and have fun!

Awesome teacher. Always answers students' questions well. Really really knows his stuff. Even if the subject is dry, he makes it funny and entertaining. Class was really difficult, with two long papers and two essay exams, but he always encouraged people to go to his office hours, which I highly recommend. Very helpful.

In reading these comments, I am a tad bummed I had him for an on-line class, which I absolutely do not recommend! He demanded a lot in the discussions, yet he left little feedback to let us know if we were answering the questions correctly. I feel like this course consumed my semester. In the end, I'm not quite sure what I learned.

Fantastic teacher, he's very smart, and you will actually learn something from the class. Attendance isn't mandatory, but he gives pop quizzes on the reading he assigns, which are important to your grade. Sometimes drops subtle hints, like reminding you to read the class before. Just read, and you will get an A in the class.

not crazy about him. yeah, he's goodlooking but he plays favorites and thinks he's funny when sometimes he's just an ****. He expects way too much out of his students and thinks that his class is the only one his students are taking. Too much work ... wish someone put a comment like this up before i took his class!
I love him. SO much. You do have to have a brain in his classroom though. It is college, folks.
Nulty's class is the real deal. Probably the best professor I've had.

LOVED THIS CLASS! NOT AN EASY ONE...Nulty is an excelent professor! You can totally see your $$ worth when you face a prof like this! Lots of notes, and please take them, that will save you! I worked very hard, never missed any class, but had a lot of trouble with the material, got a C+. So study hard, will definately not be an easy one, but totall

This class was great, but if you don't show up you will miss something important related to a test or paper. His office hours are very useful, so go to them if you can.  I learned a lot from this class and he treats you like an adult, so tough luck if you think this class is a breeze.

Honestly , one of the best professors i have ever had. He rewards hard work and roots out the lazy students.He explains things so that everyone can understand them. Riveting class , he turned me from a devout catholic , to an atheist...

As he wrote on the board the first day of class, "Nulty is a pain in the ass". But this class was extremely interesting! This is definitely one of the best courses at UMD. He made me rethink my major for sure. I highly recommend taking it. Not an easy A; attendance, notes, and readings are very important. Pay attention and you'll do fine!
one of my fav classes!

This class was one of the best I have ever taken at UMD. He knew what he was talking about and made it interesting. The notes are a big deal because the papers and exams are basically your notes! Its tough but worth it! Great class!
Tim Nulty is perhaps the greatest professor to walk to halls of UMD. He's funny, knows his stuff, and he'll blow your mind.

THIS IS NOT A BLOW OFF CLASS!!Excellent teacher. Extremely helpful and great lectures.If you don't do the reading or put in the work you will fail. If you're interested in philosophy take this class. Its worth the work.

I don't understand how there are so many possitive comments here! I'm a responsible student who put TONS of effort into this class and still struggled! Don't be fooled by Nulty's smiles and light humor in class, he's not here to be your friend. Take advantage of his offer to read your paper during his office hours! They will save you!

Loved this class. It was pretty hard when i started. If you read the text and you understand what he says in class, you'll do fine. Going to his offic hours is a plus. It helps you, and it shows your willing to put the effort in. The class gets you thinking.. I learned a lot.

I had Nulty for my first ethics class and LOVED it. He shared my impatience for slackers and bad writers. He is very helpful with papers because he wants you to improve. Take advantage of office hours and put forth a decent amount of effort. He won't hold your hand/baby you into getting an A.
STUD and an awesome professor!
hot! hot! hot!

Awesome, funny and nice, is really into the material, had a night class that was going to be canceled because the original professor dropped out but Nulty didn't want it to be canceled, so he took over, great guy

Nulty expects a lot from his students, as you should expect a lot from him.  You are not paying to be educated in a dumbed down, insulting, 4th grade kind of way.  If you seriously pursue the readings, and are willing to accept the idea that your unexamined views of morality are probably mistaken, you will find this class to be truly life changing.
He is extremely hot and it sometimes makes it hard to focus but besides that he is an outstanding professor. The class is difficult but he really helps you out. Cheers for Nulty!!!
Nulty makes ethics interesting and funny. He will challenge your moral beliefs, but you learn from it. Its a hard class but worth it.
SO so so HOT!  Worth your time & grade.

Nulty is the man, he is incredibly knowledgeable when it comes to philosophy and whatnot, he is freaking hard, slightly pompous and hilarious, if you are a jackass, he will single you out and make you feel stupid.
Great teacher! It was hard work but I definitely learned a lot in his class because he makes everything so clear. Definitely recommend taking a class with him.

Professor Nulty is amazing. He's funny, interesting, and makes Ethics understandable. He doesn't let you slack off, but he's always willing to help. He really encourages people to come to see him for help on papers, midterm questions, etc. If you must take a PHL class, or want to--make sure it's with Nulty! (Plus...let's face it. He's teh hotness)

This guy is so hot! He's really helpful and he gives study guides for each week. His tests are just over the study guides and there's only 2 papers, 2 tests and 2 quizzes during the class. Really easy.

This guy is awesome! One of the best professor's I have ever had. I have 6 classes a day MWF, and his class is my last. The only way I can get through, is because I look forward to his ethics class. He's a cool dude, and loves to help. You will be forced to think differently. Girls like him, so there are always hotties in his class.
he is a really good looking guy and probably one of the bets teachers that i have ever had. hes course is a little tough so you cant slack but its all well wroth it
No Comments
No Comments

Okay, it's pretty clear that Nulty is absolutely adorable. And intelligient. And witty. And overall really nice. And married. Haa. Seriously though, prob one of the best classes I've taken at UMD. He is demanding but it's totally worth it,if you aren't the slacker type. You will NOT pass if you're just taking this to get credits.
Professor Nulty is probably the most demanding teacher in the philosophy department. He expects a lot, but the classes themselves are pretty cool. on a side note, he's cute

Tim Nulty is hot. beyond that he explains the material well, but expects reading between every class and sometimes will get frustrated with classes that dont read often. Aside from that he injects the lectures with fun and humerous examples and knows how to keep the material interesting.

not only is mr.nulty hot and extremley nice, but hes absolutley with out a doubt the best teacher that i have ever had at UMASS... hes very clear when explaining things in class and if theres something that you dont get hes always available at his office. Amazing =)
his ethics course is tough, he likes to throw wierd ethicists in but explains stuff really well, he's cute and funny and he makes ethics really fun
Great guy, it really pays to go to his office hours. he makes the class interesting and it helps that he's funny and cute. definitly recommend taking a class with him, he's awesome!!!

Prof Z. Has been one of the easiest psych professors you could ask for. He has the same grading through and through and the only way I passed Stats was because of him. I took him for Psych States, Social Psych, and another one I'm blanking on. 9/10, use your TA if you need to and ask as many questions, he's always welcoming.
Prof Zambrotta was an amazing professor. His humor and character made the lectures enjoyable. He is a fair grader, and the class was not too hard. I do not have any complaints.
Hes great one of my favorite teachers

As someone who was dreading taking stats, professor Zambrotta made it easy to follow and do well in the class. He offers a lot of extra credit and you can easily get points with homework. His study guides follow closely to the exam so your not caught off guard. Overall a great prof who wants you to succeed! Take him if you can!
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Dr. Zambrotta is a great professor! While sometimes the lectures are long and can drag - he makes the class very informative and really wants his students to pass. The amount of assignments he gives really helps If you are someone like me who sucks at exams. Complete every assignment because it really counts toward your grade!!!
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: take him, quizzes are online, two exams which are online but in class but he not walking around, homework for each chapter due before class but he only grades if you turn it in on time not if how many you got correct. he also has 3 labs but the first one is only worth 5%. IF YOU SLOW TAKE HIM PROMISE YOU WILL PASS
This mf is the goat, classes are easy but still learned a lot and his lectures are easy to follow. Very nice dude down to earth

If you're a PSY major I would definitely recommend taking any class you can with Zambrotta. He's easy but not too easy to the point where you're bored. He keeps his lectures interesting and allows for fun class discussions on the topics. Had 1 group project during the semester but nothing too difficult. Exams are also very easy and straightforward

Stats was pretty hard for me HOWEVER, Professor Zambrotta's class is hard to fail if you aren't trying. I was able to understand a decent amount of the work because he always explained it very clearly. He is also overall easy to talk to and funny, and while stats was not easy, he made it less miserable in his teaching method.

Zam is super funny. I loved having him as a teacher but sometimes he talks way to slow and I want to fall asleep during his classes. His classes are super lecture heavy but I enjoyed the facilitations as they were super fun  and I received an A in mine! The tests are a little difficult but if you come prepared you will be fine.

Sometimes his lectures can be confusing in comparison to high school stats classes I have taken but he's super kind and always willing to help, understands when you need to miss class, and gives opportunities for good grades

This was Dr. Zambrottas first semester teaching at UmassD, and I am so happy I ended up in his class! Hes so awesome, Ive already enrolled myself in his capstone. If youre anxious about methods and want good grades from a chill dude, he is your guy (I got an A)! He also comes prepared with his own memes?! I mean whats better than that?
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Gives good feedback--Hilarious

reviews.comment: I'm pretty sure this was his first semester teaching at UMass or at least teaching a methods course but he was awesome. Very clear, very helpful, and very understanding with us. You get the typical amount of work for a methods course. Don' t be surprised to write a 15 page research paper and do a lot of statistic work.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Group projects--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: This course relies on outdated lectures from 2017. The professor speaks too fast, and while his language is simple, exams are much harder and include material not covered in class. Excel is used often, but poorly taught. Homework is easy, but tests are not. Overall, I do not recommend this professor.

A quiz every day for attendance?? Sounds terrible. If you leave the last 10 minutes?? Would've been better off skipping cause he gives the quiz the last 5. Come in 15 minutes late to your 9 AM class?? You don't even get to take the quiz and would have been better off just skipping.

I don't get the hate, Dr. Olitsky is one of my favorite professors. Class is very difficult and if you don't attend and don't study proficiently you will fail, but he wants nothing but the best for his students. He's super caring and will always take the time to help you. But, you have to pay attention to his lectures, or you'll fail. Amazing guy!

Good notes, extra credit. HW every week, isn't bad itself. But will assign EXTRA work when he cancels class. Will have HW due same day as an essay/project.Tough test grader. 2 free response worth 10 pt, MC worth 2-3pt each. If you forget one random concept, you're down 10-20 pt, even tho the FRs are same type of question as MC which are worth less.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Does not get enough credit... I came into this class dreading econ & he made it interesting & took tricky concepts & made them easier to understand on a scale that us college students would grasp & relate to. Yes, the exams are tricky, but a lot of the questions he reuses from the daily quizzes & practice exams, so study those & you'll be golden!

He just reads his notes. He uses simple language BUT his tests are way harder and a lot of the calculations he did not talk about. 
Long exams with 40 questions and in no way there is enough time to answer everything.  
 Homework assignments are easy BUT exams are way harder and different.
 This professor is terrible, and I do not recommend him.
Macro economics is a tough class however he does a great job at teaching it. Tests are hard make sure you study. The only really annoying thing is the attendance quizes.
Offers no extra credit and his tests are hard.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: This professor is extremely tough despite studying so hard received poor grades. I feel drained.
Professor Neal has great knowledge of economics and is dedicated to his work. He never gets tired of answering student's questions, in and outside the classroom!
Class moves very fast, lengthy homework assignments that do not really help with the exams that much, very inflexible, and expects quite a bit for a 200 level class. Not a class I would take again.
EXAMS WAY TOO MUCH WISH HE GAVE NOTE CARDS TOO MANY EQUATIONS IMPOSSIBLE TO MEMORIZE

EXTREMELY tough grader. Tests are 115-135 questions, but all are worth a different number of points. Some are worth 2 pts others worth 16pts. You could get more questions right than wrong and still fail the test. Does not allow you to see your test after it is graded unless you submit a written request which he can deny if he wants.
His tests are too hard. He over exaggerates errors you make. Hated every second of this.

Not a bad professor by any means, but his classes can be very tough and time-consuming compared to other econ professors. Long problem sets and very difficult exams can be expected in any of his classes and his lectures/videos don't prepare you enough so expect to read the text. He can be cold at times but is sometimes understanding.

This class was extremely hard. There were no zoom sessions and I felt like he just gave the class a link to purchase the textbook and have us do the work on our own. He is a tough grader when it came to projects.

Taking him for online classes during covid-- Do not take this class, it's the only one that is unreasonably stressful. Homework and quizzes are due every 2 days on top of lengthy lecture notes. This is the class I spend 10 or more hours a week on. Be prepared for all-nighters.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: He is a nice and caring professor, and class isn't unenjoyable per say, but I felt like no amount of preparation could have made me get anything above a B on any test. Luckily homework and classwork are weighed heavily enough to make up for some rough test scores, but the test scores will be rough.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Taken online 4 winter There's easy homework due almost every night ,lectures can be long and the tests are way harder than any of the homework. The homework, even though its due every night only counts for 25% of the grade. The rest is based on 3 tests that each take over 2 hours to complete. Lectures are super boring. It's manageable but intense.
Dry lectures, exams are very hard, must avoid! offers little to no extra credit
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: This professor is the worst teacher I have ever had so far at UMASS Dartmouth he has no personality and is honestly such a bore. I wouldn't recommend him to someone even if I didn't like them.

The exams are hard, the Aplia assignments are super easy, one essay (if you meet with him and revise it to how he wants it you'll get a 100), Iclicker questions every class. You have to go to this class or you will fail, try to attend all classes because that counts as 'extra credit'.  Drops lowest test. I got two 75's on my exams, still got a B+!

His exams are hard. There are 3 exams and he takes the best of the three exams. There is homework due every Sunday night but the homework is easy. You have to pay like $140 to do the homework but its not hard. There is one paper. He is SUPER boring in class and lectures the entire time.

Definitely would recommend. If you have taken micro before you'll ace the first exam but the next two are difficult. However, he takes the best two out of three exams. Have to show up to every class to get icliker points. Homework is due every sunday online
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: The ECO 342 online class was not too bad, but the group work was awful.  Pre-assigned groups result in working with people who do not want to contribute.  Exams are lengthy!  Super responsive Professor, though and that was great!
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: I found the lectures to be mundane, as he basically read the slides aloud.  Tests were tough.  Glad I got through this class and will never have to take it again!  He was responsive if you emailed him...

First off this subject is boring and dry and he already makes it more boring. He's a cool professor and helps you if you don't get something but the material sucks. I studied so hard for every exam and didn't pass one, they're so hard.

Professor Olitsky is a great professor, and he goes over each topic extensively, making sure everyone understands. However, his class is based on three tests that are a large part of your grade, and each test is extremely hard. The class average for the first test was failing. He also doesn't scale, so be prepared for lots of studying.

Neal is pure and simply, awesome. Take him any chance you get. He writes all of his notes during class so there's no way for him to go too fast. I have over a 100 average. He will scale tests if the class does poorly, and he drops the lowest of 3 exam grades including the final. All homework is online so just buy the access key, not the book.

As long as you show up to class every day and do the iClicker questions, along with the Aplia homework assignments, you should do well.  The tests are easy, it's the same stuff out of your notes.  For Macroeconomics at least, there was very little math to do.

You definitely wanna show up to every single one of his classes because he uses the iclicker for attendance. The iclicker questions are actually pretty helpful for you to fully grasp what you learned that day. His tests are REALLY REALLY HARD. It seems like what you learn in his lectures are elementary, but the exams are incredibly difficult
Really hard tests. A lot of formulas to memorize for exams with no cheat sheet. didn't pass a single exam

This guy is one of the best professor I have ever had in college. His lectures are boring, but  is a great professor. He really loves economics which makes the class a lot better. He always there to help with whatever you need. If you work hard you will get an A. Strongly recommend for any class he teaches.

Love aplia and iclicker because they helped me know if I actually understood the material. Sometimes he went too fast and I was confused but he never hesitated to go over something if you asked. Scaled tests slightly but average grades were quite low. I did very well (I hate math and struggle) mostly because of repetition. Barely read the book.

Had him for micro 301. Extremely hard material that he goes through way to fast as if we were suppose to already know the material. Tests are not completely relevant to the material he goes over and they are not scaled at all. Nice guy but would not take him a again. He teaches as if at a graduate level.

Deffinatly not very fair, very jolly guy though. When he teaches he goes way too fast as if we had already learned the material. grading is a little difficult. Avoids eye contact with students he doesn't really like.

Not an easy class but that's why we're in college. Sounds like some of these other students need to learn a little thing called academic responsibility. I worked hard in the class and got a good grade. Hard work equals good grade. Not that difficult to understand.
Good professor.
Clear, concise, and helpful.  He rewards effort and constantly pushes assignments past their due dates.

Test averages seemed to be in the 60 range for the whole 70 person class, although hebdoes scale, its very low and unhelpful. He makes it very difficult to study because all you have is aplia and your notes. On top of that, this guy is a complete jerk to everyone. Hes rude and I swear has a 5 foot pole up his bum. Class is doable but unadvised.

Great guy, handwrites all notes.  Uses aplia for homework.  Does in class worksheets and examples.  Drops lowest test grade and two lowest quizzes.  Hard to fail this class.  Attendance is not required but missing a class will set you back.

Great teacher, i had him for a mixed class and he always took the time out to remind us of exactly what was due for the mext week or so. hand writes his notes so you can keep up and post EVERYTHING on mycourses. very helpful if you ask. and has a great grading system that drops lowest exams, quizes and aplia HW's. i would deff take a class wid him!

yes he does have an amazing system and also drops lowest quiz, atteneding the TA review sessions before exams are helpful, attendence is not taken but it was probably why i got a D because it was my last class tu/TH so i would always skip it. overall not a terrible teacher

He hand wrote notes every class and you basically copied them. Pretty boring stuff, but he encouraged discussion when relating class material to real events. Weekly quizes are easy as long as you are going to classes. Our tests and final grade were scaled by a letter grade.

Very difficult, but you'll be happy you took his class in the long run. Has a life-saving scaling system, homework is really extensive, and lectures are intense and require mucho note taking. Overall, he has an extremely in-depth understanding of the subject matter and is willing to help. Like someone below me said, if you miss class, YOU WILL FAIL

very demanding class, but he does an okay job with it.  scaling system is amazing.  if you got a mid 70 on an exam, it'll transfer to an A.  attendance isnt mandatory but if you don't go to class, you WILL fail.

an Ok teacher. He works you from bell to bell with notes. covvers a lot. The tests seem much harder than classwork. He does scale. Pretty strict with homework and papers being on time. Can be a little confusing at times. ok overall

Rubien is SUCH a down to earth, respectable professor. What I think sets him apart is rather than telling students he cares, he genuinely shows it, and us students feel his authenticity. He can def be strict about coursework, but his teaching style is diverse and relatable. Rubien sees students as human beings, which is uncommon in this program.
Very nosy and opinionated. I felt like he was more interested in my life than my learning. Had some knowledge of content but too busy trying to be a friend and not faculty.
Prof Rubien is amazing and teaches the material in a way easy to grasp. He cares and tries his best to advocate for all students. Easily one of my favorite professors.
Very funny and caring professor and makes attending lectures worth it. Too caring at times which is annoying but overall a great person and a nice professor who knows what hes talking about.

Rubien is an excellent professor in the MLS department. He gives learning objectives which is what you need to know for quizzes and exams. He cares about your education. If you put in the work, he will like you. He may come off as strict at first, but give him time and he will open up. Its an intro class, so you will cover the basics of MLS
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: He is a really caring professor that wants to help his students in any way he can. He clearly communicates his expectations (which are lenient) and is very generous when grading. He sometimes gave extra credit to students just for showing up. He's available outside class and flexible with his students. He is also very passionate about the subject.

This is an intro class to the college. He normally teaches MLS but also taught this class last semester. He is very caring about his students and wants them to lean and succeed. He is also very fair with his grading and easy to talk to. Attending class is also important when taking his classes. I highly recommend having him for a professor.

Professor Rubien's test are not just what he postes on slides, and says not to write down what he talks about in lectures because he wants you to learn. He is a tough grader on his written assignments. He is interesting to listen to however I don't recommend taking this class unless you have to because it is not an easy A.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Take this class if you can! He is extremely nice and treats the class like what it is- a 100 level course. Extremely nice and generous grader. Definitely would take him again.

This professor surprised me. I took his class during his first year of teaching. He was very helpful and quick to respond to emails. He took his predecessors lectures and removed unnecessary info. He gave questions pertaining to the ASCP exam. My computer corrupted all my notes days before an exam and he sent me all his powerpoints.A great teacher.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: Very entertaing and kind professor. He makes any laboratory fun and I am currently taking him again!
Professor Rubien is an amazing professor! He is funny, helpful, and a fair grader. Take him if you wish to succeed!

Professor Rubien is a very down to earth professor, he is extremely relatable and understands what we're going through. He is the type of nerdy cute professor thats fun to have! He is a tough grader, but I'm definitely planning on taking him again.

Professor Rubien was extremely helpful during lab. He's my favorite professor at UMD and I would highly recommend taking his class. He's really nice, gives great feedback, and will help you to understand the lab/material if need be.

I enjoyed doing the StockTrak project he assigned and the 10 articles, they were interesting. I will admit though his lectures can be boring and sometimes his writing is hard to understand. Many questions I studied for on exams weren't on it which made my exam grades not that good. Overall, you wont be able to avoid him either way

This is the second class iv taken with him, His exams are always hard and he gives a ton of reading and homework. He doesn't give any breaks for his summer class so be ready to do 16 weeks of work in 4. This class was not as bad but I feel like all his classes are super math heavy. class was mandatory and no other professor was available. good luck
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: His tests are very long, and he includes a lot of tricky questions. Some questions aren't answerable at all, which is the answer. He is kind of rude, unfortunately. He teaches several mandatory classes, so you can't avoid him. Make the best of it and move on. If you can avoid his classes, I would recommend doing so. Excel wa godsent for the formula

Took both 383 and 484 with Professor Nquyen. The material is difficult, but he goes through plenty of practice problems. While he may not seem approachable he is willing to work with you if you do not understand the material. The Homework is tedious but is good practice, his exams are very similar to both the in class problems and HW.
WORST PROFESSOR EVER. DO NOT TAKE. TESTS ARE PROCTORIO AND ITS BULL SPIT

Professor Nguyen is tough. He is accomplished in his field, but never tries to make you feel inferior. The material in itself is extremely challenging and there's no way to frost it. You must devote yourself to this course if you want a good grade. A good work ethic goes a long way and he'll work with you to make sure you cross the finish line.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Prof. Nguyen is the most boring professor I have had at UMD. His lecture will make you bored to tears and the concepts he teaches you are not easy to understand which makes the whole experience awful. You will learn some things if you show up but it will be at the cost of your sanity, if possible I highly recommend take a different professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I definitely would not take this again. I was very lost in every lecture, the prep he gave out was nowhere close to the actual materials on the test. Lectures were incredibly math-heavy, and I was always very confused as he moved on very quickly through materials. Homeworks time consuming and took 5 ever.

I took him for a 5 week hybrid class. You have to buy Connect Access and do homework and quizzes through there. Tests are 50 questions but take about 2 hours to complete. Investment project which was a blast and reading articles from WSJ and answering some questions as participation. Would totally take again! He is awesome. Class is MATH HEAVY.

The HW assignments are too time consuming; if you aren't happy with the score you have to completely re-do the assignment(CONNECT site). If you want an A in this course make sure to study the HW, his notes and sample problems, and get ready to use your calculator or excel, you'll need to know how to do formulas in either.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: This class was the bane of my existence this semester. To get a good grade I had to study my ass off and spend 95% of my time working on this class. I had teach myself almost everything, and although his accent can be difficult to understand, that wasn't even the largest of issues. His lectures are drawn out and very confusing.
Great professor! The only downside is the accent but its understandable. I learned a lot in his class. I'm taking him again for sure!

Tough professor but learned a lot throughout the course. He does a quick review of the topics he has covered at the beginning of each class which helped keep the materials we learned fresh in my mind. I would recommend attending every class though.

attendance is mandatory but not a large part of the grade, has a slanted grading scale where a D is a 50, so its hard to fail his class. also lets you bring a sheet to the tests. Never used a book, better off just buying connect access.  He is hard to understand when he speaks at times
Great teacher but at first difficult to understand him. And he teaches at a very fast paste.

Good prof, knows the material. Hard to understand him plus he goes very fast. Had to see him during office hours every week to get help-he is very good for help. His tests are for the most part hard even with a double sided permissible notes page. In my junior year and this has been the hardest course yet (even over chemistry and pre-calc).

Horrible. I'd leave class crying. You have to teach yourself & when you ask for help he makes you feel stupid. The reviews don't correlate with the exams. And you have to ask him to slow down just to understand him before you can understand the course material.
Hands down best finance professor. Do the work and study for the exams if you want to do well.

Great professor. He's also my advisor, and convinced me to minor in Finance. He has an accent, but it's understandable. He really isn't hard. He allows a formula sheet for every exam and hw isn't heavy. 3 Exams, and 3
Best professor at this school. He explains difficult material in class very thoroughly! 3 Tests and you can bring a formula sheet.
Not a good professor, face it, your going to have him, suck it up and get through it, I did thanks to google.com

He's actually not that bad of a teacher. Hw are online and pretty straight foward. His accent you'll get used to it and it's not that bad either. There's only 3 exams, you're allowed to bring a formula sheet. Overall, material is not very interesting, it's finance, but class is not hard nor the teacher.

Not sure how this guy has a single bad review. By far my favorite finance professor. His accent really is not bad, first off. He knows the material better than the back of his hand, and is very thorough in his lectures. Tests are not bad if you manage to do the homework assignments well. Gives easy excel cases and a fun stock project. Great prof.

The Best finance professor, I've had him for FIN 212 and 383. Just pay attention in class, copy all the equations and you're golden. Has an accent but after one class it's easy to understand. Super Organized. Hopefully he teaches higher classes so I can have him again!

these people are nuts.if you dont like dong,change majors because he is easily the best finance teacher i have had yet.All the tests come from the homeworks and practice test and are not that difficult.pay attention a little bit and you will ace the class. hes not the most interesting but his teaching style is very good and works

The accent you get past.  Very boring teacher, puts you to sleep.  Tests are hard.  However, he is very organized and gives practice tests and assignments which he gives the answers to, to help study for the exams.  6 Quizzes 8 questions multiple choice, he drops the lowest 2. 3 Exams, and 3 40 Question assignments.   Class scale is as liste above
stay away from him if you can.

He can't teach first of all. He doesn't know how to use a graphing calc and forces ppl to buy a financial calc.. You can't understand him. huge scale but all fin classes have it. Makes very difficult exams and homeworks. Puts people to sleep. uses every minute of the class avaialable. mandatory attendence. passable only due to scale. avoid if can..

His class is easy because of a gigantic scale.. 0-49 is an F. 50+ is passing.. a 65 is a C. and 73 is a B. so I mean that's a pretty good deal. what isn't a good deal however is the fact that he's tough to understand and he doesn't use a graphing calc like most people. he uses a financial calc and makes the material more confuisng. He's also boring

Good class, you get used to his accent, and it eventually becomes a very small issue.  If you go to most classes, and do the homeworks, the tests are exactly like the homeworks.  He gives you great study guides with sample questions, and answers along with them, and he shows how to get answers.  Very organized professor, which is very important.
dont let the accent scare you, youll get used to it... hes a great teacher and a nice guy, i am not a math person  but i would definetly recommend taking his class

Dr. Nelles was fantastic, and his expectations were clear from the start. He's easily accessible via email for questions (this was an online course) and the projects were actually fun. Lots of reading though so be prepared for that. It only makes sense....it is a lit class.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Very understanding of outside influences when it comes to getting work done on time.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Prof. Nelles is very nice. He makes his grading system and expectations very clear and his assignments are really low stress. He does not take attendance however every once in awhile there are some in-class pop quizzes (very easy). There are 4 discussions on My Courses throughout the semester which he grades very easy and some online quizzes too.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Caring

reviews.comment: This class wasn't too bad. Pretty easy. Lots of reading. Some in class pop quizzes.4 discussion boards. A research assignment broken up into three parts, a midterm writing piece. 10 online quizzes based on the reading. Offers an extra quiz at the end to bring up your grades. Nice guy.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: I enjoyed this class; he's witty and interesting. He designs the grading system in such a way that it is as low-stress as possible. No one assignment is make-or-break. Lots of small in-class quizzes. He's a generous grader of written work; most quizzes are online and pretty easy if you've done the reading (sometimes even if not).
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--Hilarious

reviews.comment: Dr. William Nelles is a boss. He's an incredible human being, hilarious, and just super smart. He is obsessed with Jane Austen, super feminist, always sarcastic.  I would definitely take him again - in fact I did. Amazing.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Hilarious

reviews.comment: I took this online during winter intersession and that was the best decision ever! ENL 200 (microstories) A total of 3 discussions, two 500 word essays, 3 original microstores, and a final 500 word essay. He gave grades back within 1-2 days! Definitely worth taking over break! If you do the work ON TIME  it's an easy A! Definitely a GPA booster!
Chaucer isn't easy in the first place but he expects us to be the best at it. He also expects a lot from you out side of class, and on mycourses almost daily.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: Western Lit was an interesting class, and his commentary was both funny and helpful at the same time. There's a lot of reading, but Professor Nelles manages to make it fun. You need to keep up with reading though; he assigns discussion board assignments, a few papers, and in-class quizzes. Overall, he's a great guy and the class is an easy A.

Course is pretty easy!! Weekly readings totaling to 1000+ pages for the semester. Course makeup =10 quizzes online (on reading), random in class quizzes, 4 discussion boards, no midterm, 3-part final research paper (I thought I did horrible, but ended up with 90% on it), and a final which is 2 mini essays online. It seems a lot but it's pretty easy

Best English professor I've had so far. Such an intelligent guy, and so lax for a professor. Took "Chaucer" with him, and the assignments are easy. Definitely recommend him, just be aware of the 10 page research paper in his Chaucer class!

He is an amazing teacher.  I took his class on Chaucer and I could not be happier.  He gets really into the stories and has a lot of experience with manuscripts.  Taking his class was seriously an eye-opening experience.  Best class I ever took.

Dr. Nelles is great at responding to emails. He is also extremely lenient on grading. Java wouldn't work for me so he gave the whole class an A for the assignment. His class is a GPA booster, but theres a LOT of work. Midterm, final, and so many quizzes. Must like to read cause I do not and I struggled. Overall got an A-. not too shabby.

Very very very heavy on the reading.  you need to stay on top of that.  he gives a clear list of what's due, what to read, and a quiz online by the end of the week.  the discussions are cake, and a  huge grade boost.  all you gotta do is show you read the material.  class, he only goes over what you read, and sometimes gives in-class quizzes.

Everyone told me Nelles was great but I didn't have that experience with him. It may be because 311 was a brand new course. Wants class participation, doesn't allow it. It's his opinion or nothing. Paper was just "paint by numbers", oline quizzes were tough even with the reading. Reading level high. Ugh.
Highly recommend. Provides moderate coursework with expectations that are very reasonable. He is serious about his punctuality with responding to emails - often replying within a matter of hours.

All around great guy. Funny, but not conceded funny like most professors. Readings get easier throughout the year. Seems genuinely passionate and interested in the subject and the students. I would definitely recommend.
He was my absolute favorite professor of all time. He keeps you interested and assigns readings that are entertaining. As long as you do the readings, you will do well. Great professor!!!!

ENL 251 was easy. 100 short stories the longest was 30 pages. as long as you do the reading and go to class youll be fine. funny guy, eager to help students and very approachable. If you have a chance to take his class definitely do it.
Great Professor. He is funny and loves to joke aroud. Midterm was easy along with the final. Just do the story readings and you will be fine. Great class.

Dr. Nelles is one of the best professors I've come across at UMD. He's super funny and his classes seem to fly by. He assigns a TON of reading but as long as you do well on quizes you can pass easily.
Incredible.

Dr. Nelles is quite possibly the best professor here at UMD.He is definitely the best professor I have had thus far. I have never looked forward more to attending a class or enjoyed learning more. The 75 minutes flew by feeling much shorter than any of my 50 minute classes. If you have the chance to take a class with him, DO IT!

Dr. Nelles is a great professor! It is a shame that other professors are not like him. He's down to earth, funny, and smart, so classes are interesting and worth it. He also doesn't give out a lot of homework.
Awesome professor. One of my favorites at Umass. He actually made me interested in the subject. Take him.
So funny! He keeps you interested throughout his lectures. There is a lot of reading, but as long as you keep up the weekly quizzes are easy. Good class overall. Take it.

Prof is super funny and entertaining. Assigns a lot of reading per night, but if you manage to get it done, the quizzes are easy. Take-home exams and one research paper -  can't go wrong. Not a bad class to get your lit requirement out of the way.

This guy is funny as hell, and smart. Some people might not care for his style; he prefers dishing with the class on stories and getting opinions to lecturing. Personally, I like his style of teaching. It's more casual and fun, and leads to some interesting in-class debates. Note: be prepared to write your very own short story for this class.

Nelles is a great professor! All you need to do for this class is the reading, which can range from 20 to 80 pages, and the quizzes. In class we simply discuss the readings, and it's actually really fun. On Fridays we have a quiz and if you actually read the stories, you'll do just fine.
Really good!

Not going to lie and say the class was super easy, but this is a great professor-you will laugh the whole time! He is a very fair grader but you are expected to do the reading. Very smart man, sympathizes with students. Laid back. Just show up and do the reading-you'll be fine!

Prof. Nelles is awesome.  He was incredibly clear on what he expected, he was a fair grader, and he's wicked funny.  The course work is hard, you are expected to read.  But as long as you read the material, you'll always do fine in his classes.

Absolute best professor at UMD. He actually remembers what it's like to be a college student and respects the fact that his students are taking more than one class, along with living their lives. I can't even express how awesome of a professor he was.

Fair & funny guy. He's always willing to help you, and he's a great comedian! Funniest moment: He asked, "How many of you here don't have this new requirement?" A few kids raise their hands. He puts his face in hand and says, "Wow." Then in a quick and hilarious manner, goes, "Well, you're screwed."

Very engaging professor that uses shock value to get your attention sometimes (which occasionally offends overly PC people).  Knows his materials and basically is a great guy.  Plus he let us play Monty Python during an Arthurian Lit class.  That's enough to get almost any professor on my good side.
His reputation proceeds him, funny, passionate and really knows his stuff. I haven't had any bad experience with his papers yet but then again I have only written one. Seems fair, however.

easy?!?! are you kidding me.. yea maybe the quizzes if you read but not what i would call a "fair" grader..nice guy though..shocking how such a "nice guy" can write the nastiest comments on your paper though
Not an easy A, but a really good teacher.  Be prepared for surprise quizzes and you HAVE to do the reading. Funny, wants you to do good but quite a lot is expected.  Good class
He is SO smart and a very fair grader. It is surprisingly not difficult to get an A in this class. You learn a lot - like how to read middle english. not bad.

Funny and interesting in class.  Hard papers and you can't BS at all because of his quizzes (very hard!) but you'll actually come out knowing something and knowing it well.  He wants everyone to do good and if you show up and do the work, you'll be fine.  Be prepared to actually read though

Very funny, very nice and understanding.  Helpful, and wants you to get an A.  Do not BS him though, good BSdar.  You will fall behind if you dont do every reading.  Just let him talk.  Knows his stuff... i think he used to party hard.
I was dreading taking Medieval Lit and had never had Nelles before so I was even more uneasy about it... but man is he great.  He should have been a comedian, not a teacher.  He's awesome
great guy hes my advisor as well.  class is interesting as long as you do the work.  very funny keeps your attention.
ancient world through the renisance...as long as you do the reading, the class is a snap.
Good class, easy a

Nice guy, knows his stuff, although loves to talk, but who wouldnt if they knew there**** like him. He really was a teacher that knew if you were ****tin him,and it was a good class, I enjoyed it... alot.. *Bling* Bling*
One of UMD's finest.  Not only is he a genuine academic, he's also a quality teacher.  Student-friendly, funny, etc...
Totally turned me off when he (in a very whiney voice) told a group of students who were talking to shut up.
Nelles has a good sense of humor, which helps the class move quick. Sometimes I'd get distracted in class--wondering if he is married!
Made me like Chaucer and that is saying a lot...Laid back, eager to help out students.
Easy grader, easy class.  Just don't challenge him in class.  He likes to stand on his pulpit and preach.  Can't stand to lose an argument or be proved wrong.  Must have some deep insecurity.
good guy.  easy grader.
No Comments
Very laid back, show up to class, do the reading and you're all set,great grader
great guy

Made class enjoyable and relatable he was really nice and what we had to do was clear did have to do a presentation on a read that we did but he a easy grader and grades rather fast makes joke I enjoyed him and would take him again
Prof Maloof made extremely difficult readings make sense and classes were fun! One discussion post/week and I think 2 papers. Super manageable and great professor who wants everyone to succeed!

He was a very kind professor who made a lot of jokes to get us to have fun. Organic chemistry is hard. He doesn't do partial credit on tests so if you mess one small thing up you get the whole question wrong. He got off topic quite a bit and there was a lot of homework. A kind person but I think he could've taught better.

This course is extremely difficult, but Dr. Olubanwo made it super understandable with his lectures. He adds humorous anecdotes that enhance every class. Partial credit on tests is not offered. He does offer many extra credit opportunities. Last drawback is that he does not upload grades to CANVAS, so you have to figure that out by yourself.

Doctor Deli is a decent teacher but is dragged down by how difficult organic chem material is. He gives plenty of homework, quizzes, and extra credit assignments which can help boost your grade, but his tests are very difficult. As long as you do everything and show up to class you should pass but not with a great grade.
The way this class was taught made me think I was in hell every morning.

Prof Olubanwo presented this course is a very manageable way. Homework was weekly and manageable, testing material was straightforward and based on lectures, he was easily accessible outside of class and made this topic very easy to understand. 100% recommend Professor Olubanwo as a professor - this course is absolutely nothing to be afraid of!

Dr. Dele makes this notoriously challenging class very manageable. You will have to put in the work, read the textbook, and do practice problems, but you can absolutely succeed in this class. His tests and quizzes reflect the lecture and textbook content, and he does not throw curveballs in exams to trick you. Don't be scared!!

I took Organic Chemistry I with Dr. Dele and I enjoyed his class. He is super nice and is willing to help you out with anything that you need help. The material he teaches can be difficult to learn but if you come to his office hours and ask questions and practice with the textbook questions and homework, should be able to pass with a good grade.

Over all loveable guy. and its unfortunate that he has to teach a confusing class, otherwise, this would be a 10/10. Organic is very dense so he doesn't have a lot of time to go into detail about a lot of things. we are also behind because he shows up late every day. quizzes have been tough, but im uncertain how much that will affect my grade.

im mad i paid money for this class. He doesn't allow the class to ask questions and he lectures all the way through class, recitation, and even adds recorded videos we need to watch. no review, no help. homework doesnt resemble exams, paper exams with no partial credit and rarely allowing cheat sheets. unaccommodating.
Never again

Dr. Olubanwo, otherwise known as Dr. Dele, is an amazing professor to have for Organic Chemistry. He does care about his students and it shows with his bonus questions and additional videos that he gives for exams and quizzes. Although this is well and good, be prepared to put in the work! He previously gave online exams, however, now it is paper.

Dr. Dele makes lectures that are easy to follow along to with guided notes and a decent amount of examples. Dr. Dele also makes tests that are no where near the same level as the lectures. You can do the homework and understand the lectures 100% and still bomb a test. He is very rude and assumes if you don't understand, you must be the problem.
If you cant answer test questions in under a minute, savvy with chemdoodle - STAY AWAY FROM THIS CLASS

The content wasn't difficult BUT syllabus was designed to make it more difficult. All testing was done online so no way to get feedback. 15-20 questions to do in 30 mins otherwise computer shuts down! Its like taking the MCAT!!Recitation was either cancelled or used to catch up on lectures. Overall course is poorly structured! DONT TAKE THIS CLASS

This is the single worst class I've ever experienced at Umass. You will waste hours of your life doing practice problems on the "adaptive study". All the homework, exams, and quizzes are run through Cengage. We rarely had recitations, office hours were on zoom. If you want to pass be ready to do literally thousands of practice problems.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He teaches the easiest most basic examples in his lecture and tests you with comically hard problems. He says the exams are not different from the homework, but they are. It's like undergrad hw with a post-grad test. Go to his office hours with questions if you want someone to yell at you to pay attention in class rather than answer them.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I feel like he actually wants us to pass but his lectures and notes aren't helpful. He kinda also expects people to be as smart as him but he literally has a PhD in chemistry. I would stay away from his class but I also took it online so I had such a bad experience. He did not care about computer/tech issues. Also rude and doesn't get back to you.

A/B student and withdrew! Not understanding or approachable especially with technical issues. Limited tries on hw. Hw and quizzes not similar to in class notes hard to learn from them. Muted us from asking questions and disabled chat function on zoom. Hard to keep up cause he constantly posts recordings on top of our classes and assignments.

This is the worst prof for Chem at UMassD. He is rude to people in class, refuses to answer Q's, rarely gives any helpful hw for quizes/tests, and is hard to reach outside of class. It may be online but beware that he expects much more than what most can give. Use the textbook and make sure you practice a lot or else you won't pass this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: He was way too tough especially in a pandemic. Not accessible outside of class and extremely rude. Very cocky and thinks we should know this stuff. I suck at chemistry so I knew the class would be a challenge but he genuinely made it harder for us and he showed no empathy. He easily gets pissed at his students and overall I dont understand this man
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Not accessible outside of class and rarely answers e-mails. Thinks that class is easier because we are in a global pandemic and class is online. Fast paced. Gets annoyed at times when you ask questions and thinks that we should just know everything. He seems like a good decent man but as a professor, eh.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Not accessible outside of class and gives too much work. Not understanding of circumstances and gets annoyed if you dont understand concepts and ask for clarifications.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: BEWARE. Dr. Dele is the WORST professor I've had in my 4 years of undergrad. He thinks students should be able to "just get" what he's saying during lectures without actually explaining his process step-by-step. He pretends to be nice but is actually extremely unsympathetic, and will fly off the handle if his integrity/syllabus are questioned.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Dr. Dele is super smart, nice, funny, but not very empathetic. He expects everyone to understand the material quickly. I took Orgo I TWICE. On the 2nd try, I studied a LOT, went to tutoring sessions, did my homework. I got A-B range grades on the homework & quizzes, but the fina is worth a lot and I failed again. This class ruined my 3.4 GPA.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: He's the only professor to teach orgo and orgo is his life. Super smart man and will go above and beyond to help you. But his class should really be split into chem and non-chem majors. Make sure you do questions from the book because he makes his exams through that. You also get a note card for the exam which is helpful
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Dr.O is amazing. He's funny and does give a lot of homework, but I got used to it, you just have to study hard and ask him for help when needed. I would recomend him to anyone.

I got a D+ the first time, I studied maybe 4-5 hours a week for his class. Its just not going to cut it. It is challenging. But so is the MCAT. You need to want to learn it. GL if you skip. Get 100s on the HW (Not that difficult), use supplemental YT videos, do tons of practice problems until you understand everything, always triple check work.

Dr. Dele is the VERY LEAST empathetic professor I have ever had in my entire 4 years at UMD. He comes across as a narcissistic organic chemistry guru, and expects his students to be just as smart, if not smarter than him. He SPEEDS through his lectures, gives quizzes quite often, and his tests are ridiculous. He will FAIL you. Take it in the summer

DON'T TAKE CHM AT UMASSD. Take it at another school during the summer time. All the chemistry teachers, including Olubanwo, teach, grade, and think that all their students are Chem graduate majors and should understand everything and that "It's easy!" Got an F in this class, got an A in the same class at Worcester State. Don't bother with CHM here.

He is too smart to be a professor he thinks everyone learns the way he does and doesn't stray from his understandings. You basically have to teach yourself because he is not a good explainer. His exams are ridiculous nobody even finished the first one.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Lectures are based around note templates he gives. Print them and use them. Fantastic notes. Difficult class due to the sheer amount of information. Don't expect much partial or extra credit.

He likes to develop a relationship with his students, and I feel like he is more generous when giving back points if he knows the student is really trying. That being said, his grading is ridiculous and points are often taken off for things that the question doesn't ask. Hard to debate these points to him, no matter how many students argue

Dr. O is funny and good-spirited, but I feel like a lot of the class was much more difficult than it needed to be. He would show simple examples in class but assign complex ones. His grading is pretty harsh and there is no room for small mistakes.

Great professor and really funny. Brings a great attitude to teaching, and is a good lecturer. Sometimes goes a bit too fast, but you have to be willing to take the time to put in the work. Very fair and overall good person.

Dele is a great professor. You need to put in the work. He is very straight forward with everything. Exam was literally from the practice questions and lecture notes. Homework gives a major boost. Plus he does open note exams! Try him for yourself instead listening to others.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: This class was already difficult, but he made it really hard this semester. He used to give group quizzes, take home quizzes, drop an exam, and allow note cards but he doesnt do any of that anymore. SO many people dropped. He is extremely rude when you talk to him unless you already know everything. This is the worst class I've taken here.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Oh my gosh this man gave me gray hairs. So hard, so unreasonable with grading and just so rude! He is really inconsistent, some tests will be open note, some quizzes will be group ones but sometimes you don't get notes or a cheat sheet but none if this is on the syllabus so you really can't prepare in any way.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Participation matters--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Rude and harsh to students, and only goes over material once. I felt very discouraged taking this course because he claimed the course was easy and we just weren't studying enough or determined to succeed. No matter how many hours of studying was put in I still didn't get the grade I deserved due to difficult exams and unreasonable grading.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Will give open note quizzes and then take away your notes if he feels as though you have too much written down. Not that you would pass the quiz with your notes because it is on things that he hasn't even taught. You can do every problem in the book memorize every mechanism and you will still totally fail. Get ready for mental breakdowns!

Grading is unreasonable. I studied for hours on hours every single day and still did horrible in the class due to bad teaching and bad grading. Gave easiest examples in class but absolute hardest exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: One of the best/nicest professors I've ever had. The course is really difficult and you have to put in a lot of studying time outside of class. The professor is great at explaining and enthusiastic about the course. He loves adding dad jokes in his lectures and genuinely cares about students. He doesn't give partial credit & drops lowest exam grade
Olubanwo is a great professor and really loves organic chemistry he tries to make the lecture fun by incorporating some jokes, but he is good with explaining and not lecturing too much.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: nice man,VERY challenging High expectations& gets upset when students don't do well, but does nothing about it. Drops the lowest exam but won't scale. He mentioned starting an online extra help but said "I don't get paid to do this" and didn't do it. Be prepared to study A LOT & not have your hard work reflected in your grade! Glad that it's OVER!

One of my favorite professors. I don't enjoy chemistry (especially not orgo), but the energy he puts into this class makes it tolerable. While this is a VERY difficult course, he wants his students to understand the material and pass. If you don't understand something, he is willing to explain it a different way and talk to you outside of class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: Dr. O is a great professor. If you go to his office hours, he will be there to answer any of your questions and stay later if need be until you understand the material. He is very detail oriented so beware of small errors! Practice the problems and you will be fine. His exams are fair, and he posts the answer keys for quizzes & tests!

Dr. Dele really wants his students to pass!Chemistry is not even close to my favorite subject and he made our 3 hour - chapter-a-day summer course easy to understand. A lot of examples he uses on quizzes and exams are similar to the ones given in the textbook problems and was always available after class or by email for extra help with homework!

Exceptional professor, has a fantastic personality. Made my summer course bearable and understandable. Kept us engaged even when it was beautiful out. Make sure to read the text book, really helps understanding material better and reinforces everything. Do the problems at the end of chapter, will help tremendously on exams, quizzes, and homework.
Great Professor! Take him if you actually want to learn chemistry .This professor really cares about his students doing well! And hes funny!

He is a good lecturer -- goes through practice problems, answers questions thoroughly. Not so helpful after class, claiming "we should know this already". But generally I loved the class due to his demeanor, it makes all the difference. Strict grader, but the exams aren't too difficult.

On top of being a great professor, Dr. Olubanwo is a very happy person.  He's great at getting the material across, but I think where he singularly stands out is in being so upbeat all the time.  So much so in fact that it really makes a difference!  Take this guy.  If you don't like chemistry, definitely take this guy!

You will never get a prof like him again. His explanations are clear and helpful. His exams are hard though but related to the class. He usually gives extension on owl and listen to the student. He is the best

Dele is by far the best chemistry professor i have ever had. The class itself is VERY challenging, veryyy challenging. But Dele is willing to do anything to help you out. He even came in on a Sunday for a test review, shows how dedicated he is. Go to his office hours they help so much. He uses OWL. Graded fairly but expects a lot on the tests.

This is a great professor, I would take his class again.  I learned and understood like 12 fricken chapters for Orgo II in 5 weeks over the summer.  I worked my butt off, and he helped me ALOT! Awesome guy, Highly recommended.  Super fair

Great professor! The second semester of organic chemistry is by no means a walk in the park, but Dr. Dele made a complicated subject much easier to understand. His tests can be a little tricky, but if you study and truly understand the reactions before hand you will do just fine. He was always willing to help any student who asked.

Dele is a great professor and he cares deeply about his students; he will go to great lengths to make sure they understand the material. There were things in the lecture that I had no idea how to do until Dele showed me. He's a tough grader for lab reports, but he will sit w/ you and go through what you need to do. Do your part and you'll do well.

Took him for orgo II lab - if you can get past his accent, he is wonderful. If you have problems with Ojadi's accent, Dele's is only slightly better... It's worth it to work past the accent though, he really cares and is a wonderful lab instructor. Tough, does not take BS, but fair and interesting.

Dele = the BEST in Chem Dept. For org I and II, his teaching was a plus. His exams were not easy, but he made sure u understood the material. Whatever classes he teaches, TAKE IT WITH HIM! Extremely caring guy.... TAKE HIS CLASS!!!!!

Dele is by far the greatest professor in the Chem Dept. I took him for org I and II and his teaching was an absolute plus. I actually understood organic more than I did the freshman classes I took with Golen and Bates. Whatever classes he teaches, TAKE IT WITH HIM! If you're willing to study and learn the material, Dele's class is the class to take

Dele is the best.  He is very willing to help everyone.  He wants the stundent to pass his class with good grades, but his cours materials could be very hard. You need to pay attention in class in order to do well, but Dele is usually pretty clear with his expectation.  By far the best Chemistry professor at UMassd

He is a wonderful Professor.  He cares about his student deeply.  He wants every to understand the materials as well as to pass the class.  I would say the best prof in the chem department.  He is very easy to talk to and always there after class if you need help.  The class is very difficult, but he is very clear with his expectation.

Dele is the best teacher in the chem dept. at Umass.  He actually cares that his students learn, and he cares that we got good grades.  He is always willing to answer questions.  He gets really happy if you participate in class.  Make sure you study for his tests.  You have to know and understand the material to do well on the tests.

Dele is by far the best teacher i have had in the chemisty department at umass and possibly the whole school.  He knows the subject matter and is more than willing to help with any questions or problems.  Take him for lab class or whatever he is teaching.  There is no one in the chem department i would recommend more.  TAKE HIS CLASS.

Professor O'Donnell is an amazing professor. She goes into detail with her lectures at a fast pace. You can take notes with the student slides given. However if you miss the class, she wont provide the missing information you need for the student slides. All assignments and exams (& extra credit for the exams) are online. Very interesting class.
Makes things much more difficult than they need to be and grades horribly

Prof O'Donnell is by far the most unhelpful teacher in MLS. She provides vague guidelines for papers, provides no feedback, and grades way too strictly. She is very unhelpful and it feels as though she makes her classes artificially difficult in an effort to fail as many students as possible. Prepare to give 110% just for a barely passing grade.
I never wore a seatbelt on my way to school hoping that one of these days I wouldn't have to go to one of her classes ever again.

easy class tests are all online only have to go to class for the assignment days which are always group work and she makes it all clear in the syllabus. only thing hard was final paper but everything else very easy

It is abundantly that Professor O'Donnell's career as an educator is fueled by a major power trip. The MLS curriculum is hard enough without this lady's condescending demeanor and prioritization of her own sense of control over student success. She seems more focused on critiquing students rather than helping them grow. Very strange.

for honor students take her, she has 4 exams online, she only takes attendance when she has in-class assignments which are in groups, and she has one paper that is 5 pages but its all detailed with what she wants super easy and easy pass. some assignments are online and she cancells class for them

Professor O'Donnell is a great professor. She outlines her course clearly in the syllabus. You have about 4 online exams and 8 assignments throughout the semester. Her class is very interesting and in order to pass class attendance is needed just so you have notes. I will be taking her class again in the spring semester!
Professor ODonnell is a caring and nice professor that makes her entire class revolve around lectures. The class is pretty easy to pass if you go to every lecture.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Skip class? You won't pass.--Respected

reviews.comment: She is a very inconsistent grader and overall lacks empathy. Its hard to imagine that she actually went to college since she is so tough on us. I can say 1 on 1 office hours with her are very helpful. Would not recommend taking her but she has some good traits.

Professor O'Donnell is good at explaining things you need to know, however she lacks a lot of empathy, and will will tell you to figure it out yourself if you miss any lectures. Easier class, but requires you to show up a lot.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Prof. Odonnell is very average. Some days she is helpful and some days shes not. I would say shes a boring lecturer but her office hours are helpful. I would not take her if you have other options but shes also not that bad. Class was overall just very hard and is alot of work for a 1 credit lab.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I showed up to every single lecture of this professor's and wrote down every single painstaking note she put on the board only to receive a 70 on her test. The tests were nothing like the notes and presented multiple questions that had more than one answer making them trick questions. Professor O'Donnell is a terrible professor avoid her if you can
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Professor O'Donnell's lectures do not relate to the material on the exams. She is unaccessible, and unwilling to help.

Downright incompetent professor. Many students have failed due to inconsistency in grading. Also gives unclear and sometimes completely unnecessary information. Hasn't taught a class for more than one semester likely because her evaluations are always so poor.  Even as an adviser, she has no clue what she's talking about. AVOID LIKE THE PLAGUE.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Worst MLS professor I have ever had. during lectures, everything is read from her PowerPoint with no explanation. She is a ridiculously tough grader but the student does not learn anything from it. She doesn't seem to like student contact. And 95% of the material was self-taught.

-Average for first exam was 65 (AFTER the scale) -Notes given in a seemingly random order -Average student was failing for most or all of semester -Tests do not reflect the notes very much -Enough said
Prof. O'Dell is great! I highly recommend taking her courses!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Caring

reviews.comment: Her class was easy. There is quite a bit of reading, but nothing that can't be handled.

Dr. O'Dell is great! She genuinely cares for her students, is passionate about the subject and her students' learning, and she is a great teacher! One of the university's best! Take her if you can. 
Her class stimulated thought. The (amount of) work was typical of other English classes, but it was manaeable and enjoyable!
*I took the honors section

I have had her for two semesters. I had her for ENG 102 and 200. She is very understanding. She does give out a lot of homework but not too much if you have good time management skills. I would take another one of her classes if I could.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Easygoing and cares for her students!
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Clear, easy schedule, nothing is a surprise, and since the schedule flows so smoothly, you can easily plan for any hiccups you might encounter. She's also great at understanding if you have any issues, she will accommodate.

1. Perusall HW every night.
2. The same stuff was learned over and over again.
3. HW: watch a video and answer multiple-choice questions.
4. Unclear on essays, meaning no proper explanation of what needed to be in the essay.
5. Never told us when to begin an essay, NO TIME IN CLASS TO DO ESSAYS (better get it over with, than go step by step.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--So many papers--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: It is on me that I did not pass but as someone with ADHD I found it difficult to maneuver the program Pursuall. Regular coursework is already a challenge for someone with ADHD but this made it worse. I never want to see that program in a class again. I am currently at A+ in my current English class. The professor matters too!
Very good professor who cares about the student and not just the work.
She will always give you an extension if you ask

Overall a nice lady. We do the same stuff every class so it can get boring. 80% of the assignments are only worth like 1 or 2 points so if you dont do a few, then it wont hurt you. The major assignments are kind of a pain but if you show up to class then she can be helpful.
Best professor ever as long you put the effort in the class and do your homework you will pass. Shes very nice and always ready to you feedback

I am generally not a big fan of any English classes but this one was actually pretty fun. The choices of books that you have to read are good. I really enjoyed reading "Feed" since it relates pretty well to the world that we live in today. Since the class was asynchronous, I did not have much interaction with her besides the comments she left.

I'm so glad I took this class! I adore Prof. O'Dell with my whole heart. This class is enjoyable because she incorporates a lot of modern texts and films, with just a few of the more "classics." She's also really knowledgeable and helpful if you need something, so don't be afraid to ask.

O'Dell is a very passionate professor! She takes you step-by step for writing her papers. It may seem like busy work, but it all works up to you barley having to do anything when it's time to hand in a final draft. The only con I'd say is the group work.
