But other than that, very fast to respond and easy grader!

There was a lot of work outside the classroom which seemed like busy work but it all helped in the long run. I really enjoyed having her, I didn't have to work that hard on my papers and felt like I did better than I should have at times.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: One of my favorite professors. Had her for two semesters, she is super nice and the work is very clear and easy to do. Highly recommend, but there is a lot of group work in class so you might be uncomfortable if your shy
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Very nice, takes time to learn everyones names, and is very understanding. But, she gives a lot of busy work and assigns new groups for group work basically every week.
Accessible out of class but if you hate English, don't take her. Shes nice but gives tons of busy work and tons of homework
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: I like this teacher. She knows everyones name and she gives good feedback. Shes very understanding as well.

If you like to write by following very specific rules and grading criteria then is this class right for you! I was in her #Selfie class (don't take that class btw) and there is almost no room for creative writing, there is a predetermined shape your final essay has to adhere to, which makes it difficult to want to write at all. Not a great prof.

Started the semester off being VERY TEST HEAVY. Made us take a couple IN-CLASS quizzes ON PAPER -I highly detested this part of the class. Throughout the rest of the semester, we had to write an essay, two discussion boards, two ONLINE exams (only nice part about the class), and SEVERAL reading assignments. She never grades anything.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I took her WGS class. Shes OK. She tends to go on and on and ramble during lecture. Cancelled class a lot. Exams are online and shes very easy going and lenient with late work.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Super kind and funny professor, she cancelled class a LOT and the classes that were held usually got off topic or weren't very helpful. Didn't even make it out of Unit 1 and felt like I learnt close to nothing... Really easy A, though, if you're only going for the grade and not to learn the actual language.

I took her for WGS 101 in the winter. There were assignments and discussion boards and a paper. Do the readings so it supplements your work and just participate. Would def. recommend her and the course topic!

I'm in an engineering major and my schedule was extremely packed but the professor was kind enough to accommodate a few late assignments allowing me to submit them before the end of the semester. She brings candy and sometimes even gift cards to encourage the class to debate and participate. All around my favorite teacher these past four years!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Caring

reviews.comment: She's not really engaging. Her class is really easy, though. She gives two absences a month. I knew some french from high school so I was either always late/not there and it was still an easy class. She's good if you're taking french for a requirement and not because you really want to learn the language.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Only two midterm exams, each 25% of the final grade, quizzes worth 25%, and written work worth 25%. We got to the last three weeks of the semester before she gave us any quizzes or written work. Up until then, the only grade she had from us was the first midterm.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Her class is such a drag. Language classes should be interactive and fun, she simply goes by whatever the book says and all we do is pointless group work.

I absolutely loved her. There was a total of 5 books to read, all interesting choices.  There are discussion boards and occasional quizzes so make sure to read, especially because majority of the class is discussion. She also blends in the class with a bit of history. Would take her again in a heartbeat.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: She is SUCH a great professor. I have had her for multiple classes. She is super helpful. As long as you communicate with her, she'll go out of her way to help you out

Prof O'hara is a nice women but does not stimulate much interest in the class. Though learning about the french occupation was cool, she assigns a lot of reading and class is mandatory. She gives quizzes so you actually need to read the books. About half of them were interesting but she tried to make it a history class. Liked her overall
Professor O'Hara is a great teacher. I've noticed that if you're struggling in any area (homework, class discussion, quizzes, etc.) she will do whatever it takes to help you improve.
Great teacher. She tells you the direction in French and then repeats them in English. She is very helpful when you do not understand what is going on.
She is an amazing professor.I enjoyed going to her class everytime. Make sure u read the books and participatein class. It should be an easy A. Do the extra credit just in case.

BEST PROFESSOR                  HER CLASS WILL ALWAYS BE EASY BECAUSE SHE ENGAGE WITH THE STUDENTS AND SHE GIVES NO DEADLINES EVERYTHING IS DUE BY THE END OF THE SEMESTER. MOST OF HER COURSES NOT SAY ALL ARE EXTREMELY EASY, YOU DON'T HAVE TO BE ,FRENCH, HAITIAN OR CANADIAN TO UNDERSTAND HER CLASS. SHE WILL HELP YOU GET YOUR A NO DOUBT

One of my favorite classes this semester. It was basically a book club on french literature. O'Hara is definitely perfect for this class. I do not think I would have liked to hear anyone else recite Madame Bovary lines in french than her. I recommend this class to any french major
Prof O'Hara is amazing.  She's super nice and helpful.  What's especially endearing is her propensity to make random Star Wars references and jokes during class.  I would certainly reccomend her.

I LOVE MADAME O'HARA!!! An incredibly sweet woman she genuinely cares about her students and is more than accommodating if you talk to her. Learning a language is tough but she'll go as slow as you need. AMAZING. TAKE HER.

I really wanted to like this professor. Her zoom sessions are pleasant. However, her feedback is scathing, vague, and condescending. When you ask for clarification she repeats what she already said. She assumes you are lazy and/or stupid. She is condescending and unrealistic. She seems to be spread too thin with all her professional commitments.

Kristi is a good teacher she knows the subjects well tends to be biased about certain topics. There is a lot of homework for this course 3to4 assignments a week plus observation hours. Definitely requires a lot of work outside of class. But overall a good informative course.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Kristi Oliver is a fantastic teacher and she really knows what she is talking about. She gives a lot of work, but its all manageable. She gives excellent feedback on everything, and really breaks stuff down so we can understand it better.
Kristi was very helpful and knowledgeable.
The best professor at UMASS Dartmouth!!! The most caring and dedicated educator I ever had!
The best professor I had!
A temperamental professor! Avoid

Best teacher I have ever had...hands down. No one else even compares!! He taught PChem, so of course the class is difficult! Do people actually think anyone in the world can make that easy?? He is a wonderful person and teacher. He will bend over backwards for his students on a regular basis. He is inspirational to say the least.
Tough :/
Nice Man. Easy class.
Smart person just does not know how to teach an entry level chem class.
Extremely nice guy & brilliant. Teaching, unfortunately, is not his area of expertise.

This was the best chemistry professor I've ever had. Once you understand the accent, he's a good lecturer. The class is difficult at first, but it gets better. Visit his office hours; he's willing to help. He believes people emit energy when thinking positive, and hence, you're more approachable. He lives this teaching!

Ojadi's a demi god in chemistry.  he's a demanding teacher but he knows his things.  If you're not interested in a course, don't take him. But if you're serious about what you do, go for it, he'll impress you with your own ability.
This guy is a serious a**hole.

Tough teacher - VERY demanding. Would not recommend skipping class because he covers material very quickly. Gets ahead of himself sometimes, as a result. Don't skip class, do the homework, you'll do fine on the exams.
Extremely good teacher...if you do bad its your own fault...ask questions and he'll explain...really really good techer.
SUCKED

This class is horrible. He is a nice guy but your first time in his class you will not be able to understand a word he says. But you do get used to the Nigerian accent. Always willing to help you out if you can actually catch him in his office. Quizes and tests suck. Online homework is easy as long as it's multiple choice. You really have to study.
No Comments
bad teacher
Great teacher, but very demanding with very high standards - you will work very hard in this class. Has a strong affinity for pop quizzes.
Ojadi loves homework and online questions. He's always willing to help students. He's a little too quiz happy. There's always something due.

gives many handouts. If you do not study. You will not do well. is willing to help any and all people that ask for it. Great at pushing over tests and homework and due dates. Likes to give online homework. Grades test easy, but homework will kill you if you do not keep up.
Extremely hard class but Ojadi is willing to work with anyone. I went to him with questions from other classes even when I didn't have him as a professor.
Funny and nice, but a very difficult teacher to follow and he has very high expectations

Great teacher and knows chemistry.  He's always willing to help.  His accent was a little difficult to understand sometimes but he'll clarify anything if you just ask him.  Be prepared to work a lot for this class.  It's not easy but you will enjoy it and learn Chemistry.
really hard class
i thought he is man of his words, HE IS NOT. He would persuage you to get an A but only ONE GENDER gets good grades. You know ..... DONT ever trust HIM.
good teacher, VERY hard class. expect to work alot. he knows his stuff and is pretty funny, and he will always give you help.

Very hard class. You will work your butt off for this professor. Nice guy, knows his Chemistry inside and out and this university is lucky to have him. Treats his students with respect and is very funny. No easy grade, rarely cancels class, a great but hard teacher.

wow....ojadi. hes fulfills da defination of PROF . always helping, n has full Command over his subject. No one messes wiv him in chemisty. He expects from you to work in this class with ful zeal and zest.I really Enjoyed his class.A nice person and nice Professor who knows HOW to teach in university
No Comments

He's the man!!!Expect to work your butt off in his class. He's no easy grader. However he's the best teacher you'll ever meet. He loves his students and is always willing to help.You'll learn so much and have a lot of fun too. He's super-smart and funny
Expect to work very very hard in this class. Ojadi is a great teacher - he listens to his students and doesn't make them feel small for asking questions when teaching difficult material.
YOU WILL LOVE HIM he is always willing to help you...if you go see him he ill give ou an A

I really like Professor Harun. He's kind, patient, and very generous with grading. His homework is helpful, and he always makes time for questions. Sometimes his lectures are a bit hard to follow(boring), but he's always willing to explain more. I feel bad a lot of students are disrespectful in class. I'd definitely take another class with him.

Omer's lectures everyday, all day, the class is dull and mind numbing. Harun Omer attended MIT and was a Harvard Researcher, so teaching freshmen math has to be extremely boring for him, and it shows. I believe he means well, but just does not know how to make class interesting, and could not make it any more painful and drawn out than it is.

Overall not bad, if your good with math it'll be a breeze but if not I recommend the stem learning lab. Hes very generous on grading but He doesnt go through stuff very methodically and then gets sassy with you when you dont get it...

difficult to understand but is a generous grader, will accommodate if you need extensions and is overall a nice guy but, as someone who is not the strongest in math, I didn't understand the lectures at all and had to teach myself everything from the homework received. Maybe I just suck at math though. But, he was a nice guy and a good grader

Do not take this class unless you understand algebra pretty well because this teacher has a thick accent and I hardly understood him half of the time and not the best profesor to ask questions to if able to choose another profesor.

Professor Omer assigns homework every class due the next week, and 5 tests total including a cumulative final . He wasn't the best with communication such as via email. There's no makeup policies you just have to do good on the tests. Overall, not so bad just not the greatest either.

Had a good class with Prof Omer. He has a very straight-forward way of explaining the material--which is a big plus in math. I feel he is one of the profs at this place that is truly qualified, unlike some others.
Had a good experience in his class.
Had a good time in his class, I get his logic. Would take his class again.

I learned a lot in this class! The connection to the fundamentals was always there and I did not get "lost" in the details as happened in other classes in the past. If you don't understand something he will always explain it again, but he never wastes time in class so is able to finish the curriculum. Getting an A is tough but grading is fair.

I took this class to fill my math requirement, and was nervous. But, this class was basically algebra 2 mixed with basic stats, and I got a 95 on my final! Prof. Omer was GREAT about going over material people were stuck on, even if it took the whole class. His explanations were clear and thorough, and if they weren't, he'd do it again!
Was worried about math because I've always just been average and had to take it for credit but Dr. Omer had a good approach to teaching math that made it clear to me. Got an A :) Good guy.

This was a course I needed to take to fulfill the mathematics requirement, and the course material was a review of high school and my statistics courses during my sophomore year of college. Regardless, Dr. Omer is an intelligent, caring, and respectable instructor who wants all of his students to succeed. I highly recommend him to any student.

Omer is overall a great professor. He does his best to explain the concepts using a lot of examples and he will explain a problem over and over if you ask him to. He is a very lenient grader, he will give you all the points even if you don't have the right answer as long he sees that you know how to solve the problem. His reviews are very helpful
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: If you miss this mans class, youre stuck not knowing anything because hes never on the same page as the book. Going to office hours is a waste of time, because he doesnt explain at all. Hes a rather confused person and doesnt seem to know what hes doing in class. I love math but this is the first time math has been exceptionally difficult.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: He doesn't know how to make math exciting. He will teach and you will not be able to hear a single word he is saying. Most of the learning Is done on the homework assignments provided.

Great professor! Long lectures and lots of reading, but he gives you everything you need to know to pass the course. He also gives great feedback with writing assignments. Critical responses are important with his online discussions, as he grades every assignment with a rubric. I would definitely take one of his classes again.

Class was interesting, but the tests are super difficult. Attendance is always mandatory, except for Professor Sandby-Thomas, who was late every single day and sometimes cancelled on very short notice. He also doesn't grade anything throughout the semester except the tests, so grades are pretty low in general.

He's funny. I like the class, it's pretty interesting but you have to pay attention or you won't do well on the exams. Simulation is fun and very good tool for learning the concepts. Study guides are horrible though. He's overall pretty good and would take again and I feel like he's pretty understanding.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The course was interesting but he DOES NOT GRADE ANYTHING during the semester. You literally have no clue what your grade is until the very end. For all you know you could be failing but you don't know bc he didnt gradually grade anything. He gives a lot of resources like articles and lecture but I wouldn't take a course with him again.
reviews.rating_tags: So many papers--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I wish I put more effort into this course. Professor Thomas is a great dude, really interesting accent. I found it hard to understand the lectures, but everything is online (tests, HW, quizzes, even exams) so that was great. I'm just not good at poli-sci. He was very easy to talk to though. GO TO CLASS ATTENDANCE IS GRADED HEAVILY.
PSC 284 can be a fun class, however participation is very important and you have to be comfortable with group projects and speaking in front of a class.

Really positive experience. Gives opportunity for extra credit if you communicate. He also uploads a lot of videos on his lectures which is super helpful if you were absent or need a refresher. Def recommend.
He is very cool and awesome and, while maintaining difficulty, makes his class super fun. 5 stars.
He is amazing. You should definitely take him if you like people who are funny!!!

I recommend taking Professor Sandby-Thoma's class. I took it my first year of college and it definitely helped me to adjust. His lectures were amazing and full of great content. He does have a group project for this class which is fun and he also has points that you get from mini lecture quizzes. Take his classes!

Lectures can be incredibly dry. Often slow to respond to emails. Beware, grading is nearly non-existent and classes may be canceled on a whim. However, the material is easy to follow, online videos are often posted re-explaining key information or making up for lost time. Multiple events are held to attain extra credit.
literally best professor

Awful Organization, Slow to respond to emails. Does not stick to syllabus. Grading happens at end of course so you never know where you stand. Lectures are boring. Readings don't correlate  to the lectures. Deadlines are ridiculous. Did not post lectures multiple times until the very last minute (2 days before the deadline). No feedback on anything

Dr. Sandby-Thomas has simple lectures that are clearly explained when there are questions. His assignments have clear criteria. He often gets distracted by students and his inconsistent with email reply times. He wants until the end of the semester to grade most assignments but you should know how you're doing and he'll let you know if you ask.
He is an excellent critiquer

His class is relatively easy as you don't get assigned any homework outside of the end-of-semester work. You don't want to skip his class as he does daily lectures and they often contain a lot of important information that is used on the exam. The exams aren't exceedingly difficult and they're easy to pass if you show up/take notes.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I would never take his class again I wish someone had told me not to. His lectures are extremely boring. He never responds to emails and is extremely rude when giving feedback. He entered 3 grades as of December and then posted all the grades a day after grades had closed so you had no idea where you stood The whole sem. Way too hard for a gen ed

HIghly intellect, dry sense of humor, cares a lot about his students. He wants you to succeed but he wants to deserve it. Show commitment and interest and he will help. Ready to go the extra mile to explain and guide you. JUST ASK HIM! Don't take 2 of his classes at once, you will be overwhelmed. Fair grader and extra credit is given, don't miss it
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Simulation is worth 25% of your grade in which you work on it with your group all semester. He doesn't explain how to use it & it can be very confusing at times. Exams were extremely hard, but drops the lowest one. There is also a writing assignment that is mandatory which is also pretty hard. He doesn't respond to emails much which is not helpful.

Honestly, in the beginning of the semester I was super not happy taking his class. I found it to be very dry and dull. After a couple weeks and I learned his humor (along with our class CHINAAA) it became a pleasure going. He's a relatively easy grader and he does care about his students. However, he does take points off if you miss class.

Professor Sandby-Thomas is extremely funny which makes class time enjoyable. He has a point based grading system and is such a fair grader! He's always ready to help outside of class. Show up, listen and participate and you won't have a problem passing. Take his classes if you can

He's great! Fourth of the grade was group work. Online tests, very tech saavy guy. Lectures were boring but he uploads powerpoints to myCourses and you can keep up that way. Iclicker attendance and questions, but you can use Reef instead, he doesn't care.

Prof. Sandby was one of my favorite professors! He has a great sense of humor and tries to keep the subject matter light. He tries to act aloof, but he really is concerned with your welfare! So if you have any concerns just voice them and he'll be sure to help you where you need it! He's absolutely awesome!

Thomas is the best. He makes everything fun and loves to pick fun at things. Some people don't like him because they don't understand his sense of humor, but I think he's great. I've had him as a teacher ever semester since my freshman year and although he is kinda a tough test maker, he's fair and will always help you if you need it.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Hilarious

reviews.comment: The man has amazing banter/

He is a great professor. GO TO THIS CLASS. He does I-clicker every class. All exams online. DO NOT BUY THE TEXTBOOK. I did not open it once. Print out the power points he sends to help you with the exams and I-clickers. Oh and he gives an extra credit assignment at the beginning of the semester. So my advice is do it. EASIEST CLASS I HAVE TAKEN.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Great class! Only 3 tests, lowest grade dropped, and a fun/easy group project for the entire semester. Tests are hard and you really have to pay attention in class to understand the notes. Attendance is taken with I-Clicker's and counts towards your grade. There are also I-Clicker ?'s given during class that also count towards your grade.

There was only three tests the whole semester and one paper at the end. It's hard to take notes based on how he writes his ppt slides, so it helps to pay attention. Requires an iClicker for attendance & DOES COUNT YOUR ATTENDANCE POINTS. Overall, not a huge work load as long as you go to class & fill out the study guides. I missed too many classes.
Attendance Mandatory!!!
He was a really great teacher for my two intro classes. Do the Clicker and go to class and you will be fine. Tests can be tricky but they are online.

Taken for CAS 101, PSC 151 and 161. Gives little to no homework. Don't bother with the textbook if you're gonna be in class, which is a good idea to do. Does iClicker. Tests are online, open book, but a little weirdly phrased. Genuinely funny guy, totally ready to help you out if you ask for it, but will let you fail if you don't try at all.
Tests are open note, so its not that bad, just do the work and put effort in and you'll do fine.

Guy's got a UK/Irish accent, but his class is super engaging. Attendance and participation are huge, he drops the lowest test out of 3. No midterm or final, just a paper. Exams can have ambiguous wording but that is intentional. If you like politics or current affairs, this class is for you.

He's a nice guy to chat with during office hours. I've had him for two intro classes and they are pretty low effort. Just show up and do the iClicker. Three tests (lowest grade dropped) and one final paper. Tests are only hard if you don't pay attention.

This class was interesting but test were hard.  If you  go to his office hours he will help you understand the materials and we also have John as a TA that helped to review before exams.  You really have to put in work and participate in class otherwise you won't pass.  We have 3 exams, he drops the lowest.  Minus 1 for every class missed.

Peter is a very funny guy, he does waste a ton of time if nobody is answering his questions, be ready for that . If you participate he will appreciate it, some test questions are written poorly but he usually scales them. A paper at the end of the semester but really not difficult. DO NOT BUY THE BOOK you will never use it. all tests online
Ehh... this dude is from Eastern Europe or some crap.  His accent isn't that bad, and neither are his shoes-if u nah mean
He's very funny. Always willing to help students. His classes take a bit of work but are doable

I've taken several classes with him; he's so awesome. You have to read if you want to do well, but he makes class incredibly interesting. He's funny, great with students, & will help you out when you need it. Utilize his office hours! He will really sit with you and tell you what to focus on. Great guy! One of the best professors I've ever had.

I don't know what these other people are talking about. Although he is witty and mildly humorous at times, his teaching is about as dull and boring as it gets. This class will feel like an eternity, every class.

Sandby is brilliant, hilarious, and thoughtful. He knows his stuff and teaches with real enthusiasm. It's possible, but difficult, to get an A in the class without a textbook. UMD is lucky to have Sandby because he deserves a much better school.

Taken several classes with him... He's phenomenal. He's hilarious, super knowledgeable, and will help you with absolutely anything. I'd recommend reading the text, but you can get by without it. Utilize his office hours! He essentially tells you what to study. One of the best professors at UMD, hands down.

Very smart. Good professor. Entertaining in class and gets along well with the students. I recommend him but he tries to keep students to a higher standard, so know that going in. (Unfortunately at UMD it seems that the standard is rarely met and he has to adjust down a little bit)

I've had him for multiple classes, and he's a great professor. He may seem dry if you aren't paying attention but is literally one of the funniest professors I've ever had. He'll help you out if you ask, and is very knowledgable about what he's teaching. I'd definitely recommend taking him, if you want to do well you will

He is a nice guy and is new to teaching in the US. He really knows his stuff but has a hard time communicating it. Go to him if you have questions! He told people you don't need the book but you do because his lecture slides are really vague and reading the book will help! Overall, not bad.

I'll be taking this guy for the 3rd time next semester. If I didn't think he was great, I wouldn't take the torture. His tests are extremely difficult. 2011-2012 was his first time teaching in the US and it's evident as he gives 80 PAGE STUDY GUIDES! He's since gotten better. One on one this man is fantastic! UTILIZE HIS OFFICE HOURS! Good luck!

Lectures can be dry but in a good Bob Newhart way. Gives plenty of opportunity for improving grades but if you need extra credit to pass you are doing something wrong. Very funny guy and always helpful outside of class.

His lectures are a bit dry but if you have an interest in the topic they are ok. There are only 3 test for the semester so it is kind of make or break; although he does give extra credit for attendance and an optional paper. DO NOT BUY THE TEXT BOOK EVER

He is vague, his slides have too much unnessessary info and his attempt at making a review slide was a huge fail. class averages for first two midterms were terrible. He doesnt even give enough work for you to actually have a chance to pass. He's just awful.

Potvin was okay. She assigns SOOOO much busy work and unnecessary assignments. Definitely not much of an English class, so I wouldn't recommend if you're not a Business major. She is very strict about attendance, even though it's an 8am class. She is nice, but not a great lecturer.
She was very nice and easy to talk to. Class was at 8am so it was tough asf but class was interesting. She gave a lot of chances and helped me with getting better grades. Good professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Amelia is a great professor. She has very clear grading guidelines so you aren't left in the dark on why you received a certain grade, she gives great feedback, and most importantly she is engaged with the class and makes it fun to learn what she is teaching. Would 100% take again.
She is an awesome professor. Very caring, and gives good feedback. We do a lot of group activities and there are 4 papers to write. Overall, easy class because she is really good at teaching.

Be prepared to read quite extensive health care related papers. I learned a lot but the quizzes and exams were sometimes oddly worded. Make sure you brush up on the material in her PowerPoints or you won't succeed. Entire class was only out of 90 points so difficult to get an A unless you ace everything; no extra credit.

Great professor. She usually stays on topic with the course material. The class was very thought-provoking and intriguing. She is flexible, so if you can't make it for some reason, that's okay, but don't make it a pattern or you will miss key aspects. There is only reading for the homework, but pay attention because the midterm and final are dense.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Inspirational

reviews.comment: Boring lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: She's a pretty cool and easy professor. She can be awkward in someways but overall; she's not bad and her work isn't bad either.
Very monotone when lecturing. Just...go to class and study for the exams and you'll be okay. Don't bother talking to her, she won't help..
Take this class. Easy as hell. Attendance is mandatory but you can sign in 2 days or more at a time. Easy GPA booster. Not a lot of work in class.
Talks down to her students often, is no help with questions, quizzes are confusing, and is super boring overall.

I feel like she would great to have a 200 or 300 level class with. Very knowledgable in her field but very soft spoken and gets irritated when people show up late. ANT 111 is basically a 101 course, there were like 50+ students in my class, a shame that so many don't care, but she should know how to deal with it.
Course felt like I was back in high school. Quigley was very awkward and scolded people for coming in late/leaving early.  Managed to get an A despite never coming/not having the book.

She is a hot mess.  She uses her hands consistently when talking. When she asks a question & no one answers she will just wait for like 5 minutes in silence.  Her class is easy though as long as you do the readings and journal entries you will do fine.  She isn't a bad person but she is far from welcoming. Also, go pee before class, she hates that.
ha

Prof. Quiqley is pretty awkward. You'll feel like you're in high school. She frowns upon anyone leaving class to use the rest room, or speak without a hand raise. She's extremely particular about what time to end the class, yet the course is extremely unorganized. It is easy, I just wished I had learned something...

Easy class, few essays, few tests & take home final..She is weird, unsure of herself & cannot talk without moving her arms & hands ALL THE TIME. She uses up the whole class time, so even if you finish 30 mins before the end of class time, she soaks it all up and talks about nothing. Its and easy class but pretty boring.
The class is soooo easy. A couple essays, easy quizzes. She talks forever and so unsure of herself. Shes nice though. But no one answers her questons, everyon sits there and does nothing

I don't think it's going out on a limb saying she's a horrible person. She was oblivious to the fact that our class was on the verge of a mass mutiny. She's incredibly pretentious but doesn't have the intelligence to back it up and if you ask for help, she'll give you a condescending answer and send you away. Save yourself the resentment, stay away

For the most part she's fairly good. Sometimes she puts things on her tests that are the more obscure points in lecture as well as irrelevant to the 'art' aspect. But despite that she definitely knows what she's talking about and always really drives the most important points in lecture home.

She talks very fast, and at first her class was simple and all about the bare facts: Commission Date, Artist, Place.  However, she sometimes lists far too many artists and is not clear on which ones will be on the crucial tests. Sometimes, no study guide is given, which sucks.

A nice person, but a so-so teacher. Classes were almost all lectures, and she goes through the material pretty quickly. Quizzes and tests are multiple choice and based only on memorization. Not too hard as long as you keep on top of the material.

never again will i take a class with her. nice lady but can't teach. i asked for ideas to improve my grade, she said "study more, i don't know." most teachers ask for date, name and artist. she asks for date, name, artist, technique, time period,  commisioner, architecture. too much. and she never knows questions when asked.
she rambles too much.

she has seriously brainwashed herself into thinking that we are all obsessed with art.  if you don't have a die hard passion for useless facts then don't take this class.  this woman needs to stop talking so much and maybe retire.  trying to study for her tests is like chinese water torture.  only to find that she failed you newayz

This lady is straight up obnoxious.  Woman will banter on about carpet pages, whatever that is.  She needs to calm down and realize no one likes what she is teaching.  I studied for every test and failed every test.  This class is awful.  I reccomend you slit wrists instead of taking this class.

Half the time the slides/computer/overhead wouldn't work. My friend and I were waiting for her to burst into tears. She clung on through and still attempted to teach. The class itself required quite a bit of note taking and memorization and paying attention. Tests usually ask for the years things were done, who did what and various definitions.

Simple memorization for the class.  She gave us a list from the book to study.  She's pretty knowledgable and tells you a lot about what you need to know.  This was an elective and I did pretty well.  Show up and get extra points and you might actually learn something!
Seems really unprepared for class, bringing slides in black and white and without dates.  Not friendly, borderline abonoxious.  So far my least favorite teacher at UMD
This class is really easy if you can survive the boring lectures. There's only 2 quizzes and 2 tests and they're not that hard if you're good at memorizing names and dates.
Boring, but easy. Straight memorization class so if you don't know how to memorize BEWARE.

If you can regurgitate, you can pass. Only four grades for the course, 2 mult choice quizzes (15% ea) and 2 mult choice and essay exams (30% ea). Need to know title, artist, date for mult choice and style for essay. Know and use key terms in essays.

I actually had her before at RISD pre-college, haha. Very interesting, seems to explain a lot about certain pieces and doesn't have a boring, droning history voice. She gives you slide lists for the specific pieces on tests, and tests are usually mult. choice. Extremely easy.

u get to sit in the dark and look at paintings done by long-dead people. fairly easy, tests are multiple choice except for midterm and final which have an essay questions. you'll have to be able to memorize a whole bunch of useless junk that will never serve you any good

Somewhat interesting. Almost always a lecture, and the notes are pointless because the only grades are on essays that are totally unrelated to the notes. Write 5 easy papers and do 1 group presentation. easy A.
No Comments

He is a very kind and sweet professor, he does talk a lot especially in the morning but his assignments are very easy. He is also very understanding on absences (he lets you make them up through the Writing Center) and late assignments.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment:
He was the only professor I had for the semester that actually knew how to teach.
Assignments criteria and due dates were clearly expressed and gave excellent feedback on drafts and final submissions. He actually learned each students' name and had a genuine interest in each student. UMass Dartmouth need more professors like him.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This was my first semester at UMD and I was so thankful to have Prof. R as my ENL 101 teacher. This guy was so nice and helpful during the class, I wish I could have him next semester.
I wish that I could take this class again. Professor Ryckebusch is a very caring and open professor and his class is always engaging and never boring.

i usually hate english, it's always been my worst subject but i loved this man's class. he gives positive feedback when handing back your essays which i love. he's very encouraging and patient. the class i took was fairly small and he got to know everyone's names by the third week. 10/10, love him, wish i could take another class with him again

Great professor and easy course! First professor I met that tries to learn your name. He genuinely cares about your success and will check in on you if he notices a drop in performance. Only thing that was slightly annoying was that the class kept changing locations, but i'm pretty sure thats out of his control.

No one is exaggerating. He's one of the best professors I've ever had. Easy grader, wants you to learn and pass. Most of the class was made up of stimulating discussions from the readings we had to do. He gives five quizzes on them but they're open book. Four papers that you edit to death so plenty of time to improve a bad first draft. 6 absences.

Favorite professor. I hated English before I had him and he really made me like it and actually helped me become a better writer. He loves group work and class discussions, participation was a big percent of his grade. He is very friendly though & loves taking the time to help. TAKE HIS CLASS

He is such a nice guy, and I loved his class. Workload was nothing bad and we discusses our readings every class, which was actually pretty fun most of the time. Absences count but there are quite a few excused ones. He isn't a harsh grader at all, either. Take this guy if you have the option!
He is awesome, the best teacher in Umass Dartmouth. I love his class. everyone should take his class.

I had Professor Ryckebush last semester for ENL 101 and I loved his class! He was very understanding, helpful and accommodated those who needed extra help. The workload was a bit much (which is expected for a college student) but manageable. Although it wasn't the hardest class, we learned a lot and he was always engaged with the students. The fact that he has a colorful personality was awesome too! If you want to be treated like a student instead of a paycheck, I would recommend this professor!
Hes my favorite professor. Nice guy. You can tell he really loves teaching and wants to help his students.
Best teacher ever!
FANTASTIC professor! Very understanding and helpful.
SUCH A GREAT TEACHER

Literally one of the most genuine men I know! he is so nice and helpful and SUCH a fair grader. he treats his students as equals and gives everyone a chance to succeed. Almost imposssible to fail his course. if you have the opportunity to take a class with him, do not pass it up! LOVE HIM!

Great proffessor, nice guy, willing to assist you if you're having trouble on top of being a relativly easy grader. I would highly recomend him especially to anyone who isn't too hot in english. Alot of critical reading journals to do but their short and simple. Almost impossible to fail this class
He was a very good teacher and the comments he left on my papers helped me to write a better paper each time. He requires you to attend, but he is a very nice guy, and fair.
ryckebusch is the man.. Honestly this is an easy class to take, he grades very fairly, hes sort of unorganized in his speaking and lessons but it doesnt matter the work is easy..
I love Professor Rykenbush! Hes so helpful and defiantly loves teaching. He gives good comments. Your writing will improve.
A wonderful professor! Very helpful and enthusiastic. He made me like English.
Great guy, very understanding and easy on the eyes.
He is very fair in his grading.
No Comments

he is nice.. he critical reading journals are a pain in the ass but you need to them on time or youll have 100 reading and reviews due the last class..grades fairly and lets you revise essays..he is really nice but way toooo happy all the time

Loved going to this class even though it was at 8am. 5 papers, revise 2 of them. Don't really understand how you could fail this class. We had journals to do every week. I waited until the very last minute to do all of them (DON'T DO THAT!) overall good class.. highly recommend him

He's a great professor! He's very helpful and his papers are easy. He follows the book a lot so make sure you always have your book. We had to do journal entries every week, but nothing too hard. Honestly, there's no way you could fail this class.. I recommend him!
"PUT AWAY YOUR TEXT MESSAGING DEVICES!" HAHA... HE'S A REALLY NICE GUY, EXTREMELY HELPFUL...BARELY GIVES HOMEWORK, PAPERS ARE EASY.. I STRONGLY RECOMMEND TAKING CLASSES WITH HIM =)
Hello Hello Hello! How goes it? He's a great guy. Class is easy as long as you read and do the essays. He's always willing to help you out if you need it. Tests are easy. Highly recommended.

Great teacher. He is nice, down to earth, and if you do your work  a little forgiving about the mandatory attendance (I had an attendance problem lol). Had I not transfered out of UMD I would love to take one of his classes again. Also makes funny past pop culture references. "Land down under"

He is great, he's very helpful and so nice he really wants everyone to do well and makes sure everyone is happy with what's going on. easy grader if you show up and care. attendance is also pretty mandatory

The nicest teacher ever.  He is so helpful and wants to work with you so that he makes sure that you understand whats going on and pass the class.  A couple of essays and a midterm and final.  Make sure to read the things he assigns but if you miss a few he will go over them in class. Great Class!

Great teacher.  Four essays and one research paper, but honestly not difficult.  As long as you go to his class and participate you should have no problem getting a good grade.  Not much homework.  Lets you write about whatever topics you want pretty much.  I definately recommend his class.

He is a great professor, he is aways there if you have any questions. This class is easy he gives 5 essays to write, a midterm and a final. Also he is always using the books so be sure you always bring them with you. I totally recomend anyone to take classes with him

really nice guy. likes his students to do well. 4 essays and 1 short research paper. 1 midterm and 1 final, both easy bc they are open responses to readings from class... little homework, essay every couple weeks and short readings assigned. very clear, makes sure students understand. kinda boring & repetitive here and there but its ok. nice&easy
He is a very nice professor. He always wants to know how your doing and is there when you have questions.  An easy easy class.  It was enjoyable!!
He is very nice, and always willing to help. Very flexible when grading.
I really love this teacher he is the absolute dfinition of a teacher you will learn so much from him. Also he is extremily respectful towars his students. He's the best professor of all time.
I like professor Ryckebusch.It's relly funny and nice.I hope all my teachers were lke him.             (T.G.I.F)

Dr. R wants you to succeed and is always available during office hours. This class is easy if you study and do the extra practice problems that she provides. Her lectures can be somewhat confusing, but the practice problems help to clear up any confusing concepts that were taught.

Barely interacts with everyone in the class. Attendance wasn't needed; she records her lectures (which is why she didn't pay attention to the class). But the grading criteria are clear, syllabus shows everything. Wasn't a very interactive professor, which made lectures boring. But content was easy, I guess it was an okay class. No extra credit btw.

Everything for this class was online including the tests and the final. She lets you use your notes and online resources for tests. Records all her lectures and posts them online. The online program she uses for her course is also free. Really seems like she cares and wants you to do well. Easy-going professor.
sit in the front of the room so you pay attention. difficult to hear at times. do the assignments and know concepts for the exams. everything is online but make sure you know how to do the problems.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Pretty cool professor, almost all the assignments were online including the exams. She gave homework almost every week.

Dr. R is very kind, very accommodating and her office hours are really helpful. She wants you to succeed. However, her lectures are really hard to follow and the HW does not necessarily resemble test Q's. my advice is to PAY ATTENTION!!! It is really easy to fall behind in her class!!

Get a tutor, do the homework ahead of time so you go to her office hours to ask questions, lectures are confusing and the homework and exams are completely different even though you have the opportunity to use a cheat sheet (know how to solve and interpret problems). Homework 10%, exams 15% each problem sets which are on Fridays (go to class) 10%.

Tara is a soft-spoken professor, hard to hear, sit in the front. Homework every week, which the assignments can be confusing. Notes are also confusing, but she clarifies during her office hours. She allows you to have 1 paper for the exams, which is super helpful. If your write down the key points in that paper u will pass! Practice exams online!

I would almost fall asleep in class because she talks so calmly and softly. Sometimes the lecture notes are super confusing but if you show up to office hours it'll definitely help you. Put in the work for sure.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Very quiet speaker and was only on campus three days a week. Not the best with technology but if you sit in the front and take good notes you'll pass fine. She allowed us to take in a cheat sheet into all of our tests and quizzes which helped my grade, but her homework is graded which hurt it.

I took this class online due to corona, so this might not be biased: The most useless class I've ever taken and I had to take an Italian Lit class as a biology major. Complete waste of time, never found a syllabus, two large essays about her class and what you've taught yourself about the school website while sitting in her class in silence.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: While a very nice lady, Tara has some of the most boring lectures I've ever sat through. For those extremely interested in science, you might enjoy her class, but for the average student looking to fill a requirement, I'd look elsewhere. Do the work, and you can comfortably pass with a B or better. This was simply just a mediocre course.

Very nice lady, however she made what could be a super interesting course the most boring class I have ever taken. She does not teach what is on exams and her notes make absolutely no sense and are all over the place. I have never had a class as boring because of the professor and not the content.

Shes a mess, her lectures are boring and all over the place. She rarely replies to emails. However, if you just show up to class, participate you have majority of your grades. She doesnt give any crazy exams like the other bio professors. She gives quizzes every Thursday and theyre right off your notes.

Fair as far as gradin but has a weird system to it, gives make up points back on exams. During lectures she rambles and mumbles a lot, goes back and forth between concepts, starts with examples having no background info and usually does not answer students questions. Hard to study for her class and hard to apply what we "learned" on the final.

Extremely disorganized, every day she has an issue with the printer, when she actually does bring the hand out she doesnt make enough copies so GL following the class. She has AWFUL communication skills and is very unclear in what you are even supposed to be learning. She cant answer simple questions. doesnt update blackboard. Wish could write+

She's a nice person, but her class was so disorganized. She provides incorrect information, goes off on unrelated tangents, and then forgets to finish lectures. Her tests were horrible because the answer keys were wrong and she didn't teach some info. I can't believe I had to scrape for an A- in a 100 level bio class. Avoid this class if you can.

Her lectures are so unorganized and hard to understand. Most of the information is useless. Her tests are unrelated to the notes she gives you. She often goes on tangents about things that aren't relevant to the exam but skips over the important things. Sweet, nice lady but I wouldn't take class with her again and don't recommend her to others.

Really interesting class. Lots of outside activities measuring trees ect. The few lectures were boring, but you need to take good notes on them because the quizzes are all based off of them and she dosnt post her powerpoints online. Had so much fun in the class!
Really nice but doesn't make the information too interesting. Take good notes!

She is a soft spoken professor but she contradicts herself profusely. Her lectures are confusing. She makes mistakes numerously in Bio Stat. She is lenient on grading but she doesn't or rarely scale. But she is nice.

Really nice professor, but a tad disorganized and clutzy (It adds to her charm though). I'm not a huge fan of botany, but she made it somewhat interesting. The labs were kind of boring though. Way too much time spent looking at slides and labeling structures. More field work would have been welcome. All in all, good class.
Must take good notes!
THE BEST!!!!!

A sweet, young, pretty professor of Ecology; cares for students, and is willing to go the extra mile. Is accepting of papers (weeks) late, very kind and easy to get along with. Easy-going, thus the poorer students take advantage of her good nature. Presentations are very well organized. A pleasure to learn from her.

General Ecology, really simple topic and really difficult tests. Do the assignments that she gives and you'll do well, but don't bother buying the book unless you like to study. She puts her notes online and is a really warm and welcoming new teacher.

Very new professor, needs to work out the kinks in the class. Made it harder for us. She tried to make it a fun subject despite knowing none of us wanted to be there, and did her best to be clear and helpul, and was always in her office if you needed help.
very new to teaching, need to study for mid term, i got a take home final.  If you do the hw you should be good in the class
She is new and is obviously getting adjusted to teaching here, class was not too hard and she was helpful.

This class was very interesting. He made sure that all lectures related somehow to what the overall topic of the class is. Tests are mainly based on in-class notes, so make sure to show up and be paying attention
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Character wise, nice guy who cares about making class as interactive as possible. Besides that, he makes the subject so unnecessarily difficult. Exams are unnecessarily difficult. His exams are based on random things he says throughout class, so in order to succeed, write down everything, even if it's  random. Thats the biggest advice I can give!!!

Professor Randy is a nice guy, however, not a great professor. I didn't enjoy the class at all, he lectures the entire time, doesn't go over the study guides, and doesn't provide in class activities. Class is easy but very tedious, no one seemed to understand or enjoy class, it's always the same routine. Do not recommend.
Best professor hands down at umassd. I wish I was still at this school so I could take more classes with him.

ECO 107 with Professor Hall is an easy A. Weekly discussion boards (which he stopped doing halfway through the semester), 2 midterm exams, and 1 final exam and project. Exams are almost identical to the practice exams he provides. Study the slides that he gives, and you will pass with a good grade.
Lot of mentis, good way to learn in my opinion what he teaches in in the test unlike others.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I liked Prof. Hall during my time in his class. However, topics covered did not really peak my interest and the tests are heavily based off of niche things all mentioned in lectures, and sometimes even mentioned in a lecture from a month or two ago, making it almost impossible to study for. I would say this class is unnecessarily difficult.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Good teacher overall, textbook not necessary, but helps you pass.

He is pretty chill. The problem is there is no homework so you do not know what you do not know. He does not go over the study guide. Not accessible through email. You are graded heavily on exams, then there are a couple of discussions boards and participation.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Mr. Hall is a really nice teacher but his exams are very specific and based on things he barely mentions. Google won't help you, he makes his own tests. overall he's a good teacher but his tests are hard. He only bases your grade on 3 tests and 1 project and attendance.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Caring

reviews.comment: YOU NEED TO SHOW UP TO CLASS. Exams will ask questions about things he mentioned maybe once or twice and the class material is not posted online. There are practice tests but they are only helpful to a point. If you miss a class make sure you get notes from someone else or you will be sorry duding the exam.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: finally a class and a professor i can honestly say i liked.

Great guy, very approachable and friendly. The tests and final project are very easy if you pay attention and understand the concepts he introduces. He uses iClicker for attendance and participation, which is nice and easy. Would definitely take a course with him again.
The best prof I had my entire freshman year. I like eco a lot and this class is really interesting. Hall is a great professor and we need more like him at UMD
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Professor Hall was more than fair to the class. His lectures were very boring but he can tell if you are showing up to class through his iclicker questions, (not graded on being correct), but not showing up will effect your grade drastically. Exams are easy if you pay attention and he scales them A LOT, (10-15 points), at least with my class.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: This class was really disconnected for me. What he had us do on Pearson and what he'd talk about in class and what he asked us on the tests all felt like they were coming from different materials. There was A LOT of stuff on the homework that we never even mentioned in class/on tests. Do Pearson and do a little studying and you'll be fine.

The guy is awesome, you can tell he loves what he does, and the classes he teaches are interesting if you like economics. Very fair professor, he actually wants you to learn rather than just trying to see how hard he can make the class. Great professor!

Cool guy. Only homework was a few discussion posts. Two midterms and a final and a small project plus the discussion posts were basically the whole grade. iclicker questions so even though attendance isn't mandatory he can still tell if you're participating. Every class he raises questions for us to think about and discuss as lecture.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Overall a very good class and good professor. Does a lot of iclicker questions, the homework is easy. The test are a bit more difficult because they are all short responses, if you are like me and skip a couple of classes you will get a low score on the test which will bring your grade down a lot even if you had really good grades on the homework
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Go to class with iClicker study for tests and you will pass.
Voice will put you to sleep. But show up, iClicker necessary, and you will pass. Hate economics but he really helps out and is a good grader. Willing to help when needed.
He is a great teacher and so nice. I'm going to miss having him as a professor. Just take this class, you won't regret it.

Not the greatest professor, but he's a nice guy! His class is kind of interesting, his slides are helpful. You get extra points for using your iclicker. I liked using the book for extra help on the applia homework, which was extremely hard and frustrating, but most people probably don't need it. He's a little dull sometimes, but decent!
Hes a very nice guy! Pretty interesting and easy class. You get points just for being there. I deff would take him
Interesting, easy to do well.  Lots of free points for just showing up to class.

Mr. Hall is such a great teacher! I had an entry level Eco class and I got by really easily. Make sure to bring your iClicker to class for attendance points. Attendance is not mandatory but you will be thankful you went to class! Simple iClicker points add a huge amount of points to every test! Must buy book for online activities. Fun class!

This was the best class for me this year. Mr. Hall was a great teacher and cared about students' interest and made sure they learned economics. You never had to go to class once or read the book. I actually went to class because he gives extra points you if you go to class. I never read the book and barely studied and got still an A in the class.
he is  GOOD PROFESSOR
The standard 'read off slideshow' professor, not that great,

Must go to class and must answer iClicker questions! Weekly Aplia assignments that are very hard and frustrating. If you go to class and answer the iClicker questions you'll get a good grade. Lots of notes that he reads off a power point. One midterm and final. iClicker points are added, like a scale, to the midterm/final so it's hard to fail those

Economics is definately not a subject i do well in but he is a very helpful professor. Gives you plenty of extra credit opportunities along with homework to help you bring up your test grades. Attendance is not mandatory but you get an extra point added to your next exam for everyday you show up. I highly recommend him.

I got an A+ in the class and found it to be very easy.. going to class everyday gave me atleast 15 extra credit points on the exams.. he gives a normal amount of homework and explains everything on his slides, no need to read the book

first off he is a great guy and knows eco i really enjoyed him as a person, however he did not convey the information very well he did give xtra for showing up to class which helped but it was not an easy class i would take cebi or isidor before i take him

This is not an easy class, it may be because there isa  new book and he is not used to using it but it was extremely difficult, test grades are extremely low in my class, but he does offer bonus points for going to class and xtra credit. but it doesnt always help. He is a nice guy but not the most clear about what he wants his student to focus on
He uses the Iclicker for getting everyone involved. He's great.

Great professor. The your grade is based on a midterm and final essentially. Homework counts for a small percentage and attendance can add a sizable bonus. Pretty easy class and you will actually learn the material.

Nice, younger guy.  Knows material well, keeps it very simple.  Attendance highly rewarded, not really required.  Homework very straightforward and relatively easy.  No ****.  Discussion rewarded.  Easy A if you have a few brain cells to rub together.

He is a very good teacher. ECO 371 is a hard course so if you are not great at economics i do not recommend taking. The majority of the grade is based on two exams. If your savy with economics he is a great professor to take, otherwise take an easier class.

Great guy, really down to earth. Weekly homework is assigned (easy). Tests are easy, and he goes over exactly what is on them. Attendance is not mandatory. Very enthusiastic about the subject,really wants to help. Responds to email fast. Very fair grader and funny. Make sure to do aplia problems, they really help. Textbook is not used, don't buy it

Hall is definitely a good teacher. Assigns regular online homework assignments, which aren't too bad. Every class you show up for, you get an extra half-point on your midterm or final, which is nice. As for lecture, he's very clear and uses good examples to illustrate the concepts. A bit dry, but still a great teacher. I recommend him.
This guy is the man... Realy interesting teacher and the tests are fair...He puts a new light into the ECO world.  Def. take a class with him!!!!

It is nice to have a younger professor for once. Hall was better than average. Regurgitated textbook material alot but still managed to make it halfway interesting. Class attendance not necessary as you can get everything from the book. Overall I would recommend to others.

Good teacher. Used alot of examples in class and spurs alot of class participation. Tests come straight from the Homework. You should go to class everyday to make things easier for you but you don't have to show up.

Great teacher. Very clear in teaching the subject matter. Is always willing to elaborate further on subjects if that class doesn't understand and most of the tests come directly from the weekly homework.
Really great guy.  Not hard to look at either.  I recommend taking his class.

overall really good teacher. liked having someone young teach the class. Actually explained the material and taught the class. Why aren't there more teachers like this at umd. Ive had so many old foreign profs that i can't even understand.  i was really impressed by this guy, dunno about the other teachers, but this is a good micro class to take.

Good teacher. Online quizes every week can be annoying to keep up with 20% of grade. only like 2 midterms each are 20% of grade. a Final which is like 35%. Can print power points off line. Overall an interesting class
I agree with the other post.  He was a great professor kept things interesting.  Worth taking one of his classes.

Good teacher, very helpful.  Gives outlines for tests, but the 1st exam covered 12 chapters.  But he gives 33%+ scale.  Attendance not mandatory, PP's on his website. Really cool guy.  He canceled class so he could go to Game 4 in St. Louis, how much cooler can you be?

I took him for the Business Organization class. He is very accommodating with any issues that come up for students over the semester. R-studio is the one thing that is very difficult, but like I said he is very helpful with that.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Rai is an awesome professor. Super knowledgeable and friendly to all students. R Studio can be a bit tough to get the hang of but he is always willing to help. Overall class was pretty easy and I'd gladly take his classes again in the future.

He is understanding, he is a great professor. Be prepared to engage in the class. . Office hours are a great resource. The professor grades assignments and exams are clear.  Learn about the professor's expectations for class participation, attendance, and homework submission. This can help you stay on track.

Took POM333 as a 7 week course and there was a total of 3 modules. Each contained a lengthy homework assignment, a few quizzes and a test. There was a week where I submitted my homework and forgot a couple of questions, he allowed me to resubmit it for full credit. Very nice and understanding. I hope I get him for my next POM course
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: He was pretty good and helpful via email. You get the whole semester's homework upfront, along with the due dates. Overall, it was not a hard class, although some of the mid to end-of-semester work was hard to comprehend.

The professor is brilliant. Since many people were in the class, it wasn't easy to engage with him more. Overall, it was a straightforward and fun course. I thought the coursework was not fully related to the hw material, but useful regardless.

For his online class, you watch his pre-recorded videos from his youtube channel. He does have an accent, but not difficult to understand. Broken into 3 sections (for 5-week class) with about 2-3 quizzes each section, 1 homework each section, and 1 exam each section. Homework is time-consuming. The book was only used to make vocab flashcards.

Class is centered around analyzing datasets in RStudio. Lectures are delivered through YouTube. The videos are helpful, but assignments are more complicated than teachings. Email responses to questions are often uninvolved. Having never used analytics software, I found this class extremely difficult. However, the information taught is very valuable
Professor definitely knows the material he is teaching. The lecture material just does not always connect well with what is being tested on HW and in quizzes/tests.

Took this class as a half-semester course. Rai broke the course up into 3 sections, had several quizzes, 1 exam, and 1 homework assignment due for each section (2 weeks for each section). So you get 2 weeks to do the assignments at your own leisure. Since it wasn't a ridiculous amount of work I found the class super easy. Also offered extra credit.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Overall I think he is a great professor. He ties the material to everyday examples. The tough part for me was the mathematical equations, and what to expect on the test. Test were all multiple choice and 1 hour long 35-40 questions. I needed help on some HW and he always responded ASAP. I think if you do your homework, you will get a good grade.
Great class!!!

Professor Rai is a nice fellow, but his classes are based on reading slides and he can make it a bit confusing sometimes. Your final presentation is heavily graded based on peer evaluations which it is not fair as he does not grade it based on the content. Overall, I would not take him again since I did not learn much in this class unfortunately.

Throughout grad school and undergrad, I had Rai 4 seperate times. The man is one of UMD's best. The MBA courses from him can be difficult, but he's more than willing to help you out if you get stuck. He grades fairly and actually cares about his students. The guy is brilliant and has real-world experience he's willing to pass-on. Take his class.
Great guy! Learned so much.  Answers emails almost instantly if you have any questions about homework/quizzes etc.  Would take any of his classes again.

Awesome teacher! Honestly one of the best teachers at UMASS. He is clear on what he expects of you and gives plenty of time to complete all the work that he assigns. Homework and Quizzes are due on the day of the test. Wish I could take a few more classes with him. Definitely worth it. Intelligent man and enjoys what he does for a living.

Professor Rai is the best! Nicest guy ever, always there to help you, very  knowledgeable and makes the material seem very easy. This is the 2nd class I'm taking with him and I wouldn't hesitate taking another one. Do your homework, take the tests and an easy A is guaranteed.
Very strong in Research and Statistics

Awesome guy. Great professor. Funny accent but crystal clear. Online tests, so don't have a class on the days of tests. He is just great, do your work and you'll be perfectly fine. If I could I would've take him again anytime.
Wonderful Professor to study with. Very helpful and more involved in helping. Can gain lot of knowledge. Helped in learning huge concept.
Very nice professor. Willing to help if you want it. Everything is online. If you go to class homework isnt bad.

Great professor with professional teaching skill. I had his class at 8 in the morning but I had no concern of going to his class. He gives you all materials to study and his way of teaching is very straight-forward. Homework is due after every test and all the tests and quizzes are online. Easy A class, just show up and you'll get it all.
Such an easy class. HW is due on next class after test. All tests are online...and all the answers are also online. Easiest A you will ever get
He is a very good professor. All of the exams are online. If you pay attention in class you won't have a problem with homework. Do the homework cause it's 20% of your grade

Professor Rai is a very nice and generous professor. He explained the concepts very clearly and kept a sincere attitude about academy. I still remembered he led us to do the statistical experiment on class.Full of fun.

i had him for stats, very hard stuff to learn but he made it easy, great teacher, gives you answers to homework problems in class sometimes, so attend class and take notes and you'l do fine, online tests and quizzes.

Enjoyed this class. Professor is knowledgeable and makes class easy to understand. Provided lot of help with homework problems. Team exercises were interesting and helpful. Overall it was a great class.

EVERYTHING IS ONLINE. quizzes u get 3 attempts and exams are all on myCourses. EASY A if you go to the library with kids in class and take quizzes/tests together. there are 4 hw long hw assignments due before each of the exams. i never went to class either i think attenednce is 5% of grade

He is a really great professor, he made the content of the class very easy to understand. The only negative i wish the Homework didn't count as much as it does. But overall i really enjoyed his class.

The first 3 tests were all online. the last test, which was the final(non accumulative), took some people 20minutes and was pretty easy. there are weekly quizes and he allows 3 attempts at each one. homework is also generously graded. he teaches off slides, so if you understand math, this is an easy class.
I took a summer class with him. I didn't even get the book, we went over everything in slides. Very nice guy.

Really nice guy, responds to emails fast, offers tons of extra help, gives homework after each chapter and goes over samples problems in class before assigning homework, online quizzes are kind of annoying but no big deal, attendance is signing in name on pass around sheet, i missed the final and he let me make it up no problem - very understanding
Great Prof! made the course easy to understand.

I'm horrible at math and he didnt make it any easier.  He would just read right off the lecture slides and did every calculation on excel which didnt help at all.  The HW counted as a grade and the online quizzes were super hard. If you can learn the math on your own you'll be ok. Use a cheat sheet for tests.

I honestly do not understand how people can't pass his class.  He gives out slides of his PowerPoints, so you don't need to take notes.  Quizzes are online, he drops the lowest.  Has extra credit for going to the lab.  4 exams that you can use a cheat sheet on, plus he gives you the formulas, so you don't have to write them out.  GREAT prof!
THE SUBJECT ITSELF IS DIFFICULT, SO HE TRIES TO MAKE IT CLEAR AND EASY TO UNDERSTAND. HIGHLY RECOMMENDED!!!

He is a great professor and goes over all the home work assignments even before the exam. Gives practice tests which is almost what will be on the test. HW is graded. Online quizes R easy as 1,2,3. Im not a good english speaking guy buy i can understand what he says. Easy A. I Recomend him
No Comments
great!!!

He is an absolutely terrible professor. Lost over half the classes final test before finals, then said he didn't lose them and we just didn't do it. Doesn't really make sense if the lecture was full test day and everyone is emailing about not having a grade!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This was a very interesting class for me and I really enjoyed the content. There were a few discussions, two exams, a project and final paper. If you're interested in this course I would attend lecture and take lots of notes. This helps a lot for preparing for exams!

Not just for class but I also have him as an advisor and let me tell you, he sucks. need help urgently for any reason? good luck trying to get a hold of him. I have NEVER gotten an email back from this guy no matter how many times I email him, even for advising meetings. Because of him never answering emails, I won't be graduating this year.

Professor Boone is a fantastic professor, and he makes class interesting. As long as you take notes and listen during class, you'll do just fine. Also review before exams (obviously). He made me love psychology, and I even considered changing it to be my major!
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Lecture heavy--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Dr. Boone was my first PSY professor and me made me love the subject! Even changed it to my major. He related the work with his personal stories making the material easy to remember. With grading he is GREAT! If the tests did not reach the average he wanted he boosts them to help people pass. If taking 101 just make sure to read the book!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I am a psych major and Boone taught my first psych course at UMD... and he made me hate it. He comes off as rude and somewhat of a jerk, constantly going off on tangents where he puts down/makes fun of his ex-wife. He just reads from slides for the entire period and expects you to do the rest. Take 101 with literally any other professor.

Boone is a nice guy and all, but I feel that at his level he shouldn't be teaching a 101 course of psychology. If you don't pay attention or take notes it may actually affect you, but not as much as he scales the grades. I have no interest in this class, but I had to take it anyways. Grading is unclear, and Review Material before exams don't help.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: You have to learn to look into the material than just take notes on the power point and look at the deeper meaning. College tip: Right down what people verbally say rather than just the power point. He might seem narcissistic, but by using his own experiences he is able to get his point across faster, which helped me tremendously.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Great teacher that makes class interesting and fun. But if you skip class, you most likely will not pass the tests or the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Not only a great teacher, but a great person. Very respectable and cares heavily for the subject he teaches. Negotiable with grade while also grading generously. Makes subject interesting with  stories and flashbacks from his past. Highly recommend.
Great teacher! Be prepared to take notes and pay attention during lectures because his tests are heavily based on those. Very willing to work with students outside of class.

Awful teaching methodology. Reads concepts from papers. Extremely disorganized. Doesn't deliver clear ideas of what he's teaching. Loves talking about himself and his experiences, therefore he is always adding online components that are as unclear as his classes. Frequently arrives late to class. Unclear grading criteria.
Great guy, I really dug his class.

Attendance not mandatory, lectures accomplish nothing. Study guides are lists of 100+ vocab words, not the particular concepts on the tests. He scales tests by a LOT, still got only C's on them. Doesn't explain key words well, generalizes it and moves on to the next. Funny dude, but I would start to doze off during lectures. Not for 101 course
