I hated this class but I luckily I still passed it. He goes off topic a lot and has a weird way of explaining things but I only been to this class a few times but still passed though.
Hated this class but I still passed somehow. He goes off topic a lot. I only been to this class like the first 2 weeks. But you have to really listen if you want to get a good grade.

His lectures don't cover half of the material on the tests. The tests are hard but scaled because everyone does so poorly.  You have to study the review material he emails out for the tests, even though it usually consists of 200 vocab words. He is cocky and goes off topic a lot.  Expect to teach yourself a majority of the material. not recommend

Professor Boone has extraordinary passion about psychology, only I feel as though he assumes his students should already know most of the stuff. You take a lot of notes in his lectures, but you most likely will have to look up definitions. I highly recommend reading as you go along because more than half of his tests do not come from lectures.

Dr. Boone was a great professor to have for Psych of Sex Differences.  He has a lot of experience in the field that contributes to engaging lectures and conversation!  I really learned a lot by taking this course.

I am so shocked and outrageous that I got C on this class. I never had below B- on PSY class and this is the first time. I only took this class because I do well on PSY, raise my GPA, and many students gave him good ratings. He talked about himself way too much. I don't even know what my grade estimation until the final grade.

Do not take this class if you're looking to maintain or improve your GPA.The class is interesting but he goes out of his way to make sure you do badly on the exams and final paper. He is very inconsistent with his lectures and believes he's the best thing that ever walked the face of the earth. His need to show how dumb everyone is supersedes all.

His lectures are all over the place, he study guides are the most lengthy I have ever come across in my years at UMASS. The worst part is out of 16 possible essay questions he refuses to tell you how he expects you to answer just ONE! His superiority complex is nauseating! Plus he takes forever to post grades. DO NOT TAKE HIM!!!!

His lectures are scattered and inconsistent.  He gives study guides then says he cannot answer any questions regarding the exam because he gave a study guide, the study guide does not match the notes, all bullet point notes have to write everything he says down and he doesnt cover half of whats on the exam, also acts impatient when u ask questions

WAY too into himself. I don't feel like I learned anything useful in this class. Doesn't use the book, power points were a little helpful. If you remember what he says about how he feels on a subject the tests won't be too hard. He really loves to talk about himself and what he thinks about everything.
By far the best teacher i have ever had. you will never find another teacher who cares as much about his students. Stats is a hard subject, but he is an amazing teacher!!
It is a difficult class. He scaled our tests a lot and I think he did the best he could with a boring class.

Class was not as easy as one would think. Very demanding! 2 exams,1 long research paper, presentation,and discussion boards on WebCT. Interesting material and seemed to be easy, yet tricky and different on exams. Do not take to enhance your GPA, does the opposite. Prof. seems to be cool and provides great examples, however does not teach to exam.

I took him for psych of sex diff. class wasn't that demanding, and attendance is not mandatory. The material is very interesting. 2 tests, a paper, a presentation, and 4 short answers online.I think everyone in the class basically got a b, maybe a few c's- I got an A- which was my lowest grade all semester very easy to get a B but hard to get an A.
No Comments
Awesome teacher. Totally passionate about the subject, full of interesting examples and helpful stories. Pretty challenging, but definitely worth it in the end

He's a really good professor.  His lectures were very interesting, and he gives great feedback on the paper thats assigned (which is pretty impressive considering class size).  The exams were hard, and he didn't define things as much as give examples so read the book and you'll do fine.  Also, the study guides were very helpful - so do them!
Tests were hard and based very loosly on the lectures. Be prepared wear out your book.
He's helpful if you confront him, but...you don't learn much through his lectures so you have to read the book.  Tests are kind of difficult but he scales alot.

Great teacher! He's a good guy and very helpful. His tests are fair but you have to study. Very fair grading wise. You have a few opinion papers that are kind of fun to do and very interesting. Overall he's a great teacher.
Who would have thought Research Methods could be a fun class? We played Mindsweeper the first class. Professor Boone is a great new addition to UMass. Take him for Methods if you can!

Nice guy, wants all his students to do well. Take notes while reading text because he does not cover all test material in lectures. Scales grades so you usually end up getting a higher grade than what you actually got. Keeps lectures interesting by comparing material to experiences with his kids. Requires two 2page essays. defiantely reccommend
A great professor. I had her for figure drawing, and she gave great feedback. She doesn't lecture for long and gives us plenty of time to draw.
She grades on criteria's not listed in the syllabus.

Suzy is such a wonderful professor. She gives you great feedback on your work, and always respects your ideashelps to improve them. Best painting teacher by far. If you have the chance, take her! You won't regret it!
i appreciate international experiences from living and working in other places. lessons are full of real world knowledge, not just another lecturer speaking from a book.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Greatest Prof. I struggled with health early in semester and she was understanding. Syllabus is just flexible enough to catch up. Circle classroom is so great. Everyone says what they want or they pass. No judgement. We learn a lot. A lot of work, but not unmanageable.
Spare yourself. The circle thing is uncomfortable and she's very biased.

best!! learned so much from readings and lectures but also we learn how to be ourselves and how to accept others.She is so smart and understanding. We voted her best prof in CJS last year. Excited to take her elective this fall. Don't miss out! This prof is amazing.
MY GOAT!!! Had her for History of Crim and she was great. if I could have her every semester I would. She's incredibly smart and engaging, and she's very understanding to her students

Best class I've taken at this university so far. I learn new things every time I attend. She listens to what students say in circle and gives information/history to help us understand the issues we discuss/ask about. Letters to ancestors and future ancestors writing assignments are meaningful to me. Never had a class like this before. She's great!
Easy A, eight letters, three circles attendance mandatory, interesting readings, lectures are boring, but you'll learn a lot. TAKE HER you pass no matter what just do the work

there is a lot of work, and when u hand notes in on the shows u watch, u need to go to class in order for it to count, lets you redo work as long as you handed it in on time originally, very nice&asks everybody how they are and cares. shes very helpful. i recommend her as a professor 100%
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Hilarious

reviews.comment: CJS 450 senior internship was awesome. Just enough readings, small number of class meetings, everyone's opinion and experiences in the internship placements was heard and respected. Saleh-Hanna's been around for a long time. She knows whats up and genuinely cares for her students.

Uses Law and Order and other crime tv shows to teach theories! It was awkward at first cause she had us sit in circle but once we settled in it was great. I enjoyed learning from other students and learned how to speak in front of them after being nervous to speak in class for years. take this prof, she is real people.

runs class in circle and this allowed us all to learn so much from her and from each other. highly recommend her for students interested in criminology, she knows her stuff and will teach you so much.

Had to retake this class in my senior year after failing with another professor. She ran class in circle and it was amazing. I learned a lot and felt a strong bond with my classmates, we all looked forward to this class every week. This prof genuinely cares about student wellness and yet is no nonsense when it comes to what you learn. loved her!

Took her classes for 4 years. Learned so much AND saw her hold her cool when a small number of students whined and complained about workload (which was not too much at all for senior-level class). Watching her manage complainers taught me skills I'll use in my workplace. highly recommend for students who want an actual university experience.
Rude, Unkind, and assigns heavy workload but if you disagree, you have no chance at a good grade, so you have to play along...
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: I enjoyed the Abolition course for CJS 380 I thought it was very eye opening and insightful.
She's an amazing advisor but a very difficult professor. CJS 258 is very hard considering that it's a 200 level course.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: If your views are not similar to hers, you are wrong. When going into this course I knew she was passionate about the topic. However a lot of the material is very difficult to grasp and to interpret the way she intends. When you attempt to express these topics in your own views or understanding, she completely ignores your views.

I found her class extremely hard. Very stressful as I'm about to graduate. She is very passionate about what she teaches, but she makes it interesting. It doesn't take away from the fact that the terms she brings in to use are sometimes very hard to understand and grasp. Only take if you are ready to do a lot of reading and are committed.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: The worst class I have ever taken in college. Her lectures are only her personal beliefs. Most of the topics don't have to do with criminology. You can try to argue another way but good luck. The class should be called why white people are awful and class of racism instead of criminology. 85% of the class is opinionated
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: She is one of the toughest professors that I have ever taken in the CJS Department, it is not an easy A....
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: At first I was hesitant to take this class with her but Saleh-Hanna is actually pretty nice. She comes off fierce and scary but she's not too bad. She gives a few pop quizzes throughout the semester, but she counted our notes for two. There is a group project at the end of the semester. Midterm and final are both multiple choice. Funny too!

Honestly I dont see how everyone finds her so hard . She is a great professor and honestly cares about her students. I had her for an advisor and she helped me tremendously with staying in school and acquiring financial aid . I wouldnt do most of the readings but if you just listen to her in class youll be fine . Never got below an A with her

Saleh-Hanna is horribly misunderstood. She is a passionate teacher who loves the topics she discusses and puts a lot of work into each lecture. I failed her midterm with a 30, but I went to speak to her and was able to redo it and bring my grade up to a 90. Group project was easy and final was easy if you listen in class. I passed with an A.
Great professor who is extremely interested in the subject.

Seems like a nice and caring person, but really pushes her beliefs on you. She is right, you are wrong, end of story. Class lectures are confusing and reading are long and very hard to understand, some people have compared this course to a masters degree course, way too advanced and expects too much.
Very difficult, expects too much, difficult to take notes in this class. Way too many complex and difficult readings.

A lot of reading. Don't do the reading you fail. Take home final, 6 essay questions, difficult. Readings are long and dense. Lectures are long. Over 20 online reading assignments. Two novels and a book. Hard to pass because there is only a group project, midterm, and final. Do poorly on one and it will sognificanrly hurt your grade.

Saleh-Hanna comes off a little strong, but it's mostly just her passion for the class. Do the readings, go to class, otherwise you'll be very behind. The 2 tests are VERY difficult and worth 25% and 30% of the final grade. There is a group project at the end, but it is fairly simple and does not take too much time, also 30% of the grade.

This was BY FAR the HARDEST 200 level class I have ever taken!! While the prof has good intentions she is way too analytic. She expects far too much for a 200 level course, this was suppose to be my "easy" class for the semester but turned out to be a total bust!! I heard her other classes are more tolerable. Good luck if you take this course!

If you are ready to be a conscious person this is the class for you. She is an amazing professor and has become my favorite. She only gives for assignments all semester but your grade can be discussed if your not satisfied. She is blunt and introduces you to stuff that is in front of you that you can't see. Just take her class it is amazing

the only time you're going to have a problem is during criminological theory.  She got her masters in criminology so its an intense class.  Every other class I've taken with her I love.  I have 2 A's from her already and a C in criminological theory.  Shes very interesting and can get off topic if you talk about Nigeria

Very misunderstood professor. Has very hard tests, but work with her in her office and you can't get less than a B+. Extremely helpful as long as you don't act up and just respect her it's an easy gpa booster.

She doesn't hate people or try to trap them into "wrong" answers, she just doesn't tolerate crap. She is fair and knowledgeable, and has a huge interest in criminal and social justice. Does not do well in once-a-week classes, I think she's the kind of teacher that needs more than one class a week to get her points across.
do ur work and this teacher will like u..dont be ignorant and this teacher will like u..she smokes butts how much more down to earth can u get..

Saleh-Hanna has such a bad rep, but you have to get past that. She is an excellent professor. If there is any question you have or anything you are stuck on, if you go to her office, she   will completely help you out. Teaches very interesting topics.don't be afraid to take her class, unless you hate good professors.

I disagree with the other comments. I think that she is a great professor, she puts alot of time and effort into her class....She is very helpful if you go to her for help. She DOES not hate her students, she just has a bad rep...reasons unknown!

gets a bad rap (i.e. through vague lectures, assignments, etc.), but i agree with the last comment. she's very helpful and understanding if you go to her for help and show her you care through class participation, attendance, etc.

I thought she was a good professor. She is very willing to help you improve your grade if you ask her. Lectures can be confusing, but overall the class was very interesting. Midterms and finals are hard, but she tries to help as much as she can.

She is only interested in music. I couldn't stand her class as she doesn't explain things and although she asks if people understand, she leaves no real room for discussion. Acts like people with questions are stupid. I dropped the class because I hated this class so much.
No Comments

Class is very very very very boring, no one knows whats shes talking about. midterm and final are freakin impossible she doesnt care about her students, The worst professor i have ever taken here at umass
i am losing my mind taking this final...its terrible...completely uninteresting
terrible. she might want to look into another profession... blatantly doesnt like her students.
A very dedicated Professor ; competent and knowledgeable in his discipline.

reviews.comment: Excellent in teaching and lab
Easy going and always eager to hear you out

He is a very good person. He is willing and wants to help you if you need it. Very hard to understand but ask for slide print outs. Study for tests and if you dont understand ask him for help and go to his office hours. Even if you still do bad he will help you out.
he can't speak english, plain and simple...

Such a sweet guy and will help you with anything you need.  ASK if you need help, he will def make time for you.  Exams are easy if you study the study guide he gives you.  Do the homework and the project, it's not that hard.
Very dedicated and willing to answer questions at all times

OMG how can anyone say hes a good teacher....maybe a good guy but def not a good teacher... i cant understand a word he says and he cant explain anything....hw is easy but test are soo hard thank god he scales them or else no1 in this class would pass

class is easy overall. tests are hard but study it helps, he scales the tests. do the 2 HW assisnments he gives you well, they're 20% of your grade.  Do the damn project, it's 20% also.  He notices if you attend class.  VERY smart, but unclear.  But you will love him.
he's my favorite. ask and ye shall receive. most helpful in dept.  but something is NOT right about him this semester, he's not himself...but he still is the best..
Good guy, very helpful, not like others in the department.

Very nice guy. Just lectures most of the class and scales the tests he gives. No homework,and notices if you attend class or not, so the more you are the there, the better you'll grade will probably be especially if he knows your name.
This class really has nothing to do with "nursing". Although she is very sweet and willing to work with you. This class is kind of a joke, It's a 8am class and the assignments were kind of silly.

she's honestly super nice the work is all very easy but such an unnecessary class. i did not learn one thing the whole semester. she also is hard to understand and if you answer a question she always will redirect it like your answer was wrong until she just ends up having to say it because no one wants to talk at an 8am class…..

Something I must note is that when you would answer a question (per her request) she would respond to the answer as if it was totally wrong and an out of this world response. She was quite nice and was a pretty easy grader. The class/homework was pretty easy, but this class doesn't have anything to do with nursing, more with how to be better.
She takes all your hard work and gives little credit for it. The grading felt unfair, and her explanations weren't clear enough to understand what she actually wanted.

she cancels every week for her personal stuff but if you miss class she gets rude with you. she might have class one time a week but you never know until right before class starts she gives homework that is so confusing then will blame you for not understanding

the class is not at all about female sex offenders. more like people of color and their rights, awesome…….. but it's not about the class. she also doesn't grade until the last minute and she's lazy when it comes to communication. she would rather your classmates answer questions you have in a question thread. everything is a YOU problem

Do not recommend at all, all around terrible. No structure, no format, if you think you did good on an assignment nope, she'll take all your hard work and just give you a horrendous grade. Didn't even learn, everything was nonsense, she was just chatting.

reviews.comment: Find another professor. Bad grader, horrible with organization, not clear with assignments, extremely rude, doesn't respect students. She canceled class once a week due to her own things. No compassion at all. She's just a professor not even a human.

reviews.comment: yaps at u for most classes, book club presentations, essays are non stop, shes rude, mycourses are so disorganized, she tries to relate everything u learn to her own life, no redo's, no late work, FLAT OUT RUDE, the comments she leaves on ur work is just wrong and rude
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: I want to inform students that she is a respectful woman and caring. However, she is not an organized person and she could be better with giving the directions on assignments better.

Awful professor, would not take any classes with her again. Stay away from her class if you actually want to learn something. She is an awful grader who does not give enough information about what she wants on her assignments.
Awful professor. Does not give clear instructions and is very picky about what she wants on her assignments. Would not take this class with her again. Waste of my time.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Only 50 % of class was conducted . We didnt have class for the last half of the semester. On top of not having class we were given no instructions no assignments. Waste of my money. For final exam students had about 3 to 4 chapter to study and understand on their own .

This professor is extremely difficult getting a hold of in emails. I will try at all cost to avoid this professor because if I have to attend classes online, I should be getting quicker responses back in emails. She can tend to be rude at times.
She is a caring and sweet teacher. She cares about you and really wants you to do well. This is a writing intensive course so be prepare the write a lot! But it's mostly fun and opinionated writing

This class was an online class and was very easy. She has a different grading criteria whereas if you do the assigned work you will get an A. There was one assignment where there was some confusion and I had already submitted but she informed the class that she would grade what was already submitted and not be penalized if it was done wrong.
Such an amazing professor and an insightful class. Genuinely cares about her students and wants them to succeed. All the work is so interesting and worth it.
His Class should be avoided at all costs, Look for Better Professors.

Attendance is mandatory. After 3 absences, each one drops your grade; 8 total means you fail. No computers or iPads, and you'll be called out if you don't take notes or if you're late. No online materials. Exams are extremely hard, and there's a 9-page historical paper. Tough grader and expectations are intense.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Walker is a great guy and is very passionate about history, which makes for interesting lectures. However, this is a very self-guided class because he doesn't talk about assignments, and all work is done outside of class, including the 8-page essay. He offered guidance to avoid withdrawal but understood that it was the best thing for me.

This class was my toughest class this semester. I almost withdrew,I am still unsure of my grade? He doesn't use online platforms so you have to email him to see how you are doing. Also do the map book exercises early because it takes a lot of time,and also make sure to take some time to review otherwise you won't do well on the exams. Good luck!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I hope you're a great listener and have great memory because that's one of the only ways you'll pass this class. His class is very lecture heavy, he has you take a lot of notes just based off the things that come out of his mouth, no presentation, no slides, nothing. He gives 2 big tests (mid term & final) and an 8-9 page bibliography.

Don't recommend this class there's only like two hard assignments then the midterm and final (which are all open lengthy response questions and a map) made us buy 2 textbooks but honestly didn't really need to. talks the entire class but only wants handwritten notes. Refuses to use my courses or any technology and u have to print all homeworks
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: he does absolutely nothing online, he refuses to post anything online so you need to meet with him in person often.

reviews.comment: Lots of reading and homework u will not like this class..

reviews.comment: He does not use my courses and never answers a question directly, he is incredibly unclear and repetitive. He also is not big on being resourceful to students. Avoid if possible.

reviews.comment: His classes are very challenging but worth the effort. he does expect a lot from his students which at times can be overwhelming but if you love history and want to learn a lot he is the perfect professor. he response quickly to emails and will help you out as long as you try. you will not fail if you try.

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS! The worst things were the exams, map book and term paper. You get graded on a few things which is horrible. The map book was a horrible way to teach geography. You have to write a 7-8 page term paper which caused me the worst stress. Attendance is mandatory, and if you don't show up to class, I guarantee a failing grade!!!!!

reviews.comment: DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS! The worst things were the exams, a dumb map book and term paper. You have to buy the map book yourself because the bookstore is always sold out of it. The map book was a waste of time.  Also, you have to write a 7-8 page term paper which was awful. Attendance is mandatory, and if you don't show up to class. good luck passing
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment:
guy does not use mycourses. 
Writing term paper was cancer. He failed to mention you could only use book sources after the drop date
Hes a hardo in a 100 level class literally take any other professor for history and youll do well.
I do well in all my classes but this one just sucked.

This is probably one of the worst professors I have ever had the mis fortune of having. You are graded on a total of like three things. Two halfs of a mapbook, and an essay. Also he has no blackboard set up for the class so if you dont show upYou will have no idea what is going on in the class. Not even the syllabus is online. His lectures are bad

reviews.comment: His main project of the 10 ten essay is hard but I honestly had alot of fun doing it. It was tough but it definitely paid off.
Took this as an elective and it was a challenging course, but a very nice professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Walker is extremely passionate about the subject that he teaches, which is evident through his lectures where he actually talks to the class without the use of powerpoints. Doesn't use MyCourses which could be a bit frustrating at times, but he's accessible thru email. Only gives midterm, final and a 12 pg paper. Only hw given are readings.

Beyond intelligent man. Only a few grades but they are so doable as long as you care. He really reaches out and encourages you to do the same. His lectures aren't just powerpoint slides- he is actually enthusiastic and just talks to us.  Very nice and caring man.  I'm glad I had the opportunity to be in his class.

Professor Walker is amazing! He's a very funny and nice person and is enthusiastic about his topic. The only homework he assigns during the semester is reading from the textbook which is usually about 30 pages. I found him very helpful when I asked for help and would recommend him!

one of my favorite teachers at Dartmouth. he is very clear in his lectures. goes out of his way to help out and he is very understanding. i would definitely take another class with this professor if i had the opportunity.

The amount of work he gives you is ridiculous, especially for an entry-level elective class. If you wind up with him, I recommend making note cards for every single term  he gives you in each class with 5-6 bullets about each one. It will help a lot for the exams. The 10-12 page research paper sucks. Avoid taking his class.
Awesome professor!

Very nice man, and pretty funny. Lecture can get boring. Must write 10-12 page paper. MAP BOOK is HORRIBLE and a waste of time (not a good way to learn geography). Tests are really hard because he gives you a huge study guide, with about 60 ids and 20 essay questions... But then you only answer 5 ids and 2 essays...too much info to study for test.

Great teacher, especially if you are a history major. He is incredibly knowledgeable. Also, he is willing to meet with students and discuss issues at any time. The work loads are almost the same for all of his classes: map workbook, 10-12 page paper, midterm, and final.
Jerk acts like its a class that matters. Its a 104 class and he runs it harder than my calc3 class. Horrible amount of work due in this class, take a different teacher if possible.
Prof. Walker is the fun and smart. Knows a lot about the material he teaches

Walker is a great guy. He tells you exactly what he's going to put on the test. Map books are tedious and annoying but he grades them easily. 10 page paper is a bit much but he gives fair grades. Really interested in the topic he teaches. I would definitely recommend him.
v.good pro.u can learn a lot from him. exam are not that hard gives u exact reading for tests just his map book is bit boring but don't worry abt it too mcuh
very good teacher.cares for his students
No Comments

Good teacher, fairly easy class. Only gripe I have is the stupid map book exercises you have to do 21 in all. Only reason I got a B was I didn't put my effort in the 10 page research paper thats like 40% of your grade. But great teacher very nice guy

grade based on 1.) mapbook- kinda annoying 2. tests- kinda hard, asks for essay, short answer, map section 3.) 10 page paper- you pick subject, make sure you start early, he gives you all semester and i did it within last 3 days.... classes are interesting and he lets you have 3 absences, i would take him but dont expect an easy gpa booster

really interested in history, so he makes it interesting. Makes you do a 10 page report that isnt to fun but he lets you pick the topic and gives you plenty of time to do it. Tests are fill in map questions and essays no multiple choice

awesome professor.. his tests cover ALOT of material but if you fulfill all the other needs in the class like the mapbooks and the 10 page paper he assigns. you're pretty much ensured a B... He does a sick job teaching the information too so you learn alot in this class.. one of the few good professors at UMD...

Very good teacher and helpful, great and interesting class...he has a touch of professor-know-it-all-ness esp when he gives u study guides with tons of stuff on it uve never heard of and isnt important but what the hell i got an A so i cant complain...i recommend any of his class...hes a really really nice guy too

Ok while he does tend to grade a little tough, his classes are fun and do not have a heavy workload. His expertise seems to be Maritime and early European Civ. In short, if you have the option, take a class with him, he rocks.

Very Interesting, and always interested in the matertial he teaches.  He presents his material clearly and concicely.  His tests are hard, and he is a pain in the ass when it comes to paper writing. Overall a great teacher.
Very clear and engaging if you like History.  Also very eager to help students.  10 page paper seems a little much for 100 level class, but everything else is pretty easy.
A really good professor

Tim is a great teacher. His lectures are very relivant to the tests, but at the same time he follows the book so well that class attendance isnt really required (he counts it in grading, but very minimally) i missed maybe 15 classes, and still got A's on the midterm and final. i highly recoment him

Honestly, he is such a sweet teacher with a kind heart. He wanted everyone in class to succeed. He is there to answer questions and help any students who are in need. He does have an accent but it is not as bad as the other comments say. The excel homework is weekly and takes years to do. All Exams online. Great teacher!
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Can't understand his accent. All quizzes and tests are online and are easy. He's available for any questions. Homework's are on excel. Attendance is for a grade, miss one class and you're down 5 points on attendance. I used this class as a study hall.
Literally incomprehensible because of his accent, do not take him if you actually care about learning the topic.  If you have to take him, be ready to chatgpt everything

Uday is a nice and smart guy tbh. His lectures are pretty boring, gets upset when he asks us questions in class and nobody answers lmao. Bunch of hw assignments, attendence is graded, the class is pretty easy, all you gotta do is show up and do the hw on time. Hard to understand him at first, but he has CC on the screen so you can understand him.

Hard to understand, probably a smart dude, but these classes are boring. He gets upset over small things, which is usually pretty funny, but a fella is trying to get a 4.0. UMass is a great school filled with opportunity, but good lord the business math department is abysmal. These teachers are not engaging or motivating at all.

horrible if you are an auditory learner. U cannot understand the professor which makes it so hard to pay attention in class. He is kind and records the lectures for u that help with Excel but his classes are built so if u miss info you will forever be lost. !!JUST TAKE STATS ONLINE AT A COMMUNITY COLLEGE THE OPTIONS FOR IT AT UMASS ARE THE WORST!!

He may have a  heavy Indian accent, but this guy goes out of his way to literally put on live closed captioning to help students understand.  He works  tirelessly to engage his students and has a genuine passion for education and wants you to succeed. He's a kind man who made me passionate about excel. Best class I've taken at UMD. Uday=🐐

He is one of the nicest educators I have had. He clearly knows the material really well and is always very accessible outside class for help. Definitely one of those professors that would enhance your interest in the subject if you are willing.
He is so sweet and amazing. Very understand professor and just wants everyone to learn and interact in class
Professor is somewhat hard to understand. The homework is lengthy with a requirement to use excel formulas even for simple calculations. An easy class but still time consuming.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Has a very heavy accent that is very difficult to understand, great guy but hard to take in knowledge when speaking, quizlets for every quiz & test, and chegg answers to all homework if you're willing to spend the money for a subscription, overall easy professor to have, just hard to understand.

Professor Jha is a very nice guy, although he is very tough to undestand. The class has mandatory attendance, a group project case study, and lengthy homeworks. He records his lectures and I would highly recommend rewatching them to understand the concepts that are taught.
Professor has thick accent and many people complain that they do not understand him. He tries his best to slow down and explain things multiple times. Go to class and rewatch letures
Uday really wants his students to understand the material. Hes really nice, you need to study the material to understand it but overall he's a fair grader and is helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: He's honestly a great guy. he cares about what he's teaching and very understanding. I broke my hand and had to surgery and he extended everything for me to turn in. He has a thick accent but his class is not difficult.

Very nice and understanding professor. Wish I could take him again. All the bad reviews regard his accent which he can't help. He knows its thick but you can understand him if you listen. He cares about the material and his students understanding of it. Homework is absolutely do able. Loved him.

He is such a nice guy and is very knowedgable about the subject. You can tell he tries to really teach the students but he knows he is hard to understand. It doesn't help that people don't even look up from their computers all class. His homework is easy, the tests are all online and completely do able. He is always willing to help. I liked him.
Hes a homie, great guy and always there for his students classes is easy to pass if you pay attention. Rocks the freshest cardigans in the concrete jungle.

You cant understand a word he says, hes a nice guy but his lectures feel like hours. Quizlet and Chegg are your best friends for the homework and the quizzes/tests. Most of the answers are word for word on there. As for the homework i suggest teaching it yourself or getting a friend who can get the answers. You can come in at any time and sign in
reviews.rating_tags: GRADED BY FEW THINGS--TEST HEAVY--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: I couldn't believe that this is a required course. I learned nothing from this guy. I ended up teaching myself using online videos from somewhere else.

Very lecture heavy, all available online though. VERY thick accent, very difficult to understand. All tests are online though, so you still have a good chance of passing even if you don't pay attention/show up. Basically teach yourself everything. Very upfront that you did not need the textbook.
Could not understand one word this man said!

I have tried very hard to listen and understand what he said in class but failed. It is also very annoying when he keeps asking if we are with him every 5 minutes. I considered dropping from this course.
Adorable, heavey indian accent, very happy
Very thick Indian Accent and it is extremely difficult to understand him. His work load is not heavy but you will have no idea what to do in this class if you do not self teach.

Has an accent but it's not hard to understand he's always willing to repeat things for you. He genuinely wants you to learn the material. Likes participation but won't call on you or force it. Lecture/slide heavy but records and uploads to mycourses. Tests and quizzes are online and the homework isn't that bad if you follow the Excel formulas.

He has an accent but you'll get used to it, I understand it usually. And if I don't, he's always willing to repeat things. He likes participation but won't force you to. Lecture and slideshow heavy but he records them and uploads them to mycourses. Tests and quizzes online. Nice guy and genuinely wants you to learn. The hw in this class isn't bad.

Very nice guy - always accessible outside class. Knows his material well, would give detailed explanations for questions. Homework assignments can be a bit lengthy sometimes, but as long as you hand them in on time you should be fine in terms of grades.

professor Jha was sweet. you can tell he genuinely cares about his students and wants to helo them succeed. however, his accent was so thick it was so hard to understand him. it's easy to fall behind because you have no idea what he is saying. chegg/Quizlet will be your best friend. definitely do able but prepare to teach yourself/learn nothings.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He explains very carefully the concepts in class. He has strong accent which is a bit difficult to understand but you can ask him to repeat all the time. There are 3 homework and 3 tests during the semester. Hes an easy grader and also provides extra credit. If you come to class and turn in your homework on time, you should get good grade.

If you struggle with math, I wouldn't recommend taking him remotely because it is really hard to understand him due to the language barrier. He's a nice guy, but his homework is just outrageous. It can be 20-30 questions long but with 5-6 parts each. The good thing is that most of his quizzes and tests can be found on quizlet
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I took Professor Jha's online POM500 course, and he came across as a very kind man who wants his students to succeed. He is very responsive to emails, and his lectures are clear and do a good job of explaining the context & reasons for each step. Take thorough notes on everything he says- many details from lectures appear in quizzes/tests.

Best Professor Ive had at UMass Dartmouth so far. You might hear rumors on his thick accent but dont worry. As long as you follow along on lectures, you will get a good grasp on it. He is also willing to meet to describe homework to you in a way that youre able to understand. Nice guy, great class. Will definitely take the POM 333 with him.

As long as you follow the syllabus, pass assignments in on time, and study his materials you should receive a passing score. I enjoyed his class out of all of the POM classes I needed to take for the accounting major. He is very accessible if you have questions as well. Great teacher.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Homework is very hard to figure out when you cannot understand the videos. '(

Well, Professor Jha is a really cool guy, but the amount of homework is ridiculous. Theres only 4 homework assignments a semester but were talking about 20 questions each with 4 or 5 parts so its really like 50 questions plus the take home quizzes

He is very hard to understand sometimes, but is very nice/understanding. All of his tests are taken online (open book/google). You do have to show up for class, but just bring your laptop and you will be fine!
Could not understand what he said in class! Avoid at all cost!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--Tests are tough

reviews.comment: Wasting my time because of mandatory attendance. Couldn't make any sense out of his talk. Not recommend to anyone!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: just show up to class, do all of the online hw/quiz/test and you will get an A

Hard to understand, but if you can make it work, he has the class formatted in a way that makes sense and the tests were online, so, there's that. However, if tests were offline, you could still pass showing up and doing homework. Not that bad. Also pretty cute.

Homework is really difficult, quizzes and tests are online, straight off quizlet. His accent is really difficult to understand, makes learning through his lectures almost impossible, seems like a smart man otherwise.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Homework, quizzes, and exams are all online. Homework assignments are long. He is hard to understand but tries his best to reiterate material. Attendance is mandatory but he is understanding if you have valid reasons.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Very difficult to understand and confusing lectures. Needed to utilize the internet in order to pass this class, and even then he gave me a D.  Avoid at all costs.

He's hard to understand and class is boring. All the exams are online but they're time-consuming and difficult. His lectures slides don't help if you don't take  notes. I got a decent grade but I honestly can't tell you anything I learned in this class

Absolutely horrid teaching. He is a nice guy but his accent is hard to understand and he is not great with explanations. The only good thing is that everything is done online. However, homework requires a great deal of work and he does not accept hard copies. Only take this class if you are desperate and willing to teach yourself.

Can barely understand him. What he talks about in class is not related to homework assignments or tests. Homework assignments are long and most of the time I could not even complete them because i did not have the knowledge to complete them.  Luckily i used the internet to my advantage and got through the class. Stay away from this professor.

He is extremely difficult to understand, he has a very thick accent. When teaching he skips steps and then when someone asked a question he starts all over again. Took him for Business Stats. WOULD NOT RECOMMEND. Though everything is online, quizzes test, hw, so if you like teaching yourself go for it.
Great professor, down to earth, young and understanding. Discusses relevant issues, explains things in a way that's easily digestible. Weekly journals and 2-3 papers. Would take again

Really informative about things happening around the country/ world. There was 4 essays and one individual project and one group project throughout the semester. There are articles/videos he does suggest you look at to help write your essays and projects. Great professor.

Can come off as a very tough professor, however, keeps the lectures relevant and interesting. Takes attendance every class so its mandatory to show up, cancels class a few times. Grades class based on about 5 papers. Write the essay and have him look over it at his office hours and youll get a good grade.

Overall an ok Professor. Lots of reading 3 quizzes and about 5 papers. I received a low grade on my first paper but after talking with him and explaining what happened he allowed me to fix my mistake and change my grade. If you take the time to talk to him and ask questions you should be fine. He cares about his students.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Do not take any classes with this professor. I am a straight A junior and all I can get in this class is B- or C's. He seems nice and cool but he is the worst person when it comes to grading. He doesn't explain what he wants and expects us to read his mind. By far worst professor at UMass.
Brilliant professor. I would take any class which he would be teaching

Best teacher i've had. he is very easy and nice. not to mention he is funny and makes effort to make class interesting. If you go to class you will do good and if you have an opportunity to take a class with him and you don't it's your loss.
he's so frigen easy.  just come to class, participate even once in a while and he'll love you.  no homework really, in-class essays once in a while.  such an easy grader.  good teacher to have
about 100 akward moments per class.  he asks the class questions and no one answers.  throws hissy fits time to time because no one participates.  thinks hes funny.   stay away from this guy
He is a mean teacher and does not help you at all. He isnt clear on anything and no one likes him.  He shouldnt be a teacher.
He stinks as a teacher and is not a nice person at all.  He has favorites and his teaching style is so horrible. He picks on people and favors girls so bad
WICKED easy class. a lot of group work. NO tests. didn't really learn anything. very nice guy, but i don't really think he knows too much.
Very easy class, goes by quickly because of group work.  Dont really learn all that much.  No tests one paper, 2 oral presentations...id take him for an easy a/b if you can just make it to class

Biz Com.- this guy by far the worst teacher ever. he switched aroundt he sylabus. he didn't teach anything. Aced the class- no tests, attendance counts, doesn't explain anything. do not take him. he doesn't know what he is talking about. if i were him. i would just quit at life. he sucks

The course is interesting but his tests are EXTREMELY difficult. He said less than %50 pass his first exam. I managed a %66 on it but it was very difficult to overcome that grade even after doing well on the other tests. I would stay away from this class if you care about your gpa. Also attendance is mandatory.

Very easy going funny guy. I didnt do the readings but im sure they would help. only 4 grades 3 exams and one 5-7 page essay each worth 25% of your grade. Class was interesting and i enjoyed the class. Tests are difficult so make sure to study, 50 multiple choice a-e on the exams, other than that not a bad professor.

Tests are hard and he is demanding, doing all the readings will definatly help out.  Although I disagree with those saying he is crude, yes he is sarcastic but he's very funny, keeps class interesting, and grades papers very easily. Nice guy even if at first he is intimidating.

I took him for psc. He requires alot of reading, lectures were interesting but most of the time he just spent on making remarks about the students, teasing but annoying. Very hard grader and Unfair grader, he grades papers like an english teacher  rather than just grade them for the content, I would NOT recomend him.

His lectures are really interesting. He is really funny and he knows how to keep ur interest. He gives you three tests including the final and a book review. Basically you need to do stay on top of the reading and make sure you really know his study guides, and you should do well on his tests.

He makes it IMPOSSIBLE to do well in this class. He is inappropriate and he is unclear on what the tests are.  He makes crude comments and tends to put people down if they ask a question or show an opinion. Def NOT my favorite teacher

Nice guy. His lectures are interesting and I learned a lot. He calls on you even if you don't raise your hand so be prepared. Tests are pretty easy, and he only assigns 2 papers. Great professor. I'd take him again.

Very demanding teacher...had no care in the world for others...he likes to pester you with question after question, until you're completely embarassed. He even says he enjoys seeing students suffer while trying to answer these waves of questions.

Great Professor. Quite possibly the best lecturer I've had in college so far. Somehow, he makes discussion of Federalism bearable. The tests are hard if you don't read the text/ study beforehand, and if thats the case, you don't belong in college. He gives you everything you need to succeed in his class, you just need to work for it.

His class is sort of entertaining I guess. He says he's a jerk but he's really not. He likes to talk about sex a lot....but I guess because thats what college kids are interested in. Tests are pretty hard.....but study guides make them doable.

It pains me to say this, but Upchurch was one of the better CS professors and I'm sad to see him go. Yes: he presented a tough exterior, had a very sarcastic wit, was intolerant of stupidity, and gave nothing back. But he saw through the BS that goes over other teachers' heads and graded, although obviously subjectively, reasonably fairly. So long.

Had several assignments due the portal and never mentioned it. It was your responsibility to check it regularly.  Tests were extremely difficult and you needed to remember dates and other details. No way to know how well you are doing at assignments since he never leaves feedback or grades. Doing average on the tests will not get you a C.

The amount of people giving Upchurch a bad rap is unfair. He's a MUCH better teacher than most in the CIS department, and he doesn't have an accent. He IS hard - but college isn't supposed to be easy. He DOESN'T give you grade updates - but if you're that worried about it, maybe you should work harder. He definitely knows his stuff.
Nice guy feel like he tries to help but will laugh at you if you get the answers wrong. Understood the material but some how almost failed the course.... if possible take another class

He is a decent guy with a good sense of humor. But his assignments and the crazy way that they crade them are just ridiculous. I'm learning a lot but even though I'm doing all the work correctly and somehow I'm almost failing. He has absolutely no understanding, he doesn't really care about any of us and makes it very clear.

Wow the other reviews don't seem so good. BUT he is a great teacher, the only people he doesn't help out as much are those who don't come to class except on lab and test days. This has honestly been one of the most beneficial college courses i've take so far.

He comes off as a Nice Guy.  But one can still worry if he is nice come grading time.  He shouldn't give 'deadlines', in today's world it doesn't work out. We'd get bad marks all the time.  I really want to do the work and learn, just wish it was more clear on what he is asking for  He says to complete a graph yet there are really not clear instruc

It's sad to think that people like this can become a college professor. He's rude to students. If you ask for extra help he just laughs at you. You'll never get any feedback unless you do something stupidly wrong, then he'll humiliate you in front of the entire class. He'll assign projects at 8am and expect you to have it done by 11, class time.

You'll never know what the hell your grade is until it shows up on your transcript. Go ahead and look at the other reviews of him, it's a fact that you'll get no feedback whatsoever... unless of course you messed up. He'll gladly point that out. He likes to joke about how he assigns grades using a random number generator... hmmmm...
I have learned A LOT from Professor Upchurch, he is clear, easy to get along with and has great advice. I definently recommend him!

Upchurch is a moron, which is why he's not teaching CIS 480 anymore.  Plays favorites.  If you have a p*%%y you'll get better grade. Gives people he likes easy projects, ones he doesn't like the most difficult & impossible to complete. Doesn't return anything.  Grade based on if he likes you!! He's got a very bad attitude.

Entertaining professor, has his favorites, DO NOT get on his bad side.  Tests are extremely difficult and you wonder what exactly he is asking for.  Assignments are within reason.  Could use a grammar lesson or two before developing his slides and his tests.  At least the class was interesting.  I never knew where I stood the entire class.
he is the worst teacher ever. has no idea about what he teaches.

This professor is unpredictable and very hard. The subject matter is more relivant to project managers than to entry level programers. His grading is more by popularity and how much he likes or hates you. Unfortunately, you need to take this course with him in order to graduate.

This is the second class I have had with him and I am still not sure how he grades....Nice guy.  Interesting.  This class is important...you have to learn what he teaches because you will actually use it in life.

gives the hardest tests I've ever taken. I am learning useful things, but he's so bitter that it's hard to get through the wall of cynicism to the actual information.  Likes to humiliate students in class.  I hate him

Needs a vacation bad!  I liked "Alices Resturaunt". Doesn't provide feedback, see his website for his reasons.  Visit the learning portal daily for new assignments. Grades are based mostly on opinion! Bala should teach 480 instead, he'd do it be
Nice guy, funny.  Not really sure how he grades, but he is an interesting guy.  The course is useless, but someone has to teach it and he teaches it well.
Just make sure you play your cards well and stay on his good side.It's not like you won't have to do that in the real world.Honestly he's not that bad.

His classes are like life. You have to figure out for yourself what the heck you are supposed to do. Strange and probably not entirely appropriate for a college curriculum, since GPA is a reality we face, but very helpful for your professional career.
Gave the whole class an Incomplete because he didn't have time to grade. Fortunately after going about him, things were straightened out for the seniors to receive there diplomas on time.

Doesn't give a rat's ass about grades.  Believes (rightfully so) that they're overrated.  Is more concerned with your education.  Since becoming a professional (3 years ago) I've used more of the info he taught than any other.  Classes have real-world app

Better than most profs.  At first you have no clue what he wants you to do for him and he won't tell you either.  Make sure you define your processes and plans though before coding your project.  Even if it's not done you will get a good grade this way.

Terrible, gave the whole class an incomplete because he hadn't finished grading to his satisfaction.  He didn't even give a final.  Does not give any feedback during the semester either.  He's the only one that teaches 381 and 480

He's one of the better CIS Prof's in the school.Raises strong points,and doesn't read verbatim from his slides.Loves coffee.Other then the fact we never knew what our grades were I liked the class.He has mood swings though,so stay on his good side.

I've had upchurch for 4 classes. He can be a real A$$ sometimes but he doesn't fail people very often. He almost never grades assignments. He basically grades you based on what kind of student he thinks you are.

Professor Upchurch tends to grade on a mystery scale.  There is no indication as to what grade you may recieve and he will not be at all helpful in informing you of how you are doing at a certain point in the course.  Oh, and don't ask questions in class.

He's a cool professor, i wish i was able to take him for all my classes.  He jokes around all class, but you really do learn the material without even realizing it.  Wear a  I love Mac Sticker  to class everyday and he will love you.
No Comments
When he's not making fun of the class, he's a really good teacher.
He does bring up very good points about the subject in class, even though his lectures are 100% Powerpoint.  His grading system is a bit of a mystery since most of the assignments never get graded.
Awesome professor, you have to take her class!! The best at UMass

Her lectures are so interesting and she is so engaging. Just make sure you do your weekly tickets and quizzes and study her wrap-ups and you will be good! You do not need to rewrite her powerpoint, I would recommend just writing down her key points in class because the wrap-up already covers that. Would definitely take her again!!

Dr. Seney is the best professor I have had throughout nursing school. She lectures in a way that can be easily understood and is able to related content to everyday things! She is always easy to each via email and will answer any and all questions! I totally recommend taking a class with her!

She is hands down the best professor at Umass Dartmouth. Her lectures were very engaging and easy to follow. The workload was manageable and exams were very straight forward. If you have the opportunity to, I highly recommend taking a class with Dr. Seney. She is awesome!!
Prof Seney is amazing. She is one of the best Professors I've had. She is super helpful, but you have to put in the work. I would definitely take her again!

Not terrible, not great either. If you don't have to take this class, don't. Her lectures are biased and not actually based on truth. Quizzes and homework are extremely easy, attendance is randomly taken. This is the type of class you take quietly, don't express your opinion since everything is based on what she believes.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Super sweet professor, She's very open, kind, and is helpful. She will notify you in advancements when needed; she will let you know a whole week in advance and a day beforehand. Overall she's super great and she keeps her lectures very interactive and interesting.

Waste of time just avoid her if you can. How is she qualified to teach? So many retakes on everything you don't have to study just look it up during the quiz. Not learning anything I haven't seen in tv shows or youtube.

weekly online quizzes with unlimited attempts. Midterm had 5 attempts, and final had 10 attempts and were online and you have multiple days to complete these. A bit lecture heavy but its one of the classes that if you just show up, you'll pass
Class is unbelievably easy and I learned nothing. Professor tends to drone on about random things instead of actual concrete topic related to your major. Waste of time.

Lovelace was amazing. weekly online quizzes, but had unlimited attempts. Midterm had 5 attempts, and final had 10 attempts. (also were online and you have multiple days to complete these) she took attendance the second half of the semester when she noticed less people were attending class. Very lecture heavy, and there is out of class reading/media

Almost impossible to take notes in class, professors talks so fast and moves too quickly through slides. Online quizzes are based on the readings which are extremely long and tedious. Notes are helpful for midterm and final though. At first she didn't take attendance, but she started to mid-semester.
Talks very fast, can be difficult to keep up with notes. Realistically, most of the assignments for this class can be done without great notes though.

This Professor is absolutely amazing, she is accommodating, when necessary, and she is always there to support you if you need further help! Her work ethic is something I have never seen before, stemming from how passionate she is about holding this position, I was very glad to have her!

Lovelace is an excellent professor who really cares about her students and she really wants you to succeed and learn. I recommend taking her classes because they are really fun and we learn a lot of important information. Her assignments are easy to do, there are no tests in this class, just a few easy quizzes!

Dr. Lovelace is very passionate about her work.She cares about her student's success and will go the extra mile to make sure you do well.There was not a textbook for the class, attendance didn't count towards your grade, and even though participation doesn't count she loves when her students engage in the lecture. I would definitely take her again

Dr. Lovelace is an amazing professor! She helps guide you through any questions you may have during lectures or any questions you may have outside of class. She is very caring of her students and takes the time to give good feedback. She communicates very well and keeps you engaged during lectures. Definitely would love to have her class again!

This class is demanding in some aspects, but with Professor Lovelace, I felt very supported and cared for whenever I needed further information or feedback on assignments. She was very diligent with giving great feedback and she was very quick with grading materials! I would absolutely recommend this professor for any class you can get her!
This class was awesome. Professor Lovelace is great and she has very clear directions. Also, she is always willing to help.
Professor Lovelace is a great teacher, if you put in the work for the class you will get a good grade. She also interacts and has a great relationship with students.

This professor is very down to earth and makes the learning environment super fun and interesting! Lovelace is extremely knowledgeable and is always prepared to teach her students something new, her attention to detail followed by her dedication is inspirational and absolutely amazing! Her classes may be a lot of work, but it's very rewarding.
One of the best CJS professors. Interested and knowledgable about what she is teaching. Definitely would take again. Go to class, do your weekly work, and write the papers, you will do fine.
Professor LoveLace was great professor who truly cares about the students success.

There was a lot of readings but as long as you did them you were set. She is very kind and cares about the wellbeing of the students. Professor Lovelace is one of the few professors at Umass Dartmouth who is good at what they do. I recommend taking her!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: The only reason I would take Dr. Lovelace again is because her quizzes have unlimited attempts (yes, that's right). She assigns plenty of readings but they don't matter because there's unlimited attempts. This means that there's almost no student engagement, which makes the classes boring. Just show up and remember about the quizzes and you're set.

Absolute joke, always going off track in lectures, barely leaning, I'm paying for this?! All of the powerpoints have minimal info, usually asking a basic question which then leads to talking and talking and talking. Don't risk it
Take a long time to grade assignments. She is rude in her emails and never answers them. This profession is not for her.
Great Professor! Has a lot of enthusiasm for her class and is really nice! Work load is pretty easy and she is funny! Would def take her again.

Professor Lovelace is not the best and I would avoid taking her classes if possible. I had her for a mandatory CAS course. Her homework assignments were all short papers and she made the course extremely boring. I dreaded going to this class because she is rude and isn't the best at teaching. The way she organized the course did not make sense.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Great guy, quizzes + exams online and straightforward, people always forget the quizzes and he extends the date he's super nice. Homework is based on classwork. He is strict abt attendance tho and wants you to participate, be ready to read things out loud. Really funny class and it's hard to get a bad grade in his class he wants you to do well.

I loved this class, it was so fun and he is very kind and funny. He wants you to participate, and always keeps you engaged so the class is never boring. He jokes a lot and never makes you feel embarrassed for answering something wrong. It was really fun and I felt like I learnt a lot from his course. He wants students to succeed, very easy A!

Amazing and passionate professor. He loves to teach the language and you can tell the passion he has right from the get go. He wants everyone to get an A, but all he asks in return is for you to come to class and participate. Besides that the work is minimal with the quizzes being online. There is no exams or midterms whatsoever

Class is easy he is very lenient with work he just wants you to come to class and participate. He takes late work and wants everyone to get an A. Treats everyone equally and the class is interesting. Great course easy work I loved the lectures. Quizzes are online no midterm or final.

Prof Vasudev is a smart lady and knows her stuff but she's not very organized. She takes forever to grade and she struggles to keep up with course content. Overall found exams to be interesting and the project she had us do. She is nice and will give extensions also. I would not t take her if you can't handle a professor that is all over the place.

She as improved a lot over the past few years. Very kind now an she has a lot of information to give. Let her know if you need an extension, it is likely granted. Her classes have a lot of information on the slides and to digest, but her tests encourage you to think and understand what you learned, instead of asking you to memorize the information.

This class was horrifically difficult. It was hard for me to pay attention because of the material, but the worksheets were helpful. The lecturing was incredibly boring and I don't think I'll retain any of the information in this class. Professor Vasudev is nice, but not engaging at all.

Looks to take off points, instead of to grade them. Basically wants you to memorize lectures and regurgitate the information. Don't bother answering questions in your own words she'll take points off and rewrite the answer you wrote in her words. Speaks very softly, hard to understand her in class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: She has a thick accent and does not answer questions well. She will lecture a lot, and give 7-8 homework assignments, but they are difficult to do off of the powerpoint only. The lab section of this class has long lab reports, do not procrastinate or you will fall behind in the lab. Also had a difficult to understand TA that teaches the labs.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: great class

Very fast speaking professor, and has a thick Indian accent that at times is very hard to understand. She does not slow down and expects students to understand things the way she understands them. Homework questions are also not covered in the classes. Would not take another class with her again.
AVOID!
In my class, we didn't have to do a final exam, if we wanted he would average out our exams over the semester and that would be our final exam grade.

Prof Wei is a greatly good person, but he's hard to understand and his exams are so hard. But you can probably try if you study and got to tutoring an learn some of the concepts. But you must actually study to pass this class and put the effort because it's also very easy fail this class too.
Bless this man
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Love this man. It's hard to understand him during general lectures, so I really recommend keeping up with the homework and doing the practices he posts. Recitation is great. Second exam is the hardest and most people don't get above a 75. Hard class, great guy.

While sometimes it is hard to understand his lectures, he is very friendly and helpful. He was very lenient with me when I missed a test and allowed me to retake it with no penalty. He cares about you learning the material and if you reach out to him for help, you will most likely receive it. The class can be tough though.
you adjust to his accent but he's the lesser of two evils when it comes to the chem department
great professor
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment:
If you can put the work in, you'll do fine. For the accent comments it's really not that bad. Extra credit on 2/3 tests that ended up boosting them quite a bit.

He gives you the tools for you to succeed and wants to see you succeed.

Hes hard to understand sometimes.During class he reads slides that are taken straight from the textbook. During the recitation he teaches a little bit before you take your weekly quiz.Tests are not too bad.For the first 3 tests there are practice test you can use which are very similar to the actual test.But the final is in a very different format

Professor Wei's class is not easy and you will have to put in the work to pass, but he always offers extra credit on exams and even allows cheat sheets in the final. Genuinely really kind and caring and his rating on here is way too low. Don't blame your inability to work hard and put in the effort to understand concepts on his accent!
He's a nice person but his exams are very difficult and the class overall was hard. Also, it's hard to understand him sometimes.

I love Doctor Wei! Chemistry is a difficult subject, but he gives ample study material (recitation worksheets, powerpoint notes, answers to quizzes/exams) and is always quick to answer emails. Of course the class is lecture heavy, but the power points are online so you can follow along.

Dr. Wei is a very good professor. His quizzes and tests are on the harder side but as long as you really take the time to understand the material you will be successful in his class. Hes good at answering any questions and responds to emails quickly.

Dr. Wei is an amazing professor. He has an accent but other than that he is fine and has clear instructions. Meet him halfway and he will help you out so much. He responds to emails promptly and is willing to go over lab reports with you. He extends due dates for you give you a lot of time to submit work. I highly recommend taking him.
Get on his good side and you will be fine. He'sa great Professor and tries his best to help you out. He has an accent and can be rude sometimes but other than that hes a good professor
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I had him for my lab and lab lecture. His quizzes are straight forward but a little tough and right from the lecture notes. He answers emails pretty quickly and tries hard to answer your questions. Overall super nice guy and helpful In lab
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: This man is the defnition of bias this man has pre ideas before he starts class . He knows his favorites and is beyond rude has no respect for students and loves to accuse students. Doesn't help during lab. If this man likes you and you don't deserve a good grade he will pass you . But every other person who works had will not get the grade.

People who dont put in the effort will use his accent as an excuse when they dont earn the grade they think they deserve. He is actually a funny guy and you can learn a lot from him. The course is challenging but it is also a huge learning opportunity. OChem is hard for everyone, imagine getting PhD & teaching it in another language. Respect.

Yibin Wei is a good professor, his style of lectures and explaining details is just really hard to understand because English is obviously not his first language so it makes everything difficult. You skip class you might not pass because is tests are very hard. Don't be discouraged when you don't pass the first exam. His class is very difficult.
I don't know what to say besides "no". CANNOT understand a word he says. I had to teach myself chemistry all semester using youtube. Run fast and far from this guy.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Chemistry is my worst subject in general, but the fact that English is definitely not his first language and his unclear pronunciation of things made this class even harder for me to take. I basically had to not only try to understand chemistry, but also translate what he was saying. It was awful. Going to him for extra help didn't help either.
Avoid at all costs. The worst prof ever. Impossible to understand. No feedback, lies about charts given on exams. Impossible to reach outside of class. DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Dr. Wei is a brilliant Chemistry professor! His tests and class is extremely difficult, but he is easily accessible outside of lecture. Make use of office hours and do the  homework early.  Also be sure to begin studying for exams early and ask questions.

Chemistry is not easy. I worked hard for my passing grade.  Dr. Wei is a fair grader, he tells it like it is and is available for help. Do the homework, go to tutoring, practice the problems and go to class.  His  tests can be brutally hard. The order he does the chapters is helpful. He groups the heavy math concepts together.

I took Silva before Wei and failed. I love the fact that Wei breaks down the chapters and gives you a handout for each chapter (instead of a PowerPoint on the entire chapter leaving you with a lot of information.) He also gives extra credit and takes the best of 5 out of 10 quizzes. He really wants you to succeed. Great professor.

Wei is an awesome professor. The class was confusing I admit but he was always available to help during his office hours. PLEASE MAKE USE OF HIS OFFICE HOURS! Do online homework, its an easy grade booster. He makes you work for your grade but you definitely leave the class with a good understanding of the basis of chemistry.

Dr. Wei made lab fun! Great teacher, gives you everything you need to know for the lab experiments. Often will come around and do the lab for you to make sure you get good yields. Easy grader for lab reports. Quizzes are easy if you study what he tells you to study. Fun to talk to outside of class.

Professor Wei is a very reasonable man. Although he has a thick accent, I highly recommend taking his class and visiting him during office hours; he's always willing to help. If you do all of the work, attend class, and use his notes/powerpoints to study, you'll do fine. I never used the book and ended up receiving an A in the class. Go Wei!

Wei is a great teacher in Chem Department. He really cares that we got good grades. He is always willing to answer questions. He helped me a lot in his office hours. He always give students practice for exams. You have to review and understand the material to do well on the tests.

He's incredibly helpful during office hours and his handouts and exam guides are awesome!  Recitation is straightforward - the class isn't easy but he really cares about his students succeeding and encourages tutoring for struggling students.
Only take him if you can't take another professor
Not a good Class to take as a lecture with this guy

I got a good grade in this class, but it was hard work. 30/99 students in my class failed this semester, so take that for what its worth. There's opportunities to raise your grade throughout the semester (which I needed after failing the second test), and despite his accent, he is a very good teacher. Also very approachable during office hours.

Dr. Wei is a great professor. He will do anything to help students succeed. He lets you make up tests, quizzes, and allows a cheat sheet for the final. In order to be successful you really need to attend class and study. I took him twice, and I could have passed the first time if I went to class,showed more effort. He makes chem understandable.

Wei is awesome. Everyone who is complaining on here just couldn't have been doing the work. The class itself is somewhat challenging but he does everything to help his students out. Sample exams are enormously helpful...study them for tests and you'll be fine.

Professor Wei does his best to make the class as easy and understanding as possible for his students. He offers great notes and tells you exactly what will be on a quiz or exam, which makes it sooo easy to pass if you review the material he tells you to. Also, he offers exam samples which are so similar to the actual exam! He is a great professor!

I dont see why everyones complaining. this was not an easy class, but Professor Wei does everything in his power to help you pass. gives take home quizes, drops your lowest exam grade, and offers tutoring. good professor! no complaints!

The only people who complain about this Prof are the ones who do not want to put in the hours req. to do any college level Chem course.
